Mth103 as a whole is a disaster at MSU, so I wouldn't put all of the blame on the professors. Professor Allen tries her best to teach this content however, the time constraints of this class make it difficult to thoroughly develop an understanding. GO TO OFFICE HOURS, she is willing to reexplain content that you might be struggling with. LOTS of HW

The lectures were long and unengaging, Professor Allen would often make mistakes and erase or correct the notes that she was teaching us. The class used iClicker, so attendance was mandatory and graded. I did not enjoy this class at all, content was barely taught but was expected to be known. One of the worst classes I have taken.
Hard professor to understand. There are better and easier professors to understand. The homework was heavy and not easy to understand. She was also very short with you.

Really important to participate in class! Prof. Allen is such a sweetheart and is SUPER passionate about the content she teaches. Her students are mostly freshmen (I am a junior) and they don't realize that professors can make errors sometimes. She's super helpful with questions! I'm someone who NEVER understood math until she taught it to me!

I would avoid this class. Sue Allen is a nice professor, but she constantly messes up in lectures and her students have to correct her, making the whole lecture 10x more confusing. The homework (webwork) wasn't related and was much harder than what we learned in class. Used photo math for the majority of the semester. Practice exams were the exams
Took MTH 103. Too fast, did not explain a lot of things thoroughly. Came in with a basic knowledge of algebra, but wanted to take it again to review before going into 124. Did not help.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: old school in my opinion, nice lady, not so great teacher, very fast pace

Professor Allen was kind of all over the place. She was very sweet and I really like her as a person, but not as a teacher. There were quizzes basically every Friday, and I always had to watch the recorded lessons from the other professor because she either never finished the notes in class, or didn't really explain anything thoroughly.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Almost never paid attention to classes but managed my way through by watching the online lectures that is provided for everyone, really recommend them if you're slow with math like me. She replied to emails and had a very clear criteria that included the ability to drop 2 out of 7 categories including exams. Webwork doesn't help so dont rely on it.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Lots of homework, as well this teacher would always go ahead of what the quizzes would be on , taking this class just piles more and more stress like never before, students would always have to correct the professor as well, this class was required for me and I would never take it again.

Sue Allen is a super nice professor!  However, I probably would not take one of her classes again.  She would constantly make mistakes on the notes and a student would have to raise their hand and correct her.  She is a bit dull but I don't think that is her fault because the class is super hard to make fun and engaging.
make sure to go to office hours and it is more helpful than the lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The class is a lot harder than what you would expect for a college algebra. Lots of homework and quizzes pretty much every Friday which gets really overwhelming. Also, I had a university approved absence form and she was my only prof who would not give me an excused absence, even though the university approved it

Ahhhhh, this might've been the worst class i've ever taken. Be prepared future students. If you're not already good at math, you will not get any better while taking this class. If you don't want to tank your gpa because of one class.. get a private tutor. Everybody knows at MSU that the math department is a travesty, i just became another victim.

If you are taking MTH 103, Sue Allen is the go-to professor. Out of all the math professors, Sue Allen is the nicest, most considerate, easiest, and most helpful. She helps you prepare for quizzes and exams better. She will go out of her way to help students. You have to pay attention and not talk to people around you because she'll call you out.

Professor Allen is such a sweet lady and she definitely cares about the well-being of her students. But this class was really hard for an algebra class. Students are given quizzes every single Friday before even having the chance to actually learn the material. Be prepared to spend hours of your free time trying to pass this class.

Professor Allen is a nice professor who is available outside of class for help during her office hour and holds review sessions. However, students are given quizzes every single Friday before they're able to even digest the information that is blown through in class. Additionally, the exam reviews are never representative of the actual exam.

For an algebra class it was surprisingly difficult. The lecture do not do much teaching, rather they are just the professor quickly rushing through a page of notes. There is lots of online homework, and quizzes and or exams very often. This class requires an unnecessary amount of additional learning outside of class.

This was a tough class but don't be scared by the other reviews. I went to MLC like once-twice a week and attended office hours before her exams and am passing with a 4.0. You will need to print out the notes and iclicker into class but you can drop two categories that go to your grade which can be exams! Webwork is very hard for no reason though.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Absolutely horrible. Never explains anything thoroughly, but has many opportunities for hep like office hours. Rambles in her lectures so much that sometimes you have to watch the rest of the lecture through video. If I had to take this class again, I would take it with Shannon Griffen.

There is no leniency whatsoever for attendance but the lectures are pointless. Everything is on webwork, w/ 4 exams and a quiz almost every week. She's so boring, it's so bad and I've fallen asleep in this class more than I've learned in it. Assignments are due at 7 am which is the weirdest thing I've ever heard of. You have to pay to do homework.

The worst professor I have ever had forgets what she is doing every 5 minutes attendance is mandatory even though you're listening to a brick wall give information thinks students fail assessments because they don't go to office hours even though most peers I know end up back at their dorms and watch a different professor teach it via lecture video

She is an elderly woman who needs to retire. Makes a lot of mistakes during lecture, such as writes and solves problems incorrectly, and forgets to turn on her microphone until reminded by a student. Does not allow more than four unexcused absences or she will automatically fail you and all lectures/recitations take attendance.

hardest math class ever with short confusing lectures. webwork did not help learn material, exams are difficult. failed first 2 exams even with 100% on all assignments and attending MLC, ended up failing the class because i couldn't get 100% on the others. math department at msu needs work.

Honestly, she isn't the worst professor I've had, but the Math Department at MSU is lacking. Classes last too short to absorb any useful information, and by the time you're done you walk out knowing nothing. It's not difficult if you're able to go over the information yourself, (and make corrections to stuff she did wrong,) but it wastes time.

-SUCH A HARD CLASS 
-attendance taken via iclicker 
-if you don't go to class you miss so much
-webwork is horrible
-does not explain how you get to an answer or why it works the way it does

Just show up to lectures and do your webwork and its a fairly easy class.
-She's a pretty easy teacher but an easy class
Easy class, pay attention.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Professor was very nice and thorough, however, you have to pay to access the homework and pay to print out the notes because it's basically required if you want to pass the class. And attend MLC. The online program, webwork, is often different/harder material than covered in class. Overall very angering, but the material isn't hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class was unusually difficult. This class is a pre-rec for stats and stats is 10000x easier. The lectures move super quickly and are not easy to understand. All of the concepts should be easy but they are way overexplanined and overcomplicated. Not to mention there are tests every single friday. If you can test out of this class DO IT.

I hope to never have to take her again. Her exams were never like the study guides or homework we had every week. I went to office hours and she wasn't helpful whatsoever. I wouldn't recommend her at all.
She is very kind and always wants to help students. She loves to get questions and feedback about her lectures. Just go to lecture and do the homework and you'll be fine!
She wants you to do well, you just have to put in time.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Allen was very kind and seemed willing to help me if I needed it, but her lectures consist of her quickly filling out notes, with very little explaining. I am a step by step learner with math, and Sue pretty regularly neglected to clearly explain what she was doing. Anyone can read over completed math notes. I needed someone to TEACH me

Professor Allen was nice but since the lectures are so big it's not easy at all to build any connections. I think she taught well during the lectures. You don't have to go to the lectures but the class will be a lot harder so you should go. If you don't study or work hard, you'll barely pass or even fail so you really have to put the work in.
She works really well with student-athletes. Is always available and helpful in office hours. Super calm and kind!

Prof Allen really cares about her students and she stays after almost every lecture to help students who have questions. However, if you do not put in the effort to actually learn the material then the homework will be very difficult to complete. All lectures are recorded, so attendance isn't necessarily mandatory but is heavily encouraged.

MTH103 is a large class at MSU so it is hard for Professor Allen to make connections with the students unless they reach out to her. With hard work and studying you CAN 4.0 this class. I attended the math learning center and focused on the first 3 exams so I did not have to take the cumulative final.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Bad teacher lectures are forever long and if you miss even one you can guarantee yourself that you will fail the quiz. So much stuff to do and you have to take time out of you testing to scan your documents turn them into a pdf and turn them in online. I would never take her again she was absolutely no help. If you can't learn fast don't take it.

I took more notes in class than I learned. If you want to learn whats going on, you need to go back and read your notes over and over again, or watch another professors videos. Her class is also very fast-paced. Then theres all the hw on webwork, which has a registration fee. Ive also pulled all-nighters studying for exams.

If you want to do well go to lectures and print the notes she gives you. You can also drop 2 quizzes and 1 exam which is nice. She teaches at a very good pace, just stay on top of notes and homework and you will be fine. Definitely recommend and would take again.
I had to watch the other teachers lectures to understand the material.

I never went in person to a lecture, because I could never pay attention. Exams and quizzes are always more difficult than what you learn in lecture, no extra credit, but you do get drops. Overall wasnt the worst ever, but wouldnt recommend if you cant learn at a fast rate!

I am usually a straight A student and this was the only class I didn't 4.0 in. Ruined my gpa. If you don't attend her lectures (which are fast and don't help at all) you have to watch Griffens lectures which made me feel like I was having a stroke. My TA was also no help. Tests every Friday and no matter how hard you study you will never get 100%.

I would rather eat a bowl of gravel than take this class again. Instructor just does rather than teaches. If you bring the printed notes to class and are trying to follow along and fill them out, good luck. She goes so fast, by the time you're done copying down a problem, she'll be half way through the next one. Tests are WAY harder than the notes.
This class is taught by amazing teachers, but I think that the curriculum was horrible.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Whatever you do, please do not take this class. I ended with a B average in math in high school and now I am fighting for my grade and spend almost every night crying. The math department does not care about you at all. The tests are significantly harder than what we are taught and Prof Allen gets rude if you ask her simple questions. STAY AWAY.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I was advised to take this course, but I didn't listen and im fighting for my life before my exam tomorrow at 10:20. Do not take this course please listen
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Sue Allen wasn't a terrible teacher but the entire MSU math department needs work. The lectures are 10x easier than what is actually on the quizzes and exams and the 20 minute time limit for quizzes and 50 minute time limit for exams are so stressful and adds more pressure to a class I already struggle in.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Prof Allen isnt too bad. She helps with webwork if needed and answers general questions. About half the exams/quizzes are nothing like the notes or reviews though and its frustrating.

You do not need to attend lectures, and all notes are posted outside of class. If you are not familiar with the material already you need to attend lecture! All exams have reviews that are almost identical to the tests. Next to impossible not to do well in this class.

Ive never struggled in math before until Math 103. Prof. Allen DOES NOT CARE if you fail. Lack of communication and help in this class. Tests are significantly harder than any review that is given, almost like she wants you to fail. I would NOT recommend taking this class at MSU and I will be retaking the course at my local community college.
She is the worst professor at MSU. She doesnt care if you pass or fail and she can't teach anything. Don't take her class.

So, I am taking this math course for the first time my first year here and a lot of people agree that her teaching is to fast to the point where you can't retain any information. I have always been a straight A student, but somehow have a D in her class. As a professor I think she's ok, but she can d better for sure. She doesn't really explain.

The instruction was hard to understand at best, completely incompetent at worst, The TA and Professor are not hard-working when it comes to review and aren't even making a comprehensive review for the final, there was content on the last exam that wasn't in the lectures, recitation, or review. I wish I had more space to say what I want to.
doesnt teach you and blames the students for failing, i am currently in this course and I know maybe 2 people who are passing

The class overall shouldn't have been as hard as it was, I did not talk to one person throughout the semester that was doing well in the class, so clearly there's a problem deeper than our understanding of the class. Also, the fact she isn't providing an exam prep for the final is absolutely ridiculous. Good luck if you have to take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Loved Professor Allen, probably the professor that cares about her students the most out of all the professors I have had. She goes out of her way to send review videos, finished notes, and ensure her students are ready for upcoming exams and quizzes. Not a super difficult class just follow along with notes and spend some time reviewing before exam

Do not take mth 103 with Sue Allen. This is the hardest class I have ever taken and I ultimately ended up dropping it. Quizzes and exams are very difficult and nothing like the study guides given. Beware

This class was the best. I took ap calc in high school but wanted an easier math class and this was the move for sure. She was so understanding and accommodating in regards to the pandemic. Had a bunch of help sessions and answered emails fast if you needed extra help. Definitely a teacher who wants her students to succeed, 10/10 would recommend.

One of the toughest Professors I had this past semester. Homework was easy to understand and get credit for but the exams were graded tough-every little detail to each problem needed to be shown. However, the lectures were really easy to understand. Technology issues were not excusable in the class (which was difficult when everything was online)

Prof Allen was helpful considering how many help sessions she held before exams but she simply made MTH103 way harder than it needed to be. She has little sympathy for her students and was very rude to me and those who had questions during recitation. The exams were quite difficult and the webwork was brutal. Avoid class if you struggle with math!
I had quizzes every week except for the weeks I had exams! Lectures not mandatory but recitation is!

Professor Allen is extremely nice and caring. If you need help she is more than willing to help during office hours. Print out the weekly lecture notes on D2L because it is easier to follow along. Make sure to study your lecture notes before exams and quizzes. There was a lot of homework, but just use piazza and it can be really easy.
She rushes through everything and notes from class aren't easy to apply to homework or quizzes
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: Prof Allen is getting a bad rep because she teaches math and most students struggle with math. I wouldn't avoid her just because the class is difficult. As long as you show up and put in work outside of class you should be ok. The webwork is difficult but is more about practicing the material. Her class isn't easy, but college isnt meant to be easy
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Allen is not a good math teacher. She was not deliberate in lectures and made many mistakes in her work, which caused me to be very confused. She also assigns a lot of webwork assignments that are much harder than the problems we did in lecture. The worst part was, she's so bad that her OH are always full and its hard to get help. AVOID.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She does know what she is doing, however, she goes through the notes to fast or too slowly and will try to speed it up towards the end if shes running behind which leads you to learning nothing about what is being taught.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: She puts all of the notes online and quizzes are fairly easy. She gave exam #3 six days before the final exam. She also gets very upset people talking in class and would call them out for it.

I went to every single lecture, recitation, review session, and frequented the MLC along with having a paid tutor -- and even still struggled greatly in the class. Allen goes through lecture materials way too quickly and ends up making multiple mistakes. Lectures, webwork, quizzes, practice exams, and actual exams are all so different. Avoid.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Sue Allen knows her math. She moves quickly because she's been doing this for ages. It is your job to show up to class and try. There are times when she accidentally flips the screen or forgets to move her paper down while teaching--which is frustrating. However, focus on the notes for exams and not the webwork and you will pass with a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: If I could give her anything lower I would. She doesn't know hoe to teach. half the time the paper isn't even visible to the class and she is always making mistakes. She always makes you teach yourself and ends up hitting you with a hard test. DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS UNLESS YOU WANNA DIG YOURSELF IN A HOLE!!!!! p.s. she can be very rude sometimes

Professor Allen is a very nice person, awful teacher. I never skipped a lecture, went to MLC weekly, and had a paid tutor. Her reviews that she gives aren't even made by her and not even remotely close to her exams. She goes through lectures too fast and the problems she shows us are the easiest ones possible. If you take her prepare to fail.

Sue Allen is a less than mediocre professor. She moves through her lectures VERY fast and does not care to go back to any of the concepts to make sure you understand. She always overcomplicated very simple concepts during lectures. This class is not graded on a curve so you have to work for your grade. I basically taught the whole class to myself.

I would not recommend Professor Allen to anyone and would avoid taking her course(s). She moves extremely quickly through her lectures and often makes several errors while teaching the material. She also focuses too much on how many people are showing up to class rather than putting her energy into properly teaching the students that do show up.

If you have the choice, don't take Professor Allen's class. She makes you feel stupid for asking questions and often makes mistakes while solving problems during the lecture. She makes simple concepts complex and moves through lectures very fast because it's more important that she covers as much material as possible than if students understand it.
She gets really irritated if you ask her a basic question and she goes over notes REALLY fast
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She teaches "simple" things that most students have learned already in high School. This should make her class an easy A, but the way she teaches makes everything more confusing. Some examples of this are when a girl raised her hand asking how she got a certain number in a problem she said "I Just know that"
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: Sue Allen is a good professor that gives students every opportunity to succeed. In-class lectures give you a base knowledge of subjects but the webwork includes more challenging problems that you will likely have to get help with. As long as you come to class and do the homework the class should be a breeze.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Excruciatingly long and boring lectures with weekly homework that can barely be completed and has little to do with what she teaches. She's not very sympathetic and refused to round up .01% to go up a grade. She doesn't take attendance and that's probably the only good thing I can say about her.
Get ready to teach yourself because she'll show easy examples of how to find the solutions but then the webwork will be completely different. Thank god the quizzes were easier.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: If you took precalculus in high school, this class should be fairly easy. Quizzes every Friday, though, and a lot of Webwork. Webwork was probably the worst thing about this class honestly, exams were not too bad as long as you look over your notes. Class is not really that necessary, all of the notes are online.

Sue Allen is a great choice for a math teacher! I loved the was she taught and how she really cares if you do well in the class. She actually cares if you understand the material being taught. I highly recommend taking her class!
Lots of webwork and quizzes, tough grader when it comes to credit on those quizzes, but she does want you to succeed.

Lectures are hard to pay attention to. She skips over a majority of the notes and doesn't go into detail on what she does cover. Cares more about completing notes in time than if the class actually understands. WebWork is nothing like the content of the notes or the exams. If you don't do good on the exams you're screwed bc that's half your grade.

Professor Allen is AMAZING. She's such a sweet lady who is an amazing teacher!! She's always willing to help, and she explains everything thoroughly. She cracks jokes during class and as boring as math can be, her class is the opposite!

Really good teacher. I barely went to class and thats on me but you really dont need to. All of the notes are online and you can watch videos to understand things you didnt get from the notes. Good at office hours and very helpful. Super sweet lady.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: One of best online professors I have ever had. Her lecture videos are very helpful. And the exam questions come from everything she teaches. if you are required to take MTH 103, I highly recommend you take her online summer class to get those credits out the way. I could go on and on explaining how great prof. Allen is but Im going to stop here :)

Math is hard! But prof Allen makes it easier to understand and takes it slow, it also helps that shes funny and relates the material to something interesting about MSU most of the time! Make sure you print out the notes and bring them to class, those are very helpful with the online homework and studying for quizzes and exams
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Liked her abs a person and a prof, it felt like she really wanted everyone to succeed. The class wasnt very challenging but it was a lot of homework and quizzes all the time.

They drop the 2 lowest quiz grades and they're easy. Exams are NOTHING like the practice exams. Exams consist of random things and the WebWork is easily the worst part. You could study for hours and get 70's, which i guess is good since it was normal for people to get 30's. My TA was much more helpful and clear when explaining. Textbook isn't used
Got a 4.0 and never bought or used the textbook. Not hard stuff if you pay attention in lecture. WebWork was horrible though, worst part about the class.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She will not budge on final grade even if 0.1% away. Ruined my GPA
She's really sweet and helpful and has good pace during lectures.
This class is just super boring in general. Unless you live and breath math then you probably won't get a kick out of this one. Allen is nice nothing really negative to say about her.

She speeds through the lectures but is willing to stop and answer any questions. I like that she thoroughly explains subjects but as someone who naturally struggles with math I asked her a lot of questions after class and usually fell behind. The WebWork was due way too often and I always felt behind. I spent a lot of time in the MLC.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Sue is a really great teacher. Thorough, easy to understand, is willing to take questions, and wants you to do well. I strongly recommend her.

For this being my first college math class, Professor Allen did a great job of making the transition from high school math to college math as smooth as possible. The material is difficult at times, but her lectures are easy to follow and she teaches at a good pace. She is a sweet lady and makes math as enjoyable as it can be!
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She was kind and joked in class but also got stuff done. She always explained the material very clearly. Overall enjoyed the class and loved Mrs. Allen.

sue allen is the best math professor that i have ever taken! she is so caring and will respond to any questions that you may have. although I'm not doing well in the class (I'm not good at math), she makes it easy to learn! i would take her again!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Caring

reviews.comment: Prof. Allen is very sweet and nice. Her lectures are not quick but not too slow. She usually keeps pace with her timeframe so don't expect getting out early. 4 exams and 1 final, 7 quizzes, and 28 homework assignments. If you don't make it to class, you can get the lecture notes at MLC.

Professor Allen is great! She's always happy and you can always ask her for help (or the MLC, they're great). Her notes and lecture are EXTREMELY helpful and she's willing to explain things as many times as you need. The work load isn't bad at all, and if you just do the practice problems and the math homework, you'll be fine on tests and quizzes.

She's a great teacher. I am terrible at math but I still managed a 3.0. I suggest attending all lectures and taking notes. Do webwork the same day as the lecture so it is easier. If you struggle GO TO OFFICE HOURS or MLC! She will help you with your homework and with your grades. She gives practice problems that aren't due, but really help.
Very clear lectures, lenient, hilarious ( a must for an early class), and her grading is very fair.
Sue Allen is a very good professor for MTH 103! She is very helpful and has a great sense of humor! Easy to get along with and have a conversation with. Would definitely recommend her for MTH 103!

I really like Sue Allen as a person and a teacher. If you have to pick a professor for math I would recommend her. My only complaint about the course was that we had a test or quiz every week, so if you are not good at testing chose a different course.
Sue Allen is very good at explaining how to do problems and has good notes that will help you loads with webwork and tests. Always there to help before and after lectures, very friendly woman.
Makes constant mistake during lectures, exams often have questions not covered in the sections.

Sue Allen is my favorite professor! She is very helpful and clarifies everything if needed! Everyone knows she is a sweetheart! Her lectures are short. Her class was very easy for me as well. I didn't buy the textbook and you definitely do NOT need it. (unless you really struggle with math, because it just gives extra problems)
Really easy class, pretty much just review everything learned in high school. Don't buy the book unless you really need help with math. I like the professor a lot, she's very sweet and funny.

She's a really awesome person and there to help. As long as you go to the lectures and take notes you'll do fine. A practice exam is sent out before every exam and it's just like the exam except with different numbers. Easy 4.0 DO NOT BUY THE TEXTBOOK, SHE EVEN SAYS YOU DON'T NEED IT

Sue is a great lady and a great teacher.  The class itself is rather fact-paced, but she makes it manageable, and the overall material is not too hard; there is just a plethora of material at all times.
I loved her! She is awesome. Phenomenal teacher and makes the cutest stupid jokes.

The class isn't necessary to go to as long as you get the notes. Make sure to do the webwork, it's pretty easy and helps a lot. The textbook is very unneeded except for the practice exams, so make a friend who has it. Go to all quizzes, the recitation does help!
I loved Sue Allen. She is very personable and very approachable. She answers any questions you might have and she explains the notes well.
Doesn't review and flies through the lectures! AWFUL!!!!!! DONT TAKE! Recitation is quizzes and exams and unless you get a great TA you will never review! DONT TAKE!

I love Sue Allen. She is my favorite professor. She makes math easy and fun. She always cracks jokes, and makes me laugh. She has the cutest dimples and the softest smile. She makes me want to take MTH 103 twice. I love her so much. I definetly recommend her!
Funny, friendly, great.
Easy class to pass if you just show up for notes. Really nice teacher, explains everything. Don't need the text unless you want extra help.
Easy class. As long as you attend most lectures you are likely to get a good grade. Allen is willing to answer any questions and explain material.

Super friendly. Didn't use my book once. Sends the notes to you and makes them organized, all you have to do is fill them in. Tends to rush at the end of lecture. I hate math but she made it less painful and is always available to help.

Tends to spend unnecessary amounts of time on some sections and always ends up rushing her lesson as time starts running out. However, all in all, she's a good professor who is clear and straightforward!
Very nice and helpful lady.  I do not enjoy math, but she made the experience a good one.
She's an awesome teacher. Explains things really well. I def. recommend her for MTH 103!
Sue Allen is a very good math teacher, all you really have to do is the book work and you'll do good on her tests.

I love this professor. She's hilarious and makes cheesy jokes during class but it's still funny because she's just hilarious in general. She makes math easy to learn and she's extremely nice if you ask her a question, i love her. go sue!!
I love Professor Allen! She makes trig fun

Hate math, but if you fail or do poorly, it's your own falut. She's a great teacher and provides great resourses...notes emailed to you to be printed out the day before class so you don't have to write a lot during lecture
Sue Allen is the most awesome teacher I have ever had.  You should definitely take her if you have to take any math classes.
math sucks but i couldnt ask for a better teacher
If math is your weakest link, take sue allen.  She is so sweet.

Take this teacher. Mrs. Allen is the greatest college teacher I have ever had. She explains things so you can understand why. She is also just a genuinely nice person and fun to be around. I strongly recommend taking her whenever possible.

If you have trouble with math, get Professor Allen. She is a really nice person, and she even helps students out in the Math Learning Center, so if you don’t understand the subject you can get help later on right from your professor. She is a very dedicated teacher.
Professor Arnold is awesome! The class is well structured, and Professor Arnold gives very thoughtful feedback on assignments. I would definitely recommend this class to others.
Great person! Really cares and loves hearing your ideas. Interested in your work and always opened to talk.

Amazing amazing amazing. Super down to earth and relatable to students. Understanding of missing class and gives opportunities to make up points. Big project at the end of the course/throughout but I really enjoyed working on it
Best prof I have had so far at MSU. He truly cares about women's voices and the subject he teaches. Is very flexible with deadlines and cares about his students. Would take him again 100000%

Professor Arnold is a great teacher and you can tell they genuinely care about their students. It's really easy to get a 4.0 as long as you complete the assignments. Probably the easiest class I've ever taken and doing the assignment each week didn't take much time.

Professor Arnolds class was amazing. I felt that the material was good and easy to understand, and they were extremely understanding for every single assignment that there was. There were also no exams! Overall I really liked this class.

Pat is the sweetest professor I've ever had. They clearly want all of their students to succeed and make it a point to get to know every student individually. It's very easy to 4.0 their classes as long as you complete your assignments!

Course was easy and the professor was very caring and tried to make connections with students. I personally took this class for an honors credit so I didn't enjoy it too much, but i can see how others would. A lot of videos and readings. 1 major assignment a week with readings, articles, and ted talks to inform. Spent an hour a week for this class.

Prof Arnold is easily the best professor I've ever had at MSU. So knowledgeable, welcoming, caring. Everything you could ask for. Content of  class was so interesting and I honestly can say I learned a lot. Two in person participation projects required but are up to you as far as topic/event. Was fun and worth it in the end. Nothing bad to say!
very kind and understanding. easy assignments
Pat was super accommodating and did everything in their power to make sure every student succeeded. Very easy, but interesting, class.

Professor Arnold is one of the most caring professors you will have at msu. They are very understanding and want you to succeed. There is an assignment due every other week that only takes about 30 minutes or less to do and 2 campus event reflections. As long as you complete your work the best you can, they will give you a 100!

Super nice and actually cares for students. The class itself is super easy and has discussion questions every other week which is really nice. Grading is super lenient and the assignments and readings are easy. Sometimes you do not even half to read the book to answer the discussion questions. Honors option is also easy.

i took this as an elective, easiest class ever. prof arnold is very sweet and such a nice person. this class requires little to no work. there's 1 online assignment a week that normally took ab 10-20 mins. u have to go to 2 events of ur choice during the semester. no tests/final. love prof arnold!!!

Pat is a wonderful and easygoing instructor. They only have a few assignments per week that are based off of the assigned readings. Attendance can be made up each week by doing an extra assignment. They are also very helpful and their lectures are super interesting.

Pat is an amazing professor. They assign weekly homework but it is all engaging and may include readings, documentaries, videos, poems, etc. This was my favorite class and I appreciated the flexibility. If you were to miss a class you could do a small extra assignment from home, but the class is so engaging that you won't want to miss!
He is a really nice professor and doesn't assign any quizzes or exams. The modules he assigned had really interesting videos and articles. He gives lots of extra credit during class.

He's a genuinely nice person, and makes it known that he wants everyone to feel as comfortable as possible in his class. There's one weekly assignment, and most of them are opinionated, so I'm honestly not even sure what he grades,
reviews.rating_tags: Caring--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: They are so nice and you can tell he really cares and is passionate about the subject matter. The class is easy (basically never any tests, just discussion posts) and consists of a lot of in class discussions.

He did a wonderful job conveying the topics. This is an easy class for anyone who regularly engages on topics of gender/sexuality in their personal life + a good intro for anyone that doesn't. He's super understanding and genuinely wants you to succeed. The assignments help you understand the content better, which is almost never the case.
She is very understanding towards students and is passionate about her teaching.

I loved having Professor Flaim as my professor for MC 201. She can be a difficult grader and rarely gives "perfect" scores, but she will help you improve your work if you're struggling. Note: Her office hours can be busy. She's passionate about the topics she teaches, make sure to participate in class!!

Flaim is nice, and passionate. She cares for her students and her cause. However, she frequently goes off topic, which stops you from getting answers to your questions. She also grades harder than the other professors so you will probably have to work harder than your fellow classmates.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: felt incredibly babied and micromanaged. Specifically, rubrics had word counts for specific paragraphs, and she would walk around the lecture to make sure you were paying attention and didn't have your laptop out were some things that really pissed me off. She brought valuable information to class but I felt  like I was in 6th grade
Changed my mind, avoid.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Respected

reviews.comment: Tough grader, all readings are similar and not interesting. The class is tailored for specific people. It is unorganized and good luck getting a 4.0 on a paper. Her grading makes absolutely no sense. Would not wish this on my worst enemy
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Flaim is perhaps the greatest professor I have ever had the privilege of taking. She understands that students have a life outside of her class, and adjusts due dates accordingly.  There are a TON of readings , but it is JMC so it's not surprising, and she takes a while to answer emails but she is very busy so also not surprising!

Her classes are hard, I wont lie, but she cares for her students and will hold your hand throughout the whole process. If you are really trying, it is never too late to try again. I once did poorly on a paper and because I went to office hours to ask for help she just offered to let me rewrite it. I didn't even ask. Take her classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Flaim is one of the most helpful and respected professors at James Madison. Yes, her grading can be difficult. However, every JMC class holds its students to high standards. The difference with Flaim is you will receive the grade that you earn. If she sees you're putting effort in and show interest to learn she will make sure you succeed.
Best start to JMC at state I've had!! Tough grading for sure, but she'll help you through it!
Very very caring and will work with you if you happen to miss a big assignment, I missed a midterm and she let me write a paper to make it up even though I missed it for a dumb reason.

Professor Alan is an all-around good guy, and I genuinely enjoyed waking up and going to his class. While the tests can be challenging sometimes, if you do the proper studying beforehand, then you should be well prepared. Always in a good mood and made learning interesting, relevant, and fun. Would highly recommend him to anyone.
Best professor I have had at MSU so far

Very nice and chill but the class is literally three exams that are mostly on the insane amount of readings he gives you. If these were assignments it might have been easier but if you do bad on one test you're cooked.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He's okay, seems like a good guy, but the way he teaches is just droning on and on, as well as going off topic often. This class is graded mostly by exams (75%), which makes it especially irritating when he doesn't fully cover some topics in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is a nice guy, but his lectures often bored me but it would be amazing for someone really interested in geography, he knows what he's talking about. Grade is based on 3 exams and attendance, and 10 extra points if you visit his office hours. No textbook required and attendance is mandatory.

There are 3 tests throughout the course which are the only assignments in the class. The class is super lecture heavy, but the professor goes off on tangents that aren't relevant a lot so it's important you pay attention because his slides also don't give you a lot of information. He's a great person though and super nice and do the class readings!
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Love this professor as a person, however his class is literally graded on 3 exams and attendance. His exams aren't overly difficult but if you make one too many mistakes it can easily jeopardize your grade.

By far my favorite teacher at MSU! He is so excited to teach each class, which makes it enjoyable. The only issue I have with him is that there is no study guide, and the grade is based on 3 tests. This caused me to get a lower grade than I wanted because the exams were so random. I learned a lot and genuinely loved coming to that class every day.

Very very hard course for an ISS 200 level. He's an interactive and nice Prof. I would save yourself the trouble and take a different course. Way too hard to be a 200 level. You have zero assignments, class is based off 3 exams (including the final.) The exams can be tricky and require lots of studying. You must attend every class! No curve either!
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: by far my favorite professor. I was really sad to hear he is retiring, but it is well deserved. He has been at it for a long time. He really did make learning more enjoyable. Thank you, Dr. A.
He was a good professor just talked a lot and if you aren't interested in his subject you won't like his class. He can be fun and I like him as a person just wasn't a huge fan of his class

Alan is bound to be one of your favorite professors during your time at MSU. He is full of life stories and makes the lectures fun. As for the way he has his course set up, your grade is based off of 3 exams so if you mess up on one of the exams, your grade is going to be iffy.

Dr. Arbogast is such a dad, in the best way possible. Content of the lectures were boring (if you're not into geography) but Dr. Arbogast made it 10x better. You're only graded on 3 exams and then attendance, but they're all easy as long as you take notes. Overall, Dr. Arbogast is a silly guy and his class is pretty easy.

I really enjoyed his class. He was very engaging and didn't take the GenEd too seriously, like I think many other professors who teach them do. He seemed to really care about his students and gave credit just for coming to talk with him at office hours. I also felt like the things he taught were actually worth learning.

There are many other easy ISS classes, good guy. Only three exams, no assignments. So if you do poorly on one, you fail. He does not know how to teach because he yaps the whole two hours. People leave in the middle of his lecture because he goes off topic.I had to teach myself everything, and his lecture slides are only pictures.TAKE A EASIER CLASS

Super nice guy and loves what he does. The course is basically geo and the concept are hard to connect as the course bounces all over. Only 3 exams so it everything he says matter. Lots of relevant content but class seemed to drag on quite a bit
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Arbogast is a girl dad to the core. He made the course so much fun and while blunt at times he was incredibly kind. He was very understanding about my test anxiety, and was overall very supportive. His class is lecture heavy and his slides are mainly pictures. So every word counts as anything he says is fair game for exams! 10/10 class!

only 3 exams. class was hard to connect but lots of real life examples that were relevant. be sure to do the outside reading as they are on the exams. you can tell he loves to teach but class can at times drag on

I loved this class and I loved Dr Arbogast! He made lectures super engaging, and he's really funny! You can also tell that he loves what he's teaching! The tests can be kind of tricky but overall not too bad! Just make sure you do the homework and show up to class for the points! If you have the chance to take a class with him I highly recommend!

He is a great guy but the class is doing to much to be a uni req. only three exams but they make up the entire grade so if you do poorly on one you are screwed. Also wasnt aware that it would be turned into a geo course when it was just labled as Big ideas iss.

Dr. Arbogast was pretty laid back, and easy to follow! His lectures were very straight forward and he used a lot of real world examples to reinforce the content he was teaching. Pretty easy as long as you show up to class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: This man is an absolute diva. I love him, he said the funniest things, I was always giggling. I highly recommend him he's a king and he's amazing.

This man is incredible. Never stops talking about his wife or his daughters, we love a girl dad. He gives his lectures with a good mix of actual knowledge and his personal life experiences. If you don't appreciate a woke professor, he's probably not for you, but I appreciated it. Love you Alan, keep up the good work king!

ISS205 is mainly a prerequisite, and the median grade across every professor for this course is a 4. However, he attempts to be different and unique by making this class harder than every other ISS. He believes a small percentage deserves a 4 even if everyone does well. This makes it completely unfair for his students compared to other professors

Arbogast was my favorite professor this semester! His lectures were really interesting, and he gave out study guides before each exam which were very helpful. He's very easy to talk to and understanding. If you attend lecture + pay attention and do the readings, an A is very possible.
Love this class! His lectures are engaging and really interesting to listen to

ISS 205 is mainly a prerequisite that students take, he makes it way to difficult and it is definitely not Big Ideas in Social Science. It should just be called current events and geography. He brings up current events and then gives his very liberal opinion on it attempting to brainwash other students.
Alan Arbogast is the man.

Really like the guy and his lectures, but I feel like he should make the lecture material actually available to the students instead of penalizing them for not retaining every word of what he says. His slides are basically just pictures, and are hard to make sense of after the class. His exams were fairly tough, but easy enough to get atleast an 80
He has good energy when giving lectures but the class just seems off. Should have just called it geology. He also loves brining up current events and giving his very liberal opinion on the situation.

I enjoyed this class a lot, especially gaining more knowledge about Michigan history as well as what is going on in other countries. I know that I would sometimes come late, but that didn't affect my learning, but I found that the exams were tough even though I would study in advance. Overall, great class and had a great semester with you!

Professor Arbogast is THE best professor I will ever have. His exams are hard and very outside-of-class video-heavy, but his lectures are entertaining if you interact. His wife is a mental health therapist, and it shows. A girl dad who loves what he's doing and is passionate about what he is teaching: if you can take a class with him, YOU MUST.

Professor Arbogast was really good at making class interesting and keeping the energy up.  He was a really fair grader, and would correct test answers if they were confusing and a lot of us got them wrong, blaming himself instead of students.

There's a decent amount of reading involved, but honestly you could probably get yourself to a 3.0 just by showing up and paying attention in class. Dr. A knows his stuff and is more than willing to answer questions for as long as necessary for the class's understanding. Great dude who really cares about his students!

Dr. A is one of the best professors i have had. He really cares about his students and his lectures are very engaging. Its easy to get an A in his class if you just listen in class and watch the videos or articles he posts for before class. He used top hat in my class and had 5 pop quizzes and took attendance but it wasn't daily.

Amazing professor, and definitely cares about his students. Grades very clear - 5 of 6 quizzes, 10 of 12 attendance dates. Very much girl-dad energy. Makes class fun, and enjoys what he's teaching. Would absolutely take again.

I took 206 online. There were no video lectures like in my other online classes. The lectures were made up of pages of reading. There was also a textbook to read from. Very reading heavy. Grade is made up of 14 quizzes (16 quizzes but 2 are dropped), 3 papers (pretty easy), and a final exam. The quizzes were tricky but open note and timed.

Online class taught entirely by the TA. Never communicated with Arbogast at all. Class was fairly easy. A few HW assignments and the exams were easy IF you read his notes. I briefly skimmed the notes, then copied and pasted all of his notes into a word document and used the search tool to look for relevant words. I 3.5 the class.
LOVE HIM! Very interesting, very enthusiastic, a bit difficult but totally worth it.
Great Teaching Style and Clarity. Course is relevant to current events and a must for anyone interested in understanding the interconnectedness of our planet.

I had him in sp. '96 and still remember how great he was. The class was very interesting.   I almost switched majors because of him and his class.  Also, very helpful during office hours.  Would highly, highly recommend.  (I only knew the course # because I kept the book (5th ed.) and the info is on the SBS sticker - that's how much I liked it!)
great teacher!
Very interesting! His tests are tricky but class is great.

I could say the class is easy, or I could say that the material was explained so well and so well understood that the class seemed easy. Isn't that the goal as a good teacher to ensure students are learning all the material? Mission accomplished.

Clearly passionate about her lectures and it shows, they were all super easy to follow and interesting. The 2 Homework assignments are fun and don't take much time. In class participation assignments create interesting discussions, and students go to the MSU bug house at the end of the semester!

I adored Professor Lorenz! She was incredibly gracious with RCPD extensions, and provided stress-free assignments. Every quiz and test was open resource, with plenty of time to complete the task. Lectures were engaging and interesting. She felicitated a calming learning environment while also sharing her passions. I cannot recommend enough!

LOVEDDD Dr. Lorenz! She is very sweet and funny. This is an online class held over zoom so you'll have daily in class participation and pre-class readings, 3 home work, and 4 exams (all of which are open note). Genuinely enjoyed this class as her lectures are interesting and you can tell she is passionate about her work. Could not recommend enough!

The class is good, it is pretty easy and she is clear on student expectations. Dr. Lorenz is kind and very passionate about what she teaches. The only thing I struggled with is the way she talked about politics and was pretty divisive

Prof Lorenz is super friendly and clearly loves teaching her class. All you have is 3 exams which are open note, 2 homework assignments, 3 question preclass quizzes, and in class participation. She teaches well so all the information is easy to understand and you visit the bug house at the end of the semester. 10/10 prof

Super fun and interesting class. Next to no homework, and what we had was super easy. Exams were open note. Lectures were collaborative and not just her speaking to us. Very good professor, really fun class. Would definitely recommend taking this class.

Dr. Lorenz has a clear system of operation for her class, but it is easy, fun, and engaging! She has a lot of love for the material she teaches and it shows. Her lectures are very clear and informative and the in-class participation that she requires is good review of topics discussed. Pay attention in lecture and you will do well :)
This class made me enjoy exploring the outdoors once again and I had so much fun admiring all the bugs she brought to class to shows us. 10/10 Professor
Amazing professor. Very obviously passionate about her work and the subject being taught and will always answer questions if asked.
Dr. Lorenz is a great professor. This class was an easy A, just attend the zoom meetings and do the participation. Slightly boring but you can tell Dr. Lorenz is very passionate about what she does.

Awesome prof and person. Super kind and receptive to questions. You can tell she's very passionate about entomology and that directly translates into her teachings. I wish grades were updated more frequently, but besides that, she's amazing.
Dr. Lorenz is a very kind professor and cares for her students. She let us take our exams at any time of the day and only had two homework assignments. She gives many points for attendance.

I have struggled in every high school science class I have ever taken and this teacher truly turned that around for me! She breaksdown concepts Into digestible material. if you really care about how you do, so will she. If you put in the work, you will do great. Definitely an easier science for those of you who are not chemists. Sweetest professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Seriously one of the best teachers I had. For being an online class, this teacher truly cares so much about her students. She is responsive on email an always accessible out of class. Science is not a strong subject of mine and I was nervous to take this class. I can seriously say I have learned so much and enjoyed it all because of this teacher!
I learned a lot of things about bugs in Dr. Lorenz's class both interesting and things that will be useful to know for my future career! The bug house visit was especially enjoyable!

Dr. Lorenz was great!! Her lectures are easy to follow, assignments are few and far between, and exams are short, usually multiple choice, and open note. You can tell she is very passionate about what she teaches, and is genuinely excited each day to teach her students. Would 100% take a class of hers again.

Before every class there is a reading quiz that is 3 questions based on a short article you have to read. In class there are in-class participation questions you have to answer in zoom chat. Every class is worth 5 points, and you get 3 drops, exams are online and open note, you have the entire day to complete it. Take this class for ISB requirement

Dr. Lorenz was by far one of the best professors I've had a Michigan State. She is very personable and her lectures are entertaining and engaging. Her passion about the material really shows and makes lectures really fun.

great professor. i took this biology course as an easy A, and i got that easy A. she's very laid back and relaxed and her teaching style reflects that as well. the material and the course is easy, and she's very caring and understanding. ellie was also a wonderful guest to see from time to time.

Great professor and cares about students doing well. You can trust that she has the best interest for everyone and the class is pretty easy. Weekly reading assignments that take less than 5 minutes, and a few exams that are online.

Amanda was so sweet and I loved listening to other lectures. Super short quiz with articles before each class, 2 homework assignments, and open note tests throughout the semester. My favorite part of the lectures was getting to see her dog Ellie. If you have the chance to take a class with Amanda, do it!

She is a great professor and I loved her lectures. The course is very easy and simple, but I still learned a lot. My only complaint is I do think she's a bit hard to reach which can be quite annoying as it was an async class. Though, nothing that was make or break with my grade.

Sooo nice and caring, clearly loves her students/teaching/this class, super fun lectures. Much of the class is completion/participation based, and the tests are very easy. The 2 major homework assignments are fun or at least not difficult. At the end of the semester you get to visit the MSU bug house during class hours which is so much fun.

Very lenient and caring, she asked what topics were difficult before the final exam and adjusted the exam accordingly. There's like 2 homework assignments, open-note tests, and participation grades. Took it online and she showed lots of enthusiasm for the material!
This class was interesting and would be hard to fail. Prof. Lorenz is awesome, funny, and a compelling lecturer. Visit the bug house!

She never answers emails. Like hellooo what's ur job. she takes forever to grade. not mean or difficult but she annoying for never answering my emails or anyone elses. like your not the president of the united states. andwer emails
Very good lectures, not much homework but the homework that you do have is honestly pretty interesting.

Prof Lorenz is the sweetest! She teaches clearly and answers any and all questions. She loves to discuss with students, and keeps lecture interesting. Attendance is needed because a lot of your grade is in class participation. There are extra credit opportunities, but you might not even need them! This class is fun and easy to pass.
Absolute gem, and her dog is adorable. Class is an easy A.

I loved her!! This class was somewhat interesting because she is so much fun and wants everyone to learn. She makes every class fun and she is also so funny so that makes it more fun. There are 3 exams which include the final. There are 2 homework assignments and before every class, you have to do an easy reading quiz. Super easy and she is so nice
Exams were easy and open note, Lorenz is a good lecturer and I never had a problem hearing back from her.
Class is extremely easy and she's lenient with her grading on the homework's. Class should be an easy 4.0

Took this class online for Fall 2021, one of the easiest class I had so far, there are 3 unit exams and 1 cumulative final exam in which are all open book test with multiple choice questions; theres one or two assignments, the assignments are easy to complete, took me about 20 minutes. Dont really require much memorization, just general concept.

She is a very good professor and easy grader. I had technical difficulties and emailed her and let me turn in the assignment late without grade deductions. Highly recommend taking her for ENT205 for the bioscience credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Prof. Lorenz is truly the most caring professor I have ever had! She truly cares about her students and their success. Very easy class- easy 4.0. Exams were open note/open resource. She is very willing to help students outside of class. Would take her class again!
I don't even really rate professors but she was so good I wanted to go on here and give a good rating :) Really easy class as long as you participate
Assignments are easy (at least online they are). Professor is so nice and understanding.

Lorenz is an amazing professor. Had her for ent205 and the class was very easy and I learned a lot. Never thought I would be interested in bugs but she makes it interesting and fun to learn. Most of the grade is determined on in class participation, so if you show up you're in good shape. She's very well educated and kind.

She is very caring and demonstrates her enthusiasm during her lectures. Combines videos along with her powerpoints to make the lecture more fun. Exams are easy as long as you study. She does not try to trick you on the exams they are quite simple. Easy 4.0 as long as you do the work.

Easiest ISB 201 class if you are willing to show up to class. She does in class papers for attendance (so easy you barely have to pay attention) basically your entire grade is attendance, a few 1 page papers & exams. The exams are very very easy. Super kind prof! Highly recommend her but dont get stuck in the 8:30am you wont make it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Knowledgeable on the subject, but not a good professor.  Lectures are disorganized and hard to understand, study guides are long and lazily put together, and will take forever to grade any assignments.  Don't waste your time or tuition money with him.

This class was not super easy, but not too hard either. As other students said, exams make up a massive portion of your grade, so attending lectures and studying are important. Content is easy to understand, and lectures are a bit lengthy, but he usually ends class early. I would recommend this course!

I definitely recommend this teacher. 
-no required book
-attendance is mandatory (for credit, lectures: really interesting/vital to attend to pass)
-Heavily based on the three exams throughout the semester but, as long as you attend class regularly, and study your notes for a couple days prior to the exam you should be all good.

Very knowledgeable and a great professor! Great examples and associations made to promote learning. Very straightforward. 60% lecture attendance required for 3% of your grade. The lecture slides have everything; that's why I rated him a 4 instead of a 5, because he doesn't really elaborate on the slides; class is very much him reading off slides.

Brascamp is a very nice prof, and his class is pretty interesting. This class does have A LOT of content, so the exams can be a bit hard, but if you study, you will do fine. 60% of the lectures are mandatory for attending and there are studies you have to do, but they are easy. Exams are massive part of grade, so beware of that

I'm not going to lie…the material of this class is very easy. I was able to get a good grade on tests without attending lecture. If you aren't a good test taker, go to class, and trust you will be fine. (lecture can feel very boring and lengthy)
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is one of the easiest psych classes I have ever taken. The professor doesn't go in-depth, which is nice since it keeps the exams really straightforward. It is test-heavy, but honestly, the questions are super easy if you actually study and understand the material. He uses PowerPoints, SO if you miss class, you can just read the slides.

Professor Brascamp knows what they are talking about, and the lectures are very interesting. There isn't a lot of homework, and the material is easy to understand. He is super funny and makes the lecture fun to listen to.

I would recommend. Yes this class is hard and yes it is mainly lecture + exam-based. Brascamp's slides are amazing though, he is the first psych prof I've had that I genuinely learned from. He is passionate, gives tons of visuals, and explains very everything well. For exams, if you just study the slides and make a quizlet, u will be just fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: His lectures are very heavy in content and he moves decently fast through the slides. He doesn't do much extra talking outside of what is written on the slide. Attendance isn't mandatory but you do get extra credit for going to class. There are no quizzes, just experiments and SONA. You'll definitely need to prepare for exams, but they're not bad.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Professor Brascamp is very knowledgeable, but I found his lectures to be quite monotone. The material is pretty interesting but complex. Graded pretty heavily on exams which I found to be pretty difficult. Could do Sona/online experiments for credit, which was nice, but if you don't do good on the exams then you have a low chance of doing well

The lecture slides did not help you on exams, 80% of your grade is exams. There is no curve, the study guides are long and not helpful for the exams. It's basically a copy and paste of the slides. Not a good professor
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: There are three exams and two homework assignments; if you're not a good test taker, I wouldn't recommend it. Attendance matters, but he also offers 4 points of extra credit. He's a nice guy, and he seems passionate about the material. I thought the material was interesting, but if you don't, this class will probably be a difficult snooze fest.

This class is all based on exams and attendance. If you fail one exam you're basically screwed over because there's barely anything else that goes into the grade book. Study guides are so unhelpful, notes are so hard to follow and the class material is so dry. Don't recommend this course.

The class is solely graded on exams. If you do poor on exams, you are not passing the class. No extra credit, his lectures are so confusing and the TA's notes do not help at all. No curve in grades, this was by far the worst class I have ever taken here. I'm an empath, so I feel Jan as a person, but this class was terrible.

I've had him for LIN 463 + PSY 200, both of his lecture slides are 80 pages long that jumble up the material. Theirs a disconnect between the exams & lectures, you are given a study guide but it's vague topics to focus on. Overall, I wish he was just more straight forward in his lectures to give better understanding since exams were difficult.
There are no quizzes or anything to aid in learning. Feedback is required for biological mechanisms to learn, no? Am I an ANN? No. So disappointed. In person tests that you don't get back.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Nice professor, but your grades are worth basically your whole grade and non are dropped so if you do bad on one exam you won't end with a good grade in the class. Rarely any homework since your homework is mainly just studying for the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: love him class was kinda hard for a psych class tho but prof brascamp is awesome and super understanding and supportive

Prof. Brascamp is nice, and seems passionate about the course. But 80% of your grade is on 3 exams, no quizzes or anything. Because of this, I at most could only get a 3.0 due to getting a 75% on the first exam. A lot of the exams too have poor question correlation compared to the study guides- (which are just paraphrased lecture notes).

Great guy and very accommodating and considerate, but lectures are entirely based on slides that are pretty much read off of word for word. On top of that, the exams are quite difficult in comparison to the content as a whole. You have to study a lot to succeed in this class, and even with that, the exams often include unexpected info.

Don't know what happened between now and the past, but the style he teaches in isn't great. Most of the information given is via slideshows, but the information cherry-picked for the exams tends to never appear in the study guide or slides. As with previous posts, he's a nice guy and clearly shows a passion for the field, but not a good professor.

Do not take this class. Prof is nice but formats the class in a way that makes it nearly impossible for ANYONE to do good in it. Every exam resulted in a 24-hour straight study session the day before only to still receive a very bad grade. On top of that, this class is only graded by exams a few assignments and sona so fail one exam and that's it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He clearly cares about his students doing well but unfortunately, it's very hard in this class. So much content for every exam that it's almost impossible to memorize, lectures are explained like we are experts already, study guides are almost always over 30 pages long, and the tests have a lot of topics that were not talked about during class.

please dont take this man... i dont care what you read before. i read reviews and thought it was going to be so great but honestly having mandatory lectures (aka you wont pass) then exams that are 80% of your grade combined with "study guides" that are literally exactly whats posted on the slides just isnt it. he is a nice guy, but not best prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: While the prof himself wasn't bad, the 3 exams are 80% of your final grade. You just have to try & memorize all the lecture slides. His "study guides" are given out a few days before the exam & are just the same things on his slides just on a doc.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures were super important for this class! If you took good notes, you will definitely do well on his exams. This semester, they were online, but I am not certain if they will remain that way. Overall, Professor Brascamp was awesome, and I enjoyed Cognitive Psych a lot more than I thought I would!
Professor Jan Brascamp was very nice and allowed extra credit through coglab. However, it is easy to mess up in this class there a were only 3 exams and they are very heavily weighted.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: 10/10 professor. His lectures can seem long & boring at times, but it goes by quick. In class, he'll read exactly what's on the slides, so you aren't required to go to class if you understand the material. This course is test heavy since you only take 3 quizzes and 3 exams, but Coglab quizzes are easy and exams are decent. Don't buy the book!
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: absolutely awful, the lectures are tedious and redundant. the information on the slides are no help during the exams. dont bother going to the in person lectures because he just reads off the slides and goes on and on and on.
Tried his absolute best to make the most he could out of the course. I missed a quiz opening by a few minutes and he was extremely accommodating. His honors option is also really fun!!

Great professor. Tried his best to make lectures interesting with everything being online and exams matched the lectures. If you paid attention during lecture then you'd do fine on the exam. Coglab quizzes were also super easy if you actually did them and took notes. He's also super flexible about moving test times around. Grade reflected effort.

I took this class all online fall 2020. This class is super interesting. Make sure you watch the lecture videos, they help so so much! Also I suggest taking notes on the slides before you watch the lectures then add to your notes. The book isn't necessary at all. Professor Brascamp is super nice and the TA's are great. Definitely recommend.
I loved Brascamp. His lectures can be dry at times but he's really good at getting all of the information across. I definitely want to take him again if I get the chance. 11/10
Pretty nice professor. Course was a kinda hard. Wouldn't take again because content wasn't super interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Easy class, don't buy the textbook, lectures are to the point and interesting, supplemented rly well with examples, class ALWAYS ended 30 minutes early so count on that but overall Prof Brascamp is very kind and willing to help his students when needed, grades consist of quizzes and tests only.
Awesome guy! Had a very good time in his class.

He is an extremely nice man who cares about his students. Class never goes the full amount of time and sometimes it makes the lectures rushed and boring, but the class is easy enough where you could never go to class and still 4.0.

Very chill professor, more than half way through the semester and we havent had a full length class yet. Answers questions through out lecture and his powerpoints are very well structured. You can tell he really cares about his students & is willing to help!
His socks are the best.

Coglab quizzes are easy; (10) HPR credits are easily attainable during a whole semester; he has entertaining lectures that are occasionally riddled with humor; he tries to answer questions the best he can and help students understand what he is teaching.
She is the best professor I had my freshman year. She is very caring and wants to see her students succeed. She is very flexible with grades.

TAKE THIS PROFESSOR! She is so nice and always cares for her students. She is very accessible outside of class and responds to emails very fast. She uses a sheet of paper to mark attendance and it does not matter if you are a couple minutes late to class. Would take her again and am convinced that she is the best teacher of this subject. Grade-100%

Barb was a great professor. She really understands her students and the amount of work we have to do. We had deadlines on papers but she always moved them to about a week later. She took out a whole assignment because she thought we didnt have time. Definitely take her hybrid class, there was minimal work. And the papers were not even that long.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: If you can, take the hybrid class because I do not see the point of going to a two hour writing class twice a week with the work that she assigns to us. Everything is very straight forward and most of her unit schedules are clear. I disliked how our assignments kept getting pushed back and things were graded late. She rarely responds to emails.

We love Barb. Easily one of the best professors Ive had. She's super nice and understanding about any questions you might have. Deadlines aren't that important, as long as you send an email and ask for an extension, you'll get one. Not a harsh grader. Would 100% take a class with Barb again.
Not very accessible out of class. Very nice and lenient at times, but grading criteria is not as clear as it could be.
Barb is the best professor, extremely caring, and really enthusiastic. This has been one of my favorite classes to take.

Barb is great. Really easy assignments, sometimes it takes awhile to get your grade back. Literally as long as you do the work which is only like 4 papers, you will get an A. Only take Barb for WRA. Love her.
