Barb SHOULD BE THE ONLY WRA PROFESSOR YOU TAKE! One time I forgot to turn in my paper by almost 3 weeks and she let it slide and gave me a full grade on it! Very nice and understanding, easy grader, easy assignments, easy 4.0, do not miss on the best opportunity to have the best WRA Professor in the world
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She's such a good person but it's not always clear what she wants from your essays. Needless, she grades super light, so it doesn't really matter. Totally take her for an easy A.
Barb is the best, she'll work with you to make sure you do well and lets you know exactly what she's looking for with each assignment. DEFINITELY take WRA 101 with Barb!!!

She tends to "lose" the rough drafts, at least twice per paper which is frustrating but she always pushes back the due dates for papers. The revision process is nice and helps to really ensure your paper hits all of the criteria. Very nice woman.
Best professor ever! Really funny and really easy
Barb is the nicest prof ever! She's really helpful and wants you to do well. If you need more time on a paper, she'll always extend the deadline for you. If you have the chance, take WRA with her!
Very helpful and reasonable professor. Very down to Earth and friendly. The course is a lot of writing (obviously), but Barb makes the class as painless as possible.

Barb is seriously awesome.  She is in her office often and is always super helpful. Rarely gives homework.  Papers are given with more than enough time to complete. Definitely one of the best teachers in the university.
Barb is Awesome!
She is an extremely easy grader and it is almost hard to fail her class. All u have to do is show up do what she wants u to do, write papers, and then u get the A
Super amazing class would take agian.

Great professor. Uses in-class activities and explains concepts simply, making the content really program into your brain. Homework is consistent and actually helps a lot with studying. If you go to help room she's really personable and very nice to talk to.
Amazing professor. She is so helpful and caring.

This was Dr. Furqueron's first time teaching this class, it didn't seem like it at all. She is so knowledgeable of the material and made sure everyone had a clear understanding of the content for the exams. Her lectures were engaging with group work. She is just a caring, encouraging person. Definitely one of the best professors I've had so far.
Very nice and passionate about her students and class.  You could tell she really cared about everyone as well as their grade. She was an amazing professor!
She slayed!

She makes the lectures super interesting, which cannot be said about most professors. She cares if you understand the material and is super helpful when it comes to questions. It is very easy to get points off on all of the assignments in here but overall she is an amazing professor.
Dr. Furqueron is quite actually the best instructor I have ever had! Her class is set up in a way where you really learn the material and it's enjoyable to go to class!!!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: get rdy to teach yourself the whole course
Did not go into depth with the material and quizzes were tough

You can very clearly tell Dr. Bramer cares a lot about students, but he just doesn't do a great job explaining concepts and teaching students complex material. He is definitely accessible if you need it, but I don't know how helpful that actually is. The course content itself wasn't very difficult, but if you struggle with math he's not for you.

I had never taken calc in the past and I'm not the best at math, but all the biweekly quizzes made me feel so incredibly unintelligent. They're worth most of your grade and often times are very difficult versions of the homework questions. There are only 8-10 questions on each quiz. Read the syllabus and get 65% effort grade for guaranteed 2.5.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I genuinely thought I was going to fail his class. His quizzes were so difficult, and his lectures were so different from the homework and quizzes we were given in recitation. He also taught everything so fast that you couldn't even process what he was teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Oh my. This class was so bad. I am not good at math, but this was the worst way to learn. Lectures were VERY fast and the problems did NOT match the homework (they were harder). The only thing that saved me was the effort grade. Quizzes are a MASSIVE chunk of grading and are not very easy. Recitations were not helpful and my TA was rude. BOOOOOOOOO

Very nice guy But, when it comes to math, it is very difficult. In lectures, all he does is go over examples and doesn't explain too well. He moves at a very fast pace and I have noticed that the quizzes are so difficult and not very similar to things we learned in class. Quizzes are 55% of ur grade! The class may be easier if you alr took calc.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: David Bramer is a nice guy with funny jokes but not amazing teacher. He moves really fast without explaining and just jumps right in no explanation.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of the best professors I've ever had. I figured calc would be difficult and it was at some parts but he made it so easy to understand for someone who usually isn't good at math

Great prof, but the class itself is difficult. There's lots of homework and quizzes every other week that are timed(20min). It felt like the reviews and practice problems weren't like the quizzes at all half the time. There were lots of resources provided on D2L to help with content or going to office hours. Really funny and understanding prof

This class might be tough for people who don't have any experience with calculus, but if you just do the homework and come to class, you will succeed. He is also helpful in re-explaining material when requested.

Dr. Bramer is the best math in structure I've had the chance to be mentored by. 

Office hours were everything. I got 50% on the first quiz, but instantly got better grades once I started going. He would provide very helpful and effective practice and let you know what will and will not be on quizzes

Use his resources and go to office hours = 4.0
He can teach, but his quizzes are timed and short. He goes off on tangents a lot, and I think he wants us to fail.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Cool prof, exams weren't horrible, but it's just tough content overall. He try his best and is available after class to answer questions. Take notes and I highly recommend getting the book (used, it's $). I utilized it the most throughout the course for practice Q's. Lectures weren't mandatory but highly recommended to attend (same with recitation)

He's a super cool and friendly guy. Always willing to answer questions during the lectures when something comes up.  Lectures were not hard and understandable. Pay attention to the quizzes and exams because there's not so much questions on the quizzes exam, and getting 1 or 2 questions wrong can hugely impact your overall grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Caring

reviews.comment: The actual material learned wasn't even hard. The way he explained it though made it super difficult. Beware for quizzes and exams. There's around 10 questions for the quizzes. Lots of the questions he didn't even go over on the exams and were tricky word problems. He was very funny though, very nice person, just not the best at teaching.

The actual math isn't that hard, but the prof doesn't spend ANY time explaining concepts. Quizzes and exams will be your nightmare because the questions are on material not even covered, not to mention there's very few questions so get one wrong and your grade tanks. This class is ridiculous.
The class is really hard in general and I wouldn't say he is great at explaining things. He is super kind and cares about his students though.
He was very nice and clear when explaining topics. You should go to lectures to understand the topics. He was accessible during office hours and willing to help explain
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Bramer is a caring instructor who makes an effort to help students succeed. The class can be challenging, but his recorded lectures and Unstuckstudy's practice problems were a big help. Stay engaged and you'll do well.

Gave students a clear idea of what he was expecting. Bad at explaining any of the concepts. Some of the smartest people I knew would do horribly on his assessments solely because they did not understand any of his explanations. If you want to succeed in his class the best thing to do is self-study and not completely rely on his lecture.

This class is  horrible. The content on the quizzes/exams was usually stuff we had never gone over in class and related more to interpretation and business terms that have nothing to do with actual math. Have fun guessing the true and false questions because they are never clear. Only good thing about this class was that all exams were online.

Bramer is a good lecturer and if you pay attention the quizzes and tests aren't too bad although they get a bit harder towards the end.There's a fair amount of homework but if you have questions, he's there to help. It is also effort based so if you put in enough work you really can't fail.

This class wasn't too bad. To me, I think he gives good lectures and its easy to stay focused when you're taking notes and asking questions.GO TO THE MATH LEARNING CENTER. It's hit or miss on tutors but I had some amazing ones at Brody and they helped me so much on webwork and studying for tests. All tests and quizes are online with lockdown

Bramer is a great guy and his lectures are helpful and he will answer any question. Recitations are very dependent on TA. Webwork is easy and you get 3 tries so good grades always. Quiz and tests are quite difficult and often graded harshly. Put in the effort and take some time out of class to actually learn the stuff and you'll get a good grade

I really like this professor, he's really funny and super easy to talk to and is always open to answering questions. Lectures aren't really needed since this is a flipped class (you watch the lecture videos prior to the in person class) so it's only really necessary to go if you have questions.

I wish Prof. Bramer's lectures allowed more student participation and he would go more step-by-step because he likes to give a problem and then just work through it without really explaining. He does have a fail-proof grading system based on participation so that you really can't fail. He's funny and very enjoyable to be around!
He answered every question in an understanding way. If you study the given materials and consistency do well on the homework assignments, you'll do great in this class.

Though I got a bad grade, it's entirely my fault. Dr. Bramer is super funny and keeps lectures interesting. He allows students to go to his office hours to work on homework and quizzes. He does effort-based grading, so you can still pass the class if you do poorly on tests/exams. I recommend taking him if you get the chance!

Everything is online, the homework, the quizzes, exams. you don't have to go to lecture. He cares very much about students grades and has a "Fail proof plan" as long as you show that you are putting in effort, you can not fail. He puts out assignments which are basically just revisions for quizzes take this instead of 132 if you are a science major
This class is super organized and deadlines are super clear. Of course you need to put in work to do well in the class but he offers so many resources besides his office hours only having one option.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Dr. Bramer is not the greatest prof out there, his lectures cover not everything you need to know which can get annoying, but he is very open about asking questions- which in order to make the most of this class you should ask questions, and the material gets really tough around midterms but there is a few drops that he makes at the end of the sem.

Dont go to lecture your better off learning and teaching yourself , PLEASE USE "Professor Leonard" on YT best calculus teacher out there , really cool guy but cant teach nor have legible hand writing , what saves you is the effort base grading so do good on the effort based on assignments and youll get lucky by passing with a 2.0
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Funny guy and entertaining to listen to, but does not go over enough information to sustain a group of people that need to learn basic calculus in a semester. You'll often be teaching yourself every concept and going to lecture hoping you're prepared enough for the quizzes.

I took math 124 online and it was very easy for me, but I took AP calculus in highschool. I would recommend the online version because the lectures were just practice problems. You already had to watch the lectures on your own time so going to class was pointless. I would recommend in person solely for the recitations.

Total beauty, the best math teacher I've ever had. Thought this class was going to be insanely hard but if you attend lecture and ask questions he will do his best and answer everything fully. He made calculus a very interesting class and re kindled my love for math. Dr B might as well stand for Beautician

Bramer is phenomenal. There are lots of tests/quizzes, but he explains things well in class. He always stays after class to talk to students and drops 2 of your lowest hw and quiz scores; you are guaranteed a 2.0 if you do quiz revisions and participate. If you introduce yourself to him, he is even more accommodating to your needs. Also a d i l f.

Professor Bramer was a cool guy, but his class was pretty difficult. Lots of the questions on his tests or quizzes are word problems that can be difficult to understand and are things we didn't go over in class.

Prof. Bramer was a sweet guy. I did not go to very many classes, but I found that his recorded videos were enough for me to grasp the material. He gives you the ability to drop your lowest quiz and homework grades, and also gave the class some last minute extra credit opportunities. Nice guy, and decent at teaching.

Find a different professor and save yourself. Most of the quizzes and exams are multiple choice and true in false, which in a math course is very unnerving. The final only had three short answers, which you can get partial credit on. My TA taught be everything and I did everything possible to pass get a 4.0 and it was basically impossible.

Very frustrating class. If you decide to go to lecture he only goes over 2-3 questions, and there is only 2 a week. With the minimal taught you do 1-3 webwork assignments, quizzes that go with it, a weekly post assessment, and probably another quiz or exam with it. It is way too much, especially because you teach yourself everything.

The course itself wasn't excessively difficult if effort to learn was put in, however the teaching style would not work for everyone and his methods would sometimes be more difficult than necessary. If effort is put in to learn, the class isn't bad, but expect a decent bit of self studying regardless.

What Dr. Bramer taught in class made sense. He explains topics thoroughly and is a pretty funny guy. However, the topics that would actually be assessed on were taught by TAs once a week. I don't think that this issue is specific to Dr. Bramer, but rather to the class in itself. If at all possible, I would try to find another math class to take.

This class was pretty difficult. You have to watch the lecture videos ahead of time in order to understand the lectures. I really liked Mr. Bramer, he cracked jokes and encouraged asking questions. Another thing this course offered was a fail-safe that still passed you if you put in the effort. If you put in the work and time, you will pass.

No extra credit opportunities. If you choose online without a lecture, be prepared to search the internet for more content to teach yourself, since the lecture videos are not enough. You are also tested after every recitation on the content you learned only that day, so if you have a trash TA, good luck. I'd recommend going to office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Avoid taking this class with him at all costs. I have learned more through my TA than him all semester. Trust m save yourself from having to teach yourself everything .

If you have the option to take a different math instead of this, please do! The tests are half economic true and false questions that you don't learn in lecture. You need to memorize everything and it's awful.

If you're bad at math, he has a 2.0 effort grading scale (idk if this is specific to him). I'm average at math and he explained things well in lecture. Do the notes before lecture, take notes during lecture, and do the focus sessions and you'll do ok.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lots of quizzes. But he explains things in a very clear manner. Im very bad at math but he made the material easy to understand.

This professor is not very clear in lectures. He goes very fast and moves on whether you understand it or not. He is the same way when you get help outside of class. He does not explain the content well.
lectures werent that helpful but recitation was very helpful. Rather hard class but you can be successful with effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Must be self motivated. Class run down: watch lecture videos before class, go to class and work through practice problems/ ask questions (nothing new is taught but he gives helpful hints and does some of the homework for you) recitation 1 day a week then quiz once a week, exam every month and a quiz after recitations with some homework weekly.

He's actually a nice professor and gave us some extra credit, and answered questions pretty well in class. My only con is in his video lectures, he would show the answer at the end of the video by a second (so you would have to fight rewinding it back to see), it could also be better explained. I HIGHLY recommend going to office hours to pass exams

This class is not hard if you find a great Ta one Ta I would recommend would be Thad Greiner he always reviews before exams and is wonderful also the professor always helps if you ask him you just need to reach out so they would know that you need help keep up with webwork and you will do fine.

This professor was very considerate and accommodating, especially considering what happened in February. I felt like his lectures were easy to understand and a lot of the TAs are super awesome. Calculus is difficult but as long as you stay on top of the web work problems and utilize supplemental material to study with you should be fine.

This is a really difficult class if you don't have prior calc knowledge but I think it's due to the content rather than the professor. In my opinion he was pretty accommodating, he gave everyone a full 100% for one of the quizzes and reopened hw assignments for late credit at the end of the semester. Going to office hours was definitely helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: During the events of what happened this semester, this professor was very understanding regarding homework, focus session quizzes, and in term quizzes/exams. I was failing up until the final and he was able to provide extra help w/o making it seem like an inconvenience. He was also very nice when responding to emails.

Recorded lectures (30-60min/week). Weekly Webwork and Focus session. He does a good job explaining concepts in lecture vids. In lectures he does examples, if you understand the content, don't need to go. Quizzes/Exams are online lockdown browser.  Overall, the work for the class is acceptable for the content. Just do weekly work and ur fine

Avoid this class at all costs, everything in the other reviews is 100 percent accurate 
Only math class I've had a hard time with this is the hardest class I've taken in college so far

lol another math class at msu that you have to rely on ryan maccombs and khanacademy for, i don't think this class is difficult as other people make it out to be but it's definitely a "time waster". every time i tried interacting with this professor, i got deferred to a TA, so good luck actually seeing his face.

For the amount of work you need to put into this class, it should not be worth 3 credits. Beyond the D2L quizzes, WebWork quizzes, D2L exams, Focus Session assignments, etc. You still have to do the notes, which are sad attempts at teaching the fundamentals of calculus. Being forced to go to MLC or go to KhanAcademy makes this class redundant.

This class was just made overwhelmingly hard by this professor, the notes are of very poor quality, in my opinion, for example, I would spend a couple of hours watching the notes for the week only to not understand the hw and quizzes. Then a 10-minute Khan Academy video would do everything his notes didn't do. That is not how it should be.

Bramer significantly lacked a personality. Don't even bother going to lectures unless you are struggling. He works through problems very slow and its helpful to watch him sample problems that are on the homework. However, this was my second time taking a calculus class so I had a pretty solid foundation to work off of.

you do not meet him, he is non existent. The TAs do all the work. Attendance isn't mandatory but going to the recitation will help you do good on the quiz after class. This class is so hard for no reason, try to avoid him.

Dr. Bramer is a really nice guy to have a conversation with, but I had a hard time understanding the material taught in class. I feel that the homework would throw curve balls and I would just feel frustrated. The homework took a long time, and the exams were very challenging, although all online. His final grade remediation policy saved my grade.

Avoid this class at all costs if you value your mental health. The Math Learning Center is no help with this class and the professor is rude when responding to emails. If you have to take MTH 124 don't take it with this professor. You'll honestly feel so stupid even if you're good at math

This was by far the hardest class I've ever had, I'm not a great math student so this class was awful for me I became lost in the 1st few weeks of class with all of his lectures consisting of no teaching and practice problems I was incredibly lost. I wasn't able to afford a tutor so I just started failing despite reaching out for free help.

Dr. Bramer is a great teacher, he can very thoroughly explain all the topics he teaches about in a way that makes it very accessible to students. However, the WebWork assignments are just so frustrating because they're so time consuming. Good instructor just heavy workload

Asynchronous class is awful. All taught by old YouTube videos. Tests aren't super long which leaves no margin for error. My opinion is pray you get a 2.0 or better and just move on w your life and forget that MTH124 ever existed. The only mandatory HW is WW, but if you want to succeed you need to do the focus sessions beforehand. Beware all.

I watched the videos, went to lectures/recitations, did book problems, did extra practice quizzes/exams, went to office hours & math help when I could, etc. I still did poorly on the quizzes/exams, there's barely any questions on them so get a couple wrong & you're screwed. Some things on the quizzes were random & I had no clue how to answer.

There are NO curves. The exams only have around 10 questions and the range of units covered means you could try and study everything and miss one concept, then fail. Things that weren't EVER discussed were on the exams/quizzes.

Study the webworks UP TO THE UNIT before exams (even if not due) as versions sometimes end up on exams/quizzes. Good luck

Absolute no. If you receive him as a professor, I'm incredibly sorry. The lecture videos in no way correspond to the webwork, and the webwork in no way corresponds to quizzes/tests. You have a quiz every week, and on weeks you don't there will be an exam. Will not explain to you how/what you got wrong. You're very much on your own.

when i say i've never seen a teacher who is as frustrating at this one. his lectures consist of only practice problems based on lecture videos you're expected to have watched before class, so going is a waste of time. his lecture videos are messy (he messes up half the time), and i'm left needing to seek outside sources to learn the content.

I took this class as an online only option and that was where I first went wrong. The videos for lectures barely correlate with the Webwork given and it takes forever to get ahold of someone who just ends up giving you a more confusing way of figuring a problem out. I don't need to be spoon fed the questions but just better/ clearer teaching:/

In high school I always excelled at math, and yet this course is something I'll likely have to take again. The lectures provided are not helpful for learning, Bramer just does a worksheet without teaching. I've learned more from TAs and Khan academy. Quizzes and exams have so few questions (usually 7-11) so missing even one question is devastating.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would avoid this class at all costs. The lecture's are useless and Dr. Bramer doesn't teach. The TAs are the ones who teach us everything and try to prepare us for the tests. The tests also suck because the questions are not anything like the webwork or practice tests and they're only 10 questions so if you get a few wrong you already failed.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor does not teach. He links vague youtube videos that he made years ago. the recitations are mandatory because we have quizzes on them afterward. The TAs teach all of the content that is on the tests during recitation. This professor is also not accommodating at all. If you don't have previous calc knowledge, good luck!

I would avoid this professor at all costs. In person or online does not matter because they won't help you learn the material. You have lecture videos you have to watch before coming into the lecture. you have webwork homework. If you are good at math, be prepared to fail. This class is not taught properly.

I would avoid Bramer at all costs. By far the worst setup for this sort of class. "Lecture" videos are youtube videos, where the material is barely taught, and lectures just discuss webwork problems and that's it. Not to mention the quizzes were due on Thursdays at 6, instead of midnight like every other class. Avoid him for any class if you can.
first day of lecture there was about 500 people, now theres barley 100 people that show up. To get a good grade you def wanna go to lecture and take notes and do extra practice worksheets.

Professor Bramer is a nice guy and you can ask questions. However, the class moves so fast and can be hard to keep up with. If you are bad at math like me, this class can be really stressful. My advice is keep up with the homeowork and just pray while taking the exam. He does use lockdown browser for everything (w webcam).
There is no help in this class. If you are truly here to learn you need 100% prior knowledge.

I took this class because I was required to for my major. If you can avoid it, or Prof Bramer, please do. He gave horrible feedback, tests didnt even cover what we were actively learning, and when I asked for help they just told me to work harder and I was wrong about my concerns I had presented. Will be avoiding this prof at all costs.

Don't take this class with him. He's a nice person, but his lectures are super confusing and he isn't clear on questions. His exams were hard for no reason and the material was nothing like what we had been learning. I placed in this class because of the score I got on my math placement exam and I still didn't do good. I do not recommend him.

Bramer isn't a bad professor in terms of teaching, he just can be a little unclear at times and will get upset if he's asked too many questions. The amount of homework is very reasonable, but the exams are fairly difficult and don't always match the homework. It is also unclear what the exams will be on sometimes.

This class is truly hard to keep up with. The pace this course moves at took me by surprise and it never really got better. The professor is also not forgiving with assignments whatsoever, so get ready you're in for a bad ride.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: do NOT take bramer. this class was emotionally draining. i found myself crying and physically sick due to stress i went to mlc 8 hours a week, hired a tutor, office hours and still failed every quiz and exam except for the final- and im a 4.0 student. the class avg test/quiz scores were 30-40%. avoid this class at all costs.

This class is very challenging as there is little room for error with it being so test heavy. In order to get a good grade, doing textbook problems is a must. Dr. Bramer is always available for questions, however this class requires a lot of work to really master the material. I don't recommend taking this class without a background in calculus.

i wouldnt say hes a bad professor, but he just teaches really fast. hes always opened to help out with problems outside of lecture during office hours. however, get ready for weekly quizzes and webwork.

I took calc before, hired a tutor, & bought the textbook that isn't required. I still had no clue what I was doing. Thankfully, I had a good TA but your understanding of a class shouldn't be left up to the TA. Almost no partial credit is given, study EVERYTHING because he'll claim a quiz is over one thing and test you on something else.

I wouldn't take this class again in its current format. Online math classes just aren't effective. The entire MTH 124 department offered online math classes, it's not great. Bramer never stressed the importance of doing partial credit for quizzes and tests (makes a huge difference). Otherwise, not too bad. Just a bad format for the class.

I would ABSOLUTELY NOT recommend this professor/class to another student. This class has ruined my mental health!! I can't believe this class has been going for as long as it has with him but this is awful and students are FAILING this class and they are just blaming us, instead of reevaluating what they could change! Harder than a 400 level class!
pretty hard class
He doesnt really teach the class, the TAs do. The class is not offered in person so beware of teaching yourself this class. Khan academy videos will be your best friend. good luck tho

This class relies on information that was not taught in lecture, that you just have to figure out. Tests and quizzes do not cover the information that was taught in lecture or those on the notes. I have no idea what this man looks like and he is VERY bad at answering basic questions regarding the course or content.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: i did better on the tests when i didn't go to his lectures. the quizzes are easy but the actual tests have nothing to do with calculus. i got 95-100% on all quizzes and focus session quizzes and yet i got 60%s on the two tests we had. you can work ahead on homework which is nice.

This is one of those professors where instead of answering a simple and broad question about the course, he refuses answer and tells you to review the syllabus. The homework is full of questions far from anything reviewed in class and so are the quizzes. Lectures and tests are not on the days listed when signing up for the class. Do not take.

One of the only professors I've had here so far as a Junior, that just sucks overall. He needs to get his things together, and start to care for his students more. He only cares for his paycheck, and blows students off. AVOID DAVID BRAMER at all costs. Unless you want to get one thing wrong on a quiz and get a C!

Live lectures are very unorganized- would recommend not going to live lecture but rather watch the pre-recorded youtube videos, or do a combination of the two. Post recitation hw is very challenging. I have taken calculus before (and did well) but still had a lot of trouble with this class. Be prepared to spend a lot of time on this class.

I've seen Bramer get a lot of criticism recently, and I'm not sure if it's all warranted. I especially think Calc is a hard course to both learn and teach, and he does his very best to help out. He hosts study periods often and if you reach out, he's always available. The tests/quizzes are short, though. So you have little room for error.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: wasn't the worst. Not very flexible with exam/quiz times when there was interference with another class. All material needed to be studied all the time because you never knew what to expect on the quiz. He taught things quick but I liked that. If you aren't good at math you may struggle in his class. Math is my best subject and this class was hard

At least in the online format, he refuses to give partial credit and extra credit opportunities so if you don't do well the first time, you better do well the next or you'll fall behind. Not clear about what he expects you to know and doesn't explain things (just tells you to check yourself again). Overall not recommended.

Bramer felt very impersonal. If you're new to calc avoid him. Never saw his face and never seemed to care about students. Very tough grader as well. Quizzes are worth 40% and some are only 5 questions, makes it almost impossible to get an A. Never recorded his lectures either, and the exams almost never had what we covered on them.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--So many papers--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I had Prof. Bramer for MTH124. I found the way things were weighted to be unfair. 3 5-10 question tests counted for 40? percent of our grade. Lockdown browser is also used, and I understand why, but I feel like it is a violation of privacy to spy on students. I never saw his face the entire semester, his style of teaching was very impersonal.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The notes we took in lectures did not correspond with the homework problems, and definitely did not prepare you for quizzes. Quizzes are only five questions so if you get two questions wrong, there goes your grade. Same with tests. Tests are only 10 questions long. I was struggling and emailed Bramer for help and he gave me a philosophy lesson.

The class structure is disorganized, and the quizzes/tests were extremely hard (limited time, lockdown browser). This class gave me anxiety and made things harder for us during the pandemic. I wish they were more understanding and more helpful! I worked very hard for this class but I felt all my efforts went to waste. Avoid if you can!

he teaches good but his exams are wayyyy hard and I dont get why. He teaches good but whats the point if his exams are hard and you cant get the grade you want?! I got what i wanted but not easy at all. Just try to avoid him, Im saying it B cause i dont want you guys to experience what i did this course could be much better.

If this is your first time taking a calculus class or if you are not that great at calc in general, D O N T take Bramer. He takes way longer than the provided lecture time to go through the notes and leaves about 3 pages worth of questions to do on your own. I literally don't even know what he looks like and it felt very unprofessional at times.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The tests and quizzes for this class were impossible. They were nothing like the webworks assigned. Each quiz only had 5 questions, which made it difficult to get a good grade on them. He would only do 3ish problems per lecture. I always understood what we learned in class, but would do awful on the quizzes.
If you take this class with him you will start to question if he is more interested in trickery rather than PREPARING students. I studied a lot too.

Do NOT take Bramer. Unless you want lectures that barely go over at MAX two example problems and homework that does NOT correlate to tests and quizzes. No matter how much studying you do the tests and quizzes are always 10x harder and unnecessarily tricky. As someone who studied VERY heavily for this class that was STILL not enough. I like math too

By far the worst class I have ever taken. The professor makes it impossible for you to have a descent grade. Notes/lectures don't help and the practice quizzes don't help at all. All quizzes/Mini Tests are timed with the lockdown browser and the quizzes are only 5 questions. The questions aren't even fair questions and are very difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: One of the hardest classes I have ever taken. I have always gotten A's in math but don't expect that in this class. All of the quizzes and tests are only 5-10 questions and on lockdown browser. Lectures don't even help because what he teaches is not even on the tests sometimes. If you could avoid taking this class with this professor, do it.
Very organized and I always know if I am on schedule or not!

DO NOT take this class online! You will regret it, I had to drop the course and the people still in it are legit having breakdowns before quizzes and mini tests. They do not provide any useful resources for reviewing (besides a practice exam that he himself said doesnt resemble the actual test ??) its 100% just teaching yourself very hard content
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Enjoyed lectures, does a great job explaining notes. But when we switched to online, it's like he gave up teaching. Overall, I wouldn't recommend because he makes the class way harder than it needs to be. The exams are pretty hard. I liked his personality, but it's not easy to get an A.

Bramer was.. okay. If you show up to every lecture, pay attention and ask questions you should be fine. For what he's required to do, he's sufficient, so I would recommend him but don't expect anything above average. The work you do in labs and the webwork / quizzes don't really help prepare you for the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: A very good teacher and explains things very well. If you pay attention during lecture and put in the work to study, you'll do just fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: The class is EXTREMELY difficult, even if you take notes and focus. Exams are VERY hard. My exam 2 was a 58% and that was one of the highest I heard. For a solid month I was sitting at a 2.0, convinced I was going to fail a class my freshman year. I think they curved the class; I was set to have a 3.5 but ended with a 4.0 (NO idea how).
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Calc is really hard and it's a difficult kind of math to teach if the student doesn't have a solid math foundation to begin with, but Bramer is a really nice guy who definitely tries to make it as simple as possible to understand. he doesn't make the exams but those are very difficult, no matter who you have.

One of my favorite math teachers ever. Math has never been my best subject or my favorite one, but Bramer made this class enjoyable and pretty easy. He knows his stuff so as long as you show up to class, pay attention, and do what is asked of you the tests will be easy. I also recommend doing the book work (it is not required) but helped me a TON!!
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Really knows his stuff and gives great examples that explain things well. Goes out of his way to be accessible outside of class. The textbook is used for practice problems which are somewhat helpful but sometimes don't correspond well with the lectures. Tests were straightforward.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: The class in general is very difficult if you didn't take a calculus class in high school, but Dave explained things very well. Compared to other teachers that my friends had for this class, I believe he was the best one. A lot of the people who complained didn't use the book or had others do the webworks for them. If you actually try its easy.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: He discusses important material in depth during lecture and gives many sources for you to study from. He speaks with enthusiasm and it is clear he wants the best for his students.

Dave is one of the best professors I've ever had. His lectures are well planned and easy to follow. He always thoroughly explains every topic we cover and is open to any questions students have. He has the students' best interests in mind and really wants everyone to succeed. If you put the work in, you will get the grade you deserve. Great prof!

If your not great at math and want to pass calc, find a new professor. I think he's one of the worst professors I've ever had at msu. I never skipped a class and still had no idea what was going on because I don't think he ever actually taught us how to do anything. Webworks were easy and exams were impossible.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Bramer is a good, large-lecture professor for mth 124. Some of the topics can be hard to understand at first but all of the questions are reasonable with all of the information available for the class. He's always open to questions unlike some other freshman math professors and is constantly encouraging students go to his office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: I took this course and the webwork and practice exams are nothing like the real exams. Bramer does not finish his notes and tends to jump around a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I am NOT a math person in general. I had to take this course to fulfill my math credit. Overall, Bramer made things make sense more than any math teacher in the past has. Still definitely study outside of class & don't be afraid to ask him to go over problems in class! If you pay attention its possible to get a decent grade (even if u suck @ math)
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Exams are difficult because the notes and webwork do not relate at all to what's on them. Very strange but funny sense of humor.

Prof Bramer goes out of his way to go over what past students struggled with and uses examples that past students suggest. He is always open to give extra help. Tests are tough if you don't practice. The book isn't used but if you want to get a decent grade doing the practice problems suggested from the book will get you that.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: I didn't use a text book... but if you want a 4.0, use a text book. He really thinks he fully explains lessons but he doesn't. Also, WebWork does not help you study. It is nothing like the exams.

Professor Bramer is a cool guy. He's very young and knows what he's talking about. His lectures can get boring, so try and sit up front and pay attention. If you take diligent notes and show up to class, a 4.0 isn't too tough.

He is a really awesome professor. I went into this class fearing it but I came out very confident in my math abilities. Coming from someone who 2.5 Math 103 it is very possible to 4.0 this class if you work hard enough. He goes over lots of examples and the tests are on the easy side.

Dave is a quality prof, he doesnt give a million examples but the ones he does are important and he expands on them really well. He is one of those guys who has corny math jokes, but I liked that. The tests are easy - except for the second test- he makes it extremely difficult but if you study and put in the work youll be more than fine.

David gives  opportunities to ask questions and set up study times outside of class. He also allows for review (if people ask questions) the lecture before exams. His lectures tend to be quite boring and you have to print out notes, but he is the best math prof I have had.

He does not complete the notes, he will only half way finish a problem then expect you to learn it alone later. He is clear on what he does teach. If you're good at math you'll be fine, however if you struggle or are just decent I would switch professors.
YOU THINK YOU understand whats going on, but his notes are nothing like the online homework and nothing like the exams
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Bramer was alright, but the class was calculus and required a slower, more in-depth explanation fairly often. It was relatively manageable for those able to put in some effort.

So Dave was defiantly one of my favorite professors, although some other people say his lectures are confusing I STRONGLY disagree. He starts by showing you an example problem and then having you try one on your own. I always asked him for help when I needed it (which was often only because of his good looks) and he was always helpful.

They just changed the style of tests this semester so they're a lot harder(that's being said by someone who like math). Do the webwork & go to all the resitations to help your grade. If you're not good at the webwork go to the MLC so that you get it all done & the full points. For sure study for the exams. The lectures are okay he can be confusing.

He does a really good job of walking through the notes in lecture, but you have to go and pay attention. His tests are tough, but overall he's pretty great. Don't walk out or be loud, he calls people out regularly.

He spends a good majority of the lecture making you work on your own which is difficult to do since you haven't learned the  material yet. Very rough class

reviews.comment: This class was so easy, and genuinely so fun and interesting. Attendance is not required, but I still looked forward to going because the class is amazing. Lectures were very engaging, and there are only a few assignments even graded for accuracy- the other ones are just graded for completion. You get 1 drop each assignment as well.

I would take every single psychology class with Dr. Burnette if I could. She is genuinely the best professor I've ever had. Dr. Burnette is amazing. Simple assignments throughout the semester, and you get drops for everything. 5 pop quizzes but besides that attendance is not mandatory. Her lectures were always entertaining and she had guest speakers for every topic. So much extra credit! She is one of the nicest profs I had and this was an easy, informative class.

Easiest class I've ever been in. Homework is graded on completion. Quizzes are free. Extra credit opportunities. Professor is so sweet and genuinely wants to make you enjoy the class. Please Take this class.

She was very caring and def wanted us to know the material rather than memorize. Everything is open note, ton of extra credit, ton of drops. She does pop quizzes so choose your attendance wisely but she is caring and understandable

she is the best. I would take all of my classes with her if I could. all quizzes are open note, and the classes are so engaging. I'm a serial skipper and I actually WANT to go to her lectures. she brings in a guest speaker for each topic we cover too. Amazing professor, easy 4.0 and you actually learn

Professor Burnette was amazing, she is so kind and caring about her students. She offers guest lectures from professionals in each field we cover, and she is extremely knowledgeable and understanding.
Very easy 4.0. Nice prof who gives clear lectures.

Blair is incredibly kind and you can tell she cares about each student. The homework and quizzes are incredibly easy if you pay attention and know the stuff. There's extra credit opportunities, some of which being to send a picture of a pet if you read her announcement. Such a fun & interesting class if you're interested in mental health and psych!

Professor Burnette cares about her students and gives out stress management tips. She is fascinating to listen to, and gives many examples for each concept. Homework and tests aren't hard, and it's easy to miss class if needed. She specializes in eating disorders, so she will often bring this in as an example in her lectures.
Good class with easy assignments, but gets cancelled all. the. time. it became disorganized quickly and confusing to stay on track with deadlines because class was cancelled so often.
You literally will not go to class, she canceled class every single week. I went to class maybe 5 times, IF that
Cancelled class last minute so many times.

She cancels all the time very last minute so don't take an 8 am class with her. Her class is very easy. She will make you read outload sometimes and work in groups or partners. All work is easy and there is never homework.

If you're into creative work and open to learn new perspectives, take this class! She is fun, caring, and despite some of the students making this class difficult for her because of circumstances unrelated to the class, she is empathetic and a kind grader. I loved her!

She was a good person, but the amount of times she cancelled class last minute was wild. Because my class was an 8:00 am, there was over ten times where I would be in the classroom and we would get an email saying class was cancelled, a few times even after class had started. The class itself was very disorganized, mostly due to many cancellations.

She was great! Super friendly and very engaging. Class was pretty easy, she would cancel class a lot but I was okay with it. Course work was simple and she was lenient on late work! Def recommend and would take a class with her again.

The professor is not all that bad. the class is easy and the writing assignments are not hard. For me, she did not come off as rude or have a bad attitude. She did however cancel class last minute a LOT or change it to Zoom that was extremely annoying. there is also a lot of reading in class and group discussions
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: If you can help it, don't enroll in a class with her as the professor! This professor is horrible at communicating and her attitude is also very unpleasant. The way that Professor Borha chose to run class this semester was always chaotic and unorganized to say the very least. Although the class itself is easy, she made it hard with her attitude.

Do NOT take this professor's class. She makes class online for no reason and lets you know the morning of. She never explains herself, adds random parts to the projects a day before it's due, and changes the due dates 100 times. She is very unclear about when things are due. Overall, this easy WRA 101 class is made complicated by this professor.

Dr. Barondess is a nice guy and good to get along with. Unfortunately, his exams a written pretty poorly and many questions do not make much sense. This is difficult because exams and quizzes make up the entire grade. He also tends to ramble during lecture so it is difficult to tell whether he is lecturing or talking just to talk.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Barondess is a good guy, but, unfortunately, his exams are so heavy, and that's all you get graded on + quizzes. He also rambles on a lot, so it's hard to tell whether he's going on a tangent or whether you'll be tested on it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you go to class, listen, and put in effort, you'll do well. Students who rated him poorly likely lack sufficient attention span and discipline. Dr. Barondess does ramble sometimes, but what he says is relevant to course content, which is straightforward and interesting for people who pursuing careers medicine and public health.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you're a student that puts in an average amount of effort by going to lectures and actively listening, you will be fine. All content is covered in lectures, and grading based on 6 quizzes + 4 exams. First few weeks of learning statistic interpretation is boring but after that it gets interesting. The reviews made me nervous, but he was great!
He was clear in teaching and a lot of this class was common sense. hes a cool dude tbh and the tests r easy if u take enough notes and he drops one exam and one quiz
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Do not listen to the hateful reviews. Insanely helpful outside of class, lectures are easy to follow even for someone with ADHD, and the class is very interesting. The workload/tests/quizzes are very manageable but hard if you don't pay attention. I've written some awful reviews on here but this guy does not deserve the hate he has.

Will just put up a picture and lecture about it for 90 minutes. Narcissist and loves talking about himself. Was obviously bullied when he was younger. Constantly likes to remind students that he's a forensic epidemiologist with an interest in X, Y, and Z. Mentored a grad student who now teaches at Wayne who's been indicted on multi murder charges.

Would get off topic during lecture or repeat the same ideas, didn't verbally add much significant information to lecture slides. Would maybe get through 4 slides in the almost 2 hour class. You can pass the class by self teaching yourself (reading slides and taking notes on your own) and going to office hours with the TAs about exam questions.
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. take any other class or switch your major, seriously. In my four years of undergrad I have never had a worse teacher. An EMBARRASSMENT to the university.

class should be renamed psychological torture 390
seriously find any other class to satisfy the credit. any. other. class.

Do not take any of this man's classes. Thinks he is "generous" while doing the bare minimum. Awful lecturer, awful human. He doesn't care about his students and talks for over half the class about himself and spends maybe 30 mins of lecture time on actual content. Avoid at all costs, if this class is required: switch majors. Seriously, don't take

Do not take this class, or any of this man's classes. He is an awful professor who honestly acts like he wants you to bad. He is a staunch grader that won't round even at a .01%. He doesn't do ANYTHING other than coming into lecture (meaning everything is online and made from years passed) he is makes critical mistakes constantly. 0/10 prof.

Professor Barondess was an incredible instructor for spring 23. He understood and accommodated to the struggles of the semester. He reached out many times to check in; and was sure to respond to our emails if we responded. Lectures are very interesting just be sure you are interested in the material. Lectures and quizzes make up all of your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I have never taken an online class that was so horrible before. The lectures are long and provide little information that is actually needed on the exams. The "study guides" with questions and topics for students to understand are essentially useless - the exams feature questions and topics you aren't told to even learn! Horrible. TA was bad too.

Barondess is the only professor that teaches this class so there is no way of getting out of it. Your grade is based off of only 4 exams and one gets dropped. He lectures for the full two hours, so taking notes can be difficult. I tried going to office hours for him and I found it hard to talk to him. Extra credit saved me - but don't depend on it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: No learning objectives and his lectures were impossible to take notes on because it was just him talking. Class only graded on 4 exams which he allowed us to drop one this year. He also randomly added an extra credit quiz at the end of the semester. Was going to use the respondus camera for exams but our class bullied him out of it.

As god is my witness I almost jumped off the red cedar of how annoying this professor is at being off-topic while at the same time only having 4 exams that are insanely difficult. I will literally build a teleportation machine so I can go in the past and stop myself from taking this class and starting my chronic depression. Please save yourself
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Narcissist and self-centered. Im interested in learning about public health, not his life story, achievements, opinions, and what he thinks we should think.

I am a graduating senior, and Dr. Bardoness was the worst professor I have encountered throughout my time at MSU. He's a decent guy and I received a 4.0 in his class. However, his lectures are terrible and with his organization of the class is nonexistent. His "lectures" are just him rambling on without any slides and reading news off his phone.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Unclear, arrogant, and condescending. There are three tests that make up your entire grade. I went to every lecture up until the first test, took notes, studied for two days-- and I don't normally study-- and then received a 53%. I then stopped going and started self teaching the material and received 91% and 94% on the last tests. He's bad. Don't.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Barondess is a horrible teacher, a horrible professor, and this class is absolutely not worth your time. The material itself is not too hard, but his lectures are terribly unorganized and he spends half the time bragging about his own achievements. Didn't show up to both of the his appointments with me and left me waiting in Fee for hours. Bad.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Barondess truly is a genius. Although sometimes he may not always be easy to follow, this class is an upper level epidemiology and it should be challenging. I think his way of teaching is exactly how other courses should be. You don't want to miss class because he says things of importance! Also our understanding and thoughts count.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Takes two hours to make a point about one thing. Nice guy, kinda funny, obviously knows his stuff, but those lectures get very long-winded.
There are pictures and documents shown on the projector, no "real" notes. He just talks to the class so it is hard to take notes.
Seems like a nice person, but I find the class to be mind-numbing. Expect to learn the material on your own time because the whole class time is taken up reviewing the homework from the day before.
Worst professor I've had at MSU. Do not take any courses with her, I'm taking an online IAH course right now and she is so harsh with grading.
Do not take a class with this professor. She is a tough grader for a class that should be easy. I do not recommend.

DO.NOT.TAKE.HER. Has no clear instructions and its a teach your own class. This is based off of homework and says it is based off completion when it is truly based on correctness.  She says you get three excused absences, but only marks participation as grading, so basically you can not miss a single class. I advise you drop the class if signed up

Very direct and can have an attitude. Classes consist of mostly answering HW questions, which wasn't helpful as little time was left for lecture slides. Grading is REALLY tough and instructions can be unclear. However, HW is a big part of your grade which can cover up a bad exam. Very accessible outside class and helpful feedback.

This professor does not help you learn anything. She instead uses her time to send multiple emails out to students accusing them of using AI. Multiple people have now dropped the course due to fears of them receiving an academic dishonesty report, even if they didn't use AI... Adding to that, her D2L is not set up correctly. Do NOT TAKE HER !!!!!!!

This professor is terrible. Grading is impossible to understand, and there's no guidance on how to do better. I reached out multiple times but never got clear answers. The course materials were confusing, and her explanations only made things harder. It's frustrating to feel stuck with no support or direction.

FIRE HER! Worst professor ever hard grader who never gives helpful feedback. The textbook was useless. She said to ask for help, but her replies were unclear and confusing, leaving me more lost. I'm a native speaker, so it's not a language issue. She just doesn't help students improve or understand.

Carmen grades your essays as if you are already a native speaker, and will accuse you of using google translate with no good reason, to the point where i had to purposely put mistakes in my essay just so she doesn't accuse me for it. The grade boundaries are extremely high, and she will not curve it no matter how bad the class is doing.

I was terrified to take her summer SPN202 course after reading the reviews. Her character is blunt and to the point — I believe this is where majority of the bad reviews are coming from. She is, however, an incredible teacher with effective curricula. Her class was challenging, but was the one I improved in most out of the four SPN classes I took.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: A year ago I was reading the reviews thinking "she can't be that bad." I was wrong. She has accused me of using google translate when I did not with no evidence. Does not take criticism but instead makes excuses. I truly dreaded each class not because the coursework was difficult but simply because I didn't want to deal with her attitude. Avoid.

As a native speaker this class was SO hard. They DON'T round. A 4.0 is a 94% so make sure to remember that. You have to do lessons before the class (another prof teaches the content) and they are time consuming but extremely important. Your grade relies on participation (2 pts for every lecture). Be sure to do all work & do well on tests/exams.

Profe Carmen was my favorite professor this semester. She is funny and caring. Participation in class is big for your grade so try not to miss classes. But she helps make sure everyone participates. SPN 310 has a pretty big workload since lectures, homework, and quizzes are all outside of class, but she helps clear things up in class.

Carmen was scary at first because of her high expectations and advanced language use in class. LOTS of hw but is the same for all SPN 310 classes and participation in class is required for a grade. She comes off strong but is very willing to help after class!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She really cares that every student is on the same page & is very approachable if you need any help. I was always excited to go to her class because she's very funny. She fosters a great environment in class as she's always making jokes in class. She gets to know each of her students and it's clear she puts time into her lectures. Take her class!

Very brute and unapproachable, if you dont do things her way (even if they contradict the syllabus) you wont succeed in her class. She hones in on struggling students and made several cry and made some even abandon the language/minor all together. So bad with technology and communication

Overall, she is a nice lady. She responded to my emails very quickly and you can tell she is passionate about what she teaches. My only complaint is that the grading criteria/syllabus was not very clear at the beginning of this semester (new to the course), but got better as we progressed. She is also not flexible with late assignments at all.

Professor Octavio was pretty good. She is from Spain so she has a Spanish accent. She also hates technology with a burning passion. I took SPN 310 with her. None of the professors for that course give very clear feedback, and you do all the lectures by yourself. The textbook was pretty much useless except for when it was required for homework.

I had her for 201and 202 a year apart; her teaching improved (clearly as she's taken on more classes since). When you have a good group for class she is very entertaining and always joking with students. She really cares that her students are understanding and adjusts her teaching methods accordingly. Her classes are hard but appropriately so.

incredibly rude to all students in class and even picked on a specific person in my section, poor explanations of core concepts and would often not answer questions asked. heavy homework load that doesn't really benefit you in class. mandatory attendance, tough grader in class on both oral and written assignments. just take my word for it and avoid

She is amazing. I've had 2 SPN classes with her. She really cares about whether or not students are learning from her and her teaching methods. I've had private conversations with her and she cares for her student's well-being and success so much. She is so accommodating to personal issues. I would strongly recommend her as a professor.

Profesora Octavio is short-tempered, unreasonable, and difficult to work with. Directions and criteria for assignments are often unclear. She is not tech savvy, she becomes very upset when students make technology related mistakes or have poor connection, but then expects grace when she does the same. Do yourself a favor and avoid her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor Octavio requires you to spend a minimum of 70$ on books/conversations if you want to pass. She assigns an abundance of online work/TalkAbroad transcriptions that you are unable to receive any credit for if submitted late, no matter the reason. Attendance is mandatory due to in class activities everyday that receive a 0 if not present
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She was one of the best professors I've had since coming to MSU.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: SPN330 is okay, but Octavio is the worst professor I have had. She is condescending, short-tempered, and has no patience when answering valid student questions. You might feel bad for not already knowing it all. She asks for our understanding with her life and technical difficulties but is unwilling to accommodate or be flexible with her students.

In all four years of undergrad, Octavio was by far my least favorite professor. She grades so tough if it were up to her she would fail almost everybody. She also picks on struggling students and makes it impossible to want or receive her help. She even made a student cry once. I'd pick a new language before I'd pick her class.

Worst professor I ever had. Her classroom felt toxic and unsafe, everyday I could sense that everyone else was uncomfortable too. She would snap at students for no reason, name call, use profanity. Her excuse for this behavior is that people in Spain are blunt and that Americans want to be coddled. No, we just want to feel safe and respected.

Do not take this prof if you can avoid it. She is often unclear about directions or expectations, and students are too afraid to ask her to clarify because she's rude when people don't understand. She also doesn't help students when they're struggling with what they're trying to say and instead ridicules them.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Do not, I mean never ever ever ever ever take her section of SPN. She is horrible, she is condescending and creates a toxic learning environment. She wants heavy participation but does not offer help to students that are struggling with phrasing sentences. Worst professor I have had in my three year of university.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Very passive-aggressive when asking questions, almost rude even. Not good at teaching and with the most unclear grading criteria possible. I legit couldn't see my percent grade all semester. Expect lots of meaningless group projects and busy work. Weekly quizzes that are pretty easy, but oftentimes hard to understand. Do not take.

A nice guy to talk to, but awful teacher. Does not give any instructions and expects you to know everything before hand. Does not grade until the last minute and does not grade resubmits fast, so you don't know what you get wrong.

I took LB 117 online, for the most part it was the same content as my high school calculus class but he either never elaborated on what he wanted us to do on the labs, or briefly went over what material he wanted us to turn in and always took so long to grade stuff. Overall horrible experience.
I would not recommended taking this class. This professor briefly goes over material and expects 100% understanding and some. There are also labs in this class?? A math class.

Dr. Fessler does not teach, but rather reads equations and makes you solve them all class. He does not handle criticism or suggestions at all. Makes you buy a new calculator for the 3 tests that we have. Be prepared to teach yourself everything.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: horrible horrible horrible never ever take mth103 with chuck fessler. he may be the worst teacher i've ever had in my entire life. his grading doesn't make any sense, the exams do not correlate with the material we learn in class whatsoever and he does not respond well to criticism or feedback and blames everyone failing on us not being prepared.

super nice guy but grades lab reports insanely. it is insanely hard. extremely tough grader on the lab reports for the pre-clac equal. for exams in MTH 103, it was very hard to gauge his expectations on tests/quizzes. i felt like i never knew what he was looking for

I took calc in high school, and struggled with his class. He does not explain well, and examples we do in class are completely different than assessments. I turned in an assignment in the beginning of October and it was not graded until early December. It was also nearly impossible to tell what your actual grade was throughout. not a good time

Dr. Fessler was always very nice and supportive for me. I went to his office hours often. He pulls some questions from the book for quizzes and exams but you can never be prepared enough. Seek as many sources as you can for studying. I did have to study very hard for his course but I managed to 4.0 with never taking calc in highschool!

professor fessler is a great guy however he was a terrible professor. he left me and my classmates very confused after every class and expected us to know everything presented in front of us. he didn't teach properly and all the homework that was assigned per unit didn't correlate with anything we were "learning" in class.

Would give 0. Didn't grade majority of assignments until end of semester. Never taught in class + majority of the class was always confused. Tests and online homework did not match examples done in class. Hard to succeed unless you have a strong calc background. Never helped w/ problems and didn't seem like he knew what he taught. Save yourself.

Nice guy, but can't teach at all. He works very fast in class and expects students to already have some background in calc. I was also having medical issues this semester and he was very hard to work with on this. He also lost one of my exams after I took it in his office hours. Don't recommend at all

If I can give Fessler a 0 I would. He doesn't teach any of his subjects and expects you to know everything already. You have a a quiz every week that's 50% of your grade and there is no dropping but only 1 retake of an exam at the end of the semester. The homework is on webwork and doesn't really correlate with what he is "teaching".
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Fessler is an amazing guy to talk to, but he is not a very effective instructor. I often found myself leaving lectures confused and unsure of how I would be able to succeed in this course. Once again, he is a very caring, but please try to get an override if you have to take one of his courses. I left Briggs because of instructors like this.

Chuck is a great guy but should not be teaching math 103. He changes the way he grades constantly, gets irritated at students for asking questions, and barely teaches. He just makes us do the notes and expects us to be math wizards. He is very kind but if you get stuck with him for class, I would recommend taking math 103 through the university.

Nice guy, awful professor. His grading is harsh and confusing and forces you to do most of the work in terms of determining your actual grade. He does not teach the material well if he teaches it at all, and the questions seen on assessments are ones not previously discussed, found in the notes, or in any of the hw or review problems. Do not take.

Terrible professor do not take him! Grades very tough and gives weekly quizzes on material he barely covers. Treats this introductory Calc 1 course like a upper level course. Do not take him no matter what you do.

Does not teach. Once got annoyed with students for asking questions and not understanding content, which was only the case because he teaches way too fast or not at all. Sometimes is unsure about his own questions. Practice exam material does not always match actual exam content. He is friendly and a good person but not a good teacher.

Very good person and could have been a more effective instructor. He is a great guy to talk to, but when it comes to course material, I am always left confused, and so are my classmates. He is accommodating but also really likes to talk, which is inconvenient when you need help with class activities.

Great guy, terrible teacher. This class was genuinely one of the worst experiences I had in college, I would come into class and leave more confused than when I had entered. You can easily learn the content online (organic chem tutor) rather than in his lectures. His teaching style just did not resonate with me and friends who took his class. 3/10

The professor is very nice and sweet, but he's not good at teaching math, as he demonstrates how to solve things fast with barely any explanation. He also grades extremely hard on tests and quizzes, giving some problems that are barely within the range of what he taught. Everyone's left confused after every class period

Incredibly nice guy but occasionally difficult to work with. He is not the greatest at explaining material, and many questions end up being taught to yourself because it is challenging to understand them in his lectures. Would  honestly make a better English or history professor
Chuck is a great guy who wants you to succeed if you demonstrate to him that you care. He is very helpful and understanding!

I had him for both trig and calc. I will say I saw improvement in his grading between the two semesters, but it is still very difficult to succeed if you're not already good at math or have had no prior calc experience. Quizzes every week, you can't miss them without a doctor's note. I liked him as a person, but he isn't the greatest professor.

He's a nice guy but take someone else for calculus. He means well by standards-based grading but it makes it more difficult to get a good grade. Quizzes are every week in recitation and I felt like I didn't learn concepts in lecture. Our ULA would go over content before the quiz and that's when I'd grasp the subject.

He is a good person but that is it. He teaches terrible and gives you quizzes every week during recitation. He gives problems and questions that you never really learn in class. The TAs felt bad for us because we would all be so lost in his class. I would try to take calc through the university. The grading scale makes it impossible to do good.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Very nice person, but I would recommend taking Calc through university. Quizzes every week during recitation. LA's always felt bad for us because of the immense struggle we all had. Grades based on standards and I had no idea what my grade was all semester. Some questions on the quizzes 1 exams I had never seen before.
Just so bad, so so so bad. do not take. take university calc and do not take lyman calc. or u will be screwed. best of luck!!!

Actually a horrendous professor (nice guy though). Ended up teaching myself everything because of it. Study guides do not help, and a very misleading grading system. You will have quizzes every week (on and off independent and group style) in recitation which is required. You cannot skip these quizzes without a doctor's note (same for exams).

HORRIBLE class. He is a nice guy but I dont think teaching math is his thing. take calculus through university you get online exams.  if you don't want to watch your grade drop every week take it outside. really bad grading his teaching style is also wack.  the exam study guide doesn't correlate with the exam. Nice guy needs to consider changing su

Whatever you do, do not take this class. He changes the way you're graded constantly and literally cannot teach. If you are not already a master at trig, STAY AWAY. He overcomplicates everything and will take points away if you don't do work the way he wants it. He's actually a pretty nice guy, but worst prof I've ever had.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things
