reviews.comment: Entire grade is based on 3/4 projects. Attendance not required. He makes you buy his textbook. Dont buy it. Its a waste of your money. ALL of your assignemnts are graded by TA. I got a 4.0 in the class beacuse I had a nice TA.

He would constantly change the syllabus where the instructions for most assignments and projects were. He would come across as mocking of students in class after he'd ask for people to share their opinions. So many written responses, it makes a fun subject into a chore.

Youre whole grade is 3 projects basically and is all graded based on the TA's. If you get a TA that grades harsh, you're cooked. Mine graded harsh but my friend got straight 100s. Also just a waste of time learning this bs
I would never take a class of his again. Please incoming freshman take a different IAH class. This one is easy but still many more easier and enjoyable classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: You're WHOLE grade is determined by the TA you have, and they grade so hard and it is almost impossible to get an A even when you follow the rubric. PLS DROP THIS CLASS ( Afrofuturism)

Professor Chambliss is fine, the lectures can be a bit boring, however attendance is not required! I know people who went to no lectures and passed just fine, the course is based on 3 large projects (20% each) and 20% for recertation. They take attendance and if you miss 2+ that's 25% of your grade, befriend your TA thats how you get good grades!!

Although the course is about afrofuturism, you never really learn much about it as he lectures about the randomest topics ever. He wrote the mandatory textbook which is hard to understand. Your grade is dependent on your TA and most were very harsh graders. Dropped this class with zero regrets

You can take this class, never go to a lecture, and still pass with a 4.0. Your grade is solely dependent on who your TA is and how clearly they describe the projects. Dr. Chambliss will tell you to buy his book about Afrofuturism, do not buy it. You do not need that book for this class at all, I never read a single word of it. Class is super easy.

Four projects make up the majority of the grade and they're graded by the TA, not Dr. Chambliss. His class material is a lot more interesting than he is engaging, but his lectures aren't mandatory-- only the recitations. I had the pleasure of having a great TA (Journey Smith). Can't speak for those who don't-- simple projects though.

Like others have said, your grade depends on your TA. You don't have to go to lectures, but definitely go to recitations as attendence is mandatory and they recap lectures. You're graded on 4 projects and then recitation attendence, worksheets, and we had to take notes for one lecture and then teach one topic to our recitation class.
TAKE THIS CLASS ONLINE IT IS 10X LESS WORK. Also, most of the TA's in this class grade your papers like they are harvard professors.

Every IAH/ISS class is taught by someone who spent their entire career studying a niche/pointless subcategory of literature. They probably wrote a book that they require you to buy for the class (like this guy) if you have to take this class because it's required (like me) I suggest you take it online. Every assignment can be found on the internet.
I never went to a single lecture and got a 4.0, there are a few projects and thats mostly all you have to do. i would def take this professor again hes so chill

Chambliss is a nice guy but because of the way the class is formatted, your grade is entirely based on who your TA is. I would reccommend going to lecture in the beginning of the semester. Your whole grade is based on four projects and then your recitation grade, so I reccommend not taking this class because you can't pick your TA

Even though lectures were boring, I attended all of them yet still did bad. My TA was a harsh grader, you have to get lucky and get an easy one to do well. It's not worth it, take another more interesting IAH class.

Great professor, always cracks a few jokes in class. I would recommend going to lectures at the beginning of the course, but toward the end, it's less necessary. I did not read a single reading assigned to me and still got a 4.0. The TA was good; a little picky and slow with grading. I recommend going to all recitations if you can!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: My grade dropped drastically because of a missed assignment in recitation 3.5 to 2.5. Lectures are boring you don't even have to honestly. Put all that work into my assignments just to receive a C for one assignment. Just a waste of time. Save your money and enroll in a different course. Emailed TA about grade never got a response.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I maintained a 3.5 in the class the ENTIRE semester but at the very end my grade dropped to a 2.5 because I missed ONE assignment from RECITATION! I had B's on every major project that was given to me and a A on my final but somehow that didn't matter because since I missed one assignment in my recitation my grade is now a C. Don't take this course
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is a really good guy at first, but the topic can be super uninteresting and hard to stay engaged with. It is also really hard to find good sources/examples online for projects. Lecture was so boring! The one major problem with this class was my TA graded super hard and it is usually very inconsistent. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!

Seems like a chill guy at first, but when I talked to him about a grade dispute he was very condescending and disrespectful. Limited work makes it hard to get a good grade in this class, and I wish I took a different IAH since this one messed up my gpa.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I HATED THIS CLASS! I was one of the few that actually attended lectures and still learned nothing. Your grade depends on only 4 projects, so if you do bad on one your whole grade tanks. I would NOT recommend this class.
Most uninteresting lecture I've ever taken. The TA was not good and very rarely gives out good grades. Not worth your time or sanity.
Extremely rude when you speak to him. Very unclear measures for grading and takes points off for no reason. Do not recommend him. Please stay away from his class.

Don't take this class. Course description doesn't mention it's about Afrofuturism. People stopped showing up to his lectures because they're boring and useless. Grades depend on the TA, mine was a super harsh grader. Project instructions were unclear, no rubric.Talked to the professor about my grade, and he laughed in my face, very unprofessional.

Dr. Chambliss is a great teacher if you are interested in the subject of Afrofuturism. I didn't really have an interest in this subject but still had a great time. Attendance is not mandatory for lecture but the recitation attendance is, and all the grading is done by your recitation TA. Only about 4 assignments in one semester. Lots of reading.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He was a great guy, but I just wasn't interested in the topic. I didn't need to attend lecture which was nice. However, you should be aware that your recitation teacher for this class will be someone else, typically Master's students who are not the best at what they do.

Professor Chambliss is amazing! He gets everyone engaged in the lecture & gives some prompts to get you thinking at the beginning of lecture that are honestly very interesting. He doesn't do the grading but the TA I had was amazing also and a very easy grader. His lectures do not require attendance but the recitation that goes along with it does.

Class is pretty lecture heavy but overall not super hard. Assignments are very interesting and I would recommend for health majors. There's no exams so assignments and participation determine your final grade.

Dr. Carlson is engaging in class activities and lectures, but the work that comes with it is not great. Her grading criteria is very vague and it feels as though she just expects you to read her mind at times while also grading tough. She also accuses people of using AI but uses no AI tracker to back it up so points are docked for no solid reason.

(For HBIO 410) This class was more focused on research than a traditional lecture and exam style class. The exams involve analyzing a paper as a group and answering questions based on it. There are group projects that accumulate into one final project. The grading is strict however, they provide the rubric they use to grade. Fun class overall!

If you do not do the assignment exactly how it needs to be you are going to get marked down. Also, directions are not always clear read every checklist/directions! Final does include two essays and a minute long video with a partner. Weird class material but I did learn some things.

I love Professor Cesario's lectures; they're easy to follow, and he makes sure students have a solid grasp of the concepts before moving on. There are 5 exams, but the lowest score gets dropped. Extra credit opportunities and attendance is also extra credit, not mandatory!

His lectures Are very engaging compared to other professors I've had. You also learned a lot about yourself in this class through the material taught. If you pay attention and take notes, you will be fine on exam exams. Highly recommend!

Attendance is not mandatory but do not make the mistake that I did of then skipping a couple of classes. Each class you miss is then like -7% off your exam grade because the exam material only makes sense after attending the lecture even though he posts slides. Graded very largely on 4/5 exams (one drop) w/ a few assignments worth like 5 points.

Seriously take this class online at your community college and save yourself the headache. His lectures and objectives do not prepare you for the tests. No matter how much I studied, not once did it make sense on the test. You have to show up to class and listen to his lectures or u will fail. He doesn't post anything from lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He genuinely does care about your grade, and always was willing to schedule appointments for office hours if you couldn't make it to the original time. A lot of your grade is based on exams, but if you attended lectures it was impossible not to do good. I finished with over 100% in the class, as he gives a lot of extra credit opportunities.

awesome funny guy, who is more than happy to explain a concept to you. I promise you if you do the readings and study based off of his LECTURE, NOT his slides, it will be an easy A for you. Show up to class!! It matters in this class!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Very funny guy, will use lots of personal stories to explain what he's trying to teach. Tests are not hard or tricky at all. Will give you lots of extra credit for just showing up to class so a 3.5-4 is not hard to get in this class at all.

I studies for every single exam, tried to record lectures, take notes and I still did bad on every exam. He had told us in the beginning of the semester that he will have someone take notes and he will try to post them. That never happened. His exams were so hard and made me hate a good and interesting class.

Class structure was absolutely horrible! 100% exam based, lectures don't have any slides to go off of you just have to sit there and listen to way he says, the D2L page has nothing in it to prepare for exams he just tells us to look at our notes then it is very difficult to take notes when he doesn't post any type of notes from the lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is AMAZING! He is fully engaged and ready to get your brain involved; all you need to do is match responsiveness and readiness. However, I will halt my praise and say only one comment: the lecture slides he posts are sustainable for frequent absence, but attendance is SO beneficial to gain connections and associations and actually learn!
very engaging lectures, great at answering any questions

Professor Cesario was my favorite professor at MSU, as a second year student. His lectures are engaging, as he does many class demos, and connects real time news with the topics. He is also just genuinely hilarious. His exams match his study guides, and he sets clear guidelines for the papers. I would 100% take him again as a psych major.

This class was one of the most interesting ones that I have ever taken. You can receive extra credit on random days for showing up (I recommend but you can still get a good grade without it) and there are several exams and a few other assignments to help your grade. There are also a few readings he posts but you'll have enough time for them.

Dr. Cesario is very clear about what he expects from students, and I think that helped contribute to my grade in the course. You NEED to attend lecture if you want to do well in the class. You get extra credit for doing so, and majority of the content is not in the slides. If the content doesn't come easily to you, STUDY and go to office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Exams are worded horribly and are meant to confuse you. Readings are ridiculous and long. His lectures were very interesting and cool and there is a reason why it was full most classes, but having a class where 200/215 available points were from exams is brutal. Overall, this class is hard for no reason and takes hours upon hours of studying.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: His class is difficult because his tests are worded in a way that is difficult to understand and he requires you to read graduate level papers, however, he gives so many opportunities for extra credit. He is also very kind and caring and will help you with the material. Go to office hours!!!

If it wasn't for the extra credit, I legit would've failed. This class is way harder than it needs to be. The amount of readings is insane, like he thinks we don't have any other classes. Also, he tells us not to rely on the slides for notes, but then just lectures straight off the slides anyway? Super frustrating. If you can, I'd avoid this class.

Left this class confused by the other ratings. I will say there's a LOT of reading but this is also a 300 level PSY class so I'm confused on how that's surprising. Professor Cesario is very smart, and gives great lectures that always make me think. If you attend class and do the readings you really wont have any trouble with this class. Lots of EC.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Test heavy

reviews.comment: My GOAT! Dr. Cesario is one of my favorite professors I've ever had. Dude is hilarious and insanely knowledgable on the subject, bringing in a mixture of his own views and others. If you pay attention to the lectures (hard not to pay attention) and take notes, you will do very well. Can't wait to take another class with him.

This was my favorite psych class ever! Attendance isn't necessary, but you won't do well with the content if you don't go to class. He also gives a bunch of extra credit for just showing up to class. He is so funny, you won't regret taking this class. 5 exams, you drop your lowest grade (including the final).

Genuinely one of the worst classes I have ever taken. This professor is a chronic academic who views the world in data and statistics while ignoring the context. Assigned scientific papers each week that appear on the exams and are not discusses in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is the first time I've actually learned from a professor! I took the honors version of social psychology (in 2024), so it's a bit different from the regular one. The professor doesn't rely on slides; he wants us to take notes instead of just reading them, which is why I feel like I've learned so much! He's very charismatic and helpful. :)

3 or 4 exams (one dropped), 3 assignments, and extra credit on random attendance days. Pretty easy he just lectures the whole time while everyone takes notes. He's very interested in his job and goes over a ton of studies. You do have to read some articles before class sometimes but it's pretty easy. Exams aren't too hard either

I have very mixed feelings, because I really liked listening to his lectures, and I like him as a person. That said, it was incredibly frustrating that the tests (which were open-note) felt like they were worded in a way that was meant to trip you up. Not a bad class to fill some credit hours, but be warned that getting a 4.0 is an uphill battle.

Cesario is def my favorite professor thus far. You get extra credit on days he takes attendance, as well as extra credit for doing a 5 question optional quiz every week. There's 4 exams which are moderately difficult, helps if you go to his lectures. Sometimes boring, but he tries to keep you entertained.

His class covered various aspects of social psychology and he always found a way to keep the class engaged and laughing constantly. His lectures were never boring and he offers extra credit points for showing up to class here and there although attendence isn't mandatory. Exams aren't too hard but study well.
He is amazing!!! should for sure take his class. Best teacher ever

I genuinely don't know why people are dissing him. He is amazing! Love him and his class. No regrets for PSY 235 besides the fact it is making me question my individuality and existence as a human, but that is the class topic's fault lol. Study, and you will be fine. He is a lovely guy, and the class isn't difficult, but getting an A, honestly, is.
Prof Cesario Z
As long as you go to class (he takes attendance some days) and pay attention you will do well on the exams and 3 hw assignments.
I dropped his class on the first day. I'm a deans list student and this guy wasn't worth the drop in my grade. BEWARE.

He is lecture heavy, come to lecture and take notes. Not mandatory attendance, but you get extra credit. He uses current and relevant research to guide his lectures, instead of a textbook. The only grades are exams, even if you get 80's on all the exams, you can still 4.0 with the extra credit. I advise going to office hours it helps

His format is based on test scores, supplemented by extra credit which you can receive by coming to lectures. Because the course is lecture heavy if you don't come and engage you won't understand material. He encourages people to come to class and is able to meet outside of lecture time. Material is not solely his own work, but recent research.

Bad person
He's not good

A lot of these reviews are negative but the content was very interesting and it's clear he cares abt his students. EC attendance is worth the same amount as a test question along w additional ec can bump your grade up a whole letter. Just pay attention in the lectures and do the readings. The exams are easy and self explanatory if you do that.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I just really hope it was a better professor that's NOT HIM For this class.

Take my advice, live a better life and avoid his class man. There's no assignments just 4 exams. Nobody likes this guy, his class is hard having nothing else to boost the grade. 2 Extra credit points a week don't count.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He was funny and seems like a nice guy in general. I didn't attend all of the lectures, which could be why I struggled so much in this class. I wouldn't recommend this course if you aren't prepared to attend every lecture and read assigned readings multiple times. The only assignments are exams, and a little bit of extra credit
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Please avoid him.
Listen to the negative ratings, they are right.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Cesario is honestly a great professor if you take the class seriously. Lectures aren't mandatory but you will want to go anyways. There's over a letter grade's worth of extra credit if you put all the work in. If you go to the lectures and read the assigned readings the exams should be easy, even though they're really question heavy.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE. He rambles on about ridiculous caveats for half of the lecture, excluding information that may actually be useful. You get 4 grades in the book, and they're all exams. No drops, homework, or attendance grades. You better read those 40 page pdf case studies 100 times each if you want to even pass the class. Overcomplicated for no reason
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I wish I could say something positive about this course, but I cannot. Professor Cesario is not a welcoming professor and the tests are not indicative of students learnings. Why any professor makes a class entirely exams is beyond me, but Cesario decided to determine students grades entirely off of exams. Please avoid AT ALL COSTS!

Attendance is not mandatory, but he does surprise attendance days where you get one point of extra credit if you're there. His exams are 50-80 questions (there are three of them), and then there is a 100-question, cumulative final exam. That's it. There are a lot of readings that you need to memorize. It's possible to do good, but I don't recommend
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. it's set up for you to fail. I have attended every lecture, done every reading, and studied extensively before every exam and I still can't do well on them. You are graded only on 4 exams and each questioned is worded like a trick question. Exams are in person on scantrons and he won't tell you what you got wrong on them.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
I never liked this guy from the beginning
He gives people any grade that he wants not the grade you really deserve I hate those types of professors there are a lot of them and he is one of them
We just did an exam and I know for sure I got more answers right than the grade he put in he has no problem with making you fail
No assignments just 4 exams

Honestly a great prof. Just be prepared to put in actual effort when he asks you to read. Other than the exams, there's no other grades/assignments so as long as you attend class and read required reading, you will do pretty well on the exam. A lot of people complain but that's likely due to them not going to lecture enough
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He is by far the worst professor I have had in my 4 yrs at MSU. He gets side tracked every class which makes him miss material in the lecture slides. The slides are repetitive. Exams are the main grades aside from occasional attendance extra credit. Most of the exam questions are from long readings. If you can avoid having class with him at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He repeats things often in lectures and often times the lectures can be boring. This classes only grades are four exams and they are long exams which are extremely detailed. He offers a study guide but does not give it until right before the exam and it is little to no help. Did not find out about exams until a week or two before the exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I've never had such a horrible class in my 4 years at MSU. He changed the way he taught the class, made it way more challenging. He seems to want his class to be hard. Exams ONLY focus on the papers assigned. Extremely hard for a 300 level class, and lectures are very dry and heavy. Only grades by exams, and minimal extra credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's Awesome

It's so hard to stay engaged when he just rambles on about the same topic and uses absurd examples to back up his points. Half of his lectures make no sense and you're sitting for 2 hours listening to him ramble. You're graded only on paper exams, and he expects you to memorize every paper and their data for the exam. Just don't take it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: You can tell he cares a lot about teaching and some of his lectures were pretty entertaining. As long as you show up to class, actively listen, and do the study guide it is a pretty easy A

As a non-psych major this was my easiest class by far. Take good notes, come to class and you'll get lots of EC. Engaging material presented in an understandable way if you follow his train of thought, but can be hard if you zone out. Only complaint is that the TAs are not very transparent about their grading on the application assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: My favorite professor from this semester! Lectures are not mandatory, but you should treat them like they are. He gives extra credit on random days for attendance points, drops your lowest exam grade, is super funny, and clearly loves his job and his students. If I could rate him above a 5, I would.

I love love love Prof. Cesario, he's funny and is great at teaching. Exams are super easy on one condition: attend the class, the slides are not enough. It really is an easy class, and he makes it fun by doing experiments on us and showing the class's data match the data of the experiments and theories he teaches.

Class itself is easy but seems as if prof goes out of his way to make it harder to succeed Ex: having 5-10 points of attendance credit on days it's hard for students to make it. Showed studies that were only done with white men instead of looking for better studies + would nitpick studies to make them support his idea when that's not what was meant

Hard class to teach, I can imagine. Don't go into this class closed minded, be open for discussion. Material can be hard to hear and understand but you have to separate opinion from statistics for tests. He is open for in class debates and very open to questions. Often times rambles on about one topic. Great class to be in, really eye opening
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: If you are taking PSY 235 with him, I would recommend him. However, I would not recommend taking 342 with him unless you want to sit through hours and hours of curated data he finds to back up his points. He essentially lectures you, as a white man, as to how no discrimination exists and how it is minorities own fault for any problem that exists.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Honestly I just didn't love his lectures. He's okay at getting his point across, but he's a bit of an over-explainer which makes him hard to listen to. The lectures in general aren't great for people with learning disabilities. The material is fine. He wasn't terrible, I just didn't enjoy his class.
Easily the best prof at MSU. Repeats himself to get the point across, but makes sure you learn something. However, you do have to go to class and pay attention to do well/

Easiest class I've taken. Lectures are kind of long, but it is extremely easy to do well in this class. There are 3 writing assignments and 5 exams (one is optional) and nothing is weighted. The writing assignments are over anything you have learned in class and only are a page long. Also a very interesting class if you need an elective.

this course is genuinely depressing and makes you truly appreciate the wonderfully meaningless gift of human existence. thanks. while Prof. Cesario is very coherent and delivers the course material well, it doesn't compensate for the bleak nature of the course material which ultimately leaves one more depressed than when they first arrived.
Genuinely one of the best professors I have ever had. 10/10

Cesario is a super caring/nice guy. Online quiz every weekend of just 10 questions. Plus a final thats made of previous quiz questions. Thats literally all you're graded on, plus Extra credit if you want it. super easy A. he repeats himself a ton to get his point across

It's making me mad that some of these people in the comments don't realize that professors are humans too. The way he taught material was phenomenal. He actually made sure you learned it. For those complaining that you had to show up to class to learn, what did you expect? I have learned some stuff that I apply to my every day life! Great class

Professor Cesario is one of the best professors I've had. He is clear with what he expects, there are papers due but they couldn't be easier to write. The only downfall of this class is it was SUPER disorganized. Takes WEEKS to get exams and papers back, you're taking the next unit exam without having your grade from the last one. Overall easy A

This class could have been so great if it was not for Dr. Cesario being SO disorganized. We did not get our 2nd exam grades back until after we took the 3rd exam which was so frustrating. He also never responded to emails!! The TA's were useless and not helpful whatsoever.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He is literally so disorganized. This class was a joke. The lecture slides are not detailed, so if you skip class, you will do awful on the exams. The TAs for this semester are also so god awful. We had an assignment due in February... Grades are still not all out yet. This class is a joke. Exam scores take up to 3/4 weeks to know how you did.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Awesome guy. Straightforward class. He runs through all of his slides and gives explanations. There are weekly online quizzes and a final, which is composed of the questions from the quizzes. He sounds like Kermit the frog which made the lectures so much more entertaining. The only downside of Joe is that he says "ok" after every single sentence.

My favorite proffessor ever!! He is so nice and tries to get to know the students that show up and participate regularly. All lectures are posted online so you don't really have to go to class but I found it to be helpful because he'll often explain conepts more clearly and answers any questions you have. 10/10 would recommend this class!

I would highly recommend taking your ISS with Cesario, he gives great lectures and makes sure you learn a lot while having a light work load. I wish I could have him again for another class, Easy A and makes lectures interesting. Overall great prof

Cesario is one of the best professors I've ever had. He taught the highly politicized subject of social inequality in the most pragmatic way I could have imagined. Doesn't ask hard questions on the weekly quizzes, just make sure to pay attention in lecture. He definitely knows what he's talking about, and you'll get a lot out of his class.

It has been 4 years since I took this class, but it's still the first that comes to mind as my favorite. Cesario is a fantastic lecturer and the class is so engaging and entertaining. I wish I could take it again, no joke.

The only grades in this class are weekly quizzes and a final exam. Professor Cesario is a great lecturer and makes the concepts extremely clear. He keeps you engaged as well. I skipped a lot of class and still four pointed. As long as you study past quiz questions for the final exam, you'll be fine. It was a fine class, Cesario is a great prof.
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Amazing lectures--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: All you are graded on is weekly quizzes and a final. I'm a bad test taker so this makes it hard to be successful.

I'm a psych major, so going in I knew this class was going to be important to me. Cesario made lectures interesting and easy to understand, which made coming to his class even better.  Show up to lecture and you're set. He also gives out extra credit for those who attend lecture on random days.
Amazing lectures, very funny professor.  Gives many extra credit opportunities, content is fun to learn about.

Great class and subject matter, super fascinating and great for learning about the world. Teaches it well and genuinely cares about material. All lecture, lots of time to ask questions, no mandatory attendance, and does offer some extra credit. Grades based on open note online quizzes and a final. Reading not really necessary. Not too difficult.
One of the best professors I've ever had.
Really gets you involved and interested!
Cant say enough about Prof Cesario. Great guy. Lectures are usually entertaining and fly by. Exam questions are pulled right from the lectures, and assigned readings.   Highly Recommend
Super cool guy and gives interesting and funny lectures. Great professor!

I loved this class! If you show up to lectures and take notes you should be able to pass easily. He is super funny, he cares about his students, and his lectures are easy to understand. Plus he gives extra credit and drops your lowest exam! He really relates content to the students. One of his GA's made a big google doc before exams that helped too

I really enjoyed having professor Cesario as a teacher. He made the material very easy to understand and was clear. His lectures were generally pretty easy to stay engaged in and he employed many real life examples. In addition, on many occasions he even engaged the class in different optional participation experiments to provide the class withdata

He is HANDS DOWN the funniest/most inspirational professor/teacher I've ever had. Every single lecture is full of funny real-world examples of concepts that make 300+ students laugh. Everything is very applicable to your own daily life, and this class allows you to understand yourself and your life on a deeper level that VERY few classes do.

The entire class is McGraw hill information on the exams (hardly any from lectures). In class she asks too many questions to students going off on large tangents and doesn't teach a clear lecture which makes it hard to follow. Also treats students like toddlers, were adults.

Doesn't actually teach, just lightly reviews long textbook chapters that you have to pay nearly $100 to access and have to read weekly before the first lecture even begins. Wastes so much in-class time going over the "classroom rules"; we are adults and nobody came to this class to be told to wait until the bell rings to put their computer away.

So rude, offers absolutely no support but acts like she wants the best for you. Gave a speech about how she made the test really hard but even then people did good, to her that wasn't "normal" so she proceeded to make it worse. Don't take her classes unless you want to feel like you are in WW2 Germany

I personally loved Professor Clements. She's a professor that doesn't take BS, so if that's not the professor for you, then don't take her. 3 exams, a group project, and a smartbook every week (plus the final). She wants you to learn because you're paying good money to go to this university!

A lot of these ratings are definitely too harsh. She's not the best, but she's not bad. The content is interesting, and if you pay attention to lectures, you'll know a good amount of the exam. The class is hard, I won't lie. Outside of class, you have to do a significant amount of studying to do well. The smartbooks are a pain, but helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Her class is alright, just be prepared to study a lot outside of class. She'll make 50 slides for the class then go over 10, and the rest she'll say to learn on your own. If you are a busy person, I suggest another prof if there is one because nothing that I've learned from this class came from her lectures and entirely from my own studying.

She finds it sus that her students do well in a course so she makes it harder even if you study really hard and she spends most of the time trying to do "critical discussions" but skips over slides and just tells us they're important and to look over them in notes. Not my style

Exams were kind of hard, but it's just a lot of information. She does sometimes go off topic but she seems nice and encourages students to go to office hours if you need help. Lectures are long and can be boring at times. Homework takes forever but can be helpful for exams, you can drop 1 out of the 5 exams.
Exams are the only thing that matter. Lots work that doesn't effect your grade. Bad teacher.
She decided to make her exams much harder from the previous year because people had success. To many exams and crams a ton of info into short periods. She is probably a nice lady but this class sucks
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Forget what everyone else is saying, Dr. Clements is awesome. As long as you put in at least some effort, you'll do fine. She wants to see students succeed. Exams are worth 15% of the grade. Never had a better professor that prepares you for everything and keeps you engaged.

She is a great professor, but I think that also came from how much she cared about the material. Groupwork was fine, but groups were selected (by her) based on preferences, which was nice. If you get a bad group, it will be okay, just a little difficult, because she understands and cares about her students.

She is ok & nice. I can tell she wants us to succeed. OK lectures but flies thru them. Online quiz every week which is good points. A lot of class discussions & group projects (hopefully ur group participates, I got lucky). Tries to teach us a framework of thinking. Study guides were the book objectives (giving the bare minimum). Tests kinda hard.

Would definitely take a course with Professor Clements again. She is so amazing and very passionate about these topics. Very much open to answering questions and if she doesnt know the answer she takes the time to look it up later and have a class discussion. Class is very engaging and never found myself bored.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This professor is very condescending, she thinks she is above everyone. Would yell at students for whispering and disrupting, even though they were enough not to disturb anyone. Would give the wrong answers to questions, you could teach the course yourself better than she can teach you.
Worst professor I have had at Michigan State. Avoid at all costs.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Treats her entire class like it's elementary school, and blames YOU if you're struggling in her class even though her teaching methods are absolutely horrendous. Belittles students instead of assisting them when asked for help. When I took the exams it felt like every question was some type of riddle. Probably the worst prof I've had at MSU.

PSY 101 is an Introduction to Psychology course. INTRODUCTION. If you are reading this, know that exams are 80% of your grade. It's great because not only are the exams so difficult, they always have mistakes. She acts like we are 5 years old. Save your GPA and take this class online. I've never had a worse professor in my entire life.

She disrespects her students' autonomy as adults, and is dismissive of all ideas at odds with her own. She lacks the acuity to answer questions asked of her without outside sources. Her course gives excessive amounts of busywork which count for nothing, and her exams, which are 80% of the grade, are terrible. Her lectures are basically pre-recorded
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Exams are ridiculously hard, even if you go to every single lecture. But don't worry, if you do bad on them they're only 80% of your grade!!
Just no !
dont take the class

Do not take this professor under any circumstance. She is the most condescending person I've ever met and makes you feel like a kindergartener. The course is way too hard to be a 100 level, and it's not the content, it's her. I've never sat in an exam and heard five people seated around me discuss their distaste for a professor, until now.
study study study for the 4 guarantee exams. final is optional unless you need it. lectures for whole 2 hours gives a 10 minute break but other than that she is lecturing
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: very liberal and awful and teaching the material that is provided and does not care for her students.

This teacher was terrible, she treated us as children and would yell at us for the slightest of sound and threaten to kick us out of class. Her exams are nothing like the quizzes or the textbook questions. She has made numerous mistakes on the exams as well. I will never go to this teacher again. Do Not Take Her Class!

As an incoming freshman, this class is absolutely horrendous! The lectures are long, boring, and useless. There is almost no chance to ask questions and when students do ask questions the answer feels very passive aggressive and condescending. The tests are hard and there is no leniency for Sona hours. Teacher is heavy on deadlines and simply rude!
Long-winded lectures. Really difficult exams, especially for a 100 level course. Too much content covered in each exam. Not a good time.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Talks about "classroom climate" for the first 10 minutes of class and then proceeds to shove information down your throat for the remain hour and 40 minutes. Listening to her voice is a genuine challenge. Avoid at all costs!!!

Completely awful professor. There are not resources. Lectures  are not recorded, there is 0 opportunity to ask questions, and the lectures are awful. The concepts are simple, and can be explained in 20 minute Crash Course videos more effectively. She is extremely condescending to even the most respectful student. Take another prof.

Best prof ever, her class is very discussion based and very interesting. Make sure you are doing all the hw tho, she will not remind you. No exams just one very easy but large group project. I implore you to take this class!

Clements doesn't know how to properly teach PSY 320. From her passive-aggressive comments to her nasty responses to emails, this lady does not deserve her Ph.D. Instead of teaching health psychology to aspiring STEM students, she gives you 80 minutes on how to demonstrate passive-aggressive tendencies & problematic behavior as a grown woman!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Professor Clements is absolutely terrible. Her lectures are hard to follow--like she doesn't understand what she's talking about. She presents information in an extremely biased way and is overwhelmingly critical of opposing beliefs to her own. She is extremely passive aggressive. Her class is project based and there is no final exam.

She's really difficult. Every time she is asked a question she can't respond. She is also very passive aggressive towards students. I took child development and lifespan development so most of the content is familiar to me. But she explains it in such a awful complicated way. She is making the class harder than what it has to be.

She has no clue what she is doing. The content is so easy but she makes it 10X harder than it has to be with all the extra busy work. She doesn't know how to explain things correctly and wastes a lot of your time. It is frustrating

I truly believe that Kathryn does not understand the basic tenets of psychology. The fact that she was able to obtain a PhD may speak to the fact that you should go to trade school or consider a different career path, as even an advanced degree is largely worth nothing in this country anymore.

Although the class had some interesting content, I'm not sure I can recommend taking it. Group projects and homework assignments were graded harshly compared to the relatively simple course content. Quiz answers were all online. Class had atypical due dates for all assignments (Fridays at 5pm, 7pm), and there was no final exam.

I love her so much!! I signed up for another one of her classes in the spring. She's so sweet and creates a welcoming classroom climate for her students. Her teaching style is great. She encourages creative thinking, which means no tests or quizzes, and I like how we practice mindfulness at the beginning of class.

Prof. Clements uses an online version of the textbook, so you have to pay a little bit for the site but otherwise not a huge textbook charge. The quizzes are super easy as are the assignments, if you follow along during lectures and maybe go to office hours once you're pretty much golden.

Dr. Clements is very caring and accommodating for her students, especially those who are actively engaging in coursework and lectures. There are a lot of opportunities to express your opinion on difficult questions, and she gives great feedback.

Prof Clements is very good at getting into the material, and always made the class open to discussion. It was hybrid at the time, so there was a LOT of online content. There was no tests or quizzes, and your entire grade was based on lengthy, in depth reflection papers, group projects, and Packback.

she is very inconsiderate and is horrible when it comes to helping student needs. She has a very bad attitude and doesnt give you the opportunity to make up any work. I would not recommend her as a teacher if you take this class

EXTREMELY unorganized on D2L, collaborating with other students wont help as everyone is equally confused. Marks things down for no reason sometimes. Doesnt post lectures after class so you should show up in person when possible. Reflection papers are easy enough if you write down lecture notes, subject matter is pretty much common sense.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: This class being online was my biggest gripe about it, but that is no one's fault obviously (thanks covid!). The group projects for an all-online class were unfortunate, but she is usually pretty understanding of non-participating group members and her rubrics were pretty good! The textbook quizzes were also annoying but manageable.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: I honestly really liked Dr. Clements! Our class was hybrid which kind of was unfortunate in terms of having to do a bunch of stuff outside of class but it was totally manageable. Her rubrics are pretty good and the group projects were annoying but not horrible. I also really did enjoy our in-class lectures and discussions!

She's not a bad professor, but she can be a harsh grader. This class was hybrid, so you had mandatory lectures and then additional lectures to watch on your own time. Your grade depended on reflection papers, packback, and group projects, but there are some chances for extra credit. Beware of the vague grading rubrics for papers and projects.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She means well, but her grading isn't great. You will have a load of coursework between watching lectures, taking notes, packback, Revel assignments, group projects and exams, but it's all manageable. You can definitely pass this class with an A if you put in some effort, but beware of her grading because her rubrics can be very vague.

She is really a kind woman. I think she means well. But after a mix of very vague rubrics (and therefore horribly hard grading), actively comparing us (negatively) to other sections, and having 50% of our grade relying on group projects (of which she graded as low as she could), I will not be taking her again. At least the textbook was free
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Group projects--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Would not recommend this class to anyone. All homework is to just read the textbook and the material is not even on the exams. She also assigns very annoying group projects where everyone has an individual part but even if you do everything right, you get marked down for others lack of work. Very inconsiderate, does not adjust to anyone.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Group projects

reviews.comment: Not a single lecture the whole semester. She used the textbook to do all her teaching. The only thing we did was do the readings and then post a question for the class. There was reading quizzes, one project worth 35% of your grade and a cumulative final worth 25%. Grading on assignments needed to be more clear as well.

This professor didnt upload a single lecture the ENTIRE semester, nor did she host one class period. All of the content came from us reading the online textbook and that is it. Not even a study guide was offered for the one exam (the final). Thankfully the material of the class wasnt too challenging and I did okay. Unacceptable for MSU.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: She is a really good professor! The homework is actually helpful and never took me more than 20 or 30 minutes. She does expect participation from everyone, but makes it super easy to contribute. Sometimes the lectures can feel a little repetitive, but overall I loved her class!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Loved Cynthia! I'm taking her again next semester and looking forward to it!
she is amazing and personable. she is very understanding about everything and willing to be flexible if needed.
I LOVED this professor! She is amazing at what she does, super caring and understanding! Light course work, allows students to revise there mistakes, 10/10 would recommend!

She is my favorite professor I have ever had. Gives you a chance to correct mistakes that you may have made on your quizzes. She is there to help you at all times and is super understanding if you need an extension on a project. Absolutely amazing. Highly recommend taking a class with her if you can!!!!!

She was an amazing teacher. She is also very big on working with people in groups. So, if you do take her class work with people you enjoy talking to and participate. Otherwise, the class is going to go slow, and you will not want to go to class. Get the textbook, you don't really need it for homework, but you will need it for papers.

Cyndi was an amazing prof to have for TE 301 (it made me choose 406 for some reason). You have reading guides due every week but the reading is actually pretty interesting. She is also super helpful if you communicate with her and gives you a lot of class time to work on projects.

Sweetest prof. ever, poor teaching style. Made many mistakes, not tech-savvy, wasted time. Wanted us to succeed, helpful 1-on-1 but should be teaching a smaller class. Content shouldn't have seemed so dificult to understand, I didn't like the way she overcomplicated things/underexplained things. She deserves to teach a subject she's better at

Lectures and recitations were quite confusing. I thought that the lectures were a bit all over the place, and it was hard for me to follow what we were learning. She is really nice, but I do not think that she is the best teacher and I found the course difficult due to her teaching style.

Professor Fairbourn genuinely made me like a math class! She is so kind and answers any questions! One homework assignment per week, 2.5% added to your final grade if you do all the EC, my TA was amazing and helped me understand the content. The Stats Learning Center is such an amazing resource if you're struggling, I highly recommend utilizing it!

Very kind professor, uses great comparisons to get the lesson across. Attendance is a grade but you can always get at least half points and it is not weighed too heavily on your final grade. The final exam is cumulative, but the midterms are okay. Stats overall is hard so thats the main thing.

Her lectures are always confusing and her TA's are no help and make everything more difficult to understand, I would not take her class if your not the best at math because of the way she attempts to teach the material

I would try to take another professor. She makes the lectures very confusing, and most of the time, you are just doing examples and not actually learning the content, which made me have to learn it outside of class. Attendance is mandatory, but she offers make-up attendance questions at the beginning of the week. I wouldn't retake it.
Teacher didnt give me a 3.5 by 0.07% and would not round up. Other then that very boring lectures, and tests are heavily graded.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor is fine, class material is just hard. Starts off very easy and then quickly becomes impossible

Was definitely my toughest class of the semester, but I was still able to get the 4.0. Tophat and WebWork every single week, so make sure you do that. Taking notes is crucial and her lectures were good enough to where I could follow along, although I definitely got confused at some points. Use the very usuful practice tests and study for exams!

Prof Fairborn seems like a sweet woman but it almost felt like she knew too much for a base level stats class, it got confusing at times. She goes through slides fast and it was hard to keep up during lectures. If you feel confident about math you'll be fine. If you struggle with math, then you may want to make sure you can find outside help.

Fairbourn is by far the best stats prof. She is very caring and if you put in the work, you will get a good grade. Also, if you are having problems I would recommend going to the SLC. They walk through everything with you until you fully understand.

Super easy class, lectures are pretty easy to follow, only three exams; all very easy if you do the practice exams and study guides, don't necessarily need to go to class but you would need to do the top questions online if you want to get the points, definitely would recommend to anyone who has to take STT 200

Pretty easy 4.0 if you go to class, do the webwork, and do the study guides. Webwork and TopHat are required, along with a course pack. She was a great professor and I understood every lecture. Going to class is so important because she has TopHat questions for participation points! Overall would recommend
Great teacher for STT200, the lectures were valuable and the practice tests made the real test super easy.

Easily one of the best math professors I've ever had! She made statistics so easy to understand and was so nice. Grade was based on attendance (she usually gave us extra points or ways to gain back the points we missed) via Tophat, homework, recitation attendance, and 3 exams. All the exams were exactly like the study guides and practice exams!

This is the average STT class that colleges provide. It wasn't very hard, however, it could take time to get the concepts down. It wasn't extremely enjoyable but that isn't the fault of the professor for the most part. There is Tophat and Webwork due every week, so be prepared for that. Exams can vary in difficulty but they aren't very tough.

Fairly easy course and she tells you all the shortcuts possible. Course is not very difficult and she tries very hard to make your time enjoyable. She does well at not talking at you and talking to you instead. Very kind and compassionate lady.
I love camille. She is very sweet. Lectures can be confusing but if you go up to ask a question she affectively re-explains it.

Camille Fairborn is a good professor, but her lectures are hard to understand. She goes pretty fast through the slides and does not give a lot of time with required attendance top hats. Her tests are easy though as long as you do the practice exam and the study guide. Going to the lectures is very important if you want a good grade!! Shes good tho!

I liked Professor Fairbourn, but her lectures weren't great,so go to helprooms. Her tests are pretty easy as long as you understand how to do the practice exams she gives you, they are basically the same exact thing just different numbers. Attendance is graded on tophat and if you miss any lectures she has makeup questions you can do every Monday.

She does TopHat in class for attendance, however, she goes quite fast and gives us limited time to figure out the answer. She also goes a little fast in the lectures so you have to pay attention. But, Prof. Fairbourn has been great at answering questions and emailing back quickly as well as making the class fun on Thursdays with TopHat questions.

She is terrible, and when I say terrible, I mean it. By far the WORST math teacher I've ever had; not only does she have horrible lecture slides and fails to articulate what she means, she flat out completely fails her students as she cannot grasp the idea of how to teach math. I've had plenty of not-so-great math teachers, but she takes the cake.
Professor speaks and lectures but does not being concept to the main topic. Very hard to understand have to relearn everything in recitation.

Professor Fairbourn was awesome! She cares about her students and tries to remember names even though there's 300+ students in her class. Exams are easy if you regularly attend lecture and complete your WebWork. Have access to a TI-84 calculator because the majority of the course is just memorizing how to use the different functions on it.
Very organized slides. She knows her stuff as she is the course administrator for the class. You will need a TopHat account for this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Fairbourn is super sweet and does a lot of interactive parts during class. TopHat questions will count for attendance so you could get away with skipping if you're paying attention to the classroom, but the content moves fast. Each class goes through roughly 10 pages of the course pack. Stay till the end for Thursday fun questions.
The lectures actually make sense and the whole course is clear and organized. The tests are just like the study guides and the homework is relevant. Overall such a good class!

Professor Fairbourn is the sweetest professor ever. If you got to the lectures and put in the effort you will do fine. She always asks for feedback and adapts to the class to make sure everyone is on the same page. The schedule of due dates and everything is super clear. She is super caring and will help you with anything if you ask.
Lectures were amazing. If you actually listen in her lectures you will understand the whole course.

I took this class online so I never interacted with her. But, from her lectures, I could tell how passionate she was about stats. The large homework load in this class is painful and tedious. I now hate stats but that's not Camille's fault lol her lectures taught the material clearly and concisely.

Although I did four point the class, the class required LOTS of homework and studying if you weren't the best at math. Had her for an online stats class and the homework would sometimes have questions that she never went over so sometimes it was difficult. She seemed nice though.

Prof Fairbourns' lectures and notes were incredibly helpful in being successful in this class. Watching them is a MUST! Hybrid-flex class made balancing the workload in this class manageable. Content is difficult at times, but the notes prepare you well for homework/exams. I enjoyed how the class was setup. Attendance was mandatory for recitation.

I took the flex-hybrid course and it was really manageable and easy. There was a lot of practice work and extra credit that helped me prepare for exams. Also, the videos were easy to follow and no longer than usually 15 minutes. Professor Fairbourn is a great person and lecturer and she's great at explaining things.

Professor Fairbourn is amazing! We were given the option to either go in-person or do the class asynchronously. I did the class asynchronously and had no issues at all! Each lecture video never exceeded 15 minutes so they were super manageable. I recommend printing the lecture notes as it made note taking so much easier.

I love this woman. I am awful at math and barely passed all of my other required courses, but her lectures make it so easy to understand! She breaks down her lectures into manageable videos and puts helpful references and tools in between the videos! She also uses great examples :)
Professor Fairbourn is great, be sure to do the homework, don't be afraid to ask questions, you've got this!

This class was strictly online but was performed perfectly by Professor Fairbourn. The course was extremely well organized, easy to follow, and she broke down hard to understand concepts very well. Watching all of the lectures is key as well as doing the homework every week, if you do that, you're bound to get a 4.0!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I never talked to this professor as the class was online, but these are the most straightforward and easy to understand lectures Ive ever had. There are many many opportunities to do well in this class, and the lectures outline everything in perfect detail. If youre ever confused, the team of professors responds to emails quickly.

I had Professor Fairbourn during a COVID year so our stats class was completely online and I never had an actual one-on-one conversation with her, however, her lecture videos were really good and I think she is a good teacher, especially for a tougher class like statistics.
Professor Fairbourn was very thorough with her lectures and explained each concept in great detail. Pay attention to the lectures and the class will be a breeze.

Not a bad intro to stats class, took it online asynchronous and it was easy to do well in and to get help. Her lectures are nice, lots of examples and easy to understand even if you've never taken stats before. Very lenient with technical issues this semester too, gave extensions for that sort of thing. Overall good class and I usually hate math

I took STT200 all online asynchronous. I really liked the way prof Fairbourn set up the material on D2L. It was very clear to follow and hard to miss anything. I liked how she split up all of the lecture videos into short 4-10 minute videos that go by section. The detailed lectures follow the notes packet so you never get lost.

A solid instructor for an intro stats course. She explains things in a way that you can understand. Sometimes it is still hard to keep track of everything you're learning but she does her best to keep it manageable. One of the better classes I've taken for sure.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: best math teacher i have ever had. very easy going & she explains everything. i was scared to take a math class during a pandemic but her set up was by far the best of any of my profs. she made things super easy & made it asynchronous so i can do it on my time. wonderful teacher, really cares about her students. take her classes !!!!!
Really great lectures which made the course a lot more enjoyable.

She probably never learned that you learn the best from your mistakes. The dumbest part of this class was the practice exams. You could complete them up to 10 times but you never got to know what you got wrong. How am I supposed to learn from my mistakes if I don't know what mistakes I made? so I ended up doing bad on the exams. Also, no late work.

Professor fairborn is a great teacher! Her lectures are easy to understand, and the class itself is nothing too difficult. She gives great examples, and I was able to learn very well from her. I highly recommend her.

I took this class online and LOVED professor Fairbourn. She posted video modules for each week, breaking them into smaller submodules. She was very thorough with her teaching and used relatable topics to help you understand the material. Take advantage of the workbook because it is a fantastic reference as long as you followed along.

I never met her once because there were 1,800 other students taking this class this semester, but from her lectures she seems like a nice lady. She explains things easily, but I benefitted from putting the recorded videos on 1.5x speed.
LOVE prof cooperman! I had him my freshman year and he was amazing! He is super easy to work with and a great teacher!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Condescending but the class is not bad
A chill professor, let us always go early. The grading was very lenient. A few missed classes here and there didn't matter as long as you submitted the work. Highly recommend. Easy 4.0

His class is overall easy but attendance is required and there's a lot of out of class readings that your grade depends on. Overall, not hard but his lectures are long and somewhat boring and he enforces a strong no technology policy.

This guy never stops talking. If you try to use your phone during the 2-hour lecture, be ready to get yelled at for being disrespectful and not "plugging in". The class is easy, but you have to read quite a bit outside of class and watch videos for quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Class was easy, lectures were aggravating to sit through

Super fast-paced since it was a month-long course. Work was pretty easy! The only complaint that I have is that the quizzes were timed at 10 minutes for 10 questions, which was super stressful. Otherwise, super nice professor, and light work!

Caring and thoughtful professor. Came up with plenty of activities and opportunities for us to engage with the course that really got my mind going. My advice is to always do the homework as the daily iClickers are 50% of your grade. Not difficult at all if you participate. Funny guy too!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: He honestly is a good professor, he genuinely wants his students to understand the concepts and have their own peace of mind when it comes to discussion. He really wants to promote participation and build that confidence in us. Sometimes he talks too long, but he is a very caring person and is cooperative if you explain. Amazing professor.

A very energetic professor, he genuinely cared about the class and what he was teaching. Required a good amount of reading and watching videos outside of class, and attendance was required. Projects were fun, and he made good use of class time.

Prof. Myers loves to hear himself talk. There were so many times that someone asked a question and he went on a 15 min tangent on it. His grading was very easy but there was so many things to read. And  unfortunately you had to read them because the engagement check-ins are worth 40% your grade. He is very serious about phones and will kick you out
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: You really just have to show up to class and do the weekly assignments and you'll get a good grade. Very nice guy but does go on tangents but it is worth for an easy 3.0
Very nice professor! All you have to do is show up and do the assignments!
Gotta show up to class, and listen to yap for like two hours, but its free, and the assignments take like an hour to two hours a week tops
Just have to show up to class, free 4.0. Just have couple assignments a week that are free.

Professor Myers, although I feel he is a good person, always means well, and is incredibly amusing and unique to watch during class, is not a good professor. He often interrupts, rambles, goes off on tangents during group time. Some topics of IAH 207 are interesting, some are useless. Easy A if you participate but be prepared to be bored sometimes.

This professor used the word “Niggling” twice in the first class of our African history class and as an African american student this rubbed me the wrong way. Apparently it means bothersome but I thought he could've used any other word to express how he was feeling I found it very weird and offensive.

He is really Insufferable, he goes on long tangents about absolutely nothing, his projects are extremely vague and he doesn't provide proper guidance when you ask questions about them. His I clicker questions are extremely specific and focus on details that have nothing to do with the topic of the reading

Very easy class but man oh man prepare to be talked to about nothing for the full class time (and sometimes longer). Sometimes would discuss interesting things but felt like a middle school sensitivity class. Will interrupt you answering a question to go on a minimum five minute tangent. Take any other IAH unless you want an easy A.

Class is attendance heavy. The clicker questions are easy if you do the reading but they are a big portion of the grade. The class is super easy if you just show up and do the readings. I thought the books we read were alright and I liked watching The Bear. There is group work but it really isn't that bad. He was nice and this class wasn't bad.
This was by far my most pointless class at msu. He just goes on and on about nothing just to try and fill up the full class time. I suggest not taking this class and take a different IAH.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Lectures are very participation based.  He rambled a lot in my class, but I had a pretty quiet class, so that could've influenced that.  His teaching style is clear, digestible, and facilitating of learning.

Prof Myers was great! His lectures mainly consist of him talking, and sometimes he gets a little off topic, but i really enjoyed it. He's easy to talk to and doesn't give rubrics with grading- he wants you to be creative. He has a no phone policy just because some topics are sensitive to talk about!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: His lectures are quite long-winded and not the most put together. He can be quite amusing to listen to. The class was easy to pass as long as you read the material and participated a little bit. He values participation very highly.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures are composed of rambling, almost sound improvised. Hardly any real material was covered. There are a few interesting articles provided, but the prof's method of teaching is shallow and extremely dull. If you want to legitimately learn about Africa, I suggest taking another course. It wasn't too difficult too achieve a 4.0 though.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Lectures are really long and boring and attendance is required. You honestly don't have to listen to lecture or take notes. Assignments are just elaboration on basic topics. You don't have to buy the books, just read summaries online of each chapter. Just go to class, do the work, and put in just a little effort he's an easy A.

Felt like a 7th-grade course, no excuse for group projects in a college course, I am paying $30k a year to learn myself, not to carry Jenny and Carly in group work all semester. Did not learn anything in this class, and in fact actively felt like the was avoiding Africa. Would avoid it unless you like long-winded rambles and wasting time.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: This class was only one day in person, and we had a short assignment due once a week to make up for the second class day. All of the work in this class was very easy, and exams were as well. Prof was very friendly and helpful, easy 4.0

This class is very easy to follow. Don't skip because you need to do the group work each week and get the points. Homework is very easy while I learned about climate change and planets. He's a very nice Prof, and  interacts with the class! Would take again! Easy 4.0!

Absolutely loved Dr. Bob. He was very interested in what he was teaching which made the class more engaging. You have a group assignment at the end of every class that counts for your attendance. Couple of big milestones which are easy and all exams are online. Def take Dr. Bob for your ISP.

Very easy class, the assignments are not hard if you just put in a little effort. The d2l page can be a little confusing to navigate and there's not really any communication about what the asynchronous assignments are but just make sure you stay on top of stuff and look ahead and you'll be fine.
Easiest class I have ever taken! Like easy assignments and easy test. If you put in any effort you will get a 4.0.

this prof knows that the class is almost all freshman/sophomores fulfilling their isp. you don't have to do anything in class, which was hybrid for me. all u had to do was the online assignment (took 5 min) on monday and the exams were all online. he didn't answer emails but that's understandable for this class. good class to fulfill ur credit

He's a super easygoing teacher, and the class is easy too. In class, you just do a few questions with your table group, write them down, and turn them in for attendance points. There's one easy async assignment each week, one simple project, and online exams.

He deserves this high rating. Lectures are easy. Your attendance is based on a quick group work assignment, which normally takes about 10-15 minutes. You have a couple big assignments but nothing I lost sleep over, very easy.
The nicest professor at MSU. I've never seen someone ranked so high but he deserves it he's amazing.

Excellent professor. Very flexible if you communicate well, offers ample extra credit, and gives leeway for unforeseen circumstances. If you put in the bare minimum effort, you will 4 point this class. Highly recommend taking him, and will definitely be looking at more classes I can take with him in the future.

Excellent professor. Gives a lot of leeway if you communicate effectively, offers ample extra credit, and shows a lot of understanding for unforeseen circumstances. If you put in the bare minimum effort, you will 4 point this class. Highly recommend taking him, and I definitely will be looking into classes to take with him in the future.

This class was mostly hybrid, but it was super easy to get a 4.0. This class was absolutely the easiest class at MSU. Dr. Bob was great, not only was the material pretty interesting, your graded basically on completion. The in class assignments are super fun and the exams are open note and super easy. Overall, this class is a very easy gen-ed!
love love love dr bob, so sweet and the workload is so light and exams are easy. take him easy A
Dr. Bob is an awesome professor. He's a super nice guy and I would recommend taking this class if you are required too.

If you want to finish the uni requirements without stress TAKE THIS CLASS. Professor's awesome, not only does he teach well, his homework assignments aren't overwhelming. We did small in group assignments in class. There were also essay-like writing assignments that had clear instructions. Easy A trusttttt

Great class, every assignment is easy and i'm so happy I took this class. The only con was the fact that he doesn't respond to emails and some of the assignments on d2l aren't accurate as he does not do some of them anymore... BUT still a great and easy class.

Probably the best class ive taken so far at msu. He clearly cares about teaching and makes it very easy to pass. Class is once a week and barley mandatory, but there's short in class group participation assignments so you will miss out on those points if you skip. Lots of extra credit opportunities to make them up. Easiest final ever

Literally the best professor I have ever had in my entire life, he is so chill and nice and responds to emails super fast. Cares for his students and has the easiest class I have ever taken. Easier than a middle school class, little work load and all exams online. The best class ever, highly reccomend.

I also took ISB 200 with him; the main assignments were the same. TA put additional assignments up but most didn't end up being required. He's a very easy grader, plus exams are online and very easy. He's a great prof and ideal if you need basic ISB/ISP credits but will still learn. Lectures are technically required but not super necessary.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Easiest class i have ever taken. You do need to go to class because of participation, but the class is usually under 30 minutes and tests are ridiculously easy. He knows this is a Gen Ed so it is a very easy class. One thing is that you need to check your email on D2L, as that's how he communicates.

Don't expect assignment due dates to be accurate in D2L, we didn't know when we were in person/online or what was due when. Not to mention his and his TA's inability to communicate. Assignments are easy, exams are beyond easy. Just don't count on ever knowing what is due that week until he finally decides to communicate. Easy but frustrating A.
Dr. Bob was epic, and a very easy grader. He knows this is a Gen Ed, and wants his students to succeed so he actually helps you out. Would take again.

Emailed Dr. Drost in a panic because I unwillingly had to drop one of my other classes, making me a part-time student and at risk for losing thousands of dollars in scholarships. Dr. Drost let me into the class a month after it started and made sure to help me catch up. That alone makes him the most caring professor I've ever had. Easy class too.

Had him for both ISB200 and ISP203B, easiest classes I've taken at MSU ever. Do keep in mind that TAs are the ones grading the work + attendance matters (missing few attendances can drop your grades easily). Besides that, the classes end early and half the time it's zoom classes. Would highly recommend.

Dr.Bob is very chill, makes an easy class. Have to show up for lectures for group work and attendance/participation credit, but if you do, you will pass no problem. The quizzes are online and easy if you have the slides and pay attention. Assignments are cumulative over the semester to one big final project but are easy and graded very nicely.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Such a nice guy and understands that everyone is taking this class because it's a requirement, not because they want to.
