Each class has one group assignment that is used for points and attendance so go to class but it's supposed to be over an hour and I was never there for longer than 25 minutes. Exams are all online and no lockdown browser...

Prof. Drost made this prereq class very easy to pass, and kept the homework (if any) very minimal and to the point. Attendance wasn't required, but points were taken if you attended class. No textbook either. Hands down best professor I've had this year!

Dr. Bob was literally the easiest professor and class ever. There is usually some sort of group assignment that needs to be completed for a grade every class so attendance is really important. It claims to be an hour and 20 minute class, but on average we were never in there longer than 40 minutes.

Dr. Bob was one of the best professors I have ever taken at MSU. He makes a prereq that isn't meant to be difficult but incredibly easy but still fun and informational. If you ever get the chance to take him for your ISP or ISB I would highly recommend.

If you need a super easy ISB class, this is the one! Dr. Bob is a super great professor and doesnt make the material difficult at all! Very easy to understand and a 4.0 is VERY attainable! Majority of grading is the individual milestone projects which take up to 2 hours to complete at most. Loved this class!

If you are looking for an ISB credit, take this class. He is an amazing professor, he makes the class so easy for students. His exams are so easy you are basically guaranteed 100%, most assignments are super short and so easy you get 100%, you also often don't meet on Mondays despite it being a class time + multiple extra credit opportunities.

Dr. Bob is amazing, his lectures are fun and class activities are not unusual when he holds in-person classes. Lots of extra credit opportunities and the TAs were super chill. Super recommended if you need to take an ISP for class/major credit.
If you go to class, you will get an A. Vey nice guy, he makes things easy to understand and assessments are a cake-walk.

Quite literally the easiest class you will ever take. Prof Drost was the most laid back guy ever, the exams are all common knowledge (and online), ULAs are very helpful, I highly recommend this course.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Love Dr.Bob! Such a chill guy, he understands the people in class are not science majors so he is much more relaxed than other "university required" profs. We never went over 20 minutes, whether that be on the zooms or in person. And the exams are super easy as well.

easy 4.0 definitely take him for any class that you possibly can. The Zoom class was often canceled and in person never lasted past half an hour. gradin was clear and assignments were easy. exams were all found online

Easy. Class is scheduled for an 1hr 20m but only goes for about 20 minutes each day. Zoom on Mondays so makes the week easy. He understands that the people taking this course are not science majors so the material is nothing crazy and his assignments are very easy, only take about 10 minutes, projects took me 30.

Dr. Bob is amazing! If you haven't taken an ISB/P course yet, take it with Dr. Bob. He is so chill and assignments can be finished in 10 minutes. Exams are exactly like the study guide. Super straight forward. In class we go over slides for about 20 minutes then answer a few questions with the people at your table.

Professor Drost is super laid back and understands that a lot of people are required to take the course. It is important to go to class because group work is a large percent of the grade, but the tests are super easy and he makes the material interesting. If you have to take ISB or ISP, definitely take it with him.
This class was really easy. Make sure to attend class because there is group work they grade on, but otherwise it is really easy and maybe a guaranteed 4.0.
Super easy class and super chill guy. Not a lot of homework and quizzes are easy.
Super nice. Understands that this is a required class and to take it easy. Basically gives you everything you need to pass. Best professor ever

one of the best professors I've had at msu, cares and wants students to do well. definitely the easiest class i have ever taken, only a few one page papers with clear q/a prompts. 25 Q exams and study guides are the exact same as the exams. good idea to go to class as there is in class discussion group work due most of the time.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Super easy class, just make sure to show up as class group work is a large part of your grade.  Tests and homework assignments are very easy.  Professor Drost is great to have for ISP or ISB courses.

SUPER easy professor. Study guides exactly like the exams, one project that you work on throughout the semester but super easy. Includes 3 milestones and then the final proposal. Group work but super easy. He often cancelled in person class and switched to Zoom. Super nice, funny guy. Exams are online.

He always makes lectures entertaining and really cares about teaching the subject material. The course itself is incredibly easy, as long as you go to class each week and submit the group work then the class is an easy 4.0. The projects and milestones are cake walks and the exams takes questions directly from the study guide.

If you are looking to be challenged by course work Prof is not for you. If you are looking for an easy 4.0 and Credit Prof is for you. Don't feel like I learned a whole lot, but Class was about natural hazards and not applicable to my degree, so didn't feel like I was missing out. Prof has realistic expectations of students interest in the subject.

If you do the work and show up to class you will get a 4.0. Our class was graded highly on very easy papers. Many classes were moved to Zoom. Always got out of class early. Dr. Bob does everything he can to make the class easy and stress-free for students. You can also learn a few interesting things!
He is not a tough professor show up do the work and you will be fine.
R Drost is the best professor for ISP. Our class used to end way before the class ends. Small lectures with great knowledge

Super easy professor/class. In person you work in groups to answer a few questions that is graded on participation. Then you do a few easy assignments on your own which built up to the final project (also easy). He gives study guides for exams that are the exact exams and have answers on quizlet. He canceled the zooms and often shortened classes.

Super easy class. He understands a lot of us are taking the class for the credit not for our majors. Groupwork in class so go, but he usually lets you out after 15-30 minutes of class. Great professor, highly recommend this class.

He is very funny. It is an easy class. He posts the answers to the quizzes right on the powerpoints. easy 4.0 class. Lectures are not long at all, get gets the point across and would end the class which was nice.
Took Fall of 2020, assignments were clear and easy to follow. Easy class to get an A in

Drost is a nice and respectable professor. I took him Spring 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic and he made sure his students were not overwhelmed during this time, which I especially appreciated. The class itself was not difficult at all and a great choice for the ISP requirement.
The class is an easy A, just show up and do the group work. Exams are online and are incredibly easy. I feel like it would be harder to fail this class than to ace it.
This is the easiest class ever. All you have to do is show up to class and do the group work. The exams are online and you can find all the answers on quizlet. Effortless 4.0.

This class is an easy 4.0. You have to show up to do in class assignments but they are easy and lectures are chill. All exams are online and he gives you a study guide with the exact same questions as it a week before. There are a few projects, but if you follow the rubric you should get 100%. Very easy class with minimal effort
Dr. Bob is amazing, his classes are the best at MSU. I very much enjoyed having him as a professor. I applied to be an LA for the course I took and got the job. Cant wait to keep working with him!
Amazing professor and I'm taking him again as he's so great.
Easy class. Just show up for participation points. Exams online.
He is a great teacher. Very funny and friendly. Easy class.
Best Professor at MSU. Take his classes for a guaranteed 4.0. Totally loved him. Much Respect.

Easiest thing I've ever done. Class is almost painfully easy and uninvolved. Exams are all online and google-able. Class attendance is required most of the time. If you showed up to class with a beer in your hand I'm not sure that he'd even tell you to get rid of it.

The class was super easy. I found the lectures to be kinda boring since he read off the slides and posted them on D2L. You do need to show up for random attendance questions that are a part of the grade. Drost is pretty easygoing and only asks for participation.
everyone please take this class, it will boost your grade point by doing little to no work with a nice teacher!

Guy walks in the first day wearing a Hawaiian shirt and reveals his love for Jimmy Buffet and Tequila. Tests were all online and word for word from the review. Did give some online HW, but it was super easy too. One of the easiest classes I have ever taken.

I took him twice (ISB and ISP) and the class was exactly the same!. Turned in the same assignments and the exams were EXACTLY the same. Barely went to class at all and got a 4.0 (twice). He's so cute (for a geeky professor) and so passionate about what he's talking about!

Great teacher! I have had him this passed semester for his 'flipped' section where the class is split into like 50 groups of 4 people and we just do our work during class! No homework, quizzes are all online due before class and exams are all online EASY 100% on all of them! 3 milestones, final project, and a final group debate, super great class

He is a very easy professor! He gives you the exam before you take it so all you have to do is find the answers in his lectures and memorize the answers. He didn't give a final, we had a final project instead that we worked on throughout the semester. He doesn't require any textbook(s). Easy A! Must attend class because attendance is mandatory.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests? Not many--Would take again

reviews.comment: I love Bob (Yes, he told us its O.K. to call him by his first name). He always finished class at least 30 minutes early and would let us leave whenever his lecture was done. He makes a 4.0 very attainable, assuming that you turn everything in on time and actually try. The work is often tedious (No offense, Bob), but not difficult by any means.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Best class ever and best teacher ever. 10/10

This man is amazing! He is very passionate about what he teaches and truly cares about his students. He usually lets class out early, gives some extra credit opportunity, and grades very easily. Easy 4.0, 10/10 recommend.
He is wonderful and gives you the tests ahead of time so you know what is on them.
He is the man.
Great teacher. Always entertaining and very caring about his students. Learned a lot in his class.
Okay best teacher I have ever had

THE BEST TEACHER YOU WILL EVER HAVE AT MSU. Dont feel like going to class? No problem, every slide is uploaded online. About a week before you take the exams he uploads the exact exam word for word with all multiple choices included so you can study. Has about 4 "milestone" term projects to do but are not that hard and graded very easily. EASY 4.0!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Hilarious--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: It's a class on natural disasters and hazards, he is super passionate about it and it's pretty interesting. He uses video clips, and things to keep it interesting.

It took almost a month for her to get stuff graded, which made me drop the class, since I wasn't risking bad grades. The course layout was confusing and disorganized because everything was just dumped into three tabs. The schedule was also dumped into one spot. It is so much nicer when only one module is available at a time!

She is the harshest grader I have experienced, I would never take this class again or have her as a teacher. Class is way too long and you have to have your camera on if you want to get participation, hate this class and would not suggest.

Please do not take WRA101 with this professor. Save your GPA and take a different class. Not only will you be miserable the whole class, but she is also AWFUL at technology and does not know how to provide basic information like a rubric. Save yourself while you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Do not take
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She loves what she is teaching, but the grading is so harsh for an online class. You have to participate in each class and have your camera on each time. The grading is the harshest I've experienced in my entire college career.

Out of all of the classes I've taken at Michigan State. This was by far the worst run class. The professor was often expecting an understanding without an explanation. The grading was extremely strict and did not even make common sense. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Highly not recommended to take this class with her, she is very unorganized and unhelpful, she doesn't really understand the technology she is using and almost impossible to determine your grade, she is an incredibly tough grader, and don't have anything rcpd related doesn't matter to her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Troutman was very unclear on her directions and what she expected of the students for assignments. The topic she taught had nothing to do with an entry level WRA101 class. She gave us the syllabus 2 days before the last day of class, so we had no idea of the grade percentages/other details of the class. This class will destroy your GPA.

This class was the worst English class I have ever taken and it didn't even focus on English, it was all African American History. We barely learned concepts of writing, grammar, or how to properly write essays like I expected I would in a Writing class.

So far worst professor I have had at MSU. Very harsh grader and be ready for pop quizzes. She took months to grade and I ended with a very low grade. I do not recommend at all and do not take her classes if you don't have to. She tought good info and was very organized but I would never take again nor would I recommend her to anybody.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I knew I wasn't going to 4.0 this class from the start it was just one of those feelings. I actually enjoyed and appreciated some of the content but am not satisfied with my grade at all
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very harsh grading while also being extremely unclear on why you receive a bad grade.

I would not recommend this class. It was extremely disorganized, and the grading system lacked clarity. I didn't feel like I learned anything, making it a significant waste of time. The instructor consistently fell behind on grading, which made it difficult to track my progress or know how well I was doing in the course.
She is a very kind person who is very passionate but she is so horrible at teaching, she will never grade any of your stuff and doesnt even know how to explain her assignments. do NOT take this class
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Dr. Troutman's lectures were very confusing and not interesting. She is very passionate about the subject which I really appreciated. She is a very harsh grader and will surprise you with online pop quizzes. She can be quite harsh at times. Participation is very very important. I would not take her class again it was very difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Do not take any class with her ever. The grading is so unfair, and when you ask her about changing your grade, she is VERY unhelpful. Dont take any class with her please save yourself.

Seems like a kind lady who is passionate about her work but she is not a very good teacher, nor should she be teaching an online course given her inability to clearly explain anything. She is a VERY slow grader who will wait to grade most of your assignments until the end of the semester so there's nothing you can do. (this was a WGS class)

There were a lot of group projects and pointless assignments. The final exam was a group presentation. All my group projects I did all the work. She acted like she cared but was not interested in helping. This course was also not what was advertised. Don't take this course if you don't have to, find something more enjoyable.

This class is near impossible to succeed in and I would recommend dropping it immediately. She didn't teach a single thing about writing and focused on Ebonics instead. She didn't put any grades in until the last week and you need to get 96/100 points for the entire year to get a 4.0. I got a 94 but got a 3.5 in an entry level writing class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: she is bad
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She seems like a nice person with a good message, but the class is poorly organized and she just kinda makes stuff up as it goes. Essay topics were very unusual and not appropriate for a generic wra 101 course.
She sucks
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Really easy class but Troutman is awful and most of the other reviews i would say are correct. She cannot explain any of her assignments, she doesn't always respond to emails. She seems like a nice person but she cannot teach to save her life. For this class she will make you buy expensive books so you can read like 50 pages of any of the books.

Worst professor that I have had at Michigan State University. She doesn't describe any assignments but then grades them extremely hard. Told us in the beginning of the course that its hard to do well in her class which makes no sense cause she is supposed to be here to help us. Reverse Racism in this class 100% and she doesn't help at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Even though this was a writing class we only wrote one essay and it was graded very harshly. Participation in this class matters a lot. Would NOT recommend for future incoming freshmen. This was supposed to be a writing class but with only one 25 minute lecture over the course of the semester about writing it turned into a racial history course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I recommend never taking this class unless you want to shoot your GPA in the foot. Worst class I took and by far my lowest grade. She is a nice person, but not a good professor. She means well, but is just all over the place.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Never take this class with her. Grading doesn't make sense and she makes it so hard for anyone to succeed. She will ruin your GPA, I'm am 3.5-4.0 student and I received a 2.7 even though I only received ONE grade the whole semester that low and it was project that was weighted the least. Also, the class is about racism, not writing, we did 1 essay.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Not a writing based class- heavily focused on racism and the history of it. Only one paper to write but tough grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Troutman is extremely bias. This class was based on race, and she made sure that everything completed in the class was based off of her opinions. She is also a tough grader. As previous comments stated, shift your previous opinions for the time youre in this class to hers and you'll pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Troutman is bias. This class was used to talk about the specific problem of racism in America, which is not a problem, but how she talked about it was. You become scared to speak your opinion. If it's not along the line that whites are racist, then you are wrong and she will tell you that. Just copy what she says and you'll get through

She was very unhelpful. Her syllabus tells you one thing, but then she'll tell you something completely different. Her grading was very difficult and there was absolutely no way to please her. If you did not share the same ideas as her or talk a bunch, you might as well just accept the 2.0 in her class because there's no chance of anything better.

A perfect example of why the tenure system should be discontinued. She does not do her job well, and makes herself unclear with her projects. She is the worst professor you will ever have, so make sure to get out of her class while you still can.

What an awful teacher. Do not take her class. In order to get a 4.0, you have to get a 96% and she will do anything she can to find a reason to give you a poor grade. On top of this, she is also a narcissist. One of our assignments was to interview her! Please do not take her class. You will regret it if you do.

Worst teacher. She makes no effort to help students do well or improve whatsoever and does not respond to any emails. I had to nag her daily to eve talk to me and work my butt off just to get a 3.0 in the class. The tests are ridiculously hard essay questions and she grades so picky, yet can't teach it clearly during the lesson. Avoid her.
I actually liked her a lot.  She's really nice and is willing to spend time with students individually.  She really helped me do well in this class.
Absolutely terrible. Avoid any class taught by her.
HARDEST CLASS of my life thus far

She was nice and helpful when needed, but overall a poor teacher and grader. Instructions for lab write-ups were inconsistent and scattered, making them hard to follow. She graded everything at the end, so I didn't know I was doing things wrong and got low scores. Group work grading was unfair since everyone shared the same grade.

She was great. She was there to help whenever and was easy to acess during office hours and email! The ULA's do a lot but she is always there making sure they are correct and help out whenever needed. My only complaint is the grades came in very late and sudden at the end of the semester.
Dr. Davis was very kind and she is good at explain where mistakes were made and how to fix them.

Your experience will vary greatly depending on your ULA's and lab partners, but Dr. Davis herself was accessible inside and outside of class which was helpful. Assignments are fairly easy as long as you follow the rubric exactly and communicate with ULA about the exact expectations and what they specifically want to see.

Professor Davis is one of the best lab professors I have had at MSU. She's okay at explaining things and may take a moment to articulate her thoughts but she makes sure you understand what's happening and is willing to help at any point during the lab. Overall, I would highly recommend her to anyone taking this class!

Course is a lab so it will be ran by ULAs. Be hopeful that you have amazing ULAs that you can ask for help with. Dr. Davis would have multiple sections at once & rarely appeared in my section. When she did appear, she asked questions but would make it more confusing. She is such a nice and kind professor although her teaching is another story.

Dr. Davis was a good lab instructor! From what I've heard, most labs are ran by LAs. Although Dr. Davis is present, most instruction will come from the LAs. She always has very accessible office hours and is there to answer questions.

Dr. Davis was not present sometimes but when she was she repeated the same things the LAs went over with us for so long and would cold call students. She wastes time and slows you down a little. I found the lab to overall be fine but due to the LAs for the most part. Wouldn't suggest her unless you have to.

Just like in my previous semester, Dr. Davis was rarely active in the lab. When she was there, my interactions with her were neutral. The difficulty of the lab is based solely on your group and ULA's.

LB155 lab and she was awful! My ULA's were great and led the lab. she was rarely in lab, when she was there she created mass confusion and chaos. The ULA's would tell us how to do something then we would do it, then Davis would come around and tell us it was wrong, but it would be right. Was told by ULA "she doesn't know what shes doing"

It is a lab so it is run by ULA's. As long as you have good ULA's you should be fine. Dr. Davis is barely present as she is in between multiple labs simultaneously. She will pop in randomly and ask tricky questions and get irritated if you don't know the answer. Most of the time Dr. Davis and the ULA's aren't on the same page so its confusing.

It is a lab, so it was mainly up to the ULAs. Mine were fine, but I know plenty people with differing experiences. Dr. Davis likes to sneak up on you while you work, but once you get past that, she's fine. She's helpful where it counts, in my opinion. That said, I probably wouldn't choose her as a lab prof again.

Dr Davis was rarely in the classroom. The ULA's and her did not communicate well between material and what is happening in the class. The lab did not contain a lot of actual experiments. Sometimes when asked what to do Dr Davis and the ULA's gave different answers to what we had to do.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The class was pretty awful. My ULA Mithil Penta was just straight up mean, and did not help whatsoever. The ULA's grade and run everything, and they are harsh with it. There is VERY little support from the teaching team let along Dr. Davis herself. It was pretty confusing and overall lots could be done to make the course better.

Beware to never know what you are doing in lab at all times. Me and my group met for our final project that was never explained to us and was due tomorrow. Terrible course and a waste of time and money. If you are being placed into College Algebra or a MTH 103, please just go into trig with chem 1 and lab so can skip whatever this is.

Absolutely terrible prof, taught two sections at once but never came in our secfion. TAs ran the course but told us the wrong information or Dr. Davis would come in and tell us something completely different from what the TAs

This professor is by far the worst professor I think I will ever have, and I am only a first year student. She does not give clear instructions at all, gives students assignments with no clear due date. Do not take this prof. even if you have to, if the class is required you are better off waiting for her to retire. Also she burped in my face.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I rarely see Dr. Davis, but my experiences with her have been average. The problem with this lab is that it all depends on the ULAs you get. Also, the lab feels like you are being dropped into a situation without much help or preparation.

She's really not bad. Doesn't do much, but I think that's true of all chem lab profs. It's really all down to your ULAs. Davis wasn't the most helpful, but she wasn't actively bad, and she was pretty nice.

Chem lab is a disorganized mess no matter who your professor is - I think Saraha gets much better reviews but my friends with Saraha had the same confusing assignments. What really matters is who your ULAs are. Class itself isn't hard, just disorganized.

Professor isn't involved in a lot of the labs, the ULA's mostly run them. The ULA's and professor can sometimes give confusing answers/feedback. Prepare to spend a lot of time outside of class with your lab group to work on assignments. Assignments and content aren't too hard though.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Dr. Davis didn't involve herself often. LAs didn't seem to understand Davis's expectations but tried their best regardless. Assignments were sometimes confusing and very hard to find in D2L. Course is organized poorly. Davis answers questions with more questions and is not helpful. Lots of group work, so your group is large factor in your grade.

Barely saw her the whole semester. I got lucky with good LAs and a great group but it's clear that this class would've been much harder without those. Grades and points for the semester are clearly spelled out in the syllabus which I did like. I don't recommend her even though it wasn't bad for me as it's clearly a gamble on other factors.
Get ready to teach yourself literally every thing you need to know to pass.

In this lab, she was not present much, as the ULAs run the labs with her assisting as needed. The lab is just a hard class overall, so she did not necessarily make it more difficult. She always had good input during labs and made sure we were on the right track even though she was not there very often because she was going between the two labs.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Here's the deal, when you take chemistry lab through Lyman Briggs, you will not interact heavily with the professor. Your learning assistants are the people grading your assignments, so you really just want to make a good impression on them. Dr Davis is very nice, but not heavily reachable. Do your work in this class and you will 4.0

Do not take any classes! She is extremely disorganized, doesn't respond to emails, and she will only make you more confused. My entire experience in this class has been terrible the only good thing where the LA's since they were the only helpful part of this class. If you take lab with Davis get prepared  to be stressed out and permanently clueless
She made my day:)

I do not recommend taking this course with her. Everything and I mean EVERYTHING was disorganized. From homework not being posted in time, to typos on tests, to errors on the lectures, you name it. If you email her, you probably will not get a response, just keep that in mind. Several grading mistakes. Hardest class I have taken at LBC.
Dr. Davis is almost always late for class, she hardly gives any instruction for anything, and she is very disorganized.

She was one of the worst professors I've ever had. She didn't update any of the worksheets and so the flash didn't work; the pacing was too fast; she basically didn't teach at all; you are your own teacher, which should never happen in a lab; and to put the nail in the coffin your TAs are your only hope, so pray that you get a good one.

This online lab was awful. You do more work trying to figure out what lab assignments are asking (because of poor wording and various mistakes) than completing the lab itself. Dr. Davis herself is uninterested in the welfare of her students. She provided no instruction the entire semester. Take the normal chemistry lab.

She does care about the students and their grades and stuff, you just have to reach out. A very sweet professor and I think she made the best of the fully online lab. Some modules were hard but if you asked, the prof and the LA's are willing to help however they can.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: You teach yourself everything in this class. EVERYTHING. You want to do well? Get used to putting in 7-10 hours in a one credit class on a weekly basis. And depending on how poorly she paces the course each week, you may find yourself with an upwards of 12+ hours of busywork to do. So yeah, you're in for a treat if you end up with her.

Dr. Davis has good intentions, but failed to deliver on them in any of the time I spent in her class. She is very vague with her instruction and expectations. She has not taught a single thing herself and the entire class is led and taught by the LAs. Do not take LB171 L with her unless you are ready to teach yourself entirely.

Kind person, but she handled this course terribly. The workload is ridiculous, and the instructions on how to complete assignments are vague. To make things worse, none of the material covered in this class will benefit you in LB171 / Chemistry. Expect a lot of situations that make you want to rip your hair out. Pray that you don't get her.

Dr. Davis is a pretty good lab prof. I will say that 171L can be a little overwhelming at first and the profs throw you into it. However, Dr. Davis does care about her students. Be ready to work hard in this class, it is not the easiest. Really make sure you know what you are doing with labs, this gets a better chance for a 4.0.

Dr. Davis herself was a very nice lady, but the class was not up to par. The directions that were given weren't very clear and I had to rely on LAs for help most of time, but even then they too would be confused on what was going on.

Her lectures can be confusing/hard to follow as her english is not amazing. However, she is very kind and wants everyone to do well. Is very helpful in office hours and her exams are fair (and comparatively easy to other briggs exams). If you study, do the homework, and occasionally read the textbook you should be able to handle the class fine.

I do not know how to grade Dr. Davis because she had never stepped foot in the classroom the entire semester. I've met her and she is very kind. The class itself is more work than all of my classes combined. It is very unorganized and the teachers and LA's are very vague on what they expect. A pro is that there is an enormous curve at the end.

This lab was taught by TA's who didn't know what was going on (and the class being at 8am didn't help at all) The few times Dr. Davis did walk in, everyone would go rigid and pretend to do something because she's so intimidating. But if you get to know her, she's super sweet, just really intimidating and never there to help.

The class is taught totally by LAs and the labs are like group projects. I hated lab more than anything and I had a 4.0 in AP Chemistry in high school. The class is a bit of a necessary evil, and Professor Davis responds to emails really quickly if you have questions, but the class is trouble.

Class is taught by LAs. This makes it confusing, because others taking your class, but not in your section will confuse you as to what you need for each class period, lab report, etc. Generally, it was very difficult to figure out what we needed to bring to lab, though the LA and TA worked very hard to try and help us.

Class is taught by LA's instead of professor, which complicates the entire process. Overall, lab reports are daunting and brutal, so is the class. In hindsight, I should have avoided this class, for many reasons.

I urge you not to take this class if you have had difficulty with chemistry in H.S., the understanding partI got an 4.0 in chem. in H.S., and I loved science (I have 2.0 in her class now, maybe I can getr a 2.5..ruined my GPA). I just urge you LBS students to just take the university chemistry class and lab..the labs in 171L are 8-13pages.BE WARNED

This course was frustrating overall. The professor seemed unfamiliar w/the material, which made lectures unclear & sometimes inconsistent. Office hours were conflicted w/lab making it difficult to get the help needed.
Chemistry is challenging, & this course lacked the clarity & support needed to succeed. Be prepared to rely on outside resources.
One of the best courses I took. Great lectures!
Lectures are intense, but the professor is fair and supportive. I learned much.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: hej folks professor demir is a great teacher and takes care of her students. the stuff from slides and additional things i got from her made the class really interesting and exciting. go for it

Dr. Demir is the absolute worst professor I've ever had at MSU. It's a crime that she's even allowed to continue to work at the university. She doesn't care at all about the students and you can tell she doesn't even want to be there. She's dismissive and rude. I would recommend to not take the course but unfortunately it's required… good luck

Pretty sure these 5-star reviews are fake and written by Demir. One of the most disappointing classes I've taken at MSU. She is hands down the worst teacher I've ever had. She mumbles through lectures, skips class WITH ZERO WARNING, and gives absolutely no clear grading guideline. Tested on memorizing useless details. TA is literally fantastic.

I'm not going to say much other than good luck to the chem majors (b/c there is no other option). I will say she is super smart and knows what she's talking about but a horrible lecturer and doesn't put in the effort(No answer key, no recorded lecture, have to go to office hours to see exam, saying everything will be on final but only 8 questions😭
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is the first time I am taking an inorganic class. It has been difficult for me, but Prof. Demir has been very helpful. She is a great teacher. Take her class!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This is the first time that I get to study inorganic chemistry and I am really loving it. I also did well in the first exam, and I am sooo proud of it! The exam problems reflect the material taught in lecture! I went to the professors office hours which helped me a lot to get ready for the exam.

i just past my first written test and i am quite happy about the result. professor demir is a great teacher and cares a lot about students. she explains quite explicitly all stuff in an inspiring way. the course did raise definitely my interest in inorganic chemistry. i recommend it!

Professor Demir is a great teacher. I am really enjoying her skillful way how she introduces topics and explains even quite difficult details to students. She cares about the students and gives great advice how to recap the matter. I truly recommend the lecture!!
Fantastic professor! I am in Grad School now and super happy that I got to learn from her. True role model!!! Take her class!!
Brilliant professor! Highly recommend her class.
A great professor! I enjoyed the class and learned a whole bunch. She inspired me to pursue a career in inorganic chemistry!

Lots of the 5 star reviews here seem fake and are probably written by Prof. Demir herself. She is frequently rude, mumbles through her lectures, and frequently does not show up to lectures. You will need to memorize every small detail to do good on tests. The grading scale isn't clear. TA is great though.

Professor Demir is very engaged teacher. She really raised my interest for molecular inorganic chemistry and cares about progress of her students. I had some difficulties to digest the many aspects but overall enjoyed the course and learned a lot. Thx!
Just a great teacher with interesting topics in advanced inorganic chemistry. She cares and was available if questions arose. I truly recommend to take her class
Very insightful didn't thought = advanced inorganic is so inspiring and encouraging for learning more about main group element stuff. I keep reading on this things thanks to her great class
My favorite course this semester. Professor Demir is a warm and inspiring teacher. No doubt I can recommend taking this class.

She is amazing! Her expectations are high but the content is pretty attractive too. It meet my expectations for advanced inorganic stuff. She is easy to reach and gives fast feedback. Open hours help too. Brought me to really consider doing research in inorganic chemistry in future.

Professor Demir is a great teacher. I really enjoyed the lectures and learned a lot from interconnecting topics in advanced inorganics. Homework is needed to digest the content fully. No problem because she cares about students and gives quickly feedback.
First time for me to really enjoyed listening nonorganic stuff. Learned a lot and love to keep going with the topic of organometallic chem. Great and easy feedback from Professor Demir if needed.
Hey if you are eager to take a fresh class on fundamentals in inorganic and organometallic chemistry. Thats the on. Highly recommended

She is a great teacher of fundamental inorganic chemistry. Love main group chemistry ::-) Professor Demir cares about the success of her students and she responds quickly to inquiries. Never had a better class.

She is a very engaged teacher on fundamental stuff and gave me a hard time to digest the content. My background knowledge was not good enough. But after lots of homework and conversations with Professor Demir I made it! Glad that I took the class and passed it.

She is a great and friendly teacher but expects a lot. Lots of very interesting stuff. Passing examination with a good means requires quite a bit homework to digest the content. Luckily she is also available outside of class and responds quickly.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Demir is a great teacher. and very friendly person. I have learned a lot in her course about fundamentals in molecular orbital theory and basic inorganic chemistry. The way she introduced electronic structure-reactivity relationships was an eye opener to me. Just super inspiring. She is easily available for students if questions arise.

Professor Demir was amazing! She made everything easy to understand and cares about her students. She provided direct feedback and helped in many ways when it came to demanding teaching topics. Its more of the some specific content that needs extra work to learn all this and not the professor. She is easily one of the best teachers I've ever had.
She is a great teacher! You wouldn't want to miss out on this. Her class connects stuff in fundamental chemistry in a very inspiring way.
Great professor and amazing lectures! She explains things very well and is accessible outside of class. She also provides valuable feedback.
Professor Demir is very good at teaching. She is extremely smart and approachable. She is also always willing to make an appointment to talk about the lectured material. Would take again.
Great Professor! I enjoyed her lectures and learned a lot.
She's great
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: one of the most frustrating and disappointing classes i've taken at MSU. professor demir cannot communicate with students at all. class material is from a german textbook. 1/3 of the course was completely pointless material. the grades are so heavily curved they might be fake. expect to fail her exams. interesting material, horrible professor.

The professor uses notes from Matthew D Law from UCL WORD FOR WORD. She does not elaborate beyond these PowerPoints. The exams expect you to MEMORIZE every small thing that was mentioned in lecture. This is not a learning based course. SHE DOES NOT USE A GRADING SCALE. The average determines a 3.0. The exam average was a 31%, making it a 3.0.

Dr. Demir is easily the worst teacher I've ever had. She is dismissive of students, unenthusiastically mumbles her lectures, misses class frequently, and does not provide a clear guideline on how she grades exams meaning no one knows what they have in the class until the very end. I have had more insightful bowl movements than this class was.

She is uncompassionate, and unaccomidating. Lectures are completely plagiarized from UCI, and she mumbles. Often does not come to lectures or exams, and shows no care for the class or her students. Do NOT take this class if it is not required. The TA is fantastic, but he can only do so much before the prof has to step up and actually teach.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: just dont.

do not take this class. only took it to fulfill a requirement and honestly it is definitely the worst class i've ever taken at msu. if ur not listening to every word she says in the lecture for the full 2 hours, then you will fail! exams sucked bc she doesn't give study guides. don't take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Take ANY other ISS. She goes to the exact second and every class is required, but you will never learn anything or be prepared for the exams.

I think she needs to be re-evaluated as a professor. She has cut me off talking and been dismissive and rude to me and other students on a daily basis. The class feels like a waste of time; could be condensed into 30 mins rather than 2 hrs. I can't believe this is a requirement for graduation because I can't say I've learned a single useful thing.

Dr. Muhich refuses to give out 100%, even if you miss one thing on a project she will knock you down to a 90%. She should be teaching elementary schoolers at the rate the she teaches, she is the type of teacher where "the bell doesn't dismiss you i do". She WILL make you miserable if you weren't already when you walk into that class. Good luck
do not reccommend

On the second day of class, she spent ALL TWO HOURS talking at us about how to write group contracts and how to force members out of our groups if needed. After that, she spent at least 30 minutes of each class period talking about random class policies that are already on the syllabus.

She keeps you the whole 2 hours every single time, grades difficultly and gives little to no study material for exams. Would not recommend. Tests feel like they were made for you to fail. Content is simple but she makes it hard
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: If you want to get spied on and feel like you are a second grader with no ability take this class

Worst professor I have ever had. Treats class like a high school class and likes to hear herself talk. Makes you move around a 200 person lecture and will randomly make you stand and stretch while she continues to lecture. Tests are way harder than needed and class does not properly prepare you. Would never take this professor again.

Makes the exams way harder than they have to be by putting outside info not from what she has taught into it and treats you like a Child, patrols the room during group discussion, her teaching style belongs in elementary school AVIOD AT ALL COST
Avoid this teacher at all cost. Unfair and I would rather be cleaning toilets instead of being in this class

Long, numbing lectures with yapping and picture slides that could be condensed. Strict iClicker attendance. Poor and ambiguous test prep and resources. Locked-down tests with irrelevant questions or questions posed in ways that leave no clear-cut answer. Lots of readings. Walks around class, hovering over students. I would avoid this professor.
bad bad bad bad
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
- The day before the exam she made us do a debate irrelevant to the exam content
- Randomly assigns you into groups 
- Talks all class and will yap and argue with you 
- People would show things she didn't see, and she would tell them they were wrong
- class is harder than any Broad class I've taken
- I made this account to write this!!!!!!!!!!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Worst professor I have ever had, the slides don't even have notes on them it's just photos. She loves to talk about the most pointless things. She treats the entire class like a child in 1st grade. She has this many people going out of there way to rate her shows how bad she really is. A better use of time would be to sleep for 2 hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Genuinely might be the worst professor I've ever had. Treats you like you're in high school still and doesn't tell you what to expect for exams. Do not take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I am currently taking this professor for my second ISS credit. She treats this class like it is a grad level chemistry course. The material isn't that hard to learn but she makes it feel like you need to put in so much effort to do well in this course. This is the worst 4 hours of my week

If you went to class, wrote notes about her lectures, cared about the group work, and stayed on top of the iclicker then you will do well in this class. She was a tough grader at the end, and her quizzes are very lecture heavy.
Treats you like an actual child with no respect. Forces you into moving around a giant 200+ lecture everytime. Good luck, she's one of the worst profs I've had at msu.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: This class is entirely group-based work. She grades so harshly and deducts points for the most minor things. Must attend class to understand quiz questions. Debates are weekly and basically pointless. The content was interesting, but unfortunately, the constant discussion with groups took away from that. Cold called our group so many times.

Not as bad as others may say. Attendance counts for grades and all assignments except quizzes are done with a group randomly assigned in the beginning of the semester. I found the material fairly interesting but you have to listen during lecture and take notes to do well on the quizzes. She can sometimes be oddly strict but she is nice enough.

Absolutely horrible professor she should be teaching elementary school or something. Grades way too harsh for a entry level gen ed nothing class. when people say college is stupid this teacher is a perfect example.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: the only way you will succeed is trying to write down almost everything in lectures, and having a good group. content wasn't too bad but the quizzes do make it more difficult. her questions are very specific and things she mentioned very briefly in lecture. however, do pop culture and not the taylor swift class.

You are basically betting on if you get a solid group or not when you take this class, since that is who you do EVERYTHING with. Her and her TA lurk around the room and sometimes you will look behind you and see her climbing across the bars in the room to sneak up on a group. The quizzes are impossible to study for. The slides in class are pictures

She tends to target specific groups and listen to their group conversations more than others. In addition, I feel that she often takes points of for things that are not important and feels like a very random grading system. There are a ton of groups projects in and out of class, and there are iclicker questions to account for participation.

I took her last year, and it was a genuinely fine class. She's definitely very lecture-heavy, but so long as you take lots of notes, you'll be fine. It is a lot of group work, but if your group is bad, she is more than willing to help you switch to a better one. It can be a bit boring, but as far as ISS classes go, it wasn't too bad.

Do not take Taylor Swift class, it was the biggest waste of my time I learned nothing. All she does is yap and lurk around the room. An ape on lsd can teach better than her. This class is so bad I genuinely had a nightmare about it. It was my longest class and it was the most useless a better use of your time is to walk around campus for 2 hours
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Do not take any classes with this professor. It's all lecture heavy, and they don't even make sense. By the time the exam's role around she puts material on there that we didn't even cover, and their insanely hard for no reason. Heavily focused on groupwork so pray you get a good group. Do not take her classes if you care about your grades and time
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Lectures are extremely boring. The unit tests were extremely hard to study for. A huge amount of group work is done in this class, and if you get stuck with a bad group, you might be screwed. Not enough time is given to prepare for debates, and if you have members not helping out, finishing the assignment is almost impossible.

The slideshow presentations she makes for each class leave out so much of the material she actually discusses in class, making it so hard to study for the tests. One of my group members got Mono and had to join our final project recording via Zoom and she is taking off 5 points (6.5%) because apparently, that demonstrates a lack of group management

Group debates were extraordinarily pointless, prompts always had one side that was clearly favored. Most of the lecture material is irrelevant or uninteresting. Not necessarily a hard class but a lot of pointless work to be done for a gen ed.

It's mainly group work but that was all fine for me, she just didn't give enough resources to do as well as I hoped considering I had a 4.0 in every other class. If you miss a class and the information she shares you have to find the notes somewhere, and better yet no one takes notes in this class so good luck
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This woman has your entire grade riding on the attendance and capabilities of groupmates that she randomly forms in the first week.  If you have a bad group you will have a bad grade.

Professor Muhich is a great person but bad professor. Lectures are hard to stay engaged with and oftentimes are pointless. I get it is a gen-ed but most of us were excited for a Taylor Swift focused course but were thoroughly disappointed in the material and layout of the class. Takes a lot of needless points off group debates which is frustrating.

Do not like this class or professor, she should be a first grade teacher, treats the class like were 5. Got in trouble for not walking around the class and meeting the 200 other students on first day. Material does not match the main focus of the class and is super pointless. Bad professor and worst class I have ever taken, would not suggest.

She is an alright professor but her lectures are really boring and she gets very mad if she sees you on your phone. Her grading is also really confusing and you should really hope you get a good group because she will take points off if your group members aren't in class.

Professor Muhich is a great person and not that bad of a teacher. She tries to keep students engaged through a lot of iClicker questions. Pray you get a good group at the beginning of the semester, show up to class, and you'll be fine. One con is that the lectures can get very boring.

Prof Muhich is a fine professor and a great person, but her teaching style is incredibly high-school coded. Group work is mandatory, but since you spend so much time with your groupmates, it's not horrible. Notes need to be taken in class, as she doesn't give guides or reviews. Attendance is extremely important (2 missed classes allowed).
A very group-heavy class; quizzes are not easy to study for, but overall, it's not too hard of a class. Attendance is required.

She feels more like a high school english teacher rather than a social science professor— not that it's a bad thing. She's a kind person but classes are pretty boring. I think she tries to shake it up even if the material is uninteresting. Lots of group work and debates & many extra credit opportunities.
it is boring
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Overall, I liked Dr. Muhich. She was caring and you could tell she enjoys teaching. As someone who took this class as a pre-req it was boring, but not difficult at all. There is a lot of reading in and outside of class and a lot of group work.  Attendance is mandatory and there was extra credit at the end of every unit.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects

reviews.comment: Don't take this class if you hate group work. There's some form of group activity almost every lecture, but they're not hard. Missing class drops your grade quick because of all the in class activities, but the actual work isn't bad at all. You can tell she enjoys teaching and cares about her students. Overall, really liked this class!

She is a nice professor, she tries her best to make class interesting. However, if you're just not into history even a bit you will be so bored. I do enjoy the debates we have as we read about current issues. We are put into groups at the beginning of the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She highkey has no idea what she's talking about. The slides have like, two things on them in total and she'll say “you don't need to know this” and then it'll show up on the test. You gotta read a lot before the class because she references them a lot and you literally won't learn anything in class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects

reviews.comment: This class was surprisingly interesting. She was very easy to understand and work with if help was needed. The final group project isn't bad; it just depends on your group members (you'll be with them the whole semester).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: This professor was great even though I bought two books and barely read them and still passed the exams she had us in groups so it was really easy to communicate with others outside of her class I love the professor and the ta but hated the class it was very long and kinda boring but you will still pass if you get the whole concept
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This professor was average at best. She freaked out if we packed up a minute early after a two-hour class. She always seemed annoyed or upset. Ironically, her attitude made me learn more than I typically would. Just go to class, even though you will hate being there, and you'll get a 4.0.

I would not recommend taking this course with this professor. Interesting content but no lecture notes are shown in class, she will just speak at you most of the lecture. Most points are based off attendance and heavy on exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She really cares about her students and makes expectations very clear. As long as you go to class you will be fine- if you miss class you will lose points quick. Don't do the outside readings. They were a waste of time because she goes over it in class. Lots of extra credit. Lots of group discussions in class & final is a group project but not bad.

Overall, it is a simple class; however, she does not use any slides for help, does not provide students with anything to study with, and makes it very difficult to recover after missing a class or two. I would recommend not taking this ISS class and choosing another one.

Dr. Muhich is very passionate about the subject she teaches, I just didn't like her teaching style. You have to type what she says as she's saying it because her slides are simply just pictures. Every day there is group discussion which was fine but a bit too much. There is a good amount of extra credit which was nice and the exams weren't horrible

Professor Muhich was nice, she was very passionate about the subject and knows a lot, even if class content wasn't always super interesting. Class is test heavy, but the exams are multiple choice and just like the I Clicker questions. Having a good group is important for the final project. Pay attention and take decent notes you'll be fine.

most boring class I've ever taken, but the professor thoroughly enjoyed teaching the topics to us. However, the final being a group project is the worst because I got stuck with the lousiest group ever. The exams were fine, all MCQ and most of them reflect iClicker questions. DON'T buy the books, wasted $60 just for her to talk about in class.
Don't choose

- She was really great at lectures and really cares about each student. My one problem with this class was the group project being the final. I did not have a great group and have had a bad experience with it. Also the books are a waste of money as she goes over them in lecture. Overall, the topics we learned were interesting and the class is easy!
She really cares about her students

I don't know why everybody's hating, I loved Dr. Muhich! She made her expectations very clear from the beginning and always reminded us of deadlines and upcoming tests. I was never once blindsided by anything in this class. She is super sweet and always makes sure everybody who wants their opinions heard is heard. Plus there's tons of extra credit!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She have very heavy notes but she says everything about slides with pictures so if you aren't listening and typing you'll never get the notes down, she's also quite rude
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
Unfortunately, she isn't my favorite for a few reasons.
-She doesn't post study guides or practice questions
-There are no clear ways to study for exams
-There aren't that many opportunities for points besides attendance and exams
I recommend taking a different ISS because this specific one isn't worth the stress when there are much easier options.

if you go to class, do clicker, and take notes you'll be set. attendance is key for this class as her lecture slides don't have words. didn't read any of the books and got As on all the exams, write down the clicker ?s and you will know some exam answers. she's a nice prof and wants to help you succeed. a bit boring but she does what she can.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: this class was very lecture heavy, but the professor presents them very clearly, as long as you show up and take good notes you'll be good to go, reading the books was honestly unnecessary, she gave a summary on important parts of the books in class and in iClicker questions so you should be all good there too.

The class makes me feel like I'm in middle school because it's so suffocating. It's very lecture heavy & her presentations are just pictures so they're no help. Attendance & exams are pretty much all that count to grades. If you miss a class, good luck catching up. I only took it because it's a requirement. Wish I took it with a different prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great professor would recommend to any person!
Super easy and nice professor!

This is a great ISS class to take if you are busy or if you just need an ISS. The set up is the same every week with a quiz and discussion post. Open note and open AI. He is a caring professor who wants you to succeed. Extremely easy 4.0 that you can actually learn some great things from. Couldn't recommend this enough as an ISS class.

Dr. Ericksen is a great guy. He encourages (and grades on) participation and is always quick to respond to emails. Quiz heavy but all exams are open note, open AI, open lecture, and had a dad joke/pun extra credit question. My class was "Social Science Perspectives on Happiness" and most lecture materials were short articles or TED talks :)
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: He was super nice and funny in his lectures. Content was easy to understand. Just a discussion post and quiz each week that were super quick to do. Great for a busy schedule.

He's amazing. If you're looking to take your first ISS and his class is available, this is who you'd want. He was completely online and modules were great. Weekly discussion posts and quizzes were easy and quick. He links many readings and Ted talks but honestly the lecture notes can get you most of the quiz questions pretty quickly.
Such a nice man. For the weekly quizess its open note but a little tricky, so maybe take with friends. Super nice and caring!
Best professor

This professor seems like a nice guy. The weekly work consists of watching a lecture (~30 mins), taking a 10-question quiz where one question is a joke, and doing a discussion which usually isn't too bad of a prompt. This class is on happiness and I found it to be very helpful to improve my outlook on life so I highly suggest it.

Usually returns graded assignments within the week and is very lenient on discussions and quizzes. Easy to get in contact with if you need help or have simple questions. I would 100% take another class with him if given the opportunity.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Fantastic professor. Would absolutely recommend taking a class with him. Work load is totally manageable and grades very fair.
This was the easiest class I have EVER taken.

had class online asynchronous he is the best professor there is one 10 question quiz every week with 2 questions that are dad jokes so he gives away points easy and then a discussion and reply every week that has to be 5 sentences, he is super funny and interesting lectures are usually around 20 minutes take his class if you can easy 4.0
Such a kind person and a great teacher. His class was fairly easy, too.

I personally loved professor Ericksen. I had this class fully online so I never got to speak with him personally, but he loops in personal stories in his lectures and I feel like I know a lot about him without ever actually meeting him. He's a great guy and his class is easy. He puts little dad jokes into his quizzes for an easy extra point :D

Very fun guy! Super empathetic and is very lienent, but makes sure you will still learn! His lectures actually feel very useful to everyday life and his requirements are clear. All of his assignments feel necessary and are easy as long as you understand the curriculum.
Good guy, easy class, maybe an hour of work each week, great choice for the is requirement

This was the easiest and most enjoyable class i've taken. Lectures covered everything on the test so as long as you paid attention to them and wrote notes you'll do great. I took this accelerated in the summer and had no stress from the class at all! I highly recommend this professor!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This class was very enjoyable. Every week we had lectures, two discussion posts, and a quiz. We had a few writing assignments throughout, but those were two paragraphs maximum. The professor was understanding when it came to a problem or concern any student had. I liked that he genuinely cared about teaching us skills for the future.

Such a sweet and genuine man! I loved how he made personal references in his recorded lectures because I felt like I got to know him really well without actually meeting him. The quizzes are pulled straight from the slideshows from his lectures. Don't bother watching any TedTalks or reading anything he assigns. Incredibly easy.

I really enjoyed this class! the lectures were interesting and I liked how the layout was straight forward as well. I learned a lot. I kinda wish that this class was synchronous because I enjoy lectures more in real time. Professor Ericksen was a really chill professor and as long as you take notes and listen to the lectures you will do well.

This was an insanely easy class. The exams were online and youre allowed to use the posted lectures. Professor Ericksen does require attendance, which was the only thing I didnt like about this class, because his lectures can become very boring. Overall, I learned a lot of useful information from this class and its an easy 4.0.

Prof. Ericksen is truly a guy that wants you to understand the material and is very patient to students who cannot afford the resources to pass at that very moment; something I am truly grateful for as I was one of those students. He also is very good at preparing you for the tests. Overall an easy A but his lectures can get kinda stale at times.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Caring

reviews.comment: Wonderful Professor. Interesting lectures. Not very much work outside of exams and papers. Cares about students and material being taught. Highly recommend this professor and course! Gives great feedback on assignments too!

Easy 4.0 just have to do the assignments. Really good professors actually reads your assignments and gives feedback even though he has probably thousands of students. Quizzes and exams are exactly what are on the lectures and can use the lectures he posts while taking them.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Super funny and nice. Easy class

Loved this class, would take again. He's just starting out with it but he does a really good job of getting the message across, the homework is easy, the quizzes are open note, all you have to do is show up to pass basically (he takes attendance with top hat). He's just an overall great teacher and nice guy.

Professor Erickson is very passionate about the topics he teaches. It was clear he wants students to actually apply the concepts to their lives. All information needed is posted on his slides which are uploaded. Exams are online and are open book/note. Reflections are guaranteed 100% as long as you try. Buying the book is not necessary

This was his first time teaching this course. Throughout the semester we had 4 exams and 4 reflection papers. The exams were 20 questions and online given a 2hour span to complete. Questions pulled from lectures. Exam and reflection not hard!! Lectures posted online. Easy 4.0. Would definitely recommend taking this class with Professor Ericksen.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Monaca is the best. Very caring, understanding, helpful, and supportive. Class is organized, fun, engaging. Really good lecture and activities in every class. She gives very helpful feedback and great advice on how to be a good social worker.

Monaca is one of my favorite professors. Her lectures are filled with things we can apply in practice. For a 3 hour class it is a good mix of activities, small group, and large group discussion with lecture in between. She is incredibly supportive, helpful, and responsive to her students. You know that your learning matters to her.
Take anything she teaches. She is super caring, does tons of different types of things in class and wants to see you grow and succeed.

You can tell Monaca is a social worker who cares about teaching and her students. Her classes are really intentional in helping us learn. The conversations in the class are great and pushed me to learn.
She is awesome!
in her 310 class right now favorite class of the semester. great in class discussions class is pushing me to think  in new ways. can tell she cares about students.

Her self-care class is the biggest waste of time and money. Be ready to read a bunch of self-help books, do silly activities, practice yoga and meditate. The whole basis of the class is on Pinterest and you don't really learn anything about self-care. It's an overly positive, unrealistic class. Disappointed.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Her self care class has taught me more than my clinical msw courses. This class is invaluable! Every week she makes a video answering our questions and gives feedback to each student individually each week. Most connected I've felt in an online class so far.  Wish all online classes could be this good.
She's the best, helped us learn. First time a professor has stopped and taken time to figure out what we wanted from the class. I hate research yet this class was pretty good.
Monaca is the greatest professor. She really cares and you can tell she loves social work. Class was fun and the discussions really pushed me to think. I can't wait to take her again.
best class and instructor I have ever had. she is interesting, fun lectures, good and interesting assignments.  made learning fun.
The class was really organized. The videos were very well articulated and detailed. All we have to do is pause the video and follow directions. I learned a lot!

I love Professor Rick. He's enthusiastic and makes assignments fun! He will personally get to know you and have your name memorized by the second lecture. Make sure to talk in class so it's not awkward. Class meets once a week, assignments take a few hours each, and grading is fair. It's not a class you can just blow off, but it's not hard either.

I have met no professors that match professor Rick's amazing positive energy and enthusiasm, with genuine care for his students! That's why I found CAS 111 class exciting to attend, on top of the already fun open-ended projects. It was one of my favorite classes at MSU, and the workload is moderate and fair. Great professor if you enjoy creativity.

If you are not good with adobe do not take this class. The first few weeks of the class I cried on every assignment and spent hours doing it because it's so confusing. The professor is nice just chatty and hard grader. Overall, it gets easier as you learn more on adobe, but if you are looking for an easy elective this is not the class for you.

Can't say enough good things about Professor Rick. He's experienced in his field, gave great feedback, and really made an effort to get to know students in a large lecture hall. He even reached out to me personally to compliment my work. Took this class as an elective but enjoyed it a lot more than expected.

tbf, this was a one credit class, but prof was so sweet and fun i loved him. this class was only for half of the semester and was just different adobe PS and Ai assignments that were pretty easy. only annoying thing that assignments were due at 5pm on tuesdays? he gave great feedback, answered emails, and made his video tuts very easy to understand

Professor Rick is one of my favorite professors so far at MSU. I wasn't sure about this class originally but ended up loving it more than any other class this year. He is a very fair grader and a great teacher. Show up and take the weekly attendance quiz (which he gives you the answers for, so guaranteed 100%) and do your projects = great grade.
I'm glad I took this class, I plan to take all of his classes in the future.
Best professor I've ever had. So kind and knowledgeable about what he teaches. Wants his students to succeed.

Absolutely has no idea how his own D2L works. Got a zero for working ahead and using pictures he forgot to update on an assignment. He claimed I reused a past students work when I spent hours on that assignment. Waste of time and energy, very disappointing.
He is the best professor I ever had, he gives interesting projects and knows that not everybody is at the same level, he really encourages us to do our best and be proud of it.

CAS111 is a great opportunity to get your foot in the door in photoshop, illustrator, and indesign. The projects are all fun and he will grade well if you add your own creative elements. I will say, you will be called on multiple times in class to critique other people's work or talk about your own work. But this class is fun and so helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: He is very thorough in his explanations and makes sure you understand concepts, offering additional help if you need it.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: I would recommend Richard (Rick) Epps and CAS 111 to anyone that's new to the Adobe Creative Cloud. Professor Rick is very knowledgable and approachable and he cares deeply about the success of his students. He provides video tutorials for each assignment but wants everyone to make it unique. Very enjoyable class if you are passionate about design.

Professor Rick is a good guy and gives solid step-by-step instructions for the assignments. You'll have to look up how to use the programs in adobe creative cloud in order to add your own creative elements, which are required. If you don't like sports news, his lectures will be pretty boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The professor makes you work for your grade even though it's only a one credit class. The material is relatively easy and should be an "easy A", however he refuses to give out 100% on anything. Make sure you add a lot of added creative elements to each assignment because essentially 20% of the assignment grade. Also a 94 and up is a 4.0

Loved this class. Make sure you add creativity to every assignment. He tends to knock off points if you don't add something extra. The assignments are a little time-consuming but I found them really enjoyable.

Great guy. I actually had him for KIN220 and it was super easy class, lab practicals can be challenging just study for them extra. He truly wants students to succeed and the course is designed that way.
great teacher

All of the things that are on his exams are from the slides that are reviewed during lecture which make the tests/exams very easy if you study the slides. He is very nice and helpful and he knows what he's talking about which makes the class so much more interesting and enjoyable

*KIN 220 
He is an amazing lecturer, very clear and concise! The lectures are only 50 minutes, and the labs depend on your TA. I had Ashton is he was so funny and really chill. 4 exams all in person, no cumulative final. He gives exam review slides that are condensed, I recommend generating a practice test with them and practicing that way!
Eric is awesome! I had him for KIN 217 and KIN 310 and he was great for both! He cares about his students and is willing to help you succeed in every way possible.
Eric is awesome! If you like exercise physio, he will make it exciting and enjoyable.  All around cool dude.

Hands down my favorite prof, he wants to know his students and will remember your name (even in huge classes). He is very kind and tries to make each lecture as relatable as possible.
