I had him for KIN310 and KIN217 last semester. He offers extra credit, responds to emails quickly, and stays after class for all questions! He's an amazing educator!!

Eric is one of the best professors I've ever had. For such a difficult course, he makes the class enjoyable and fun. He is super caring about the mental well-being of all his students. He gives multiple opportunities to learn about human anatomy. He is easy to reach outside of class and typically responds fast.

Grades very strictly and does not provide individual feedback on quizzes, which makes it hard to learn from mistakes. The material itself is manageable, but the grading focuses heavily on small details, so even minor errors can significantly lower your score. Be prepared to rely on your own understanding and outside resources to improve.

I've never had a professor actively sabotage/undermine my academic and professional life until now. I won't get into specifics for fear of her seeing this but she was not only working against me the whole semester, she implied herself into my professional life unknown to me. Taking her class was not only a bad thing for school but also for my life
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I've had Professor Connors twice (ACC 341 in the summer and ACC 321 in the fall). I will confidently say she is the best professor I have had in Broad. Many of the reviews from her feel more targeted at her personality, but if you put in work, you will be rewarded in the class. As long as you show up and participate, the class is great.
Not as bad as many of the reviews that many people make it out to be. The class does require a lot of work, but Professor Connors was helpful on the discussion boards.

When I first read these reviews I expected this class to be awful but after taking it, I think Professor Connors is a pretty good professor. She will tell you almost exactly what is expected of you with homework and exams. She initially comes off cold but if you show effort and ask productive questions she is kind and willing to work with you.

By far the worst accounting professor at MSU, if you even consider her class accounting related. She wouldn't criticize your work, she would criticize you. Her big rule was to not ask any questions in class. The effect she has on her students life is extremely negative, and the material she teaches has zero application to anything.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: You need 30 points for 4/4 in participation but there aren't even 30 lectures. She expects you to chase points outside class. Not to mention, talks to you in a demeaning disrespectful tone if you ask her questions (even in office hours). She doesn't allow course questions over email. Brutal grader, wouldn't take again.

Professor Connors makes it tough for you to want to show up to class and frankly do anything for her class. She is a tough grader and gives little to no leeway when it comes to attendance. She expects you to show up to every single class and ask questions throughout the semester despite her class being hard to follow and extremely boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Tough grader. Very Tough.

This Professor is awful. She forces you to attend class and still won't give you full participation credit even if you do attend every class. The class average for participation was a 57%. Avoid her classes if you can. She is disrespectful if you ask a question. I should have had an A, however, participation pushed me under by .7%

This was the second class of my second master's. I have taken numerous college classes and this was by far the worst and most frustrating class I have ever taken.  I don't think it matters how many times you listen to the lectures, read the books, follow the discussion board her questions on exams leave it near impossible to do well in her class.

Majority of the grade is from the midterm and final. Coursework expectations are unclear and no individual feedback is provided. This professor does not respond to emails, all course questions must be posted on the discussion board. Her clarifications to student questions are often vague and confusing and strike a condescending and arrogant tone.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She won't give you any sort of personalized feedback on your work, and you must constantly post to the discussion board and look to your fellow classmates if you want help with anything. Considering the cost of tuition, you'd expect better from a professor.

The professor does not give any individual feedback. You cannot email her for course questions. She requires textbook readings; her posted slides are based on the textbook; but she actually disagrees with most of the textbook. I spent a significant amount of time crossing out terms on HER OWN SLIDES. Extremely unprofessional, not easy to learn.

The material can be confusing at first, but if you ask questions and go to her office hours, she's very helpful. There's a lot of small quizzes in the first half of the semester and then two group projects in the second half. The exams weren't bad as long as you understand the quizzes.
She is generally rude. She is not there to help you, she is there to get paid. Unfortunately there is no one else you can take. Do not trust her when you ask her for help!

She is an awful teacher, she goes on tangents half the time. Will mark you down for everything and showing up to class every day does NOT get you full participation credit. Refuses to work with students and around student's schedules. If you miss any of the exams or quizzes you're done for because she won't allow make-ups.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor flaunts her so called intellect but forgets she studied longer than we've lived. The real problem? Arrogance, especially when students ask questions she seems intent on making them regret it. Surprising that someone with supposed expertise can't show basic respect. Time for a reality check on her inflated ego and so called intellect.
Very rude! she probably doesn't like her life so he has to take her frustration out on her students. If you have questions about a subject be aware that you might be looked down upon.

Class is test-heavy and participation-heavy, with lots of group stuff. Her attendance/participation policy sucks. The modelling "quizzes" are a pain to do but they're done with a group, so it's not terrible (plus she'll look them over before you turn it in so you'll get a good grade). Overall not a terrible class/prof, it's just tedious
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She has a really bad attitude when it comes to teaching and is pretty rude to students. The class was not terrible but her participation structure is really annoying and forces you to ask questions on the discussion board and you have to compete with the person asking the most questions. Connors is always complaining and talking down to students.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Connors was great! She gave us the exact format of the exams and pretty much told us what we needed to study for.  Although attendance was not required, she was quite a stickler about students coming to class.  I would not have done so well if I did not attend class that is for sure.  I would recommend her for ANY accounting class!

I would have spirals every week after class because he would talk so much and I couldn't keep up. We were guilt tripped at the end. The only reason I don't regret taking the class is because of my classmates. RELY ON YOUR CLASSMATES! When you work together you can make it through this, and hopefully with an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This professor was one of the worst I've ever had at Michigan State. Be prepared to spend all of your money on books because he required 7 of them. He had a fill in the blank portion on exams, and it's filled with the most random and niche details that you probably wouldn't remember anyways if you did read. He also sucks at responding to emails.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: AVOID!!!! So much reading, weekly in-person hand-written quizzes are on random material from the past weeks. Final/ midterm are in person and you have to write an essay that you won't get the prompt for until you walk in on final day. Harsh grader. Didn't take attendance, but there are quizzes every other class that cannot be made up.

The class itself isn't hard if you show up but there are a lot of textbooks to purchase (some which I spent an upwards of $20 only to read a small passage and then discard). He doesn't allow technology so you can't use a pdf. Your buying books for no reason just to read 20 pages and never use it again. Also a lot of group projects and readings.
Makes you buy SEVEN books, never stops talking and can't stay on one topic for more then 10 minutes. Take any other IAH instead.

Brian Burns is extremely difficult to work with, he will not allow any absences as well as make up work. He expects you to sit and listen to him talk for an hour and 50 minutes every class while also ready up to 150 pages a week. If you don't think his lectures are boring that sucks because no technology is allowed so you're forced to stare at him.

Avoid at all costs. Rants about AI constantly, syllabus is almost never accurate, blames students for doing poorly on his terribly written exams, won't answer questions you ask in class so he can continue his meaningless rants. Surprise group projects not on syllabus, extensive reading assignments, never posts slides, worst class I've ever taken.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Don't take this class

Please don't take this class. you will suffer it's ridiculous how much quizzes and reading there is. Every week there's a quiz also the mid term and final are the hardest exams too. You have to remember lines from the book and know what word fits in the sentence and you have to remember 15+ portraits for the exams.

Do not make this mistake on taking this class .This is the hardest class I've ever took in my college years. You have to buy 200 dollars worth of books where you read not even half the book and then move on to the next one. He Makes things more complicated than they need to be.Don't take it trying to help you out seriously. He doesn't stop talking
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: After spending $200+ on books, you get to then sit through a 110 min lecture where he cries about his sad life and ai more than teaching. Then maybe putting on a movie or quickly throwing up the laziest slide show (same one all sem btw) After learning close to nothing in class expect a multiple page no notes written response only exam. Disgusting.

never take a class with him. no technology is allowed at all but expects us to spend over $200 on multiple books throughout the semester that we read within a week. one quiz per book based on the reading for the day (which is about 70 pages of reading.) exam consists of writing an essay and 32 questions quoting all the books we've read. pls run.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: First of all, seven books and no technology allowed in the classroom... He talks more about how technology is poisoning society than the actual topic. Be in class or you will fail, and there are impromptu group projects. The grading is easy as long as you read what he tells you. I would never take this class again. Choose a different IAH!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: 7 required *paperback* textbooks that we don't even read the entirety of. Talks about his personal life more than valuable information. he's a waste of my money and time, and that's kind.
Non. Stop. Talking.

If you value your free time and sanity do not take this course with professor Burns. He does not know how to stay on track with the course materials and will bore you to death by going on tangents about god knows what.

I saw reviews that said he yaps a lot, but I figured it couldn't be that bad. Do not make my mistake, it IS in fact that bad. None of it is relevant to the course, and is useless information. He took years off my life in this class, please save yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Had to buy 6 books for this class. Very fast paced and was reading 50+ pages a night. Pop quizzes on books we overlapped reading. Would update and repost the syllabus every week so we never knew what was coming. 8 quizzes. Had an essay, group project, and final exam due within the last 3 weeks of classes. Never answers emails so don't even try.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: No phones, laptops or tablets in class. No textbooks but over $230 of individual books. That list of books was not correct until the second week. Does not update course schedule. Impromptu essays on exams. D2L was not available until the second week and is rarely used.
Sent me into a mid-life crisis at 13. Now I'm only gonna make it to 26.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: Sent me into epileptic shock every time we had a pop quiz. No study guide, no warning, had one every week and so much reading plus an essay. Made my hair turn gray. Never even talked about the topic, one student asked a question and was ignored by this teacher so he could continue talking about his personal life. Hate this class, going to cry.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: My biggest complaint is that there are so many books that you have to buy for the class that we only read for a week or two before moving on and despite having a paid apprenticeship, I'm parting with money that could be going towards groceries. Not to mention, most of these books have a free PDF version online, but we are not allowed to use it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Dr. Burns is extremely talkative. He often lectured for the entire 2 hour class, mostly about pointless stuff that had little to do with anything that would be on quizzes or exams. He was a fairly easy grader, but i read almost all of the books (i believe there were 7 total) and went to every single class, which most kids did not do.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Worst experience of my life. Never again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: If you don't enjoy spending $100+ on books and 60+ pages of reading A NIGHT, this class may not be for you. While Professor Burns is clearly passionate about the subject matter, I can not say the same for the students within the class. This class is 2 hours long, and often the entire time is spent watching PowerPoints and listening to him ramble.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: get ready to spend money on several novels that you will read one chapter of but is “required” for assignments when you dont need to use them. group projects basically every class, the most boring two hour long lectures ever. he doesnt try at all to engage you. he doesnt use technology, so everything is on paper and very inconienced. nice guy tho
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Burns class gave me an early onset mid life crisis, 100% would NOT recommend.

I would NEVER take this class again. Dr. Burns took 3 weeks to respond to my email. We have work every week for essays. He grades super hard. Uses big scary words to confuse us. He always forgets what he's talking about. He can't go one second without talking about something else. He wants us to devote our entire lives to him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Dr. Burns's class was by far the worst I've ever taken. He wrongfully assumes that everyone understands him when absolutely no one does. Essays are every week, with very unclear prompts. He expects everyone to drop all other activities and only focus on ISHALL. He uses big and scary words to confuse children. Praying I don't get him for ISHALL 2.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Dr. Burns is a really nice guy and cares about what he teaches a lot but that doesn't come across to his students. There is a ton of reading which is fine however he often gives quizzes when the majority of students are unprepared making it difficult to complete questions
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Class is fine, he's a very poor academic though. Ramble for hours about nothing and makes it impossible to keep track of what matters and why doesn't in class. Should start a podcast instead of trying to teach students material.o

While his lectures can go way off topic and beyond, Dr. Burns clearly enjoys his job and cares a lot about his students. While initially heavy on the pop quizzes, he does lean off of them as the year goes on, and they are actually quite easy if you complete the reading on time. Lots of reading and essays, but it is well worth it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: If you like a professor that goes off topic about random things and is hard to understand but kind of funny and good looking then this is the one for you! His lectures were so boring I almost always fell asleep. There are lots of papers to write and the quizzes are based on the many books you read throughout the semester. Read the books!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: Very insightful class and learned a lot. Mostly good selection of books to read. We watch a movie/documentary with each book we read. Not a timely grader, he graded my midterm paper after I submitted the final. He is very talkative which sometimes makes a long class feel even longer. Overall great guy, just not a fan of his teaching style.

Dr. Burns' entire class consists of 2 hour, long-winded lectures that don't engage students. He is boring, rants, gives vague instructions, and won't respond to emails.  You will have to spend over $100 just to buy books because no computers are allowed. Also, he hasn't graded half the quizzes and our final is next week. Avoid at all costs!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He was difficult, and had group essays which I've never done before! But he really helped me grow in my writing, and he definitely talks a lot. A difficult challenge but a welcome one to be sure. If you do not wish to read a lot of content don't take this class then.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: I was very unhappy with Dr. Burns. I emailed him several times for help, and he basically told me I was out of luck. The workload is intense, and we read multiple books that were extremely difficult to understand. The quizzes aren't bad, but he pulls a lot of tricks and is a difficult grader. I would never recommend him to anyone.
teaches well focuses on some things that dont matter. Easy grader

Lots of writing and reading which is expected of the course! genuinely helped me grow as a writer so so much- his lectures were always amusing and you could never quite guess what would happen next. A little off track with lectures sometimes, but this class helped me improve a ton and was the right level of difficulty.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I had Dr. Burns for both ISHALL 1+2. Genuinely the worst classes I've taken. He goes on for ages on irrelevant lectures and never drives it to a point. He has no respect for students' names+pronouns. He really forgets he is teaching 13-15-y/o, not adults. WAY too much of a workload for children, regardless of gift or talent. Not worth the credits.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: While I did a good job grade wise during my year of ISHALL, the year was miserable altogether. Writing prompts often made no sense, we were expected to pretty much devote all (and I mean all) of our time to this class. I do not feel as though I learned what was advertised that I would learn. If you do even one extracurricular on top of this; don't.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--So many papers

reviews.comment: Mr. Burns is a very nice man, but not a very good professor. I'm a lover of literature, but he makes the subject boring by going on long (sometimes hour long) rants that have very little structure. He was very unprofessional when it came to promoting his own political opinions. He is also very narrow minded about Lit. Very Postmodern. Beware.

Best professor I ever had. Definitely chooses favorites which I was one of. Hes an acquired taste to say the least but genuinely wants you to succeed and loves his job. Took two classes with him and never gave me less than a 4.0. Be ready to read a ton but besides his like for Henry James he has good taste in novels
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Burns is extremely unorganized, he does not know anybody's name in the class, his essay prompts are very long and unclear. I did not enjoy his lectures at all and he can't go one minute without going off on a tangent. We rarely went over the huge essay projects because he would spend the whole class rambling about unrelated things.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: fun guy, but you have to have a unique sense of humor to like his class. defintley has favorites, so make a good first impression. One of my favorite teachers of all time though, would recommend his class. Talks alot and gets distarcted easily.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Get ready to read is an understatement. Burns is an interesting guy and can talk your ear off for the entire two hour lecture on any subject his tangent is focused on that day. You will read 8 books and be tested in detail on the fill in the blank exams. If you just need an IAH find a different one. Cool guy, too much for a prerequisite.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Ishall 1 has to be one of the greatest classes that I have ever taken. One of the most difficult, of course, but it is just such a great opportunity for HS students. The lectures are always engaging, as is the large load of classwork, if you put in the effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: not a bad teacher, but his lectures are boring and the readings are long. sparknotes is your friend. put effort into your essays. overall; boring, but nice pro tip: ask him to draw a map of europe. you will not be disappointed
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--SO MANY PAPERS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: As long as you have a general idea about the readings you will do well in the class. Lectures can be boring at times, but slides do have useful information when it comes to the midterm and final. Overall funny guy, and great class.

I did not like this class very much. My TA was very bias and opinionated. His lectures are possibly the most boring thing on this earth. Going to recitation helps a lot. TAs of all the grading. class is mandatory due to the 7b unannounced quizzes over the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Burns was an okay teacher. He had a lot to say about small details and was always going off on tangents that did not make sense. He is an easy grader, but the homework is definitely not easy. Long readings and difficult prompts. Very difficult to understand and is often not helpful when it comes to group work or homework.

Says cool a lot - which automatically makes him cool. I recommend actually reading the material so you know what you're doing. Grades all details and challenges you to improve. NOT AN EASY CLASS YOU MUST WORK FOR IT! Extremely helpful teacher. I recommend taking this if you want to improve your English skills:

Had him for a year, he likes to ramble about small details which maybe mean something to him but not to the class, cares about the content rather than the grammar, digresses into irrelevant topics, wasnt really clear at all, easy A if you use sparknotes to help you get the main idea, using his analyzations in papers  usually gets you an A.

He does seem like he knows the material pretty well, but he repeatedly interrupts group work with random anecdotes and stories that ramble on. He's an easy grader and seems more focused on content than nit picking the grammar and structure of a paper which i like. Can be funny at times, and is way better when you're one on one with him.
Topic was Frankenstein. Was an ok class with a solid professor. No complaints.

I came into the class without any interest in poetry. However, I managed to get a 4.0 because he is very clear about what he wants in the papers and very willing to answer questions. The quizzes were another story, but if you do fine on the papers, it's not hard to do well in the class.

It is very obvious that he knows his stuff. However, he is not very good at coming across clearly to students. Class consists of 1 hour and 20 minutes of rambling information and disorganization. He frequently ignores student questions and is vague on expectations for tests and essays. One of my worst experiences at MSU this far.
He tells you exactly how to get a good grade. If you do those things you can't go wrong. Good teacher.
He tries to make his class fun. Class is very easy if you do what you're supposed to i.e. papers, midterm, and readings. I got a 4.0 and didn't study much.

Although the class itself was boring, the professor was VERY helpful. You could tell he really loved his job and was willing to do anything he could to raise a grade. The downfall was there were 4 books and they are all necessary. He is an easy grader and a funny guy.
Wouldn't recommend taking this class.
Good teacher and willing to help out. He is VERY specific with what you should get out of a poem on tests.
He is a nice man, but very confusing!  It is hard to decipher exactly what he wants from students.
If you fail this class, it is genuinely on you. He makes it so easy to get a 4.0 he wants you to get one.

Very easy to follow and an easy 4.0 I still learned a lot even though it was all online. You can complete the class at your own pace for the most part. Very easy to email and sent out pages of the lectures to read/use. Also offered lots of EC and retakes. No writing assignments, and the final is earning a certificate online. Would take again!
easy class, his lecture videos aren't bad, but he does give a lot in additional readings

SO much to say, and all positive things. Prof Timke is so caring and his personality shines through. He is so passionate about advertising and always sets expectations very clear for the class. Everything is posted in D2L. If you're taking him for ADV200, prepare to have an easy yet very insightful class. I promise it'll be worth the credits.
He is amazing

I love this professor! Such a sweet, grounded, and wise person in the world of advertising. You can tell he is passionate about what he teaches and hopes it lands with his students. I took this class online and it was all so easy to follow. Everything is in D2L, Weekly quizzes+PackBack but super easy to do. TAKE ONE OF HIS CLASSES!

Great professor! I'm usually hesitant about online classes, but Professor Timke made is super easy to stay on track with the course. Everything is laid out perfectly on d2l. We just had to watch the lecture plus materials, then do a packback and quiz weekly. I would recommend this class with Prof Timke!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Awesome professor! I had him for PR260, which was online this semester. Even though it was online, he made sure to make the lectures engaging as possible. The content was very diverse and I learned a lot. The exams were easy and followed what was discussed in the lecture videos, which I really appreciated. Would 100% recommend to anyone!
Honestly cannot say enough good things about professor Timke. TAKE THIS CLASS IF YOU CAN. Very clear and concise on expectations, cares about students.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Super easy class! The prof is amazing and I would 100% take another class of his. He taught a lot of lessons with personal experience which was awesome. Lots of guest speakers.

Professor Timke is the BEST!!!! He is a great professor, his classes are super interesting, and an overall great and cool guy. He is super understanding and accommodating. I highly recommend. My favorite professor at MSU by far!

PR 260: Amazing person and professor. So kind and makes the course interesting, although you don't need to take heavy notes to get a good grade. The projects are engaging and fun and as long as you try, you'll get a good grade. No sweat class. If you need this PR requirement, it's Timke every time.

Was a very nice professor and seemed to be very understanding. Course had weekly quizzes that you were able to retake as many times as needed to get a grade you were happy with.
Only had 2 homework projects which were graded hard but fair, and had only 3 scheduled exams including the final

Best ADV professor in MSU. Great guy and is willing to help his students succeed in class and in life. He is enthusiastic about what he is teaching and makes learning about advertising actually fun unlike other professors. Overall great guy 10/10.
I loved him. He was a great professor and you could tell he loves to teach:) Definitely recommend taking a class with him! You will learn as well as enjoy being with such a nice professor!

This was a very disorganized class, the grades page was all over the place. Lots of busy work and pointless little activities to do. Overall an easy class to pass but I don't want take another class in CSUS based purely on this class. A big project was a video book review we had to submit on youtube, I don't know why she bothered. Very slow grader.

Took online. Nice professor, answered emails quickly. The class was a module every week which was lectures, a open note quiz, and sometimes a case study which was longer writing quiz. One final exam, have not taken that yet. Overall easy class, and interesting material.

Prof was very accessible and responded quickly to emails. Class was weird and mostly just busywork and tons of assignments but very easy to complete. Wasn't a clear order/structure of the class (random assignments every week) but prob better in person? Some weeks were less work according to holidays/breaks. Overall decent but wouldn't take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This professor was very... different. One of the homework assignments showed us how privileged some were than others and there was a quiz on how to apologize to people. It's super easy but there are assignments every week. I wouldn't recommend it but definitely not hard.

Pros: Easy content, extra credit, and professor is responsive if you need help
Cons: D2L page is very disorganized and lots of busywork

This was my second course with Dr. Eustice, but CSUS 200 was much more disorganized. The content is not difficult, you just have to pay close attention and there are lots of unnecessary assignments.
A lot of busy work for this class but not too difficult to succeed. Just make sure to keep track of all the assignments and pace yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dr. Eustice is a great advisor and is good about communicating with students. The intro class has a lot of busywork, but it's all very easy. As long as you remember to check in with the class every week, it's easy to succeed.

Crystal is a good professor and a great advisor. This class can be boring at times but not bad for a 200-level class that all SPRT students have to take. Staying on top of the work she assigns is generally easy and assignments were never overly stressful.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Lots of busywork in the class, not hard and not graded very hard. Shes super slow when grading, and needed a technology break the week we had a 150 pt project due. Her office hours were through zoom so there was no way to contact her. Wont respond to emails, but isnt a tough grader. Wouldnt recommend her if you dont have to take the class.
A lot of assignments and readings are kinda pointless but they are easy enough to get through.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Caring

reviews.comment: Many reviews seem overly harsh, often from students upset about not getting a 4.0. She isn't easy, but her class isn't the hardest at JMC. Go to office hours! She values students who ask thoughtful questions and actually do the readings. Overall, she's a great professor and one of my favorites.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I don't feel that the rest of these reviews do her justice. She was very professional, gave clear guidelines, and assignments and lectures were very engaging. While the class did require all of the readings to be printed, she was open to conversations about accommodations. I would definitely take her again; this class is what got me into my major!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Extremely vague and unclear with assignment directions, which made it hard to know what was expected. Responses to questions often came off as passive-aggressive, and the feedback provided was not helpful or constructive. The course also required printing a large amount of material, which ended up costing a lot of money throughout the semester.

All expectations were so unclear and if asked about them, she would be passive aggressive and borderline offended. So much reading and you will not go over majority of it, unfortunately felt like a waste of time/money. Had all scholars of color last and were given less attention than non POC scholars. Hates teaching undergrad and it shows. AVOID!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Do not take this class! Not good at all with communication and passive aggressive when communicating. You have to print out every reading, so when it's 60 pages, it's extremely expensive. I practically went into debt printing her assignments.

Literally don't take her if you don't have to, avoid at all costs. Not only is she not a great professor in the sense of teaching, she also is just a not very nice person and is super condescending towards any participation.

She has a power trip that reaches an extremely unprofessional level. Her instructions are often unclear, and she becomes frustrated when students do not understand her vague guidance. She dislikes having to “repeat herself,” even though the information she claims to be repeating is not included in the syllabus or clearly addressed in lectures.

DO NOT TAKE HER. Terrible person. Does not answer questions in emails. "it's on the syllabus" bs. ONLY HAS ONE DAY OF OFFICE HOURS?? If you ask a question on an email she'll send a class announcement acting as if answering questions is not her job. Terrible essay feedback. Has a superiority complex against her students?? ruined my gpa; avoiding her
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor is such a fantastic, knowledgeable lecturer who has a deep passion for the topic. He explains these topics so well and with such excitement, it gets me excited to learn. I learned so much from this class without it being difficult.
Very nice guy that seems to care a lot about his students. Easy and fun class to take, good elective.
Cool teacher with easy class

He made the classroom a fun environment, and was very understanding near the end of the semester. His grading is very relaxed and the weekly assignment was a paragraph on whatever I wanted. I really enjoyed understanding music i'm gonna miss that guy
He seems to actually care about what he is teaching and that makes the class so much better.

Very nice professor, you can tell he likes teaching about music and he's also very passionate. One of my favorite professors I've had at MSU. Makes essays fun because you write about almost anything you want related to music. Great choice for an elective.

It wouldn't let me choose the correct course but I took IAH 241a with Professor Fields, which was Music and Society in the Modern World. I loved this class, especially for an IAH. Prof Fields has such a caring, kind demeanor about him which made me want to participate even more. Easy workload as well. This was the way an IAH course SHOULD be.

I took this class very last minute not knowing anything at all about music. One of the best professors I have ever had so far at MSU. He is really engaging and cares that you pass. Not even a music major! I am business but was my favorite class this semester.

TAKE ANY OF FRANNY'S CLASSES!!! One of my first GD professors and definitely the best out of the others. Her course plan is extremely organized and straight-forward. Pushes you to be a better student and designer without the extra stress. 1000/10
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: One of if not the best professor I've had. She's nice and she gives great constructive feedback on your work. She's a really easy grader, and her lectures are concise and to the point.  She will happily take the entire class time to answer any questions. Highly recommend this professor, especially if you have ADHD. She will make your life easier.

I came into GD360 with no interest in graphic design, & left with a clear understanding of how to use Adobe programs as well as a curiosity for the medium as a whole. Franny is an amazing & compassionate educator, and as long as you apply yourself you should have no problem in her class. I wish I could have her as a prof for the rest of my degree.

Professor Francoise has been one of my favorite professors so far. She is caring, hardworking, and devoted to her students. She offers assistance whenever asked, and is always open to help out. I would 100% take a class from her again, and 100% recommend her to anyone taking Graphic Design courses!!!!!

Dr. Fenn is a great lecturer and definitely knows her stuff. However, you can tell that she dislikes her life.  She is extremely hot and cold towards students. One moment she will be nice and the next she will lash out at you with no warning. I would avoid her if given the chance. Also expect rude emails and responses if you ask questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She did teach me important information. I highly reccomend to not miss classes even though the lectures are quite boring they are important. She can be very passive aggressive and asking questions becomes daunting because of her responses. She is very knowledgable though, study for the final as best as you can.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Can be a bit hard on you, but just have common sense. Attendance not required, but it becomes an easy A if you do. Just show up to class and complete the homework, and you'll pass easily.

Professor Fenn is very straightforward about all lectures, assignments, participation requirements, and extra credit opportunities. While some may find that the content is difficult to understand, if you attend class and do the homework, it's hard to do badly. Overall, I really enjoyed the class and learned a lot!

She is a fine professor. Easy class, the only problem I have with her is that she is very condescending to students. It is a mostly freshman class and she kept going on and on when the class was confused about filling out the scantron with no instruction from her how to fill it out. If you don't want to be yelled at don't take this class.

Professor Fenn is good but can be rude towards students at times. Just dont ask questions and dont let her see your phone(she will call you out). She also called someone out for leaving class 10 mins early. There is extra credit which raises your grade +3%. Homework isnt too hard its due every friday. Tests are just alot of memorizing.

I enjoyed this class. Fenn has great informational lectures, it is sometimes hard to be there for an hour and 20 minutes, but it is an overall easy class. Homework and exams aren't hard. My only advice is to not ask questions during lecture, you will see why. But overall worth getting as a professor.

Attendance is not mandatory but HIGHLY recommended, as lots of test questions are directly from the lectures. Homework is a weekly inquizitive + research participation. you'll be fine as you just do your work. Her lecturing is also great and she's very knowledgeable, and just be polite.

Professor Fenn is definitely knowledgeable, and her psych 101 class is really easy if you just go to lectures. You cannot get all the information off of the slides. She is sometimes rude to students, but usually only when they ask questions that waste time. Overall, if you just go to class and don't talk, you'll probably get an A.

Prof. Fenn is an engaging lecturer who makes the content very applicable and easy to understand. The comments of her being snappy are true, but if you pay attention and don't ask silly questions or talk during her lecturers it won't be a problem for you. If you go to lecturers, do the readings, and study for the exams they are pretty easy.

I personally though that Dr Fenn, was amazing you can tell that she cares about her students and about her craft, she is a great professor, make sure that you are going to class, and pay attention. She can be condescending sometimes but overall as a professor she is amazing. I have seen Her yell at a student for the dumbest reason, she can be rude

The class is very lecture heavy, but the lectures are very informative and interesting. She is quite rude especially to students whose questions she deems a waste of her time. Attendance is not mandatory but I highly recommend going because I found that going to lecture and just going over the review slides, was enough to do well on the exams.

One of the rudest professors I have ever come across. Will ask her a simple question, and will get a passive aggressive answer. I don't know one person that thinks otherwise and isn't absolutely terrified of her.

Undoubtedly, Professor Fenn definitely knows what she's talking about and she is a great professor. The only thing that throws me off is how she is sometimes rude to her students. Other than that, she goes in depth into the material and ensures that you understand it. Be sure to pay attention to what she's saying. Only reading the slides won't work

Personally, I thought Dr. Fenn was a great professor. She could be a little snappy at times if you were rude to her, but overall she kept things professional and educational. She didn't just read off of the slides presented and made the audience engage with her. She can go through topics a little fast, but luckily the topics aren't too difficult.

Great and engaging lectures. If you take basic notes and study for a few days before the exam, you will do amazing. I can see why people call her rude, but she doesn't want her time wasted and lowk I respect that. If you show up to lectures and pay attention you'll pass.

Went into this class thinking I would actually learn & realized very quickly that I will have to teach everything to myself. Her slides would help if she was acc talking about the course but she side tracks often and tbh is very rude in general. I have witnessed her being rude to her TA's which isn't a good look and put me off. Very tough grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The class itself was fine it's just really important you attend lectures (she'll get really heated if you don't). Honestly one of the rudest, most disrespectful professors I have ever encountered. She was rude to her ULAs and especially in her emails. I never once sensed an ounce of kindness nor care in anything she said or wrote. Beware.

Dr. Fenn can come off as standoffish, but you can tell she really cares about her students under her rough exterior. She taught me a lot and I would take her again. Just pay attention and do your work its not that bad.

Nice but can be a little condescending. This class was so easy but it might be a lot if you fall behind. Try to attend most of the lectures, even if you don't take notes they're still helpful. Also, the extra credit is time consuming but so worth it as something to fall back on just in case you don't do so well on one of the exams.

Professor Fenn was cool. The class was easy if you study a normal amount. Homework can be annoying, but not bad. Her lectures are organized and engaging use real-world examples to make things understandable. She can be passive aggressive, but the bad reviews are exaggerated- she is a fine professor. Textbook is unnecessary- don't waste your money

This prof is so rude/passive aggressive with all of her students. Every encounter I have had with her or watched her have with another student she has not shown any kindness, which spoils the whole class for you. The class isnt that bad, she gives extra credit points, attendance isnt mandatory, but is urged. Her attitude just ruined it for me.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Fenn is very educated in her field, and definitely knows what she is teaching. Although, she tends to be rude to students and passive aggressive. No attendance is required, but I strongly advise to go because all the exam information are from her lecture notes and videos played in class. Weekly homework, but fairly easy if you stay on track.

Professor Fenn was very thorough and outlined all expectations of the course. There is research included so be sure to get this done on time! Generous curves are provided and overall it is easy to succeed. Going to lectures are important and the course is test heavy---but there is a drop and a final will replace lowest score.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Fenn teaches an informative and no-nonsense course. Some people refer to her as rude, but seeing as most of those people ask questions about things provided since the beginning of the semester I can understand her frustration. Lectures are not mandatory but you WONT pass this class without continued attendance and detailed notes. Would take again.

Make sure to attend class and take notes! The exams are based on lectures, the slides will not help you, attendance is not mandatory so don't let that tempt you. There is Inquisitive homework due every Friday night which is easy. Part of your grade is from research hours, and extra credit will be available to you.

Super knowledgeable, probably more than most professors in her field. That being said, GO TO CLASS!! The exams are almost entirely based on class discussions. The lecture slides barely help and the textbook is basically your only option. The homework is chill but can take a very long time. Can be passive-aggressive if you ask dumb questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Fenn is knowledgeable and respectable. Some find her passive-aggressive, but if you put in effort and check provided information first, she's chill. The class is content-heavy, so attendance and focus are key. Exams are curved, homework is a little bit long but an easy 100 if you're trying. TA's are helpful, and office hours are highly recommended

She gives pretty massive curves which I am grateful for. Make sure to buy some index cards to make flashcards. She gives occasional extra credit problems which you would only know if you attend lecture. Weekly inquisitive HW due on Friday 11:59pm (can take up to 4 hours so plan accordingly). All exam material in inquisitive textbook

Very rude and condescending while talking to her and in her emails. Exams are completely based on discussions from the lecture and the review slides she posted commonly didn't include stuff from the lecture that was on the exam. If you must take it with her make sure to go to every lecture or else you're cooked.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Fenn is not everyone's favorite professor and definitely wasn't mine. She is very knowledgeable about psychology but sometimes it can get the best of her where she can forget that it is a 100-level class. She is very passive aggressive in lectures towards students. Tests often have questions directly from class.

Even tho she doesn't take attendance you will fail if you don't pay attention to her lectures. Also if you don't read the textbook you will also probably fail. Your grade is basically just based off exams and the questions are absurd. Don't take this class. Kims also just rude to every student that talks.

Annoying homework every week, give yourself more time than you think you need. Lecture heavy and questions on tests are based on things she says in lecture. She drops one test so if you do good on the first 4 you don't have to take the final. 8 hours required SONA with 6 more extra credit hours (3%). Curves tests.

Fenn is extremely advanced in Psych and her lectures were very organized. To do good on tests attendance is SO IMPORTANT. Not only that but also writing in depth notes of all of the slides & memorizing all of it. Fenn was aggressive at times and made me feel like I couldn't ask questions. She will single you out if you are off task or leave early.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Overall, she's fine but was super snarky in emails. Her lectures are very interesting but can get boring quick.

Interesting lectures, but LOTS of info. Minimum of 8 hrs of psych research participation (max 2 online) and extra hrs are bonus. She cares about helping you succeed as long as you put in the work required. Lowest exam is dropped, so if you pass the first 4, you don't need to take the final. As long as you memorize everything, the class is easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: The reason I didn't pass the class my own fault. Although attendance isn't mandatory if you do not go you will not pass which is why I did not. She can be passive aggressive at some points but the lectures are put together well.

Dr. Fenn is a fair grader and is a no-nonsense type of professor, but she can be passive-aggressive and condescending at times. Not easy to approach. Attendance was not mandatory, it is NOT recommended to skip often. There are 4 exams, 5 with the final, BUT one of them is dropped, so you could drop the final. Also, there are curves. She's solid.

Most of the questions on exams were based of things she said in class that were not included on slides so although attendance wasn't mandatory without going to class you essentially would fail. You could possibly not hear something and miss a question on the exam. Homework was once a week and took forever. 8 hours of research required too.

Professor Fenn gives amazing interactive lectures that made me enjoy going to her class. She has a very professional personality that could seem strict but she is funny and a great professor. Homework is easy but don't wait til last minute cause it can take a few hours if you dont know the content. She is very lenient with a curve on exams.

Dr. Fenn was an amazing professor. Attendance was not mandatory, but some questions on the exam are about things she showed in lectures so if you don't show up, you'll miss easy points on the exam. She is very, very passionate about psychology. Listen to what she says in class because it provides a more in-depth understanding.

I love this professor! I saw all the comments from Rate My Professor before the first day of class and I was worried. But she is an amazing teacher. The information is clear and if you need help there are multiple opportunities to get help. She is funny, smart and values hard work. Be prepared to work but the class is only hard if you don't try.

She set a very clear criteria on the first day of class and what to expect throughout the semester. If you put effort and work into the class, it's not hard to get a 4.0. I highly recommend starting research hours early to give yourself enough time. Dr. Fenn is very blunt but funny at times and seems to care for how well her students do.
Didn't make me feel great about myself in the way she talked to me or responded to emails.
Extremely rude in emails.

Professor Fenn's class is very lecture heavy, and is not a good fit for students who don't like to show up to class. It has more requirements than I expected for a 100 level class, however she is very clear about how exams will look and what is expected of you. If you are someone who doesn't mind putting in some work, I would recommend her course.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Extremely homework heavy without any guidance or actually helpful advice to not spend hours on it a night. Fenn was weirdly rude after I had just asked for a bit of help with her system and told me not to contact her about this again. Not to mention 8 hours of research studies. This is a crazy amount of work for a 101. I ended up dropping it.

Professor Fenn is a super sweet woman who knows what she is talking about, I really liked her and the class, however the class itself is so lecture heavy and has a huge emphasis on remembering things for tests, so it was difficult for me to learn that way.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof. Fenn was a good professor she is very knowledgeable. Exams were lecture heavy. If you didn't go to class you would miss easy points on the exam. She used a lot of examples in her power-points which were very engaging. Her exams were fairly easy if you attended class regularly. Decent amount of homework, but it was easy and for participation.

she told us the exam was online open note, then 2 weeks later said we were "cheating" so changed the exam to in person. I hated going, I hate Kim. during exams her goofy little minions would walk around me and distract me when I was trying to focus. The questions would be like "In Friday's lecture we watched a video based where" I didnt go idk kim

You can do well if you don't go to class but will do better if you do. Lectures were very engaging and she uses lots of examples for each concept. Exams lineup with the lectures. A lot of people didn't like her and besides the occasional attitude I don't understand why. Lots of professors just read off the slides, she actually teaches you.

3 practice quizzes a week for homework, across 14 chapters on Macmillan Achieve. 4 exams with a final, class adjusted to in-person exams after reports of cheating, avoid cheating on exams. Very good lectures and readable/understandable slides for review. Overall the class was very enjoyable and you get what you put into it.

Prof. Fenn was someone who was blunt and did not have a ton of patience. However, a lot of the complaints against her are ones you won't deal with as long as you go to class and actually try to pay attention/do well. Tests were very consistent with in lecture material, and she does a great job explaining things.

the class started off good in the beginning and then it just went downhill. she would go off on tangents about herself and would not stick to the syllabus. her ego is so high it is torture listening to her. if you do take this class with prof. fenn make sure you go to lecture because most test questions are on stuff in lecture.

Professor Fenn is very educated but was weird and lowkey rude. She suspected our class of cheating on our first exam due to having a higher test score than her previous year (it was open note), so changed all exams to in person and no open note in the middle of the semester. I never went to class and ended up okay. Would've taken online instead.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Fenn was a great professor. Sometimes she could come off as rude but most of the time she was a very joking person. She is very open to questions and student participation during lectures. The textbook was $90 but you must buy it because it is what the homework is done through. Just study, go to lectures, complete the homework and you will be fine

professor fenn has a very rude demeanor and tends to put students down a lot when they ask simple questions during class. our exams were originally online but she changed them to in-person due to suspected "cheating" because we had a higher class average than previous classes even though it was all open-note. she seems to have an ego issue.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Go to lectures!! Her tests are primarily on her in person lectures so take notes and pay attention. You will not do well on her tests if you don't go to class. Other wise, the work load is no big deal and she's nice.

Professor Fenn is very educated, but she is overall super rude. She changed online exams to in person tests because she claimed there was cheating because we did better than her prior class. She is harsh when it comes to answering questions in lectures as well as picking on students who leave early. She makes very unprofessional remarks in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She doesn't follow what's in her syllabus and is super rude when she's asked questions in class. She changed our tests halfway through the semester, and her reasoning was the class average for the first exam was higher then the other years she taught it. I would try to take this class with a different professor.

Please do not take this professor, took class suspecting an easy 4.0 because it's a 101 class but professor Fenn has an ego problem. She is very educated however is rude and lacks logic, changed entire format of class over people 'cheating' on a online, open note exam, and then made it in-person, no notes to benefit us? Take a different elective
She was sort of mean when it came to asking questions in class. She has a bit of a short temper, and grading was kind of harsh. Class was VERY lecture heavy.
Snippy with questions so be careful about her short temper. She gets incredibly angry if you leave class early so watch out for that as well. Overall rude and unkind but educated.

Dr. Fenn was in all a great professor. Whenever I went up to ask a question, she was friendly and was willing to help. Although the exams were changed from being online to in person, they were also made significantly easier. As long as you pay attention and attend lectures, this class is a breeze.

Changed the class & exams to in person because she refused to believe we were smart enough to do as well as we did on the 1st exam (and this actually came out of her mouth), automatically suspected cheating and held this over the class for the whole semester. Also, she stated that the original exam was open book, note, etc.
Had exam's online to start the year then on first exam the average was "too high" so she suspected cheating and made all exams in-person

Good professor knew what she was talking about but was snotty. Exams were supposed to be online but she changed them to in person due to "cheating." Which idk how that is possible because they were open book, open note, open google, open everything, as she literally said that to the class. Also have to pay $80 for homework access.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Fenn was one of the few professors I had this semester that was very passionate and skilled in what she taught. Make sure you go to class if you have Dr. Fenn because the exams are pretty much based on the lectures. So as long as you go to class, ask questions, and study some, you will do well and the exams will be a breeze.

Professor Fenn is a decent professor. If you pay attention in class, study, and do your homework, you will be fine. Homework is easy. She's a good teacher, but sometimes she has a short temper. She will get mad at the class if she finds people cheating or not paying attention, so just beware. Very interesting lectures. Try not to skip class.

Prof.Fenn was very knowledgable, but is not very open to asking questions, and often responds in a rude demeanor. Homework and exams are online which is super nice. Homework is just taking notes from the textbook, and taking non-graded quizzes at the end. Very interesting information, and you will succeed as long as you put in effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: good professor very entertaining during lectures, a couple of homework assignments per week, a couple of exams throughout the semester, but overall very easy. the one downside to her class is that the required homework and book resource is $80.
Her lectures were interesting. Used a lot of videos that were interesting. Around 2-3 homework assignments a week.

Fenn was an amazing, well-educated, and well-respected professor. The only downside to the course is that you do have to spend about 80 dollars on a service for the textbooks and the exams. Course was easy to do well in as long as you manage your time well!

She's a nice professor, but the class lectures are just repeated info from what we had to read from the book (it was ~$80 and required). The textbook is a good read, though, and the assignments are linked to the textbook, which is really cool b/c you can find your answer in the section of the book without looking for it yourself. Not for exams tho
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Respected

reviews.comment: She was great loved her lectures but the exams are very long but she offers a lot of resources to help
Loved her. Respected. Interesting lectures.
I loved PSY 101. Super easy if you just go to lecture and pay attention with a little studying before exams I think I got close to 100% in the class.

The professor is nice. But the content is huge in this course. The exams are very lengthy and require a thorough reading of the book. She gets irritated if you do email the questions she addressed in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She's rude when responding to emails, doesn't give good lectures, and grades one of her assignments (personal statement) based on whether she thinks you would get into med school, even though this is a psych course and it's subjective. She's a harsh grader and unfair about it. This is the first prof I've felt the need to rate bc of how bad it was.
I took this class as asynchronous, but the lectures were easy to follow and the assigned reading was 100% necessary.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Took this class online. Every week you had to read a chapter from the textbook, answer homework questions, and take a quiz. Mid-term and final were based completely on textbook. Super easy. Extra credit is available, BUT MAKE SURE YOU GET YOUR RESEARCH PARTICIPATION DONE EARLY. Professor was nice, even though I never spoke to her.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I wish I had her in person. Yes, this is an asynchronous class, but her video lectures are great. She replies fast to emails if you ever gave any questions. Definitely have a textbook handy at all times, reading that book is what will help you in this class and on her assignments.

Professor Fenn was incredible in my opinion. Her lectures (although online and asynchronous) were extremely detailed yet so intriguing! She does an amazing job of explaining things. The exams were on the difficult side, however the extra credit offered is a great grade booster. I would definitely want her as my prof again!

I loved going to her lectures simply because she was just a joy to be around. She makes lectures fun but I have never been a fan nor really good at psychology but the nice thing is that the text book is available online. Just make sure you read, practice the material, and study for exams because that will help understand the course material to pass

You must participate in at least 7 hours of the psychology lab. This is demanding for busy students. You can get extra credits by participating in up to 6 hours of lab. So it is not easy to get extra credit. All the exam is a multiple-choice but doesn't mean it is easy. Especially don't recommend international students cos she speaks so fast.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Fenn covered a lot of content in easy to understand terms (lots of examples). She gave reasonable expectations and clear guidelines. She gets frustrated when people ask stupid questions and email her constantly, which is understandable due to her class size and research load. Would 10/10 recommend her for any course--amazing prof.

One of the best professors I've ever had. She is so nice and clearly states the do's and dont's at the start of the year. Lectures were almost always interesting and she gave a good extra credit assignment.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Class is composed of 5 exams including the final one of which you can drop. Exam questions come right from the study guides that are provided on D2L. Clicker points are used for attendance. SONA Research is required and extra hours can be used to raise your grade up to 3% overall. Exams are all multiple choice and have an extra credit question.

Honestly as long as you show up to class you're good for the semester. She's very funny and personable but can be hostile if she needs to be. She connects real life situations or examples to the material which helps. I'd take this class again!
Great Professor! I really enjoyed her lectures. Very fair tests if you go to lecture. Extra credit possible through psy studie hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Lectures and recitation are mandatory, however you are permitted to miss more than enough. Your grade is heavily based on exams, but the exams are fair and she always curves them (dont rely on curves). You can get up to 3 percent extra credit. Drops your lowest exam score, so I didnt have to take the final. Never got below a 94 on an exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: although she can be rude when asking dumb questions, she is a great professor and made learning psychology fun. she was able to make lectures engaging and is always wanting her students to succeed. she offers extra credit at the end of the course too which helps a lot!!

Good teaching style. She does take attendance using clickers. I haven't used the book once, it's online so you don't have to buy it if you do want to use it. Tests are easy, just pay attention to the lectures and clicker questions.

You don't need to read any type of textbook with her. she supplies study guides and if you understand and know everything on it you'll do fantastic on the exams. There aren't surprises. Questions are VERY easy on exams. Basically know the terms she gives you and know how to apply them to real life scenarios and you'll be set.

Clicker points - so you should attend class. Lectures are easy to follow and she explains things very well. Have to participate in research projects, but can also get extra credit by doing more than required.

Definitely makes lectures very interesting, but you'll want to read the material in the textbook to have a solid grasp of the info. She was very generous grading, everything on the exam was presented during the lectures at some point. Would definitely recommend her if you're interested in psychology.

Fenn structures her class great. There are 4 exams throughout the semester, but there is no final because of all the material covered. With that being said, the lectures are boring, but you basically have to go to receive credit for attendance and to understand whats going on. You also have the opportunity to receive 3% extra through SONA.

Professor Fenn is pretty funny and sarcastic sometimes. She does a great job at making her lectures interesting by using videos of real life situations that relate to the material. I would recommend her!
Easy professor if you study her powerpoints. Interesting presentations as well, good choice if you have to take this class.
Shes's pretty sarcastic and can be disrespectful of the students but she presents what will be on the exams. Will not change grades.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: I enjoyed Dr Fenn's class overall. If you're respectful to her she'll be the same to you. Her exams are reasonably difficult but she provides clear and helpful study guides and offers lots of extra credit. I liked taking this class.

I loved Dr. Fenn, I'd definitely take another one of her classes. You don't technically have to go to class but clicker questions count for points. She's really sweet and nice if you're respectful towards her and the TAs.
She talks very quickly and seems to have a desire to be the "cool prof". She wasn't terrible though.
Great psychology teacher! Lectures were interesting but attendance is mandatory. Tests are clear and if you take notes during lecture should do well.

She was a good professor and I would take a class with her again but her tests were exceptionally hard. her pacing is distracting but you get used to it really quickly. Her lectures are hard to take notes from I recommend going to office hours and the TA's.

She was an amazing professor. I absolutely loved being in her class and you can tell she has a passion for this subject. She provides so many opportunities for clarification, to ask question, and extra credit. She really cares if her students succeed and I felt her tests were set up so that anyone who paid attention in lectures would pass.

I was in the High Achievers Summer Program and she was an amazing teacher. Very interactive. Attempts to teach visually and in a hands-on fashion. Debate days were the best. Do the work, and you'll easily get an A
She reinforced my love of psychology.
Fenn was a great professor, she made the class very interesting. Always posted lectures online and answered any questions.

Dr. Fenn is a very interactive teacher that is friendly and funny. She gives you  multiple extra credit opportunities and asks if anyone has questions during lecture. She posts the lectures and study guides online. Overall easy intro class.
Mrs. Fenn is a wonderful, interactive teacher

Dr. Frilingos was by far my favorite professor that I have taken a non-animal science class with. He is extremely caring and respectful and truly wants to know what's going on with his students so he can help and support them along the way. I truly cannot sing his praises enough!

This class was the best class I've taken, Professor Frilingos is caring and understanding, truly cares about his students and really loves what he's teaching. He's very open to other ideas that people may bring up in class and he leaves room for in class discussion. The best professor I've had!
This is a professor who loves what he's teaching and you can tell, it makes the class so fun and engaging. Truly a caring professor and the best professor I've had at Michigan State by far!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Frilingos is such a nice and knowledgeable man. He learned everyone's names (I've never had another professor do that before) and seems to genuinely care about his students and their success in the class. Although the class was a bit demanding at times (mostly with memorization), I ended up passing with an A.

Frilingos created a phenomenal work enviorment for everyone in the class and anyone who does not rate him a 5 is trippin. Class was not mandatory but mostly all information was in class or online. The class was interesting on its own but if you can go to class a decent amount then you can easily 4.0, He will make you want to go to class.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Frilingos is one of the most kind, compassionate professors I have ever had and he TRULY cares about your education! He will meet with you during office hours and make accommodations within reason for you to learn the best that you can. If you pay attention in class you should have no problem getting an A. Great guy all around.

One of my favorite classes I have ever taken. Professor Frilingos is exceptional and really cares about is students and is very accommodating about late work. He is very straightforward about the content on exams and does not try to trick you in anyway. If you show up to class you are bound to 4.0.
Excellent professor topics were not very hard.

Dr. Frilingos is absolutely wonderful! He is clearly passionate about the material and very caring towards students. There is a required book in the first half of the class, but it is interesting and easy to read. Good lectures and great organization. Challenging, but not HARD. I loved this class!

Chris is genuinely one of the best profs I've had at MSU. He is so caring about students and make the expectations of the class very clear. Not only was he a good prof, but his class creates amazing discussions and things that you've never thought about or noticed before. He can make boring text from the book so interesting. I highly recommend him!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great class, attendance is not mandatory, but just show up to class and your bound to get a 4.0
Dr. Frilingos is passionate about what he teaches, and you will be too. I look forward to taking more non-major classes with him in the future.

Professor Frilingos is an amazing professor. He is so caring towards his students and he is so knowledgeable on what he teaches that it makes lectures so interesting and he allows you to see things in a new perspective. Overall, loved this class.

Professor Frilingos is my all time favorite professor. Professor Frilingos makes MSU a better place. He tells you exactly what is expected of you and I look forward to his class everyday. Participation is important but just to keep class rolling and entertaining for everyone. He sees you as a person and a student, not just a student.
Protect this man at all costs. Homework was not a heavy workload. Explained content and assignments clearly and thoroughly.

I had a ton of fun in this course and he was part of the reason why. Fun elective course, led by easily my favorite professor I've had as a student. Actually cares about his students, doesn't just say it, but shows it. He is the GOAT

Amazing professor. Genuinely cares about his students. One of the easiest classes I have taken, but was still able to learn a lot. I would recommend him to everyone. Most grades are participation, so it is very easy to do well in his class.

He was a very nice dude and I would recommend him. There were a lot of readings to do, assigned on Fridays, and due Mondays. I didn't love his papers, they weren't extremely difficult but I had to do a lot of deeper thinking for them. Overall, great class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Professor Frilingos was fantastic! This was one of the best classes I have taken at MSU. He truly cares about his students and cares about feedback. I highly recommend this class!

He's a genuinely nice person. Definitely cares about students and the material in class was well rounded and interesting. Was unbiased and seemed like he just wanted us to dive into the material and get our own perspective.

Great teacher, but he was somewhat monotoned so it was easy to get distracted. Readings are not that important as long as you pay attention during class. Homework is graded really easily as long as you put in a little effort. Nice guy and kinda funny.

Graded on midterm, final, 3 papers (pretty easy), and 7 homework assignments. This is my 2nd class I have taken with him and he is my favorite professor! Class is a lot of fun! Lots of small group discussion. Exams are almost all written but he tells you exactly what is going to be included. 3 books required but you can find them used for cheap.

This professor is an absolute gem. He starts every class with a deep breath and maybe even a subtle joke or two, which is helpful for me and my class anxiety. He explains things really well and uses timelines all the time to ensure we understand the importance of the time that we are studying. Even though his class is hard, he is an awesome person.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Frilingos is very educated and passionate. His lectures were clear and enjoyable. I found this class to be very interesting and I thoroughly enjoyed going. There isn't a lot of homework and if you study/take good notes, you can do well on the exams. I would gladly take one of his classes again.

Chris Frilingos is the greatest professor I've ever had. Do not take the class if you want to validate or disprove Christianity. Do take the class if you want to see the Bible from an academic perspective and/or want to take a class with a professor who asks how you're doing and actually cares about the answer.
