Frilingos is a certified OG.   He genuinely cares that he learns everyone's name and that you do the readings.  His exams are somewhat difficult but not bad by any standards.  You have to identify dates during the first section of exams, then define ID terms, then identify biblical passages.  The identification of passages is difficult so read

The course was interesting and Frilingos is really nice and helpful. The exams were difficult and all of them were "blue book". There is also a lot of reading in the course. You don't need to have all the reading done for the class it's due on, but you will end up needing to read most of it to study for exams.
Best Class Ever. He is such a nice, helpful teacher. I never wanted to miss a day of class. Pretty easy, as long as you pay attention and do your reading.

Fantastic guy. He has an interesting personality and sense of humor. He makes an effort to learn names and things about you. The class itself is very interesting and he makes it very easy to understand. GREAT PROFESSOR, GREAT CLASS

He's a nice guy and knows his stuff but he just goes so slow and talks so much its hard to actually pay attention. Half of what he says he's just repeating himself unnecessarily. It may have been because it was an introductory class, but I felt really talked down to in general, not academically but emotionally like we're all high schoolers.

Prof Garnett was kind and taught at a great pace. The class is straightforward; go to class, and you will pass. I recommend taking this class with a friend or making one in class for the in-class assignments. There is weekly homework except for the 3 exam weeks. It was simple and just extra practice class materials, mostly. Way better than BS161.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The professor is fine, but she assigns the most aggravating assignments ever. You will spend 2 hours redrawing THE EXACT SAME process with slight differences about 12+ times per assignment, on topics we were taught in middle school. We spent a month doing punnet squares. Dr.Garnett is a good professor, this class is a laughable waste of time.
Very solid professor, very understanding and caring.

There are a lot of assignments and areas to learn content and improve in this class. Prof Garnett explains topics very well in lecture and will be thorough enough for students to master content before exams. The exams were very straightforward and weekly homework assignments provided extra space to work on material taught in class.
BS 162 was made interesting with Dr.Garnett. There are in-class group activities for attendance. Exam reviews were very helpful.
BS 162 with Sara was a great easy class. Much easier than 161 in my opinion. Attendance is not mandatory but you will be working with groups during class so make sure you show up for them.

Attendance isn't exactly required but there's an occasional in-class activity which is graded. A few group projects but they take literally 20 minutes or less. This class was extremely easy, I took AP Bio in high school so I felt like I didn't really learn anything new. No weekly homework, just watch the lectures!

I wasn't sure what to expect with this class being online, but Dr. Garnett made it really easy. Her lectures are easy to follow along with, but they are mandatory. The short in class activities each lecture and homework assignments definitely help prepare you for the exams. Dr. Garnett is also very kind and always responds to emails quickly. 10/10

The class was all online due to Covid but lecture attendance was mandatory. She posts pre-lecture slides so you can be prepared for class. Group work in lectures is easy and you have a few outside group projects that are mostly through Powerpoint. The final was just a 3rd exam on the information from the last 2 weeks. Would take again!

Dr. Garnett makes bio really easy to understand, as long as you attend the lectures and participate in your groups, there's no reason not to do really well. She and her TA's also have a ton of office hours you can go to if you need help, and for online classes, everything is done using D2L, which makes it super easy to keep track of.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: great. only complaint is the ammount of online assignments. (usually 2 300-500 word asignments a week)

Prof made the class very interesting, and the material was relatively engaging. The assignments due before class weren't overly difficult, and they all prepared me for the exams. While he provides lots of help prior to exams, the exams weren't the easiest and he ended up curving due to low scores throughout the class. He is overall a cool guy.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Gabe is the goat. One of the greatest men I've ever met and easily my favorite professor that I've had at MSU so far. I will admit it's a higher workload than a typical ISB class, but if you aren't a bum it's easy. Sit in the front and talk to him! You won't regret it!
Great man, terrible exams. the study guide he provides does not help at all. if you dont listen or pay attention, youre doomed. a lot that he talks about he doesnt put on the slides.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Gabe is probably the best professor I've had at MSU so far. Attendance is tracked through half sheets which are things you write a bit of notes on and turn in, so bring a pencil and paper. There'll be around 3-5 online assignments per week. With the pop quizzes you can talk to your classmates so there's no need to worry about them.

He is an extremely harsh grader, if an assignment is even a minute late he gives you 50% on it. Also he doesn't tell anyone their grades, you have to talk to a TA who also never responds or emails, in person about assignment grades. You don't even get your full grade until the end so there's no way to make up anything.

Gabriel is a really good professor, but the class requires so much homework. Like a lot of busy work. The overall class is pretty good and focus on the importance of our environment. Just be prepared for an hour or 2 of homework before every lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Gabe is by far one of the best professors I have ever had. He takes the time to learn names and make himself available to anyone who needs help or just wants to hang out during office hours. He has great sense of humor and is genuinely so fun to have as a teacher. I was not excited to take this class, but Gabe made it so much fun and very enjoyable

Gabe's class is very homework heavy, not hard just A LOT of busywork. He spends 20-40 minutes at the beginning of class not really teaching much, just kind of messing around, then gets upset at the end of the semester when the class is behind. He also takes forever to grade things.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She was very strict about her grades.  She wasn't really good with technology either, so if something goes wrong it takes so much longer then it should.  Half the time you can't hear or see the videos either.

I usually don't write, but I had to this time. When someone asked for feedback, she said it was too much work. When I asked to make up a quiz the next week, she said yes, but when the it came she ignored all my chats and emails. When someone asked how to get graded, she put it in the chat for everyone to see. Grading is also bad.

Her grading makes no sense. She will grade you a 50% one week and a 200% the next. So slow at putting the grades in, a quiz every week, so much homework and writing assignments. The lecture is so boring and I never knew what she was talking about. You probably have no choice of another professor, so good luck.

Her grading system is ridiculous, she barely gives out extra credit even though on D2L there are lots of extra credit opportunities listed, and the lectures are so long me and several of my classmates fell asleep.

If you want to pass this class, here is a tip. when answering the field assignments, write essay length answers (or a large paragraph).
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Worst professor at MSU. She will give you points to act like "you're earning points" until the final test comes around, you get a terrible grade for no reason. I don't understand her thought process when she grades. I think she just wants to give people a bad score for fun. Thought about switching my major a couple times, if that says anything!!

Terrible communicator. I emailed her so many times over the semester begging to get assignments graded that I completed on time, it took her months. She constantly posted wrong dates on D2L and never corrected them, leaving everyone so stressed. The class was very boring, she just reads the slides and then ends class with a NEVER ENDING WORKLOAD.
The lectures were pretty boring, but the class in general wasn't too hard. She provides a lot of extra credit opportunities, and the exams aren't too difficult.

She had the dates on the quizzes wrong and I could not take them. She took a month to fix it and I had to take all the past quizzes in one day. She is a super harsh grader and does not explain why she took off points. She assigned about 15 pages of reading each week along with about 3 assignments a week plus a quiz. A 95% is 4.0 and a 70% is a 1.0.
She wasn't that bad. Seems like a nice enough person but very tough grader. She does offer EC assignments, so look out for those.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I loved Professor GT. I was really struggling in the beginning of the semester and I met with her to talk about it. She helped me feel so much better. Going to her office hours makes a huge difference as well. It is a heavy course load but that's partly due to the fact that they are preparing future teachers. I would take this class again!
Super sweet professor! Ended the class with over 100% because she gave several EC opportunities. Assignments went with the course and weren't super hard, quizzes were short and eas

I am shocked by her reviews, but my personal experience was very different. She offered several extra credit opportunities, and she gave us access to the assignment guidelines very early on, giving us plenty of time. The assignments weren't hard but also weren't just busy work. She was always sweet and encouraging in lecture, I really liked her!

I was confused by the bad reviews at first because my first couple assignments went great. Until recently, I think this is targeted towards the TA, Sylvia Lin, it took forever to get grades in. Now im scrambling and worried about the final. Despite doing all the extra credit assignments and being an honors student only taking this for an ISS sub

i dont dislike her but the layout of the course. you need a 95 to 4.0 and even a 93 or something is a 3.0. she does offer ec which is useful bc of the grading scale. the whole zoom she talks the whole time almost every class and i wish we had more opportunities to speak and expand our knowledge. very slow grader

levelle haunts me in my sleep just thinking about how she would keep up on a zoom call for 3 hours watching outdating facebook reels. grades on opinion and changes her mind every lecture. very confusing set up of how the course runs but if you have questions dont waste your breath asking because you wont get an email or she wont know the answer.

There is a week left of the semester and I still have an A in the course, and she hasn't graded me harshly yet. But there is a lot still not in the gradebook so I truly do not know my grade, even though I have gotten an A on everything, reading her reviews has made me a little nervous.

GT is by far the worst professor I have ever had. She only teaches online, but does not answer any emails, and does not show up to office hours. When you ask questions during the Zoom call, she often does not even know the answer.  She also uses outdated research and language in class. The only good part of this class was the TA, Randi Nelson.

worst professor ive ever had at msu.  she grades on opinion and changes her mind every lecture. the lecture is always the full 3 hours long and you dont get any skips. very unorganized and confusing, but dont email GT about questions because she wont answer! Will ramble about something irrelevent for at least 45 minutes every lecture.
Doesn't respond. Said she'd change my final grade but hasn't after 2 months. Quizzes based on her personal opinion. Marks assignments down with no explanation. Terrible

Class is run in an absurd way. We start class with a quiz then we have a three hour long lecture about the quiz material. Why would you need to revisit that topic you just tested on. Harshest grading I've had at MSU. Teacher does not follow up with student emails. Field assignments are excessively long and are extremely repetitive. DO NOT TAKE

Soooooo boring. The class itself was not particularly hard but her lectures were not engaging and her homework grading criteria was not clear at all. Had random participation assignments that you could only do if you were in class, but she would frequently forget to tell us about them on the days they were due. Wouldn't take another class with her.
LaVell

GT is a very tough grader. Her lectures take the full 3 hours and she goes on many tangents. A lot of assignments, most are not difficult if you do the readings. There are 2 exams that are each worth 26% of your grade. Exams are very subjective to what she thinks the right answer is creating a lot of second guessing. She also used a slur in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I felt this professor was a very tough grader. She was very picky about your assignments. It was also difficult to get ahold of her outside of class time. There was no leniency on assignments.
A 94 is a B. She tries her best so you won't get an A.

Worst class I have ever had to take. All material is subjective and based off of GTs opinion. I attended every lecture, turned in my work, studied for tests and quizzes and met with both the TA and GT outside of class and I still failed. Very harsh grader and marks you down for very specific word choice. Need a 70% to get a 1.0, 95% for a 4.0.

Grades are still barely coming in the day before the entire class closes and we can no longer see them. Everything is graded harshly. Emails are answered very, very slowly. Quizzes and exams ask more specific questions than what the material we read suggests. Overall, not a great class to take, especially when you need a 95% to have a 4.0.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Super specific about answers for homework and quizzes. class is always all 3 hours. she never knows what she is talking about and relies on her TA for everything. Never answers your questions and won't answer emails ever! she's a tough grader and attendance is mandatory! you get 1 excused absence. she takes 15 points per absence or early leave.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She is a super harsh grader and assigns SO MUCH work... but complains about grading any time you have a question. Lectures are super boring and she does not try to engage students. She never emails back. This class took up so much of my time this semester. Huge assignments every week, but I thought the midterm and final were okay.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Probably the toughest class I've had to take so far. If you have a question, they will respond in a very confusing way, they never give a straight forward answer. Her grading scale is so strict that you basically need a 100% on every single assignment if you want a 4.0, which is impossible because they are very nit picky about what we put down.

She does not teach anything in her lectures. You will find yourself crying because of your midterm grade. No matter how much you study there will be trick questions that you will get wrong because she personally believes they are wrong. Don't email her, because if she does it will be a rude response about how you didn't pay attention.

She is an extremely hard grader and it doesn't help that you need a 95% to 4.0 the class. She is rude and unprofessional. She does not answer emails. I waited three weeks for a response and was told she did not have time to address my concerns. Lavelle is overall and awful professor and everything she grades is based on her opinion of teaching.

Rambles on for 3 hours, touching on the actual lecture material last minute. Assigns busy work complaining about grading. Horrible at answering + getting grades back in a timely manner. Brought up unnecessary examples that was no support to the topic. Made T.A. do all the work. You may get stuck with her as I think she is the only professor for 320

She was difficult- not once replied to my emails, did not explain ANYTHING, huge project you have to do but has vague directions and doesn't bring it up a single time in the semester. Can't use specific words yet doesn't tell you which words, you'll learn when you get marked down for them. Videos in class are all VERY old, like her teaching style

The class itself was interesting and not difficult at all. However, the interpersonal communication wasn't there. She doesn't have office hours and she says she doesn't respond to emails about homework if it's within the 72 hours of it being due. She also said to allow her a 48 hour time frame for a response to any email sent to her.

Not a good professor, harsh grader and does not explain things in class or outside of class. Lectures are from the PowerPoints and are very long. Very harsh grader even when it comes to reflections about our experiences in our lab.

I have never had such a hard time in a class, ever. I got over the average score for every single assignment other than 3 and I still did not get a 4.0. Her grading is actually the toughest I have ever encountered and I would not recommend her to anyone. If you can take a different professor, do so because her average is a 2.6.

The class was not particularly hard and the quizzes were not too bad. The teaching team however was very unclear and hard to reach. I emailed them twice in the semester and they never responded. I needed help and clarification and they ignored me so I did not like that.

I legitimately cannot say one nice thing about GT. She is the worst professor. She grades extremely unfairly and gives so much busy work that doesn't prepare you for the exams that are ridiculous. She never replies to emails or helps us. Its nearly impossible to get above a 3.0 in her class unless you have a 95% which is impossible with her grading

Most inconsiderate teacher I ever had. For example, during finals week she assigned a final, a HUGE final project, and two other major assignments. Doesn't respond to emails. Grading scale is almost impossible to get above 3.0 because she is the toughest grader- you basically have to be perfect. Most overwhelming, stressed, anxiety driven course.

Very good professor, I got 100% on all of my assignments. This class was taught online this semester so I do not know how she is in person, but overall this was an easy A. I wouldn't recommend skipping class because we had "virtual participation" tracked six times throughout the semester where we had to answer questions after class and turn it in.

worst proffessor ive ever had unless youre taking the lab at the same time youre going to fail. lectures go til 9 pm and have quizzes everyday along with readings and so many assignments. Super unrealistic in teaching how you should act around children. Change your major before you have to take this dont make the same mistake as me
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The GOAT. 1of1 professor.

Love this guy! The class was very easy, and there were only 4 assignments the whole time. Guys, please laugh at his joke of the day it makes me sad when people don't! Kinda quirky, but a very passionate teacher and someone who would help you in the future in a heartbeat.
Such an amazing professor! Genuinely made every lecture so engaging and enjoyable. He is so funny and goes out of his way to accommodate and help students, could not recommend him enough!!
He is the best. He is so nice and funny. He genuinely cared about each student. The class was so easy too.
He is so kind! This was such a fun class and I would definitely take it again

My favorite professor by far! He truly cares, adds humor to keep lectures engaging, makes students comfortable asking questions, and is always responsive to emails, even if you're a former student. He truly helped me love the class and want to be there and engaged. You have to write your name on a piece of paper when you go, but it's not mandatory.
George is AWESOME!! So helpful and truly just wants the best for everyone

George is one of my absolute favorite professors. Super engaging with the students and genuinely cares about his students' learning. You'll have to study for his exams but nothing too difficult. Also a little bit of homework but totally managable
Very respectful and has your best interest in mind. 190 is extremely easy.

I took this class only because Professor Harnick was teaching it, he is the best professor Ive had at MSU! He is so passionate, entertaining, and funny. All the content is super useful and he makes it easy to learn. If he makes a joke, LAUGH!!!
GEORGE IS MY GOAT. BEST KIN PROFESSOR IN THE DEPARTMENT.

When I say George is the best professor you will ever have that is an understatement. He is super nice and is really excited to teach his students. His work does require a bit of work but everything he assigned was pretty easy. If you have a question he will gladly answer it and help you if you don't understand something. George you are amazing.
I fking love George Harnick.

THE BEST PERSON EVER. he genuinely cares about every student, and he knew everyone in my class by name. though I only showed up to like 5 of the lectures, I still managed to 4.0 the class. most of the material is common knowledge. he is really passionate about kin and make the class interesting. I would go out of my way to have him again.

George made the class fun. He is very caring and respectful. He is also a good source for any information, KIN related or not. The class is half a semester long so a rather fast-paced class, but you learn a lot. It is also a pass/no pass grade. Attendance isn't mandatory but highly encouraged to get all the information and they are also fun.

George is awesome. Teaches you so much about the world of disabilities in the lab, and he is extremely patient and caring about both the participants and the students. Top three profs I've had at MSU.

Very passionate about his job and kinesiology in general, which makes it easy to pay attention in class and makes you actually want to go to class and learn. He is also a great source of information if you have questions about anything kin related, he's a huge help and always willing to answer questions.

George is THE best, if he makes jokes just laugh. The class is kind of annoying because you lecture for almost 2 hours once a week and then have a gym class for 50 minutes twice a week but it's a requirement so just do it. The exams are a mixture of easy and hard.

He is one of my favorite professors here at MSU. You can tell that he loves what he does and is very accommodating to students. The class is easy and the labs are super fun! It is not hard to do very well. The only thing i wish was different was better feedback on assignments especially our presentations from the labs!

Super amazing professor. He truly cares about his students and is genuinely a great guy. The class is the easiest I have taken ever. I studied one hour before the exam and scored above average. TAKE THIS COURSE WITH HIM!!!!

This class is a bit annoying because the recitations are basically a PE class, but the lectures are good and the material is interesting. Professor Harnick is a great and passionate lecturer who cares about us learning the materials. The class has three exams but he gives study guides that outline what it is on. Grades are very attendance based.

Prof. Harnick is an awesome guy. He has a great personality and is always happy during lectures. He is always open to asking questions, and loves engaging with students. Lectures are important, but the textbook will help you if you missed anything. The lab for this class was also super fun, literally just a high school gym class. Fun class overall!

Prof. Harnick was by far one of my favorite professors so far. I never missed a single lecture and he always knew when to give breaks. You can definitely tell he is passionate about what he does and knows how to teach the content. Notes are the most important part of the whole class and before an exam always study the exam review!
He was one of the best professors I've had at MSU. He really enjoys teaching and is very clear when teaching material. He's also super understanding and is super nice.
He is a professor that genuinely wants you to be successful!

Professor Harnick is one of the best professors I have ever had. He is super funny and I enjoyed going to class. It is a note heavy class, but he makes it easy to follow along and understand. Professor Harnick is super easy to reach outside of class and usually responds super fast. Overall, one of the best professors I've ever had

If you don't know anything about philosophy this class can be difficult. He does make you buy a textbook but it helps with understanding the texts and you can use it on exams. You aren't graded on a lot: 2 midterms, a final, and random in-class activities to get you in class. But Dr. Grey is amazing, would take his class again in a heartbeat.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures are pretty boring, but if you're into sentience/consciousness topics you might still find the material interesting. Grading feels harsh, a 91% is only a 3.5 and you can't make up missed in-class assignments. Overall, interesting content but not the most engaging class or flexible policies.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of my favorite classes that I have taken! Dr. Grey knows his stuff and is willing to answer anything you throw at him. Weekly papers, but: Go. To. Class. Not only will this help you understand the content better, but he does random in-class activities which can help your grade. Not a Philosophy major but I will be taking more of his classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--So many papers

reviews.comment: no real assignmets except in class discussions and weekly deliberations which is easy. no quizzes or exam except midterm and final so u rly gotta study for those 2 but he makes a review to follow. great teacher love him
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: easy class as long as you actually study for the midterm and the final
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Don't take this class. He doesn't teach you anything more than the 274-page book does, along with the 4 other books in the class. You can't use outside resources to study because it's so based on niche books. He has 2 exams covering 60% of your grade. Also, the recitation pretty much exists solely to turn in your homework. Do not take this class...
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Grey is an amazing professor and knows what he is lecturing on, attendance is mandatory (especially for reviews before exams) if you want to receive a good grade in this class. Annotations every week which are about 1 page and are easy to write and definitely help you understand the material better, and he hand grades each one with feedback. :)

Professor Grey knows what he is talking about. The material is very difficult to understand without a philosophy background, and the tests are very difficult but Professor Grey dedicates a lecture to group study before each exam. Other than the difficult material, I enjoyed the class.

Genuinely the most boring class and professor. This class sucked. His exams were in person closed note (LA, SA, MC, TF) over lectures and readings. He has like 40 book requirements and all dumb/expensive. Readings are super long as well. Do not take him for IAH. There are way more interesting professors, classes that are easier for this credit.

Probably my favorite class I've taken so far, really interesting materials covered in a fun way. The class is super enjoyable if you're groupmates are also interested in the topic, would def recommend.

Prof. Grey has engaging lectures but if you zone out for even a second you are done for. Discussions in class aren't mandatory and attendance is taken via in-class group assignments where you record what you talked about. Fair graded papers and curved exams. Content heavy, you can scrape by without reading if you attend the lectures religiously.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I would highly recommend Prof. Grey. He's very understanding and genuinely cares about his students. There are random in-class assignments, but they usually aren't difficult. The exams are very fair (only midterm & final). There are lots of class discussions, but he never requires you to talk if you don't want to. Overall a great professor.

Really chill guy, good takes, spells out explanations well, but there is a lot of random quizzes and difficult problem sets. Attendance is not mandatory but you will most certainly be lost if you have a choppy attendance. You have to stay engaged to maintain a good grade.

He is, by far, one of my most favorite professors. He is extremely accommodating if you ask for an extended deadline, and he tries to meet the students where they are at when it comes to understanding. I genuinely debated on dropping this class when I had walked in the first day, but I am so glad I didn't.

He posted lecture videos which were extremely helpful and clear. I would say the textbook isn't mandatory, but it was useful to have for further information to write the papers. There were a total of 4 papers to write for the semester, but they were all less than an 800 word word limit. Overall, he's a nice guy and a very good professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great professor, love talking to him and learning from him. He keeps his classes interesting and I'd definitely recommend him.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: While he seems like a cool dude, I really did not enjoy his class. A majority of the points are in 10 assignments, which each weight about 5% of the grade for each question. So, if you mess up one you're gonna have a bad time. The course itself was fairly boring and I did appreciate his efforts to try and make it less so.

Professor Grey is such a cool guy that it almost makes this class worth it. He's sarcastic and funny and encourages debates. But it's extremely difficult material, with a problem set every other week and random in class quizzes. There's only a written midterm and final in terms of exams. A 92% is a 4.0 which is really hard to achieve

Prof. Grey's powerpoints don't take away from his lecture. The relevancy of his arguments and his snarky sense of humor keeps one invested. The clarity in his communication is evident in his concise syllabus, midterm survey, and when he allows students to bring in their own arguments to debate in class. Reach out for help and the class is easy.
She's a great prof, very sweet and caring. Would definitely take again, this class is a easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She's the sweetest! Her lectures were so engaging, I always looked forward to the days she was lecturing. I preferred her to the actual professor, and you can tell she has so much passion for the topic. I would take her class every semester if I could. Love her.

Sarah is so kind. Her passion for teaching really comes through, making lectures engaging and enjoyable. She's always open to questions. Quizzes are taken online without proctoring, and exams can be done from your dorm with a camera + mic on. You'll have discussions and engagement activities, but you're given at least two weeks, sometimes a month.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I loved her so much. So easy to talk to and ready to have a conversation due to her passion of the topics. My only complaint is the sort of bias she has on some of the subjects which I am unsure if it's related to ciriculm guide or her opinion. Overall, I would recommend this professor and course to all students

This class was awesome, super easy to follow and actually interesting to learn about. Such a great professor and I truly recommend this class to anyone. Attendance is taken randomly using iclicker. You have an assignment every week, but they're short. Final was a paper and wasn't bad to write at all. She's always helpful and answers all questions.
Great professor! Her lectures are interesting and the material is easy to understand. She is very easy to talk to and she responds to emails quickly. I recommend her to everyone!

Prof Griffes was a great professor! There were only two assignments weekly and usually a biweekly reading quiz, but you get access to your book and it's super easy. She likes class engagement, and attendance is mandatory through iclicker. Only three or four tests I believe and you can take them in person or at your dorm w a camera on. Great teacher

I respected Prof.G a lot, she is a very caring and listening person. This class is a really fun and easy elective class. She usually post study guides for exams, which are really helpful. There are ton of extra credits in this class to boost your grades as well.

She was an amazing professor,her lectures were very interesting and she does class discussions which help. The study guides are very helpful for the exams. She cares deeply about her students, and knows what she is talking about. She is also very flexible and she is open to talk about anything. Overall would definitely take a class with her again.

Amazing professor at MSU. Hw due on Sundays. Great study guides because everything on there is actually on the exam and does the review with the class helping. She knows what she is talking about. Interesting lectures. She gets to the core, essence, centre of the matter or subject. Exams are easy when you study!! I respect her a lot!!

He always knows how to make the assignments fun and interesting. He doesn't have monologue lectures. He plants seeds of creativity and lets students brainstorm and sprout that creativity. Mostly conceptual than detailed.

Gary is such a kind professor. I took ADV210 with him and it was the easiest class ever. Weekly group projects on concept development, 2 weeks per project, easily accessible outside of class and accommodating. Easy grader, just put in the work!

This is my third course with Gary and he is BY FAR the best, most genuine and caring professor I have had at my time during MSU. Great feedback and is super knowledgeable. He's a great professor but an even better person.
He is one of the most laid back professors I've ever had at MSU. He is extremely flexible with due dates and is really passionate about what he teaches.

HE IS the most understanding and caring human EVER. Most of the class is small assignments that you present. He gives great feedback on what you did well and didn't, so on. He doesn't post in D2L though. PLEASE take him if you can/have to. Great guy and good to have in your life. Attendance is encouraged since that's where assignments are discussed
great teacher

Dr.H is funny, and stays after class to help people and is open to it. I asked to talk with him because I missed due to illness and he said no, thought that was weird but otherwise chill and communicating through email. I failed the class but I'd def take Dr.H again!

He uses tophat for attendance which you can do remotely. Overall if you attend lectures and recitation, understanding the content is really easy. Exams are a huge portion of the grade so you have to prepare for them. There's some extra credit built into the homework too.
Really well taught course. Dr. H is a good professor and makes this course easy to understand. Pretty easy 4.0 if you go to lectures and recitations.

He was a great stats professor. If you go to recitation and go to class everyday, you will do great! One thing that annoyed me was him letting people go early which was super distracting to people like me who stayed all of class. Overall, super easy math requirement with only 3 exams and a lot of extra credit offered to bump your grade 2.5%.
He is very nice. As far as a Stats class goes this one wasn't bad. He does a lot of things to help you succeed so just stay on top of your work and study for tests and you will be fine.

This class was sick! So to start off im one of those laid back chillers but me and hairy long lets just say we live on the wild side. To start off were both diabetics and we would wake up and shotgun dr peppers like dawgs and I swear me and the boys would be buzzing off the peps like no other. Will Long is a lifetime friend.Class is mid ngl.
He was a good professor
Great professor and very personable, as long as you pay attention in class, attend the recitations, ask questions, you'll do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Go to his lectures and do the tophat participation questions. If you miss them, you can make them up the next week for full points. Would have probably gotten an A instead of B if I showed up to all his lectures

Friendly professor for STT200, down to earth and tries to make his lectures fun. Class becomes fast paced after first month. One hw assignment a week (hr to complete) and one recitation a week. Complete both and attend lectures and the exam material will come to you very easily. 2.5% grade boost for extra credit

Going to class is important because its easy to fall behind. Lots of extra credit. If you show up to lectures and recitations and do your homework and extra credit you will be fine. Tough grader however, Would not round me up .2 to next highest grade which was frustrating but like i said there is a lot of extra credit opportunities getting me close

He is a quick teacher, and there is a note-pack. Attendance is kind of mandatory since he asks questions on TopHat during the lecture, but he also has makeups for the attendance open for a week after. To succeed, stay on top of homework and do all extra credit after completing homework.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment:
Don't need to go to class can do top hat makeups from home and still receive credit. 
Complete extra credit and an extra 2.5% is added to final grade. 
Course isn't hard go over lecture notes, he moves pretty fast.

STT 200 exams/curriculum is all the same but Dr. H is the right professor to choose. He is kind, funny, and very clear in lectures. Make summary sheets to make sure you remember everything. Extra credit can up your grade by 2.5%. Only one homework assignment/week. He posts all the lecture slides & info so don't worry about missing a few classes.

Dr. H was a very nice and friendly man, but I felt like his lectures were too fast paced and at times he didn't fully explain the material. I would go to study for exams and would teach myself the whole exam material the week before the test. The content was not difficult it just took time to learn. There are many resources to get help such as SLC.

Dr. H was a great professor and super down to earth. This class was so easy it literally just requires you to do the bare minimum of go to class and do the webwork. Lectures are only 50 minutes but are 3 days a week so it's a little annoying but nonetheless, super easy class and exams are EXACTLY like the study guides

Overall, I found Dr. H enjoyable to listen to and learn from. If you follow along in the textbook/workbook, you will not have a problem taking his class. The homework is almost entirely things learned in lectures, and he posts the lecture notes after every class in case you miss a lecture. I would recommend taking Dr. H.
I just could not understand the way he teaches. He would bounce around from page 30 to page 120 in the span of 5 minuets, lectures were all over the place.

Super clear lectures, accent isn't thick at all, don't worry! tests were hard, 3 exams, all non-cumulative which is good. Class gets really boring though. DEFINATELY recommend the printed lecture notes, you WILL need them.

Overall the course was easy as long as you attend lectures and get top hat points. I landed 0.33% below the 4.0 grade. I emailed Professor H and he proceeded to give me a 3.5. Just thought he would be more caring then that so make sure to do all extra credit homework assignments, may save you in the end!

Dr. H's lectures were a bit fast paced as the stats classes have to adhere to a strict schedule, but he'll post the lecture slides after and there's a stats help room as well. From what I heard, he's one of the stats professors who tends to cover content slower in comparison to the others. If you complete all the extra credit, you get 2.5% boost.
Dr. H is awesome!! He made the material very easy to understand, and he just is such a nice guy. He was my fave professor this semester. Would highly recommend!
So sweet and great at explaining. Every thing is so straight forward and coincides very well with the exams and homework. If you have to take STT200, make sure you have Dr. H.

Dr. H is a fantastic professor. His lectures were super easy to follow, there was not a lot of homework, and the 3 exams were super easy. You can tell he really cares about what he teaches and is always willing to help. If you go to recitation and class, it's an easy 4.0. There is also extra credit that adds a whole 2.5 percent to your grade.

This course requires a lot of time and effort, especially if you get stuck with a bad recitation teacher. I recommend going to the SLC if you're struggling because that was the only way for me to pass since the lectures were fast-paced and confusing. Exams are similar to practice exams and study guides, but the questions tend to try to trick you.

Overall, Dr. H was quite good, but occasionally his accent made it difficult for me to understand the material. Also, the lectures were fast-paced, as he tried to cover a lot of content along with the TopHat questions. However, the recitations were very helpful and managed to review most of the lecture material in just one hour.
Not a very organized lecturer. Rushes towards the end of lectures because he's on a timeline and things get lost in between. Overall, an easy course tho.

Dr. H was a fantastic professor for statistics. Attendance is mostly mandatory, there will be participation questions throughout the lectures but the points can be made up with extra credit opportunities that come every week. Very helpful and knowledgable. He basically gives you the exams beforehand so preparing for them is extremely easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. H's classes were amazing. He would randomly talk about little stories he has (mostly about his cats) and he would teach the content very well. Show up to Classes and Recitations, do the HW with the Extra Credit and do the practice papers before exams (they are EXACTLY like the actual test) and it's an easy 4.0. I ended up getting a 101.5%.

If you're taking STT200, defo take Dr. H. I had a great time in his classes and he's very funny, always talks about his cats. Show up to classes, recitations and do the homework with the extra credit (extra credit is actually more helpful than you think) and it's an easy 4.0. Also, the practice exams are EXACTLY like the actual exams.

This class was overall very easy. Lecture attendance is mandatory as there are multiple participation questions throughout each one. Review sheets before exams are identical to exams. Overall he is a very nice guy and often makes jokes.

Very good professor who teaches statistics very well, you have to go to class to get points every week but the lectures are only an hour long. There is weekly homework that isn't very long. If you go to the lectures the class isn't very hard, Dr. H is also a nice guy so the class doesn't get boring.

His classes are too fast, leaving little time for the in class and timed questions, which require calculations and written answers. Only 30-60 seconds per question makes it tough to keep up. Lectures are less helpful compared to recitations. Good beside that. Exams 2 and 3 are harder.

Good prof, probably the best one for intro statistics. Ensure you get a physical copy of the coursepack at the beginning of the semester. It makes things so much easier. Go to all the recitations/lectures and you will get a good grade.

My favorite professor thus far! He's a very personable guy, and I felt very comfortable in his lectures and always made sure to go to them. He was a very clear teacher and made sure to explain everything thoughly. When they print out the notes and bind them it's super useful, and I 100% reccomend! He really made me enough learning stats!

The class isn't easy and imo very boring but it was a box I had to check off, if I could go back in time I would have showed up to more lectures as it's very easy to get behind.  Show up to every recitation or you it will negatively effect your grade and you cannot make up points.

Go to lectures or you'll fall behind and be confused real fast. Exams were like the practice exams and study guides. Homework was confusing but do the extra credit homework first and it'll make it easier plus that is the only extra credit you will receive so that helps boost your grade

Dr. H was a very straightforward, fair professor in my opinion. As long as you do the homework assignments and show up to lectures/recitations, you will get a 4.0. He provides a "study guide" and "practice exam" before each midterm and final, which was almost exactly how the tests were on exam day. Those were super super helpful.

Great professor who explains things clearly. With each homework assignment there is a corresponding extra credit assignment with unlimited attempts, meaning you should do the EC before the actual homework to ensure you get a good grade on it. Exams are fair. He WILL hold you right up until the end of class with Top Hat questions so be warned

this class was honestly easy if you paid any attention to the lectures, showed up to recitation, did the homework and extra credit, and then did the practice exam before the real one. I have never taken stats and did not think this class was hard. When he talked too fast I would go to SLC tho just to get extra help.

Professor H was a very good teacher. He explained the material very well and was extremely responsive to any questions you had. It is clear he wants students to do good in the class and succeed. The homework goes with what you learn in lectures so make sure to go and pay attention. Do the practice exams to help you prepare for the exams.

There are many things your grade will count towards (ie. recitation activity, tophat, webwork, exams). The content itself is not too hard I'd say as long as you go to the lecture and do the notes you should be fine. However, the professor was too fast-paced and did not want to slow down when asked.
everything was easy but makes you second guess yourself. grades are based on 3 exams, top hat in class, webworks, and recitation activities.

I loved this class and that was definitely because of the professor. He's always prepared and has a positive attitude. I thought the layout of the exams was easy to predict because of a lot of preparation materials. One of my fav classes I've taken!

He talks SO FAST and I don't know a single person who could keep up with him. Lectures are mandatory to get TopHat points. The notes they recommend you buy don't follow the slides super well. He's a nice guy but the class is so chaotic and I never have a clue what he's talking about. Exams are a carbon copy of the study guide though.

Exams are worth 70% of your grade so beware. They were somewhat difficult but also exactly like the study guides. He was a fast talker and I relied on having friends on the class to do homework and pass overall. He is super nice and tries to connect with his students.

He reads off of slides that he creates himself with one example done in class on TOPHAT. 30% of the class will get the correct answer and he assumes the class has the concept mastered due to the 30% getting it correct. He does not give adequate time for exams either.

He often teaches in a fast paced and confusing manner, and without having prior knowledge of stats I would have been lost. He is kind and professional, randomly discusses his cats which is nice. Recitation TA answered any questions I had. Quite a lot of material is covered, Middle of semester most difficult, I wouldn't recommend missing lectures.

My largest problem with this class occurs in the middle of the second semester. I took this class in fall 2022, and halfway through the second semester the professor around the Thanksgiving break the classes jump around the coursebook and introduce some of the most important parts of the semester which got very confusing and tanked my grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you did well in AP Stats, you'll be totally good for this class. Class is kinda fast paced, but there's just a lot of material. I had to study a lot on my own. Prof is nice and funny and good at lectures, talks about his cats a lot. Tests are very important, but they're not too hard if you keep up with the class. Once a week homework sets.

He is a good professor. You should go to the lectures and PAY ATTENTION especially during the middle of the semester bc it has the hardest content. The practice exams are just like the actual exams, If you put in the work and understand the concepts you will get a 4.0

I took this course online asynchronous. It was pretty easy for me. There is extra credit that you can complete with every week of homework through webwork. The site isn't the best but not the worst to use. There were lecture videos that went along with the notes. As long as you completed everything every week, you should get a good grade.
Poor professor

I took this class during MSU's first full Covid semester. The lectures were immensely helpful for understanding the concepts introduced by the class, and the professor was sure to re-introduce concepts that became relevant again had we not used them for awhile. The structure of this course worked really well for me as someone with ADHD.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: took during covid and the course was taught by 3 professors. all were amazing! while the material can be confusing they do a great job of explaining and have help rooms with tutors you can go to. there is extra credit which is extra problem sets and seems like a lot but DO IT! very accommodating due to health/personal probs and want you to succeed
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I never had an actual encounter with my professor since it was taught online and a different professor was the one teaching in the videos we had to watch for notes. But if you have to take this class online, know it was very well structured and well taught. The notes are easy to follow and the practice exams are identical to the actual exam.

Do not take him for online math. I chose him as a professor because he was so well rated on here. He has been unwilling to help me during the entire semester. Every email that I've asked for help after explaining my notes and videos arent working, he tells me to use my videos or notes or go to the learning center. unwilling to adapt teaching style

Took online During Covid-19: For an online professor he is unhelpful and not willing to adapt teaching style to help those who really struggle in math. I eventually had to go to another professor in my online section to understand anything that was being taught. Asking him anything in unhelpful. Tells you to go to the STT center, use videos , notes

Due to COVID-19 My math class was switched to online. Harish Sankaranarayanan is the most unhelpful and rude professor I have ever had. Math is my worst subject and each time I asked for help through zoom or through email, I was met with unhelpful explanations and comments like "this is simple you should know this" and "I already went over this."

Dr. H is so understanding and kind! He genuinely cares about each and every one of his students and wants to see them succeed. After meeting with him once, he remembered my name out of the other 900+ students that he taught over the semester. I thought that was truly amazing. Dr. H is an incredible professor with some pretty great dad jokes ;)

He is awesome. I learned so much and was able to pass the class with flying colors. Attendance is taken by TopHat, but he has makeups each week if you decide to skip class. He was very easy to understand and made sure his students understood what they were learning.

Dr. H is my favorite math teacher ive ever had. Although the work can be challenging, he tries to do his best to teach. Class is 3x a week and uses tophat points, but you really only have to show up to 1 or 2 lectures a week to get the full credit. Theres also 2.5% extra credit if you do the practice webwork, so do it. Exams are same as practice!

He is not a bad prof at all the class is just so boring that paying attention is very difficult. Not paying attention makes the class harder than it needs to be. Recitation helps a lot. If you show up and listen you will be 100% fine
Dr. H is one of the best professors I've had at MSU. Gives you everything you need to succeed. The class was hard by its nature, but Dr. H worked hard to help his students. He is funny too.
Awesome professor! Clearly a great guy with good lectures. I did not 4.0 the class because of my own lack of effort.

Harish is so funny. His class is very confusing and theres are SO many pages of lecture notes. Webwork is super annoying but it ends up helping you in the end. Going to recitation every week REALLY helps. This class is an easy A if you study and go to recitation.

He really exceeds the expectations of being a good professor. I love him. He really cares for students to pass. Approachable, will help you any way possible, and very reasonable. The work is fair. The test aren't too terrible but this is not a class to slack. Have loads of respect for him and became one of my favorite professors

Pretty solid professor, he's very approachable and charismatic. He takes attendance everyday. The homework is large percentage of the grade, if you don't do it you will fail. The tests are pretty easy as long as you understand the basics of the material.

He truly wants ppl to master the course material and is willing to do anything to help students succeed. This is difficult in large classes like his, but he did an excellent job making it work. Always willing to meet & even adjust deadlines if I felt that I needed more time. OFFERS SO MUCH EXTRA CREDIT! I'm bad at math & he made this so easy!!

Not enough grading opportunities to accurately judge a student's ability on the subject at hand. His lectures also follow a random pattern with no chance to study correctly due to his lack of assignments and withholding proper study materials, such as the slides.

ISS 320 consists of three exams, 1 extra credit opportunity for 5 points (usually a random lecture midday on a weekday) Exams are poorly written, shows up late, gives indirect answers to questions, and studying effectively is nearly impossible because he doesn't post slides. Goes on unrelated political rants. Exams are opinion-based, not an easy A.

I was so surprised to see negative reviews on Dr. Hourani. He was truly one of my favorite professors, and while his classes are reading heavy, he is very patient and understanding. If you make an effort and have open communication with him about any problems, you will do well in this class. He is engaging and makes the class feel connected.

He is a good professor, but he expects a lot from his students. There are at least two readings per week (and I mean long scholarly readings). Most of the class periods are discussions so I would at least skim the reading because he does call on people. He is very knowledgeable but not the best at communication basically never tells you your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He is not a bad person, but not a great teacher. Never tells you your grades and the readings are impossible unless you have prior experience with dense readings 20+ pages long.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--So many papers

reviews.comment: Is this a professor for real or just a guy with ego trying to feel powerful and condescending on his student? I felt so abused in the class! Such low class IMO. Such incompetent's (teacher) I would say not a professor. Real Professors manners, they treat their student with respect and professionalism. We need better management in this "college".

I enjoyed Dr. Hourani's class a lot. I found the key was to take notes on any topic from the reading he mentioned in class. I thought he was usually pretty funny, but his exams were definitely a lot of work. The TA had given us a couple extra credit papers to write which def made the class easier.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I took a course in "Special Topics in Urban Design and Urban Regeneration" with professor Najib in the German Jordanian University, and he is wonderful! Gives a lot of amazing readings, holds great discussions, he is very competent, really knows his thing. He gives great feedback and motivates everyone to do their best. Definitely taught me a lot.

If you are looking into whether or not you should take a class with this professor, consider yourself lucky that you went to rate my professor first. I studied my ass off for this class and barely came away with a passing grade. This is the type of teacher that would read this review and be proud of it. Heed my words, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!!!!

This was by far the worst class I have ever taken. Exams are tough, and the only thing you're graded on the class is exams! This class is very lecture heavy, and there are rarely any power points to look at. The Professor easily gets off topic for most of the class and the concepts he is teaching are very hard to grasp. Do not take this class!

This class is called "World Urban Systems", so if for some wild reason that's what you're expecting, do NOT take this class. The entire course content is up to the jurisdiction of whatever Dr. Hourani wants to talk about that day & issues/history in the Middle East. Hourani seems ok, but he is very closed-minded to those with opposing viewpoints.

Dont get me wrong he is a good guy (hence 2 not 1), but the class quickly transitions from Urban Planning to being a class about his interest in Islamic culture and "relevant" topics in the middle east. He says you wont pass if you do not read, but if you take good notes on the review sheets you will pass the exams with ease. I would NOT recommend
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Not a good professor I recommend DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE THIS PROFESSOR!!! He is the worst professor I have ever had and that is not an exaggeration. When you think you've done everything you can do to pass, professor Hourani finds a way to write a test that you are not prepared for. I was an honor student, don't make the same mistake I did. (I actually took ISS 320 but it wasn't an option)

I took ISS 320 with him. The class itself was about "urban planning." However, rarely have we talked about any cities, and when we did, we only talked about Arab cities. He has mentioned several times how he is from Lebannon and that is mostlyall we learned about: Lebannon and the Arab world. Only grades are 3 very difficult exams. I HATE HIM!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: The reading load is heavy, and the exams are insane, but this class has been the best I've taken at MSU. If you want an old-fashioned course experience, take this class. No powerpoints or busy work, so if you want the easy way out take a different class. If you're interested in social & cultural theories  of anthro and you want to learn, take it.

I took his class based on the reviews here and he is a good teacher but he is kind of a dictator in the class. He gives lots of reading and you have to be ready for class discussions, the first exam was easy but the ones after that were hard. All I can say is keep up with the readings and GO to class most of the exams are from class discussion.
He's a really cool guy! But geography is not his thing. He should stick to antropology. Assigns a lot of readings and you have to be ready when you come to class, gives points for participation.
No Comments
He challenges you and makes you think. However, he's a bit scatter brained, quite disorganized, and often appears to be on the verge of a breakdown!

So kind and easy to reach through email. Wants everyone to succeed, and easy to 4.0 if you come to class and watch the lecture videos. She is very knowledgeable and helpful if you have questions. She makes this class run very well and it is not a class I ever worry about. Lockdown exams with no camera too and other assignments boosting your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Go through allllllll of the material, it is a lot, but it will help you because the exams are kind of tough. The exams are pretty similar to the in class assignments

Dr. Hedges is very willing to work with you, and she provides a lot of course materials for you to understand the material. I didn't do too well, but looking back on it, this is only because I was stressed with many other difficult classes. Dr. Hedges gives amazing feedback, make sure to attend class and office hours!!

The flipped classroom style isn't for me, but Prof. Hedges was great. Her assignments are very interesting, and you can attend classes in-person OR online (Zoom). Do the study guides, keep up with assignments, and learn the content well- you will get a 4.0. One tip: Make sure you have a good grasp of the material. Tests are application-heavy.

Dr. Hedges was amazing. This class was super helpful to my learning and it was relatively easy to get a good grade as long as you did the assignments and understood how to do the exams. The class is hybrid which is nice if you can't make it. Be sure to do the in-class assignments and weekly quizzes and you will be able to 4.0 this course.

best professor ive had in all my 3 years. i have never learned as difficult information and actually understood it as quickly as i have in this class due to the way this class is formatted. i got the highest grades on everything without having to study once.

Dr. Hedges is a great professor. She gives opportunities to fix exam grades and it is largely independent based outside of class. She uses class time where we can work together and ask questions on the content. The structure and flexibility is great. She is very clear about the grading policy. Definitely Recommend!

All content was online despite it being an in-person class. If you went to class, you just did an assignment that can be done at home. She stressed how much she was there to help, but when I politely emailed a question, her rude response was off-putting. Basically, she doesn't do anything. Her grading scale is nice and allows you to do well though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: GO TO RECITATION. you will regret it if you dont, as half of the time she gives away exam question answers and is super helpful. Do the in class assignment and the additional practice questions, as they are helpful on exams. However, you have to earn a "strong" score on all exams to 4.0 the class, which is a 32/40 on all of them.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: She does not help her students whatsoever. I have tried to ask questions in class and they never get answered. I have emailed her questions and she does not answer them. She made me feel worse when I told her I was feeling overwhelmed.

Only a few weeks into the course but reasonable, accommodating, and respects privacy/student autonomy. Treats us like adults. Exams online, no lockdown, no invasive tech. Difficult material, but I want to actually learn it when profs have personalities like this and trust in students. Kind, reaches out when she se's missing work, easy to access.

I love the length of the lectures. She is very knowledgeable and easy to talk to and contact even outside of class. I do feel as if her exams were harder than the content if you didn't understand the recitation and I think the exam times are too short. So many opportunities to make up your grade though

Dr. Hedges is super caring and makes a huge effort to get to know her students. I wasn't the biggest fan of how she organized her classes, but I still felt like I got a lot out of the class. I recommend going to her recitations though, if you take her class.

I had Dr. Hedges for both 301 and 302 and I loved her! The grading scale is a little weird but she has made it better and you have the opportunity to do corrections on all the exams. Also DO THE RECITATIONS! I didn't for the first semester and regret it greatly.

I can't speak for previous years but both of my semesters with Dr. Hedges in NEU have been really beneficial. I find the tests to be easier than quizzes but still good assessments of what was taught in lecture. Lectures are asynchronous and online-might not be everyone's strong suit. She was fun, lenient and understanding.

I really enjoyed her class and the way she teaches. Her class is online asynchronous with a once-a-week recitation where students work on a worksheet. She mades an effort to come around and talk to students one on one and did a pretty good job of clearing up any confusion. Her lecture videos are shorter and easy to follow.

Super clear and concise lectures. There are ~9 lectures per week spanning 5 mins to 15 mins so it's not that bad. The quizzes are exactly what was covered in the lecture. Exams are a bit harder I feel because they tend to reach a little further than the lectures but still doable. She does offer practice questions each week that help with the exams

I didn't learn very much about neuroscience after taking this class. It was like really easy. It was kind of a bummer, but I got a good grade out of it so... who cares? The professor was really nice and funny though.

Very nice and definitely knows a lot about the material, but this and NEU301 are not an easy 4.0. I never benefitted from the lectures but other people might. Recitations are absolutely necessary to have a grasp on the material.

She was okay, but her lectures are a bit messy, and the grading scale is ridiculous. Her tests are insanely difficult and barely have anything to do with lectures. Also, you barely have any time on the test, so if you don't know the answer right away, good luck. The extra credit offered is your only chance at a 4.0.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very, very easy class, with a very good textbook. Probably the best textbook I've seen. Exams can be kind of tough, but they're all open book and open note, so you can Ctrl+F your way through them a lot of the time. I didn't feel like I learned a lot, but it was still a fun class, and definitely a good introduction to neuroscience.

I enjoyed the format of her lectures, but the lectures weren't very helpful for the exams, so what's the point, ya know? The recitations and bonus assignments helped much more. I was glad that 0.5 was added to your grade at the end if you completed the extra credit every week, but everyone needed that 0.5 because her grading scale is ridiculous.

Dr. Hedges is very nice and tries her best to help students understand the introduction to NEU. The exams can be constricting for time, and sometimes confusingly worded. She allows for exam corrections for 1/2 credit on missed content. With a fantastic method of grading and a good course structure, a good understanding of basic biology is required.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Hedges is very kind, and easy to contact outside of class. However, the criticism is within her grading scales for her classes. I understand the objective, but it makes it significantly harder to succeed in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Good not great. People leaving 1 star reviews must just not be able to handle the material, because she wasn't that bad and it was very straightforward on what needs to be done to succeed. 5 star reviews also confuse me. Overall pretty good tho.

I love neuro and admittedly did not struggle with the subject matter, but I can only imagine how much more miserable this class would have been if that were not the case. Dr. Hedges is truly terrible when it comes to communicating expectations and does not offer students equal levels of assistance. I would avoid classes with her at all costs.
She is awesome

Dr. Hedges is a great professor. The lecture material is all inclusive and the weekly quizzes are not that hard as they could be. Neuroscience is a difficult topic regardless of the professor. Dr. Hedges challenges you, but if you put effort into understanding the material and be ready for exams - easy 4.0. She gave extra credit & exam corrections
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I really enjoy Dr. Hedges, she never fails to make me smile even while taking this class remote. The content is hard, but she lays out the information clearly and provides review questions throughout lectures. It is a hard class but she manages to make it bearable. As long as you study and keep up with lectures you'll do well in the class.

Dr. Hedges presents information in a clear, easy-to-understand manner, but you will need to put in the work on your own to fully comprehend it. She made the online class more enjoyable with weekly discussion boards, and while there is a weekly recitation, quiz, and self-assessment, these help you to understand the material much better.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Switched my major because of her. Extremely difficult tests. Weekly recitation, quiz, and self assessment. Would not recommend at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Bad explanations could teach material better

Neuro is difficult but Dr. Hedges does NOT make it simpler to learn. She is a great person but bad prof. I studied for WEEKS prior to exams and still failed due to trick questions & harsh grading on the short answers. Multiple choice options can range from A to J with some saying "B, E, and F are true" or "Only A and G are true." A nightmare.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I love Dr. Hedges and would recommend her to anyone! She's very funny and straight to the point.

Dont blame the prof for the material. the class is not a walk in the park but it is so interesting and if you put time in to it you realize how well she explains and prepares you. she gives daily extra credit and if the whole class is not doing so hot she gives out even more extra credit. shes cool and just wants people to learn and do well.

Sometimes rude. The type of professor that only cares about a select few students. I always go to office hours but still have a hard time. Goes out of her way to make most questions on her exams and quizzes very difficult. Like others have said, even if you know the material by heart, the wording of her questions is very frustrating.

Exam questions are too difficult, even for someone who visits office hours and completely understands all of the material. This also applies for the weekly quizzes. She definitely needs a reality check and realize that we are undergrads and not phd students.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She does not know how to create fair questions for an intro level course. Even if you understand the material, her questions set you up for failure. So many friends of mine spend hours studying for her weekly quiz and still end up confused. Don't let the grade averages fool you. I hope she is either replaced or learns to create fair questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I didn't really like her, he exams are tricky and confusing. she grades really heavy on the short answer questions. shes rude sometimes when answering qts. her quizzes are hard too, their is one every week. I dont recommand her at all. weekly quizzes are 18.5% you can drop 2 only out of 14, exams are 61%. donttakeher. I wish I didn't take her
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I did not really like Dr. Hedges. She tries very hard to be relatable which can be annoying, but her class comics are funny. The way she grades isn't very friendly to bombing an exam. I didn't even fail the first exam but to get a 4.0, I had to get all the rest of the points for the rest of the semester. Her exam questions are also very confusing.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Dr. Hedges is ok at teaching I dont fall asleep while I'm in class so that should say something, but her exams are ducking hard she words it in really hard way no matter how much you study eithr you understand it or you're ducked, her study guide is objectives which is annoying. 61% of your grade is exams and barely any extra credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Wonderful professor. Content can be difficult and although the book is not essential, it's recommended for good reason.

Dr. Hedges is so absolutely incredible I literally don't have enough characters to convey it. Probably the best professor I've had at MSU. She's very new to the university but She. Knows. Her. Stuff. Probably the best hire MSU neuroscience has ever gotten. Exceptional and effective teacher and wonderful human being.

This was Dr. Hedge's first semester teaching here, and I WORSHIP HER. She is super fun in class and loves to incorporate memes into the presentations. She keeps it simple, even when the material is difficult. All the info you need to know is on the slides, so no extra note-taking needed. Much extra credit. DOES NOT BELIEVE IN CUMULATIVE FINAL EXAM!
He was a very chill professor. He just requires attendance to do well as the lectures aren't typically posted. If you go to class you can easily 4.0.

His class was very interesting, and he was a really great professor overall. Make sure you go to every class; he gives extra credit, which lets you skip the final at the end. However, his exams are worth a lot, so I recommend taking many notes during his lectures and reading the book.

Honestly, Doctor Hefner is AMAZING. If you do your homework, study, and go to lecture there is no way you would not be able to 4.0 this class. Also, just be honest with him he is so accommodating. He enjoys what he does and he loves teaching/talking to the class. The only way to fail this class is on your end, he wants you to succeed. TRUST ME.

Teaches anthropology and made it fun! Exams are a little difficult but if you study and go through the study guide as well as some of the topics/practice questions from the smartbook you will do well. It is more lecture heavy but the content taught was not boring because he didnt make it boring.

(ANP441) Dr. Hefner was an amazing prof. Took the class for an Hbio selective and loved it! He's very understanding, caring, and easy to reach. Gave pretty clear study guides before the exams and lots of extra credit! I hadn't taken an anp class before and had no issues. Go to lecture and study the bones/ their characteristics and you can 4.0!

Dr. Hefner is an amazing professor! His lectures were great and tests were reasonable if you paid attention in class. I normally won't go to class if attendance isn't necessary, but Dr. Hefner made his classes very enjoyable and worthwhile. This class is the reason I'm picking up a minor in anthropology!

Very good professor gave credit towards the final for showing up to class. He can be a little difficult to get a hold of outside of class and his office hours and location were wrong in the syllabus. Overall easy class and the professor wants the students to succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Hefner is very charismatic and funny prof, his lectures were very engaging and fast paced. He posted the slides for the most part and the class is exam based. Exams were comprehensive and a little challenging, I would advise to take notes and READ THE BOOK to prepare. He did offer many extra credit opportunities if you attended class.

Funniest professor I ever had. I wish I took this class sooner because it made me fall in love with anthropology. He's passionate about what he does and it made the class so much better. This class was also extremely easy. Def take it with him.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Respected--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Hands down one of the funniest and most caring professors I've ever had. My major isn't even close to Anthropology I was just taking the class for my Natural Science credit through the honors option.
