I had little interest at the beginning of the course but by the end, Hefner made everything super fun. I also recommend taking the honors option.

Awesome prof. Very interesting, knows a ton about forensical anthropology (he goes to crime scenes and such). Drops the final if you get enough attendance points. Exams aren't that hard. Gave out a lot of extra credit (usually for showing up on snowy days and such). Just go to class. Won't have to put really any other work than showing up.

I am a business major and knew nothing about anthropology but this class is very easy and extra credit is provided for even attending class so definitely try not to miss. Also it says to get the book , but save yourself some money and don't bother, all the material in the exams is taught in class.
Dr. Hefner is the best! He gives the best lectures, and his class is never boring he is so funny! Plus if you do bad on the exams he will give extra credit if you need it!
Great professor!

i love this man. the class wasnt hard at all especially if you have taken anthro classes in the past. he is the funniest man ever and legit decided to make things extra credit on a whim it was great. i wish he taught more because i would take every class.
Loved his class, the material was very interesting and fun to have. He likes to connect with students and really does care and understand.

My favorite professor at MSU so far! He is so funny and keeps kids engaged. Go to class though! It's so worth it. The lecture slides matter and you need to be in class for those. The project is so fun! And try to get the honors course, smaller class and more engaged.

While you don't really need the book, the tests are very lecture-heavy. It pays off to go to class instead of just looking at the slides online. Hefner is very hands-on and loves projects, but he does make them fun and very open-ended.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Hefner is definitely one of the best professors at MSU. His class was the absolute best class I've ever taken. The content wasn't too hard if you study before the exams and he covers everything so you know exactly what to expect on every exam. Another bonus: he's hilarious and really cares for his students.
Dr. Hefner is great! Ive taken him twice and hes very easy to work with but also very straight forward.

By far my favorite professor at MSU. He was so passionate about anthropology and even made me want to change my major! He always asked for our views on some subjects and never judged. I recommend reading the book and taking notes before class and add anything he says in lecture that you did not get and it will be an easy A.
He is such an amazing professor. He incorporates humor into his lectures and keeps students entertained throughout the lectures and labs.
Great guy, material is hard

Great lectures!!! I was entranced the entire time and this class made me change my major to anthropology. The class was long because it was only 2 days a week but he gave us breaks and the information was so interesting I just had to know more. Go to class a lot of extra points are earned via top hat and hints for exams. Best professor!!!

This man is a beast. Great teacher in a really interesting filed of anthropology. Very funny and makes his lectures worthwhile. Attendance is not required, but for everyone attendance quiz you participate in you get extra credit.
Honestly an amazing guy that I hope to have again. Not the easiest 4.0 but it's attainable for sure. DO NOT BUY TEXTBOOK!

Tough to stay focused in big lecture classes but Hefner tries to make it entertaining. Haven't fallen asleep yet & that says a lot. Lots of side jokes and awkward (but 10/10) humor. Makes the best out of a required class. Hard to do well in if you're a bad test taker like me.
INSANELY funny guy! He makes taking an ISS class more than a chore. Be ready to get out of class early and play lots of ping pong. GO TO CLASS! it is a good time.
Although the topic isn't something I would choose to study, he makes it enjoyable and is very funny, which you probably wouldn't know if you don't go to class.

The grades in the class were based off of 4 exams, plus a small percent of attendance. If you pay attention and take notes, you"ll get good grades on the tests and you'll pass. 1 extra credit opportunity this semester, you had to go to a zoo for it. Hilarious wonderful guy, lots of helpful anecdotes. Would have 4.0ed if hadn't skipped at all
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Hilarious--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Lectures can be boring at times but are very interesting for the most part. Professor is hilarious and can keep the class entertaining. He is very relatable and understanding. No homework assignments besides reading. 4 exams make up the entire grade(plus some extra credit) and they aren't too hard. Would definitely take this professor again.
Took ISS 220. Good class, good professor. Show up, pay attention, get a good grade.

Super interesting guy and doesn't make the class too difficult. He identifies with students but still maintains professionality. There aren't  many homework assignments and if you went to class when others didn't you were often rewarded by hints for the exams. Fair grader and a super interesting class!

Pretty standard freshmen seminar class for BLD students. Attendance is mandatory but the class meets once a week for 50 minutes so it isn't super demanding. I honestly did not buy the books but instead read them online but if you miss a reading it is not a big deal. The exam is not difficult, and neither is the class. She keeps it real.

This course was very test heavy but as long as you study the Learning Objectives you will pass very helpful for questions but the sheer intensity of so much content can make the course difficult if you don't invest enough time into the course which is stressed on by the professor. She seems to love the field and constantly updating the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: It is graded on case studies and section quizzes. To be quite honest, focus well on the learning objectives and it will be fine. Some of the material is difficult, but many of the facilitators were helpful and so was Dr. Hoag. study well and you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Hoag is a good professor at heart, but she heavily relies on you to do your part as a student. If you are looking into graduate school, she does help prepare you. She is not easy whatsoever and there are times where you may feel frustrated, but she does it out of good intent of making you into a smarter and more dilligent student overall.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She's definitely strict, but I enjoyed this class and thought she did a great job teaching.

Only a 1 credit course, but Dr. Hoag is very approachable and makes use of the class period helping BLS majors prepare for the 4 years. She gives very good advice and it is a good class to take. Easy to do well and you might learn some good tips.

Dr. Hoag is a pretty good professor. However, in class you only go over the learning objectives for that day's lecture video with your group and rarely get to hear her explain things in class. The section quizzes are not bad if you study for them. The final was worth 40%, but it is very similar to the practice final exam that she gives you.

I think the other reviews paint Dr. Hoag in a bad light. The class is very doable if you complete the course schedule exactly how she created it. Watch the lectures, do the learning objectives, do the readings, and you will be fine. This is the kind of class where if you put in the work you will be greatly rewarded.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The last part of this class was very interesting, but the rest was not. You have to watch a lecture before each class and take notes, and then come to class just to discuss the learning objectives with your group. The textbook was MUCH more helpful than her lectures. I wouldn't recommend this class. Take something else.

All I have to say is get ready to be on your own, lecture is mandatory, but guess what its useless, she just preaches and then you work with ur group for the rest of the time. you have assig and quizs due, there is one final it's worth a lot and cumulative. She doesn't care about students. if you ask her questions in the class make you look dumb
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Hoag seemed to "preach" too much in class, talking down to students or belittling them. I would have appreciated the semester more without her consistent underestimation of students abilities.
Too harsh of a grader.

GIVE THIS MAN A RAISE!!! Content & exams are seriously tough, but most of the grade comes from pre class & in class assignments & the project. Show up every day and you'll do great. Dr Haut is an amazing & chill guy who TRULY cares about his students (more than any prof I've ever had). I highly recommend him and getting to know him in this course!
Super hardass over a 2 hundred level course.
Super smart guy. Will give you high-quality detailed advice so make sure you pay attention.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Very helpful with content questions. The in-class assignments aren't too complicated and I learned a lot.

so nice & genuine! really thorough w answering questions, i had a great experience w this class even as someone that doesn't like cs :) one thing is that the ML unit is quite hard, and it's common to not finish the in-class activities (although they are graded for completion). the exams are lowkey rough but they're not heavily weighted.

Anyone who rates him poorly probably never asked him a content question. He will sit with you and help you through your work. His explanations are clear and he is very thorough in his descriptions of the content. Very kind and engaging guy.
Not ideal teaching format

Professor Hamann is a nice guy, and he seems to genuinely care about the course and the people taking it. However, he isn't always very clear, and a lot of the time, when asked a question in lecture, he would give a kind of non-answer that wasn't too helpful. The class itself wasn't too bad as long as you keep up, but it's very test-heavy.

This class made me want to jump into the Red Cedar. The assigned chapters and office hours are necessary for comprehension but honestly make a study group if you want to pass the exams. The class average for most exams was around 60% and the curve applies after the final. He starts out with quantum mechanics so be prepared. Good luck
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: CEM 181H is a tough class, but he does his best to make it accessible. A lot of self study because homework is light.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is a shy professor, but very passionate, but he is too shy that sometimes he is not very helpful

It's a very difficult class, but he is a very good teacher and passionate to teach. It is quite test heavy and be ready to study a lot, but he's really qualified to teach it and explains it to the best of his ability

The issue I have with Dr. Hamman is that he cannot teach well at all. I am confident that he is a fantastic chemist, but he taught in such a way that made lectures, homework, quizzes, and tests incomprehensible to myself and everyone else in the class. I studied, I went to office hours, I asked questions, I came to every class, but nothing worked.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The course material was very interesting, and the readings required for each class were interesting and the content was expanded upon in lecture. Biweekly writing assignments were short and helped with my comprehension of the reading, and her feedback was very helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Dr. Harris is a very intelligent and interesting lecturer. I found tests pretty easy but there was a lot of writing. 3 books but you really only had to read the one for the final paper. She is really good at facilitating conversation and I learned a lot from this course.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Harris was such a nice lady. At first, the material might seem like a lot and the pretty much all written exams might seem overwhelming, but she tells you exactly what she wants you to know for the exams. I hate reading and barely skimmed for her class and still did well. You do have to go to class though, but she lets you miss 3 classes
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Harris is a great professor. She is very flexible and understanding. The material in class is very much interesting and you learn so much every class period. She loves engaging with her students during lectures. Exams are not hard because she gives out study guides with all the material you should know. I got a 3.0 and I almost failed 2/4 exams

I loved her class! I thought she had really awesome lectures that were interesting and informative. The tests were very to the point and she didn't try and trick us on any questions which was fair. I really had a good experience in her class.

I LOVE SARAH. Best class I've ever taken. She created a fun, supportive, and playful environment where truly everyone was welcome. I felt like a little kid again; every class was like recess with my friends.

Nothing bad to say here about Sarah. Easily one of my favorites, because she cares about all of the students. Theatre classes are always fun as long as you participate and show that you care. You need to be in class every time, though, but that's to be expected.

I love Sarah! She is always so fun and a great teacher. She is there to help support every single student in her class and makes sure that everyone has a good time. She really understands the course and how to help each student with it. I'm never concerned about failing her class or confused about any material. Overall amazing!

One of the best classes I've ever taken!  Never felt like class, was super chill and a great time.  Knows what students like, and gives feedback in a way students can learn and grow from them.  As long as you participate and have fun with it, you'll enjoy it.  Would take any class she teaches in the future!
Sarah is the best
she slays:)
Sarah is very clear with both teaching and feedback, she will also challenge your skills and push you further as an actor.

She is not that bad. Sometimes she can be a little unclear or confusing, but overall it doesn't really matter since she grades so easily. Like a month into the class she started saying we can just talk in English for most discussions so that we can have better comprehension. She's also pretty kind and funny.

She's not as bad as the other reviews say, but she's definitely not great. Her class is pretty tough and there is a lot of online homework and participation is required for every class, but she's pretty leinent on grading essays, assignments, and there's no proper tests. She can be a little bit rude, but will give you help if you ask for it.

Definitely the worst spanish professor I have had here. She is unclear with directions, not understanding of personal life events, and makes fun of her students when they mess up pronunciation or spelling. She does not make learning this language enjoyable. I have considered no longer minoring in spanish because of her. Do not recommend.

This professor caused a lot of stress and frustration for me and my peers. She was very unclear about expectations during class time and would frequently make people feel uncomfortable by singling people out for not understanding what she thought was basic knowledge. She would also make fun of/mock our language skills and culture.

Easily the worst Spanish teacher I've had. She was consistently passive aggressive and abrasive with students and always had a condescending tone. She also often contradicts her own directions and is super unorganized.The class was easy but I learned so much more from things I did on my own outside of the curriculum. I do not recommend her.

She contradicts herself constantly and doesn't understand why students are confused. She will criticize and single people out, creating a judgmental and passive-aggressive environment. 
The class itself is fine. It's department-run so there is a lot of online homework. Being in class often felt redundant and pointless, but attendance is necessary.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Her class itself was fairly easy, but she would give unclear/confusing instructions a lot of the time and get frustrated when we didn't understand. Also, her attendance policy is very confusing, and even when you are sick with a doctor's note there isn't much leeway.
Don't let these other positive reviews fool she is very passive aggressive, shifts blame, and not helpful at all.
Very difficult to deal with. Doesn't seem to understand her own attendance policy or her attendance form.

Very easy class. It is somewhat like high school because she will stop the lecture to tell someone to put way their phone. Very strict tardy policies and will even mark you late for being 30 seconds past. Other than that the class was easy and repetitive

Her SPN 202 class was a breeze tbh. The class had a lot of hw but besides that as long as you attended every class (besides your 3 skips) I don't see how you couldn't 4.0 because she gave ALOT of leeway on written and oral exams. A lot of people in that class probably couldn't conjugate into the past tense and she didn't really care Take her class!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Irma was a wonderful professor to have for my second semester at MSU. Her class was more tricky than I expected, but it was still one of my easiest classes that semester. She was an excellent teacher AND an excellent person, and I would love to have her as my professor again!

This is more of a warning for the actual SPN 202 class. It's department ran and a teacher is placed to teach the course. This means class will never be canceled as they can just find a replacement, skipping class requires filling out a form and all the homework is accuracy based. As for Bartolo, she's a nice person, just strict on participation.
Profe Bartolo was great! Would definitely recommend for students starting to learn spanish.
great professor
She was amazing. I almost wish I had to take Spanish again so I could be in her class again. If you need to take SPN 201 or 202, take her class.

Like any language class, you have to participate to pass the class. This isn't a negative but don't expect to sit quietly the whole sem. There is a lot of busy work, composed of short quizzes for reading comprehension. These are extremely redundant and, in my opinion, insufferable as a weekly thing.

I did enjoy this class, Irma was nice & funny and wasn't afraid to go off topic. A couple negatives were that there were a lot of assignments that were very time consuming & sometimes hidden, and some seemed opinionated but there was only one "right" answer which she refused to see otherwise, some directions were also confusing, but otherwise good

She is a really understanding professor that is supportive in the students efforts to learn Spanish. Shes patient and pushes participation to improve learning. The class is focused more on understanding in context rather than memorization.

Only graded on hw, in class assignments and attendance. No huge finals, just short speaking & writing tests that are about 80 -100 words. You are allowed 3 free absences and then everyone after that deducts 1% off of your grade. She really pushes for participation! The class is more conversation heavy rather than grammar. Overall great profesora!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: All around great professor. Super nice and lectures are interesting. I really enjoyed WRA 101 with professor Schweitzer. The semester consists of 5 projects and are relatively easy and manageable. The final is just a reflection essay.

She was such a sweet professor, very organized and clear. Pretty homework heavy because of the 5 papers written over the semester, but she does try to give you as much work time and flexibility as possible. Overall the class was enjoyable, and she is very easy to talk to.
She is the sweetest. 4 main assignments are essays so prepare to write about anything you like.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Isle is sweet. I have bad anxiety but she doesn't randomly call on someone to answer. If nobody answers she just answers herself (THANK GOD)! Since this is a writing class prepare to write tons of essay's. Overall it's an easy class because you can write whatever you want.

Ilse's WRA101 was an easy and sometimes even fun class, you do 4 projects over the semester but you are given a ton of time to do them as well as peer review phases. Ilse is also very kind to the class and an approachable person. The lessons were fun and interesting as well (especially the conspiracies one!) and we visited a few places on campus.
She is okay, her class is very boring and it is hard to miss cause you are constantly doing a new assignment or paper.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed this course and professor! Assignments are laid out very clearly and are open enough that you can write about something you care a lot about. Final assignment is a remix, changing one of your first 3 writing pieces into another format. 8am was a rough class time but overall worth it. Easy 4.0.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I had her for BS 162 + BS 191H. I can confidently say that she is a fantastic professor and very caring. In BS 191H, she gives tons of feedback and really wants you to do well and understand. Now, BS 162 was genuinely one of the worst classes I have ever taken; however, its MSUs flawed curriculum, not her "lack of expertise." Take 162 at community.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Going into lectures was just a waste of my time, we didn't learn anything and she just gave us busy work. She isn't very good at teaching. All class lectures was online that you had to watch before class. The exams are half online (proctored) and half in person, they were pretty hard. If you have a background in bio then you will be fine.

In my opinion, the professor is very wholesome. She deeply cares about students. The thing is, this class has tons ( REALLY A LOT ) of assignments, and missing a few affects grade by a significant percent. Moreover, Dr. P speaks in between in class assignments and Iclicker, sometimes, which kinda makes it difficult to focus in class.

if you have the choice, don't do this class. She is very sweet and wants to help, but is just horrible at teaching. When you do the in class assignment, she doesn't shut up and just rambles. tests are very difficult, and you have 2 quizzes every week, and you pretty much have to teach yourself the material.

I had high hopes for this class. This is a confusing class, the lectures are all online and when we go in person we don't really learn anything. She is very kind and knows what she is talking about, she just can't explain it. There is a lot of other stuff in the class that helps your grade so it is hard to fail.
Never take her if you have the option

she makes everything super confusing. This class is taught horribly and you're better off taking it through community college because it is difficult to understand. The class is basically online and the Task give 5 different answers to the same question.

This is the worst set up class I have ever taken. All learning is done by yourself, outside of class. Class time is reserved for group work with people who don't even show up and when they do, no one knows what's going on so it's the blind leading the blind. There're 8 million worksheets to complete. Just thinking about this class infuriates me.
bruh actually worlds most disorganized class is the world. this class is causing me acne. took calc and chem, those were easyyyyy compared to this. bio should not be this confusing. awful class bro

She is very kind, caring, and knowledgeable. She can be a harsh grader, but as long as you follow her grading criteria, you shouldn't have any issues. If you have concerns, don't be afraid to talk to her. Even if you want to talk about something unrelated to class, she's great for conversation.

Dr.P was super caring and sweet, she was knowledgeable and willing to help as well, the course work was just so much in so little time. I felt as soon as I finished one thing, something else was waiting for me right away. Attendance is for points and mandatory, along with everything else BUT there are a lot of drops and curves.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class comes with a LOT of homework. We would go over SO MUCH content in class that it was hard to complete everything in the 80 minutes we had. There are pre-class lectures/activities, questions, and quizzes. Then, in-class lectures, Iclickers, synthesis & prep, FR quizzes, and tests. Super nice and a great teacher, just a large course load.

She was not helpful. Even though every member of our group put in the same amount of work, she picked favorites and made mean and upsetting comments to the others.  Her classwork is also very confusing, theres 10 documents each week that are given to you in different platforms and very hard to keep up with.

At first Dr.Pavlova made class confusing. It was taught different and she graded class off of "learning objectives" However, as I was in the class longer I saw her as an amazing professor who was easy to reach and really cared about her students. She just wanted to think outside the box with her teaching and it was a bit confusing but fun!

I took the honor's version of this lab, and I had an amazing experience. I had a seizure about a month into the semester, and she was so caring and helpful. Even in general she was very lenient with deadlines as long as you talked to her. I got 100 on all assignments without any difficulty. We had a connections project, but that was fun and simple!

Really caring professor, but the course wasn't set up great. A few times different instructors would give me different answers for the same question, and mostly everything is learned outside of class by yourself. Also the exams make up half of the overall grade so if you do bad on any of them it's a lot harder to get a good grade.

If you don't absolutely have to take this professor, DON'T. The class was taught poorly and although the professor was nice, she wasn't helpful by any means making poor analogies to answer important questions. 3 quizzes a week, online. Don't absolutely have to buy the textbook but it is a helpful study tool. Don't recommend the class overall.
Her Directions make no sense and everything needs to be done perfect to get a fine grade

She was very sweet and helpful when answering questions, but the way this class was set up, you teach yourself. She taught this class along side John Stolzfus. The class average was close to a 2.5 but do you think they cared?

Very patient and explains concepts well. Great professor overall, but the course had some quirks to it that were frustrating such as the RIQ's not giving feedback and the in-class quizzes and in-class activities weren't balanced well for the time given for each. I appreciate the drops being available for grade improvement.
Bro is chill to talk to
Truly one of the all time GOATS. You are lucky if you happen to get him. Cares about his students. If you go to office hours he WILL go out of his way to help.

OH I hate him. He is not undeerstanding at all. He doesn't even know the course policies of his class. He is not what is seems like in the class. Very reluctant to help in the name of "being fair to other kids"

Miralavy has been assigned to restructure and teach most of CSE232, and thank god for that. He has completely swept in and saved this course from the depths of hell. Miralavy is an amazing professor and lecturer, everything is very clear, and is just all around a great guy. If you have the chance to take any of his courses, do it.

Professor Miralavykomsari is amazing. Aside from being a super nice person, He's also has passion and is knows a lot about the topics. Grading is projects and exams. As long as you pay attention in lecture and review the slides then you'll be set for the exam. Projects are slightly covered in lectures, But they may take more time than expected.

Prof. Ingersoll is the platonic ideal of a professor, role model, and citizen. Sometimes—if you're lucky enough—you have the privilege of spending time in the company of a truly great mind. She is a genius. Nay—a visionary.  She slays. She sashays. She commits to the bit.

Lectures were online and when we had to come to class once a week we basically did nothing. Exams were difficult but she was generous with the curve on the midterm. Lectures went into tons of detail and exam questions were really specific (barely any guidance for studying). Loved her as a person (very sweet) but disliked structure of the class.

This was an extremely hard class and Dr. Ingersoll made it impossible to do well in it. She was not concerned with how terrible the class as a whole would do on exams and blamed it on our poor study habits. The study guides were so long it was impractical for the students to know all the material. I wish I didn't take this class for my PSYW credit

This was the worst professor I have had in Psychology. Her mentality is that there's nothing wrong with her tests, the students just need to study harder. The tests were impossible and made up a large part of your grade and the literature review was extremely hard to do well on. I would not recommend this class to anyone.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Ingersoll is great. She is incredibly passionate about her job and that shows in her lectures. She also gives great feedback on papers.
Not a fan, lockdown browser tests that record you. Exam questions poorly worded and makes the exam harder than it should be. Pop quizzes for attendance.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Overall good professor who cares a lot about class engagement. There are in person checks (10 of them) but homework isnt too bad and exams are online but are proctored. Not that hard of a class if you make somewhat of an effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I absolutely adored Professor K! She genuinely cares about her students, and she wants everyone in the class to absorb as much as possible. She asks for a lot of answers from the class, and interacts with everyone during the entire lecture. She makes clear power points, and posts everything before class starts. Loved her!

Best prof ive had so far for criminal justice. Her material was interesting and she kept it engaging. Her exams were based off the study guides, as long as you paid attention in class youd get 100%. Passed all exams and homework. Attendance is mandatory, but you can get excused if good reasoning. 10/10 would take again!
Random attendance checks
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Take notes during her lectures and use that to double with her slides while studying. She uses her own version of her slides to base her exams. She appreciates participation and will ask frequent questions about the slides and she goes through them. Pay attention and study for the exams and you will have no problem.
Great class, she gives great lectures and they are easy to follow. She gives review guides for every exam. If you go to class and do the readings you will 4.0
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Best teacher, and easy to pass
Cool dude

I am a terrible math student and I was really worried about taking a calculus class. He does have an accent but he explains things extremely well and if you come to class and ask questions he will make sure you do well. Like I said I'm not good at math at all but I did the homework and came prepared and got a 4.0. He's also really nice and funny!
He has a russian accent, but you get used to it. Very funny at times and loves to teach. Goes quickly so be prepared on the first day.

I don't know what everyone else is talking about. His accent is not bad but he is a terrible teacher. He doesn't explain anything. Last class I asked whim where a number came from to which he replied, "I put it there". Unless math comes natural, stay away from Ignat!

Ignat is great. If he sees that you are actually trying and participating in class you will get a 3.5 or 4.0. At the end of the year I got a 91 on the final and an 88.5 in the class and he rounded up my grade to a four point! He is a really great guy and anyone in 124 should take his class!!!

I took his calc 2 course this summer. It was extremely fast paced, and very time consuming. Ignat really pushed all of us to do our best, and I feel like I learned a lot in his course. His summer calc 2 course was very difficult, and I feel like a lot of people will fail, due to the course load, not his teaching (much easier in sem). Great teacher.

Good teacher, highly recommended. Slight accent but very nice and helpful. Go to class and you will ace the exams and quizzes. He really wants you to succeed in the class and it shows. Most people got a 4.0 or 3.5.
overall, pretty good teacher.  cracks a joke here and there.  Off the boat from Russia, has a slight accent.easy to understand.
Good proffessor, gets you thinking well.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Directions are somewhat confusing for projects, get ready for random tangents during lecture, but overall really solid prof and solid guy. Just not my cup of tea.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Caring

reviews.comment: Dr. Record genuinely cares about his students and passionate about the topics he teaches! His course is also very easy as he allows retakes on all assignments until you get full points. Take him if you have the chance!!!

Dr. Record is the best!! He is very nice, understanding of absences when you can't make it to class, and will always provide help when needed. Sometimes, there is a heavy amount of homework, but the assignments themselves are never particularly challenging; they are just time-consuming. I would definitely take a class that he teaches again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Caring

reviews.comment: TAKE DR. RECORD!! He's so awesome and very sweet. His class is a little bit boring if you aren't fully interested in the topic, but he is very gracious and understanding that you have a life and are probably in harder classes. I would do anything to have his class again!

I took LB 326A over the summer-not lb118! 
Extremely manageable workload, be ready to read and write a lot, HEAVY on discussion posts. BIG group aspect in this course. He takes feedback very seriously and wants you to succeed. Great prof overall and very passionate about philosophy. Take this class if you can! Do your work and you'll succeed.

This class was harder than my calculus and organic chemistry classes. I had more work in this class then all my other classes and it was because of the professor. He isn't clear on due dates and gives you so much work to complete. If your schedule is already work heavy, I don't recommend taking this professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: There is a lot of writing and homework in this course. Also, some things can be kind of confusing, like a lot of our readings for homework were never talked about in class/didn't really align with what we were learning. I do like Dr. Record as a person, though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: He's super nice & the class is easy to do well in, but the lectures don't relate to the projects. He tends to go on tangents about readings that don't get used. Lots of homework/reading that doesn't align with projects & project guidelines are confusing/contradictory. "I don't care how long the paper is if it's between 100 words to 10000 words"
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Dr. Record is very nice, but he definitely has some flaws. The requirements for assignments are unclear, and the out of class work can be brutal. Prepare for lots of reading that feels like it shouldn't belong on a computer. His lectures don't relate to the current project, even if it does the information is contradicting to what's provided online.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dr. Record is a fantastic professor. He is incredibly considerate and prioritizes learning over simply grading. While he's not always the most organized, he's constantly communicating with students so you'll rarely feel out of the loop. Dr. Record promotes self driven learning and teaching and helps students develop skills needed for the future.

Such a caring and approachable professor. He truly wants the best for you and offers lots of extra opportunities to raise your grade. His lectures were incredibly boring but is such an easy grader. Would take his classes again 100 times over.

Absolutely loved Record. His deadlines are all pretty much soft deadlines and he cares way more about your mental health than getting things in on time. He genuinely cares about you as a person and is actually really funny. He uses lots of group projects, and besides that, doesn't grade a whole lot of assignments. Would take his class again

Honest and caring professor however the lectures and the assignments typically do not coincide. Class instructions sometimes conflict with online instructions for group projects but he would always respond to emails in a timely manner to make sure everything was resolved.

Even if you do exactly what he wants, word by word, you will not get an A. He refuses to give out 100% and can never be reached in time. His lectures have NOTHING to do with anything you do in class, but since attendance is mandatory, it is a waste of time. There are projects back to back with no clear grading criteria and hundreds of assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: Dr. Record was an amazing teacher considering that he was teaching a completely online format. He was extremely well-organized and made sure due dates were very obvious. As long as you keep up with the assignments the class is an easy A. Would highly recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. record is a really good prof. He listens to the students concerns and helps a lot. I would love to have him again. Be prepared to do projects without a ton of guidance. You are welcome to ask questions, but also be independent.

I absolutely adore Dr. Record! He's my favorite professor and his lectures are always super interesting and insightful. His class is very engaging and he always makes sure to check up on how we're feeling which is appreciated.
Love Dr. Record! Really easy class, just show up and participate a couple of times. The group projects can be a pain but if you complete them with effort this class is an easy 4.0
Great professor. He really cares about students and explains things clearly. The class is fair, and he's very understanding, but you still learn a lot.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He's a super kind human. Always thinking of his students before the class. He is also willing to let you turn things in slightly late and gives opportunities to redo assignments! There is one group project, and you are required to discuss it with your base groups in every class. Overall, it's a super easy and engaging class!

Most teaching classes are easy to get a 4.0 in and so was this one. I do not think this professor is permanent, he is a Ph.D. student but I hope he returns to MSU as a full-time professor. He is funny and understanding of students' lives outside of academics. Show up to class and you will get a 4.0.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of my favorite professors. Very effective at teaching Arabic, but if you don't actually want to learn, don't take him because you will fail. If you care about the language and the material and you put in the work to learn a second language, he will help you succeed. Also he's hilarious.
As long as you are passionate about the class you will succeed. Saddam is the best professor I have ever had.
Class was easy, but never graded my final and other assignments even after emailing him and talking to him in person. Assignments were turned in on time and done correctly
A really nice professor and was very interesting to listen to! Lots of participation needed in class and must attend all classes. He was very fun and easy to talk to and get to know.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: One of if not the best teacher I've ever had. He takes something as difficult as Arabic and somehow makes it easy to learn, and is engaging at the same time. This is the kind of class where you have to want to get better at, but the prof gives you the resources to be very successful. also he is hilarious

Dr. Sadam is the BEST professor I have had at MSU. He is funny, kind, smart, accommodating, respectful, professional and by far, has such a good heart. He really cares about his students and whether or not they are excelling/ learning the material. He encourages and helps students when they need it. His teaching methods are fun and effective.
He was a great professor very funny and takes into consideration a lot when it comes to this class especially with the homework and the assignments due. I would 100% recommend!!

Lori is SUPER nice but I genuinely have not learned a single thing since coming to this class. Every day I go to her lectures I stare at the slides and have no clue what's going on - and I think this is because she didn't create any of the material she uses. The workbook makes no sense and half the time doesn't even correlate with the slides.

She is a nice lady, but her lectures are all over and hard to follow. She doesn't show much energy, has bad slides, and overall the class doesn't seem full ready to be taught, since the course book isn't complete and HiTa isn't set up. Be prepared to learn the material yourself.

Very nice teacher but it's impossible to understand what she's teaching. The notes packet doesn't match the slides and I can't tell what she's trying to get across most of the time. First exam was easy but after that it's downhill.
Absolutely loved this professor, she is so sweet and genuinely passionate not only about accounting but also about her students' success. By far one of my favorites, especially for a required course.
I don't know what I was taught the entire semester. The teaching wasn't on point.
Beware! Didn't have a nice time doing this course. Kinda difficult to understand what is taught.
Great professor, content can get challenging at some points.
Super nice professor, online exams, attendance not mandatory, one group project at end of semester. very caring. Curved everyones grades at end of semester.

Don't take this class if you don't like discussion. Most boring class I've ever taken, we literally talked from the start of class to the end and I don't think I gained any knowledge whatsoever. As for the professor he was great! Just not a teaching style I could learn from.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Every once in a while you run into an absolute gem during your academic career. Prof. Lamb wasn't just a phenomenal professor to me, he was a kind-hearted humanbeing. Extremely understanding, very open-minded and was super receptive to feedback given to him. One of the best professors I've ever had. Take his classes, I promise you won't regret it!

I actually took ENG 280 with Professor Lam and it was pretty easy other than sometimes the quizzes were a little difficult. He based the class mostly on student discussion which was a little frustrating because I felt like he should be doing the teaching that my tuition pays for. There's mainly 3 quizzes 2 essays event reflections and participation
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: We had to do weekly discussion posts and replies which weren't too bad. Prof Lam was really understanding and gave great feedback throughout the semester. My only complaint was his lecture videos were quiet and his quizzes are a little hard

Lam is a great professor and he's very smart but the entire class is him talking. It's hard to even stay awake for the full time we're there. He's very knowledgeable and I feel like I'm learning a lot. A lot of the grading is by one single group project and attendance.
Josh is a good guy, and is very clearly knowledgable about the source material (which is very interesting). He makes you think about the material you consume in a new way.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Josh is a great professor! He made coming to class really easy, even though it was fairly early. He connected with almost the whole class, and I still talk about how his class has been my favorite class and I took it last year.
O.O  O.O O.O O.O O.O

reviews.comment: James Mariani and Betty Cheng are the power couple of the year. Keep flirting with each other and teach the class. Age is just a number
Chill guy, but wears an Italy hat every day, so 4 stars.

Other Professor: How do I make my students ace the exams?
Mariani: How do fail half of the class and get away with it?

even russian professor are better than him
The class is brutal just like the broken US Immigration System. Where Mariani is the border char and Cheng is the people who lost the election
Betty Cheng likes Mariani, which is the Tea for this semester.
Was more interested on Rizzing up Betty Cheng.
Love Betty Chen and try to attract attention to her what a romatic story suggest play TS love story
Make sure you have a good insurance policy that covers therapy sessions because you will need it.

I would have gotten a 3.5 or a 4.0, but prof mariani did not excuse my 2 quizz absences caused due to HOSPITALIZATION VIA BURST APPENDIX. For both the absences I had documentation from 1. The emergency room 2. The hospital. Due to this I lost 12% of my overall grade (6% each quiz) and got a 3.0. Do not reccomend him.
hard class
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: CSE 380 is very poorly structured. Pop quizzes/tests make up 85% of the grade with no homework to build up to them. Grading is extremely stringent and includes no feedback. Projects make up a tiny fraction of the grade despite being the most involved part of the course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Took this class Fall 25, it's really not that bad. Go to his office hours (not the TAs, they don't help), he can help SO much especially with projects. Pop quizzes suck but no HW in the class which is nice. Also drops many quiz scores, we took 14 quizzes and he dropped 4 which is nice. He reuses the same type of questions for quizzes/exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Though Dr. Mariani grades strictly on pop quizzes and projects since he expects perfection on everything to get credit otherwise receiving 0 points, he was a pretty good lecturer who explains the material really well so that we can understand the material and gives many drops for pop quizzes despite some low scores to help people's grade
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He is a VERY tough grader. One mistake and you get a 60%. Does not want to release statistics for quizzes (since everyone is getting bad grades). If you do anything wrong or something different than how he wants, he will fail you. He doesn't even have office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Worst professor in the Computer Science department at MSU. Makes the class way harder than it should be. Genuinely feels like he does not want his students to succeed. Harsh grader and will give you an ADR for anything. Do not take this class if possible!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The pop quizzes were annoying and specific, there were also a lot of slides covered. The lectures are boring as well. However, Mariani does help you a lot if you go to office hours especially for projects, and the content is really helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: My guy really needs to chill with the pop quizzers—he literally goes full Hitler during lectures. Honestly, he should just be called Hitler Michael Mariani.

He designed the class very poorly by adding pop quizzes that sometimes are at the beginning of class. He also adds the most obscure topics to the quizzes and truly believes that he's some genius wizard of computer science. He runs this class like a dictator, and this was not a pleasant experience at all. Would not recommend taking this course.

He really just does not want to see you succeed. If your work is anything less than perfection, you get a 0. He preaches breaking problems into smaller pieces, but compares your work 1:1 with expected answers and gives no feedback. He releases no test cases beyond a screenshot of correct results, and releases no correct answers for study questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor gives random pop quizzes, and his TAs randomly enter the room like ICE agents before each quizzes. The other professor rambles about Tesla before class even starts, assigns 100+ slides per lecture to get through, and gives very difficult quizzes. About 90% of the grade is determined by quizzes and exams.
If Joshua Nahum is from Mars, he is from Uranus.
New Ancestry Hint! You are a direct descendant of Benito Mussolini!
Avoid if you have a choice of another professor.

Professor was not helpful throughout the course. When I did interact with him directly, he came across as dismissive and rude rather than supportive. Lack of communication and unapproachable attitude made the learning experience frustrating. Pop quizzes every week, even though I received a good grade, studying for this course was a mess.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Worst class. No help, even outside class, responds in Piazza barely. Lots of quizzes, extremely hostile when asked for reviews or practice problems.

This class is fully test and quiz reliant. Projects are worth 10% with quizzes and exams worth the other 90%. He will give a few sets of practice problems with no solutions ever given. Then will grade harshly on quizzes and not explain why you get the wrong grade unless going to office hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Always need to come to class, take good notes. While his grading policy and can feel strict, he's got VERY clear guidelines (esp 4 projects). Read syllabus thoroughly so you don't get surprised by policies. Overall, good guy. He means well. Recommend reviewing SQL outside of class. He wants you to have a high grade, he only uses top 10 pop quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He is not a bad professor, but he can be a harsh grader sometimes. He has a lot of pop quizzes but they are all from lecture or practice problems, you should be reviewing the slides almost every night to ace them, make sure you know your errors. Projects are hard to start but get easier. One of the easier 300s, put in the work and you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Mariani only cares about trying his hardest to fail you without getting fired.

Do not take this class until you have built up your programming skills by completing most of the other CSE 300 levels. Lectures are ridiculous with thousands of powerpoint slides, however Prof. Mariani is very approachable in office hours. Pop quizzes are annoying but quickly fall into a pattern. Projects are challenging but useful.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Do you love taking insufferable CSE courses ? Is your life becoming too easy?Look no further than CSE 380. Pop quizzes that are 60% of total grades and have to be taken every single week Every element of this course has been designed to maximize anxiety and stress. Getting a 4.0 is nearly impossible unless you are within the 1% top students
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you thought 232 was bad, brace yourself. This course is built to fail you.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Horrible class, dont take it

He just wants to see you fail so bad. He is not interested in actually teaching the course materials. He almost never had answers to questions that people asked outside of the slides he had already prepared for the lecture. Terrible, terrible experience, would NOT recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I have about an 84 percent quizzes section, 90 on the midterm section, and will have at the lowest a 90 on projects section. Isn't it crazy that it's impossible for me to 4.0 this class. Even with a 100% on the final. I don't what it is, there is no point in putting in effort in this class because it is impossible to succeed.

- Pop quizzes every (almost) week, and you wont get the grade back for them before another pop quiz.
- Pop quizzes are worth nearly 5% of your grade, and there are 12 of them with 3 drops
- You get no study material (except for the first 3 pop quizzes, afterwards you are on your own)

If pop quizzes aren't your thing, please don't take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: You get basically no study material. When asked what to study he will say "everything" and quiz you on tiny easy to miss details with harsh grading. Gives rushed bare-minimum responses when asked questions on the forum. Grade is only projects, quizzes, and exams all of which you get no practice for. Not necessarily difficult but very time consuming
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: There are no assignments in this course so all there is to do is study the slides for 6 weeks straight until the projects kick in. There are sample problems but they're not really helpful for learning and just end up taking more time than they're worth. The projects were very annoying but the lack of assignments gave a very light workload.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I got 4.0 but I still see the course is pretty tough and he will be Josh Nahum 2.0. Focus on quizzes and exams. I need to remember everything in slides to do the quizzes
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment:
Avoid if you can, he will make you hate CS. He is very egotistical and doesn't teach what is needed to do the projects. From project specs, "I assume that you will be able to read
the documentation and figure out how they work". So be ready to teach the class to yourself. Extremely harsh grader on the pop quizzes that occur nearly every class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Avoid at all costs. This professor is completely unorganized, unhelpful, and condescending. Lectures are incoherent, grading is arbitrary, and they refuse to answer questions. Expect impossible exams with no resemblance to the material covered. If you value your GPA and sanity, stay far away. Worst professor I've ever had.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The class is useful, but the way the professor teaches it is horrible; rather the requirements he has of the students. He only releases a couple practice question sets for the 10+ pop quizzes and exams you will have, does not take criticism and is very egotistical. His reasoning for failing to provide enough study material doesn't even make sense.

one of the worst professors at MSU. this is the only class I've took where the professor doesn't give a study guide for the midterm and expects you to go over hundreds of lecture slides and example questions from the first week that he doesn't even give the answers to.

This class is test-heavy, poorly structured, and inflexible to student concerns. Pop Quizzes make up 45% of course grade. Very harsh on small mistakes. I saw a student ask for more information on the upcoming midterm and the professor said that he talked about it in class and refused to clarify. Avoid this class if possible
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Blames the students for the poor course format. The 40% worth of randomly assigned quizzes has been proven not to work in past years based on the class's grade statistics but he still continues to use the same structure.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The class structure is awful. Pop quizzes are worth 45% of your grade; all we are told is that "it's on the previous slides, " in which we cover 45+ slides daily. Small mistakes on quizzes cost you huge. Graded by quizzes, exams, and projects which I hear are the worst part. I was interested in this class, but now I regret taking it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This guy is for sure inflating his own ratings, and you can clearly see by the way those reviews are written. That says it all in my opinion

Is this class meant to fail students? I don't understand. The requirements of pop quizzes that make up 40% of your grade is ridiculous. After a certain point of failed students, maybe it is the way the class is set up why students are failing rather than the students themselves.

I only know him from the capstone class, but genuinely speaking, this guy is extremely egotistical, even though he barely talked you could sense the disgust he has for the students. Interrupted a group once and started asking them irrelevant questions then said they ran out of time. He needs to learn how to be a human

You receive 0 feedback throughout the semester on quizzes/exams/projects, making it impossible to know how to improve. There are three sets of practice problems released for the semester, with no solutions given, making them essentially useless. He is a good lecturer,  but the class design is obviously awful to anyone who has taken a CSE class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Threatens ADR and 0s often for something like not naming a file correctly. Doesn't give back tests or quizzes to study or allow an in-class period to look at feedback unless you go to his office hours. Petty and doesn't take complaints well as he will punish the whole class if someone complains. Projects are extremely difficult and time consuming
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures are dense with very few assignments to test you on the material, but the material itself is taught exceptionally well.  I found the projects practical and manageable if started early enough despite a relatively high level of difficulty.  The professor did a great job of answering questions out of class.  Favorite CSE prof so far at MSU.

Lectures are an hour and 20 minutes of slides, there are 2 lectures/week and 3 sets of problems released for the whole semester. Thats it, thats all the content you receive for the entire course. Blames students lack of studying for poor results, and is very strict with attendance. Small changes would make this course a 8/10, could be Interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Supposedly wrote reviews for himself to counter a 1.0 rating. Extensive ADRs for imports of not allowed libraries, ADRs if code is suspected to be copied, even for a 1% match. Projects require a checkpoint covering 70% of functionality, and missing this deadline reduces the grade by 30%. 7 exams and quizzes, all cumulative. Strict attendance policy

Taking 380 right now and I can say that it was one of the most useful CS courses I've taken at MSU.Some course policies might be too harsh, but this class teaches the concepts pretty well. James is a pretty good professor who knows the concept and could easily answer any question you have. Start studying for tests early because they are cumulative.
I do like his style of teaching stuff, he does a great job in it. But his requirements are insane

I do think James is competent and good at what he does, but you shouldn't have to rely on Office Hours as heavily as he makes you in order to not fail this class. I think there is a delicate balance between being overly lenient on your students and blocking all access to class materials from review which Professor Mariani fails to strike.

I hear stories on how for 491 he was pretty chill, but for the first semester as an actual required course… I don't really see it. Very strict and even if you study hard, attend helprooms, etc but don't do well, he will say it's your fault that you did poorly.

Mariani is a great professor! I wouldn't pay much attention to the other reviews on this page. He is very helpful when you have a good attitude, but he will not put up with BS.

Coming to lecture and engaging is essential. Thankfully, Mariani is is good at answering questions and helping students grasp the content if they will put in the effort.

Don't take a class with this guy, ever. He has just found a way to do the least amount of work and makes students suffer because of his laziness. All exams/quizzes are cumulative, but he barely helps out students with review guides. He is strict on absolutely everything and just an overall horrible professor.
Put 5 months of back end development on your resume after this class, because you'll be teaching it to yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Provides incredibly few resources for learning and makes thoroughly liberal use of automatic zeros and automatic class failure. Berates students for asking for more resources or complaining about the class. Why he is a professor I cannot comprehend.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Not a good person. Doesn't know how to teach

This class made me hate college. Prof puts absolutely no effort into the course. He takes a potentially really cool class and makes it unbearable.

He is extremely adversarial and hostile toward his students and takes no accountability for himself.  

This class is so bad I created an account to make this review. Avoid this class if you can.

-No extra credit
-Really harsh on exam/quiz grading
-Sick without a doctor notice or something official? No problem! Class dropped.
-Only take this class if you want to drain yourself mentally and physically
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: the class is very hard but he is making lots of attempts to help people pass with extra credit and lots of office hours and piazza help. probably dont take this class with him if you have a heavy load of other classes
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Honestly the most pointless class. I am currently in EGR102, and it has helped me so much more than EGR100 ever has. All group work, and because my groups were so bad it was so much unecessary work. I could have been putting more work into my actual engineering courses with the time I put into this class; it needs to be restructured.

This class was fine and Dr. Morgan gave decent lectures. I will say the lectures weren't that interesting or necessary for the class. Most of the points for the class come from the lab. 

There are mandatory exit questions for the lectures that are a bit confusing but she will help if you ask and they are graded by accuracy.

The lectures are kind of redundant and your grades mainly come from the two take-home exams and the two group projects that you definitely have to pray you have a good group for otherwise its up to your TA to decide whether you get a decent grade or not.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: You will be primarily graded on group projects. The TA does nearly all of the grading. The lecture and lab have very little to do with each other. The class is overall boring, generic, and basic (block coding, using excel, etc.). You are required to get a >$100 textbook in order to complete the assignments which can be done without reading it.
super easy, all the exam answers are online. chat gpt is king
EGR 100 is just generally a bad class, and Dr. Morgan tries her best with what she has. The lectures are generally not interesting with a small quiz at the end.

The class should be an easy 4.0, Morgan will not affect your grade at all as everything is dependent on your TA and your lab group, just do your work and you will be fine. That being said Morgan's lectures are  long, boring, and completely irrelevant to any work we are doing, and she makes you buy an expensive textbook that no one will be reading

Lets get one thing straight the class is not hard. I think most of the ratings are because of the lab, which is a teacher assistant, not Professor Morgan. In lab, you might find yourself in groups that are lazy and uncooperative so you may end up doing most of the work. Anyone rating this professor below a 3 is one of these lazy people.

PICK A GOOD GROUP. The labs are all group-based. Dr. Morgan sucks. The lectures have no value. The labs are where you actually do things. Don't buy the textbook; just use the free trial to do the reading assignments and skip the last 4 or so. But yeah, she is awful.

She's very unavailable and the kectThe class is a weed out class so don't be discourage if you're passionate about your major. It's an easy A when you put the unnecessary amount of work in. Switch your group of its bad and meet people in your lab! There's a lot of busy work for it o be a 2.0 class but use your resources.

Dr. Morgan seemed like a nice person. Although nice, her lectures were very pointless and people only showed up for lecture assignments. The whole course experience depends on if you have a good group or not. You spent way to much time out of class doing homework and assignments for this class being two credits.

From what I can gather, Dr. Morgan is a generally nice person. However, the class is structured very poorly. Overall, it was mostly just a report writing class. The lectures were a mix of vague engineering and physics topics that aren't fully explained, especially the physics. The class wasn't hard, but the textbook is only for homework questions.

she is very rough in grading, and forces the TA's to grade you horribly and harshly. Her office hours are horrible with 2-3 hours a week, and always 5-10 people waiting in line. Be sure to wait for 45 mins to talk to her. And she is very harsh, the course is structured horribly about stuff and not being clear. She also speaks like a robot in class.

Dr. Morgan is a very sweet lady, don't get scared from her low rating. The entire class is based on if you do the work on time. She does make her TA's grade kind of hard on the reports, but it's only to prepare you for the actual reports you'll have to do in the field. They also give you really good feedback so you can improve for future reports.
her class is kind of pointless but easy. pretty much don't understand what she's saying but it doesn't matter because we don't ever use any of the stuff
she is super nice and helped a lot with any questions i had

Very kind and easy to talk to. Although office hours are limited, you can always email your TA with any questions. Overall, it's an easy class if you actually follow the report rubrics and is a pretty fun course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Haven't interacted with the professor much but the class wasn't bad. Depends a lot on if you get a good group, but otherwise it's mostly about following instructions and keeping up with deadlines.

Grade is entirely dependent on your lab group and TA. Lecture is a little boring and usually doesn't relate to lab content. Attendance mandatory as there are lecture assignments. TAs are pretty harsh graders and will give you a 0 for submitting something even one minute late. Also the textbook is crazy expensive for how much you end up using it.

The only thing you need to do to pass the class is focus on the lab. She does lecture assignments but they're pretty trivial. Exams are open note. You don't really gain much from lecture. It's mostly group projects and report writing so its reliant on a good group. I had a good TA which made it a little easier. Keep the lecture assignments.

The grade that you get is largely dependent on the group that you have in lab. But, you do have the option to switch groups after the first project. I don't like the $150 textbook that you have to purchase to do homework. She is pretty decent at Lectures but most of the things are pretty general that we are taught.

Think her average grade is more indicative of the course itself and not her. Experience largely depends on group members and TA. Book costs an insane amount of money for its use case, and lectures are usually pretty boring. She herself is a nice person, course is just repetitive and quite boring.

This is the worst teacher I have ever had in my entire life. Expect to be spoken to as if you are 3 years old. Has two total office hours per week despite a 500 student class size. Therefore, if you show up to office hours expect to wait 30+ minutes for a short conversation that will be her lecturing you about your questions before you finish aski

most work youll have to do for a 2 credit class, lectures are very boring and filled with unnecessary info, course work is mostly busy work and group projects, overall not hard to get a good grade but not a good class

Dr. Morgan is fine. She seems like a robot most of the time during the lectures. The content of her class is important but is very boring. Even though the TAs do the grading, they follow a strict grade scale for each assignment. You will get a 70% on your first paper, the excuse being "It's a tiny portion of your grade." Do the work, you'll be ok.

Dr. Morgan is one of the most soulless people I have ever met in my life. She is very rigid and unaccommodating. If you come to her office hours with questions regarding grading, expect to leave the room with no shreds of sanity left. Her class may not be difficult, but she is certainly difficult to be around...

I earlier gave her a 5 star thinking she was a great professor. I recently got an email saying my honors option was rejected for this class. I LITERALLY PUT IN HOURS OF EFFORT INTO WRITING A 8 PAGE REPORT for honors option and due to some minor error,SHE JUST DECLINED MY ENTIRE REPORT. I mean how could a professor be this tough on an assignment .

I dont know why do some students hate the professor. I agree a lot of content in class can be done in a short amount of time rather than a semester, but professor does teach well and tries to make students learn.. However, her office hours line is really long, it needs to be fixed.

Your grade in this class will be completely dependent on the group that you get, but all other work is extremely easy. My only gripe with Morgan is the $150 textbook which you HAVE to buy in order to do the homework. It's unfortunate that this class is required since its completely unnecessary but she makes the class as easy as possible.

The professor seems great, but lectures can feel boring and pointless. The class heavily depends on the group project, so your experience may vary based on your team. The TA also plays a major role—having a helpful one can really improve the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--So many papers

reviews.comment: The class is HEAVILY dependent on the group you get. If you get a good group it will be an easy 4.0 with little work. If you get a bad group, like myself, you will have many late nights and the class will be equivalent to a 4 credit course. My group did zero work and it was the most miserable experience I've had in college so far.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: The lectures were useless, and the assignments reminded me of 8th grade math. The lab experience depends solely on the TA. I had Matias Leon-Miquel. He was the best TA and was so kind and helpful during lab and was a pretty lenient grader. Dr. Morgan did nothing. The TA makes it or breaks it. Overall very easy class though.

The lectures and lecture assignments taught me nothing; it was just busy work. She is also not very lenient on changing grades. The class is heavily focused on group projects, and when people in my group didn't submit the work like they said they would, I got zeros, even after going to her office hours, she said she wouldn't change it.

The course itself is very stupid, so I can't put that on the professor, but her lectures are actually very boring and useless when it comes to teaching us content. She is really strict on changing grades and won't give you even 0.01% credit if that meant you would get a 4.0 in the class.

EGR100 is an unorganized class and the lectures and labs are disconnected. The labs have a ton of reports and projects that are often too much. Lectures are to prepare for exams. The class doesn't introduce students to much engineering besides how to write reports and make scatter plots in Excel. Dr. Morgan is kind and accessible outside of class.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Easy class but the lectures are so boring and not useful. The lecture assignments are so easy. Grading depends on the TA for labs but they are mostly lenient and good with material.
good overall

Class was pretty fun and easy mostly involving a couple of hands on projects and some highschool level math and physics. She's a pretty nice and caring person. $150 for the text book was crazy, another class I'm taking uses the same platform and the text book is $40 with about 10 times more content than this class.

This class is honestly more work than the 2 credits it is, but it is very easy. Make sure to attend lecture as there are lecture assignments you must complete to 4.0. Dr. Morgan is very accessible outside of class. If you ever need help with anything (grading, late assignments, etc.) don't email her, just visit **office hours**.

This class uses a $150 textbook which has content that can be summarized in a YouTube video. The lecture/exam is mostly an arbitrary combination of thermodynamics, physics, chemistry, etc. This class lecture/exams doesn't really serve a purpose for engineers. Labs are useful, as they introduce report writing and collaboration to students.

She is nice and educates you about careers in engineering. all work in this class is very easy. I've seen people on here claim good group members are important to your success in the course, but you could do all the work alone it would just be a more significant time investment than necessary.

She was okay, but the class is just pointless. I don't know why the university forces engineers to take it. You learn quite literally nothing, the lectures are at 8am, and you just talk about random BS and powerpoint/excel. Joke of a class, and Prof. Morgan was just "Okay". HATED the course though

Honestly this course solely depends on your group members and your TA. Also be prepared to spend around 170 for a textbook that is useless. Once again you need immense luck for your teammates and TA to have a good experience.

Yeah, this class wasn't fun, but all engineers have to take it unfortunately. $150 book was useless but required for points. TA was not a good grader. Lecture assignments were easy. Just unit conversions for the most part. Class just annoying rather than difficult. This class should be an easy 4.0 but there were some close calls from bad grading.

Most problems have already been said. Overpriced useless online textbook, big ego, bizarre grading. Keep your lecture assignments (Yes even after they're graded). The lab TA did all the heavy lifting and was very nice. You will not learn anything in this class.
Dr. Morgan gives what are some of the worst lectures at this university. she explains everything badly. also she makes you buy a $150 textbook that she didn't mention a single time.

Genuinely the worst class I've ever had. She seems nice, but had quick, useless lectures that taught us nothing. My lab groups were DESPICABLE- had to do all the work and I didn't know how to ask for help from prof. No lecture content came up in lab at any point. USELESS $130 TEXTBOOK??? GOOD GOD. AND WE HARDLY USED IT. Bad reviews are true.
