Hey guys welcome to the 8 AM lecture, let me speed run 30 slides, so you have no idea what I'm talking about. Oh, hey you also have a lecture assignment on said slides, but you only have 10 minutes, good luck! Oh yeah, don't forget to go to that 2 hour lab and jerk each other.

In her lectures, it's fine 35 minutes of her talking and 15 minutes to do an assignment. HER LAB? SUCKS! You do two projects throughout the semester with a team, if you have a teammate that isn't doing any work and you want to leave their name off? The rules for that are very fuzzy. I hated this class, but it is the first step to being an engineer.

I have never seen any professor that tries to "fail' a student as hard as she does.
She's unreasonably strict and seems like love to give students penalty.

Dr. Morgan is kind but inflexible and overly strict. Missing a scatter plot cost me 60 points out of 200, and despite scoring 98/100 on the final, I got a B. Submitting assignments even one minute late results in zero points. The course demands too much for a 100-level class, the $100 e-book feels pointless, and emails often go unanswered.

Not outstanding, but not necessarily an awful professor. You have very little time to take notes in lectures, and the projects are super mundane and not exciting. It's very unclear what the curriculum is supposed to teach or prepare you for. Depending on your TA, you will be graded super heavily or not at all. Try to take the class with a friend.
Everything taught in the lectures is common sense, all of the real work comes from the lab. Textbook is barely used and very expensive. Professor is nice enough but this class is absolutely useless.

Made me buy a 130-dollar textbook to not use it. This sums up the whole class experience, overall, lectures were unorganized and she went extremely fast. Labs vary greatly depending on your TA and group.

She goes out of her way to stop people from doing well and wastes their time. For example, if work was submitted on time in the wrong portal (she teaches first sem freshman mainly), she makes you come to office hours which are at an awkward time and still might not accept the work even though it was submitted on time just at the wrong place.

the professor seems nice, but the material is so unorganized when it's taught. i had no idea what was happening during lecture assignments. even the labs assignments were confusing to understand. the labs are also two hours and you basically just sit there. they need this to at least be a 3 credit course for all the work and group projects.

Dr. Morgan (you MUST call her doctor or else she will not even respond to you) is extremely difficult to reach and very difficult when it comes to communication. The class itself is not hard, however she instructs the TA's to grade VERY difficult, and would not communicate unless you go to her office hours. I pray one day this class isn't required!

Dr. Morgan isn't the greatest professor, but really isn't as bad as her rating makes her out to be. As a person, she's quite nice and respectful especially while answering questions in lecture. As a professor, her lectures seemed kind of unorganized and hard to follow, sometimes giving us only 10ish minutes to do the required lecture assignment.

Professor is unorganized and teaches concepts that are generally common knowledge. Book is expensive for class that is useless. The only part that is useful is working in groups to complete projects. Should be a 3 credit class as many groups are not efficient and require one person to carry the team.
She assigns a very useless textbook which is 130 bucks. Super easy class.

useless lectures that taught us common sense things like unit conversions. didn't learn anything new, everything was something I new from middle school science classes. labs required a ton of work for a 2 credit class. had to buy useless book for homework, which just glazed engineers and didn't teach us anything.

Overall, class is mostly busy work. Your grade will be determined by how lenient your TA is. Had to buy a $150 textbook that was utterly and completely useless for 10% of the grade. Lecture attendance is required. However, Dr. Morgan is very kind and was always friendly in lecture. Great person, boring class. Wish we didn't have to pay for textbook

This class is a joke. Your once a week lectures are spent sitting there having no clue what is going on, until the last 10 minutes when your handed an assignment and told to do it. Your 2 hour long once a week lab is very TA dependent but with no clear rubrics or guidelines you'll have no clue what to do. Don't even bother with office hours.
Lectures were so boring and I learned absolutely nothing. To be fair, that's mainly a pitfall of EGR 100. Just read off of slides that were mainly dimensional analysis.
Not lenient at all.

This class as a whole is the single most useless information dump ever, get ready to learn about microsoft excel and "problem solving" aka doing random projects for no reason. Other than that, Morgan is not a great prof, she goes through the lecture information too quickly and all the lecture assignments are just copying info from previous slides.

This Professor is the reason my Engineering has so many problems in the modern day. She has said, "We have new students" in one of her lectures she is the reason that so many good engineers quit Engineering because she trys to kick the creative, and questioning students out of engineering.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Dr. Morgan wasn't as bad as her reputation makes her seem. Her lectures were decent, but the material presented was pretty useless, as was the class as a whole. She also wasn't very available outside of class, as she usually was not able to talk before/after class and her office hours always had a long wait. Not the worst... not the best either.

If you have any questions for Dr. Morgan that do not directly involve lecture content, she tells you to attend her office hours no matter the question. Once you attend her office hours, she is incredibly rigid and makes you get back in line if you have any modifications to be made. Working with Dr. Morgan is extremely difficult.
Dr. Morgan was very nice and caring, but her lectures didn't make any sense. It felt like she just talked about random stuff and it didn't seem to be relevant or relate to anything.

This is not a hard class to get a 4.0 in, but this was also an annoying class to take. The professor is a good person but not the best at her job, she speeds through the slides and there are in-lecture assignments that you have to do. The labs could be easy or hard depending on your group and TA.

Unfortunately this class is required for engineering majors, otherwise I would recommend that nobody take it. Lectures are super fast and basically useless, it's difficult to keep up. She has mandatory graded lecture assignments so you can't skip. Lab is based almost entirely on group projects and varies WILDLY on your TA and group members.

This class is a waste of time. The lectures are very boring and she doesn't do a good job of explaining so you don't really learn anything. At the end of each lecture, there is a lecture assignment, which is most of the time hard as she doesn't explain the topics but rather reads them off a slide show. This was one of my least favorite classes.

One of the most useless classes in MSU. Her lectures were fast paced and boring. Those assignments during the lecture were so easy. The projects are just waste of time regardless of only 2 credits. Wouldn't take this class again.
egr 100 is the most useless class of the curriculum. that two credit class is more painful than anything.

Morgan is not bad herself and her lectures are fine, but a bunch of calculations are thrown at you and you don't really learn anything. Exams are easy as she walks you through it step by step. BUT, I cannot stress this enough please get a good group for lab or this class will be horrible. Please don't feel bad and leave them as it is your grade!

Dr. Morgan's class was a typical first year intro class. It's easy to manage a 4.0 in her class if you provide group effort and have a interest in engineering. The biggest takeaway from her class was learning how to work in groups. Her lecture assignments were my biggest struggle (not too hard though) due to the intensity of the calculations.
easy class, lectures were just really boring and fast paced. there is lowkey no point in showing up to the lectures besides the lecture assignments because the videos she uploads are the same.

Jenahive herself is nice but it's the TA's to be aware. Most of the class is group work and the TA's are very picky on the projects. They don't grade based on the rubric or requirements and take off points for the smallest stuff. The TAs also aren't very clear on what the projects or reports are supposed to have. Midterm and final exam are easy.

She was ok. Good person, but treated kids like they didn't know anything, like kindergartners. Projects graded unnecessarily hard. Most of the grade is based on 2 projects. Lectures were generally unhelpful and did not flow at all.

Class felt absolutely useless for later in life, way too much work outside of class, especially for 2 credits, and a $100+ textbook that is essentially only used for homework problems (that you absolutely have to buy new; can't get secondhand). Only a 2/5 instead of 1/5 because she was easily accessible through email.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr.Morgans lectures have nothing to do with the Labs and are very unhelpful, even for the lecture assignments. You never know when anything is due and she fails to remind you to keep the assignments even when you have to submit them at the end of the semester. Its almost like Dr.Morgan sets you up to fail.

Dr. Morgan is a nice, helpful woman but her lectures seem pointless. All the consist of are her going over random calculations that seem to have no correlation to anything we do in the lab. The lecture assignments are repetitive, and the midterm was really weird. The lab is fun as long as you get a good project which can be unfair.

Terrible. No late work even by 30 minutes even though they grade 2 weeks later. Some TA's are the harshest graders. I had more work in this 2 CREDIT class than my four credit classes. This class is also completely useless and your grade is based on luck whether you get assigned an easy or a hard project. This being a mandatory eng class is a joke.
I have spent a cumulative 12 hours in her lectures and have not learned a single thing. At least the labs are pretty fun.

You are required to purchase a textbook and complete reading quizzes which ask questions that are utterly useless. You have to resubmit already graded papers a month after they are done getting another grade to "make sure you keep the papers." And the classes talk about things you would learn better in other classes but she goes way to fast.

Will give you lecture assignments to do, then takes them for points, gives them back graded, and then expects us to turn them in again for what? Why what reason? She does things without reason. This woman teaches with rules. She doesn't flinch at students asking for help or needing more time on slides. If it can be avoided avoid

Never accessible for help outside class except during office hours - will never do anything over email. Makes you purchase a $150 textbook for 5 homework assignments. Attendance in lectures are required and there are graded assignments in them. Dr.Morgan along with her TA's are unsympathetic and unhelpful. You will teach yourself everything.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Morgan is one of the most caring profs at MSU. Although the lab projects can get hard, get with a good group and you'll do fine. She also helps you on the graded lecture assignments each class and goes over stuff to help. Theres lots of opportunities to earn credit as long as you complete everything. There are also only two tests for the class.

Dr. Morgan is a good person, but terrible professor. I had more work in 2 credit EGR 100, than the rest of my classes combined. I didn't buy the $115 textbook, got zeros for the reading quizzes, and still got a 4.0. The group work is terrible if your group is bad. Grading is done by TAs. The midterm was a cake walk if you follow her example demos.

She acts like her 2 credit class is the most important thing in the world.  Her level 100 class had more work in it than my level 300 class.  It was boring and taught me nothing and costs $1000 to take.

This course is completely irrelevant, but a requirement for all engineering majors. The lectures are useless, and group projects are tedious and trivial but can be fun if you get a good group. If you don't get a good group, you will not enjoy this class. Dr. Morgan has office hours twice a week and will help if needed, but this should be an easy A.

The course lecture and required $100 textbook are totally unrelated to the lab and exist solely to be mined for final exam material. Expect to do poorly on the exams and lecture assignments if you do not have a solid physics foundation coming into the course. Labs are all group work, which is great if you get a good group, but awful if you do not.

The lecture, reading (which costs $100), and labs are irrelevant. The TAs vary; some could be very harsh, even on a tiny mistake, but others care. The professor herself is really nice, she tries to make everyone understand the concept and does well in this course. She truly cares for her student and provides constructive feedback towards questions.
The group projects were awful and the TA's grade insanely harsh. It's easy to forget to do work for this class so make sure you stay on top of your work.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This course was irrelevant: lectures had nothing to do with labs and most of the work was done in lab. Lectures were mostly spent going over slides and most people couldn't keep up with her and take notes because she went through everything very fast. TA's did most of the teaching throughout the course, and every TA grades differently so beware.

Dr. Morgan's class (specifically EGR 100) is unnecessarily difficult because there is a lot of irrelevant information in her lectures. Basically the entire semester it is unclear what you need to know. However, I think that Dr. Morgan does try to help students when she can.

Professor Morgan puts in zero effort for her students. The class is unnecessarily harder than it needs to be.  The lectures by Professor Morgan is a waste of a hour but you have to stay till the end to complete the lecture assignment that she gives the answer to. It seems they pay her for her PhD title and not actually teaching proficiency.

Dr. Morgan was of minimal help the whole semester, and her lectures were very irrelevant and unhelpful with the labs. I first entered this course with high expectations and quickly became disappointed for many reasons. Also, the required $110 online textbook was completely useless other than needing it for a few 5-minute quizzes the first month.

Dr. Morgan is honestly not a terrible teacher or person, she's just not a good lecture professor. She goes through things thoroughly but it's almost boring and the lecture assignments are somewhat hard. If she was a TA, it would be much better

EGR 100 is a complete waste of time. The lectures were just boring and the only reason I went was because of the assignment. 99% of the work for the class is done in the lab portion and which is taught by the TA. Class is super easy but will ned to do work on the group projects, so pray you to get a good project group. Projects are also super easy.

Lecture is required but has absolutely no relevance to the class. I'm not sure I've ever taken a class and learned nothing before. Lab is enjoyable but make sure you have a good group or else you are in for it.
They grade based off of "creativity", which is an arbitrary and poorly described metric.

EGR100 is a simple class. Most of the work is in lab and if you pick an easy project, you'll be fine. In lectures, however, Dr. Morgan speeds through the notes and students have no time to write anything down. Everyone took pictures of the screen before she clicked to the next slide. Lecture assignments can be hard, so ask around you for help.

The lectures and lab work are very different but they do relate to what you're going to be doing in engineering. Most of your grade is based on lab work, so hopefully you get a competent group. Projects are decently easy the TA's help you out a lot and the prof gives good resources to use on projects and the midterm in d2L. Attendance is graded!!!

Professor Morgan seems to care about the students and was usually really helpful when I visited her during office hours. However her lectures were completely unrelated to the stuff you did in lab and if you don't get a good lab group you'll have a tough time. Some projects are also way easier than others but you have little say in which one you get

DON'T PAY FOR THE ONLINE COURSE BOOK. It is over a hundred dollars and it's five hours to go through all of the work assigned using your free trial. As for the rest of your class, your group will make or break this so make an effort to actually get to know them. The course material is very basic, be ready for a lot of high school science classes.

The lectures were all recorded and posted, but the assignments were difficult as she doesn't go over the material in detail and expects you to have prior knowledge. The bulk of your grade is the lab group projects, which were awful or great depending on if you got a good group. The midterm was easy since she outlined it in lectures.
I like Dr. Morgan, she seems nice. The lectures are fine, nothing amazing about it. I really hate this class though, most engineers won't use the skills taught.

I saw Dr. Morgan twice the whole semester, I had an in person lecture but realized watching it online was easier. This class is stupid at best the projects you do in lab are equivalent to what middle schoolers do. Hope you get a good group because it will be an easy A. Midterm is worth a lot of points but she has videos outlining how to do it.

Morgan doesn't seem awful, but she isn't really the one doing the teaching outside of pre-recorded lecture videos. Your TA is who does most of the class, the projects (a lot of the grade), and the one who grades stuff, most of them mark you down for the smallest of things. If this isn't required for your major don't take it. Hope for a good TA.

You would have a better time by converting your tuition into nickels, filling bags with the nickels, then having people hit you with said bags. If this was my first taste of engineering I would want to change majors. Unfortunately, at least during my time, she was the only one to teach this class.

Interesting course. Heavy group work. Decent amount of work for 2 credits. Hope for a good group cause some of these sorry engineering students won't do anything in these groups and you will have to pick up.

Dr. Morgan, overall, was not the best professor, but not the worst. Had virtually no student interaction besides pre-recorded videos, which were very slow-paced, and the course required a 100 dollar online textbook that was barely used. Class itself was easy for me, though apparently some TAs are extremely tough graders, so watch out for that.

First, you are required to pay 100 dollars for an online textbook that contains graded reading quizzes which have no bearing on anything else in the class. Your grade is VERY dependent on your TA and project group. Dr. Morgan herself seems nice in her videos but it's not like I would know, since almost nobody in the class ever met her.

We never met with her face to face. She was completely asynchronous and basically did nothing besides create very boring and irrelevant lecture videos. The group projects consumed our time primarily. The TAs handled the project grading and project directions alone. Your grade is entirely dependent on having a competent TA. Pr. doesn't do anything.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Group projects--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Congratulations! You just spend a space in your scheduling learning how to use excel. Sorry if you already know how, that's not MSU's issue. As far as Dr. Morgan goes, where was she? She did the lectures which were boring and irrelevant. She didn't interact with the 2 large projects which make up almost all your grade. Pray for a good TA and group.

The class was super random and the content was not related. Your grade completely depends your TA. I've had friends take this class and do great but I did not do as well because my TA graded so aggressively hard on our group projects that even though I got 100% everything else I still couldn't end with a A and there was nothing I could do about it

She has the teaching assistants do 99% of her work (holding meetings, grading work, etc.) while she sits there and does nothing but assign you busywork. Such a poorly ran course, very lazy professor. And of course she makes you pay $100+ for a textbook you barely use and frankly don't need. Absolute clown professor.

Her asynchronous lectures are awful. Be prepared to teach yourself everything if you don't have a good TA to reach out to. The workload is ridiculous as well; we lost two weeks (scheduling error) to work on our first project, and we're still getting lecture assignments that burn through time and don't actually benefit us. Avoid this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: As an online class you don't really have much contact with her besides office hours. She was helpful whenever I went to office hours, but I mainly went for guidance on an honors option and not the course content. Her lectures and assignments are kind of random compared to the projects but they're okay.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Group projects

reviews.comment: .A ton of this class is dependant on your TA, and if you get stuck with one that isn't too great-good luck. The grading is mostly done by TA's and ULA's as well, and is often super inconsistent between classes, especially because grading criteria is super unclear. It is not that hard to get an A, but it is very draining at times.

Dr. Morgan was great. She helped a lot during office hours and always responded to my emails. Her lectures dont go along with the labs but its still helpful. Final is hard if you dont study. The honors essay seems hard since its 10 pages but if you choose a good topic youll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Group projects--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: The group projects in the class can be difficult if you don't have a good group. Dr. Morgan is pretty understanding about it though, I reached out to her during her office hours and she was able to help me out a bit with my group. I would say the class itself isn't very enjoyable in that regard, but Dr. Morgan tries her best to help.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Group projects

reviews.comment: Grading criteria and other instructions were usually quite unclear, but in the end if you put in any amount of effort its an easy 4.0 it just might might be a frustrating experience. Lectures were unhelpful and disorganized. Lab depends on your TA. For anyone considering completing the honors option, it was a 10 page paper.

Horrible at describing anything in detail and quite frankly the worst professor I have had at MSU. Her lectures are painful to sit through. I would not recommend her to anyone even though the class was pretty easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: Here is how you enjoy the class -Bring a laptop and sit in the way back -Pay zero attention, as much of it so basic you saving yourself sanity -Work with some kids who did pay attention to pass lecture assignment(20%) -In lab it's 20 min of work, and then an hour of chilling -Study like an hour before the exam and you will ace it.
Dr. Morgan is a great prof, but her lectures and the lecture assignments were the worst. Also, you are put into groups, so if you have a bad team you might find yourself doing most or all the work.

Professor Morgan is a nice lady but the course is straight booty. If you don't have a good team for the lab project, get ready to grind like Hell. Lectures were pointless and there were pop-quizzes at the end of some of them. I think I got less than a 50% on the finals but still ended up with an A, suggesting that this class might've been curved.

Lost some brain cells in her lectures, she made me catastrophically dumber. Her explanations of anything and everything is minimal. The midterm is very confusing and you will need help. Lab and everything else is pretty easy though.

For most people that ended up with As, they either ride on their own work, or the work of someone else. Lie about being unskilled in the first lab, that way youll get placed with people who actually have a work ethic. This class gives more work than a 3 credit if youre doing everything on your own. Try not to lose any points EVER.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--So many papers

reviews.comment: The Lectures are unnecessary and boring and the amount of time spent on the class isn't worth the number of credit hours.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Lectures are unnecessary and the amount of time spent on the class isn't worth the number of credit hours
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: The lecture is no really necessary. Most of the things Prof. Morgan teaches in the lecture can be found online. Prof. Morgan is really nice, but her explanation and teaching are really confusing. ( She doesn't do well on "teaching" us anything, we as student figure it out most of the time)
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: Lectures are the most useless and obvious things in the world, but attendance for those is a huge portion of the grade. Pray that you get a good group because you may just end up writing half a paper meant to be written by 4 people. Also, don't schedule a class during open lab hours or else you wont be able to work on projects
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Group projects

reviews.comment: Morgan does not care about her students or their success. Her lecture is basically mandatory because she does in class assignments which are a large part of your grade. There is nothing valuable that she teaches in lecture, I'm convicted she does those assignments to boost her own ego. Dont take a class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Group projects

reviews.comment: This class was pretty easy. She is very good at explaining things. Attendance is not mandatory, but you have lecture assignments that are 20% of your grade, so you can choose if it is worth it to skip. Most of the work for this class happens in the lab, where you do receive attendance points. The lab is group work based.

Went to less than half the lectures and still got a 47% on the finals and STILL 4.0 the class. The lectures were extremely stupid, and I promise, you won't learn a thing from them. The class is group project heavy, so if you get a bad group, you're in huge trouble. Dr. Morgan also looks and sounds like a porcelain doll. Blatant cash grab from MSU!
the lecture is so boring but theres only two tests that aren't that bad at all
Pretty easy class, There's only two exams: miderm and final
Doesn't actually do any teaching. She just reads articles the entire class period and then gives you a lecture assignment to do in 10 minutes when you really need 20.

Almost all the important stuff happens in lab. Hope you get a good group. Occasionally, there will be lecture assignments and those are the only weeks you need to go to class. Don't expect to learn anything very useful in lecture. Dr. Morgan is nice, but not a great prof. There are readings assigned but not checked, and nobody does.

The lecture is completely disconnected from the lab. 50% of the overall grade is group projects. If in a dysfunctional group for the two group projects then getting a 4.0 will be extremely difficult. The lectures are simplistic yet in-class assignments are 10% of grade so skipping is not advised. The final is nearly verbatim from lecture slides.
So boring and the easiest information being explained over and over for hours

Dr. Yelinek is a lovely professor, and SOC 241 is such an easy course! Your grade is made up of (open-note) exams and in-class assignments, but both are super easy. She is a caring professor who facilitates interesting conversations throughout her lectures. I strongly recommend taking this course with Dr. Yelinek - it's an easy 4.0!

It's very easy to four point her class as there's only two things that the grades hinge upon: in class exercises and exams. Both are open note and pretty easy to complete. If you're not coming to class, make sure to have D2L open on your phone or computer at least twenty minutes before class ends!! Lots of discussions but they aren't enforced.

This class was really easy but mandatory attendance (in class assignments and sign in sheet). There are no tests or finals, but one group project which is a presentation. Professor Yelinek is really nice but also lowk a talker and class always goes the whole time.

Amazing professor that presents the information in a way that makes it easy to understand. In class activities are randomly at the end of class and cannot be made up, so thats kinda an attendance aspect of the class. No homework, 5 total quizzes that are open note and not in class. Honors option is a very short in-class presentation - do it!!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The professor is very nice, and she knows what she is talking about. However, she talks for so long that time gets away from her and you will go over the end time for the class. That is really my only issue with her but this is an easy class where you do learn a lot from it.
I absolutely loved Dr. Yelinek! She is such an empathetic and warm individual, and her lectures are very engaging and thought provoking.

Dr. Yelinek was great and I really enjoyed her lectures. Just come to class as in-class quizzes are a good chunk of your grade (but they are super simple and based on the lecture you just listened to). Quizzes were online and you didn't have to come to class for them. Just go to class and you should do great.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Yelinek is extremely kind. No homework, quizzes online and not taken in class and open note. There is a book you're supposed to buy but you can pass without it. Attendance not mandatory but I would recommend coming because sometimes she has mini pop quizzes at the end of class.

Great teacher but often talks specifically about my culture (and no others usually) as highly conservative and dogmatic. As an atheist and the only person from that ethnicity in class I felt it erased my experience and made it seem like I have to represent my ethnicity/a religion i don't even believe in instead of just be a student in class.

Second class I've taken from this Professor. Quizzes are straight forward and slides were uploaded to D2L. Occasionally short, open note quizzes at the end of the class. Highly recommend if you need an easier but still interesting class.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Yelinek is very nice, but attendance is very mandatory and her rubrics and assignment guidelines were pretty unclear. Lectures sometimes got out early and were very boring.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Dr. Yelinek seems strict but just values attendance (don't take if you can't attend classes; expect brief in-class quizzes). All exams were online, remote, and open note, but were, in my opinion, ill-timed (very brief). Lectures were a bit repetitive, but exams reflected the slides and in-class quizzes. Free online textbook.
Started off by saying she didn't believe in race. Would mark you absent even if you were there and had proof, if you had evidence of you being sick she still wouldn't care.

Already signed up to take her course next sem. Very understanding and very caring. Topics are interesting which made me participate in class willingly (and she never calls on students who don't have their hand up). Also is considerate about poor weather conditions.

Her guidelines were very unclear, and she would post conflicting guidelines for the same assignment, making it confusing what was expected. She takes an unreasonably long time to grade assignments, making it nearly impossible to know what your grade currently is. The lectures are boring and long. There were a couple of interesting assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Super easy 4.0 class. NO EXAMS - only open note quizzes (no class on those days). No homework & no projects!

Random "in class exercises" which is a short, opennote quiz at the end of class that usually has a 10 minute timer. They can not be made up and none are dropped. Sit next to the same people since we always turn and talk.

This is okay if you're taking it as an elective. If this is something you actually care about I wouldn't recommend it with this professor. She seems nice, the lectures are just so unengaging and a bore to sit through. She shows a lot of old clips from youtube and TED talks rather than explaining material herself.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Class is easy, but lectures are painful. Constant clips of outdated TED talks and other BS. Nothing too thought-provoking if you actually want to learn something. I feel like this is what the entire SOC department at MSU has become.

personally, i loved this course. exams were online, open note and typically ?s were pulled right from her slides. she does just read off slides but we also have a lot of class discussions which i enjoyed. attendance assignments are random. i took this as an elective and it was a simple 4.0 if you show up.

She uses slides made by the textbook and just turns around and reads straight from them. No expanding or teaching. She also likes to bring up politics here and there which is off-putting for some. Overall she is a very nice person but not a great teacher.

Too easy and boring for a 300-level class. Learned nothing, and Prof Yelinek clearly doesn't want to be there. Most days, we just watched videos/ movies, and random others took over teaching. If you need an easy elective, take it I guess, but if you're a soc major, you will be bored to death and have wasted your money. DO NOT BUY THE TEXTBOOK.

Very insistent on class participation and didn't excuse the absence of students in a gas leak until people complained to higher ups about being unable to commute to class. Was very rude about her attendance policy and didn't let students do the attendance quiz while they got ready or were walking out the door. Also no sick policy without note
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I was not a fan of the way this class was formatted. If you plan on not attending-- you will not pass. I also stopped using the text after the 4/5 week... She will not excuse you (despite what the syllabus says). I know a student had to come to class with a positive covid test. It was hard to talk to her as well, it was draining. Super easy though.

I took this as an elective and loved Professor Yelinek. She is so caring and considerate. Attendance isnt mandatory, but she would do random VERY easy quizzes in class that if you missed you could not retake. She had quizzes after each topic and they were decently lengthy, but easy open-note and taken from home with no class those days (amazing.).

Attendance is VERY mandatory. Sign in sheet at every class. A good chunk of the work is done in trios You all get the same grade regardless of what portions you do and what portions your partners do. Choice of a presentation or a paper as a final. Rarely answers emails. Is mostly understanding of personal events. Rubrics could be clearer.

I did not like this professor. The quizzes were online but she got crazy about attendance. She gave multiple students 0 on the in class participation even if they were in class. Not very good professionalism. All her lecture slides were posted on d2l and in class all she did was read off the slides so it was kind of pointless to go

Dr. Yelinek is a wonderful person, but her teaching style is not my forte. Attendance is overdone; she made a student come to class with covid (even with proof) for attendance points. I didn't use the textbook after like the 4th week, class can be 4.0-ed with your eyes closed. Not a lot of work, hardest part is the attendance for sure

This class is fairly easy. Classes are lectures, but all lecture notes are online. Attendance isn't mandatory, but she does do random in class assignments that are worth points. There are 5 open note, online quizzes and no exams. She also gave us a extra credit quiz at the end of the semester for up to 70 bonus points (which is a lot).

class was easy, tests were all online. Yelinek is crazy about attendance. I showed up to every lecture and completed her attendance checks on d2L. More than once she changed my grade to 0 and claimed that I wasn't in class and wouldn't change it back unless I emailed her multiple times. She is not professional AT ALL.

Don't take this class if you genuinely want to be taught the material, every week a different group has to give you a presentation where nobody actually retains or feels interested in. Also Jodi, has lots of good and bad days, and I feel like she personally took her bad days out on her class. It made me feel like I was getting group punishment.

This class was super easy and all of the tests were online. She is also very nice. The only downside is that she is a little bit crazy when it comes to attendance. Overall though this class was very interesting and I definitely enjoyed it. I would 100% take it again.
She was just very difficult to work with when I talked with her I always ended up feeling worse.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Quizzes are easy as long as you take good notes, they are timed a minute a question though (not as bad as you think). Textbook is informative and helps as well. Sometimes the ends of lectures were rushed, but overall a good class and would definitely take it again.

All quizzes were online, taken at home, open note and open textbook (super duper easy). There was no final exam. Attendance is not mandatory, but there are in class assignments that are due during classtime on random days. Dr Yelinek is kind and very knowledgeable about her field. Her class is extremely easy!

I would have been more willing to honor the mandatory attendance policy if half of the lectures weren't student group projects. You can pass this class if you just show up, but class is deeply boring. Overall Dr. Yelinek is fine and has clear assignment criteria.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This was such a great course. No homework, only tests and puts online and easily can get over 100% with extra credit questions. NO FINAL. If you want, you can take the course completely online because she just assigns an "in-person" assignment randomly but you can easily complete it from home just watching from D2L in the quizzes section.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: she spreads so much misinformation in this class from claiming and showing us videos of people espousing the benefits of supplements to cure or treat bipolar disorder and yet as a sociology professor describing her own family, she says "low" or "high" functioning autism which is not accepted by the wider medical community; also very disorganized
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I think Dr. Yelinek needs to do some self-reflection. Regardless, if you do the work and go to all classes, you'll get a 4. Quite a few things contradict her syllabus regarding grading, but if you bring it to her attention, she will make amends. I've noticed she also likes to take off points for minuscule reasons.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: Jodi is a suuuuuper kind person and you can tell how much she cares about her students and the topics at hand. I was not a big fan of SOC350, I felt like there were so many random assignment requirements amongst other things that made the class 10x harder than it needed to be. I wish I had taken a dif class with her, but I really like her!
As long as you show up to class and pay attention you'll be just fine. She asks questions about your experiences as it relates to the course and I encourage you to respond.

Dr Yelinek was amazing!!  She is so sweet and you can tell shew is genuinely passionate about the topics she teaches about.  The class was so easy and there was so much extra credit offered that if you did not get a 4.0 that's on you.  She said the book was mandatory but I just took notes from the slides and I was just fine.  Take this course!

honestly prof yelinek is a super sweet person outside if class, but she does not answer questions, doesn't help her students, and grades SOOO harshly with no chance to fix anything. also doesn't grade in a timely manner so you never know your grade in the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: The only negative I would give is grading criteria doesn't match the syllabus. Otherwise, she was very caring towards students and willing to help when needed. Class was mostly lectures and projects, homework was straightforward. She's a good professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Love professor Yelinek. Would 100% take any of her classes again. She really connects with the entire class. But she does not give trigger warnings for some of the lectures. Which some of them might be needed. But love love loveee her.

Was an amazing professor very caring! The extra credit quizzes and presentation helped if you missed an actual quiz. Only 6 quizzes the whole semester try not to miss them because making them up will be hard. There is a textbook but I found it free online

Dr. Yelinek is by far one of my favorite professors I have ever had. She is kind and caring towards her students and is very understanding. The grade is based on weekly page papers based on reading and lectures. There is  1 final paper and 1 film paper, and a group presentation. Overall great course, and would recommend it even for an elective.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Yelinek grades entirely on the 6 quizzes given in class (600 pts). This is the only thing that determines your grade. It's nice as there is no homework but you have to do well on the quizzes. There is no final and there's a large EC opportunity at end of semester. I'm taking her again next semester
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Yelinek's class is a good choice. She takes attendance every class but if you miss a class you get points taken off for weekly assignments. So, if you don't plan on showing up, take a different class. If you follow the rubric and assignment directions you will get a 4.0. She is super nice and helped me a lot with learning APA formatting.

Dr. Yelinek is a great professor and cares about her students. There are no quizzes or exams in this course. You are grades on weekly papers based on the textbook, a group presentation, film analysis and a final project or paper. I 10/10 recommend this class you will learn so much useful information.

This class was so easy. Your grade is made up of 6 Quizzes that are only 25 questions each, so no homework. The quizzes are not difficult either, and she offers a huge extra credit assignment at the end of the semester. This class is a guaranteed 4.0!! I spent 30 min max on this class a week.

Would highly recommend her if you need to take this course. Quizzes are easy A's as long as you review the material and take notes.(open note & textbook). Material itself isn't difficult either. I actually forgot to take one of the quizzes and would've lost a 6th of my grade but she opened an EC quiz that made up for the lost points.

She is an amazing professor and incredibly caring. One of the easiest classes to pass if you follow her instructions. There are weekly reviews, one presentations and a few papers. She grades very well! Take this class and you won't regret it.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Really simple class with only 6 quizzes that made up your grade. Excellent professor with solid lectures and someone who wants you to succeed! Helpful for a senior looking for some upper level electives to easily 4.0.

Professor Yelinek is a great educator and wants you to succeed. One thing about the class that I disliked was the amount of busywork this course had. Get ready for weekly assignments, group presentations, several dense papers, and a large final project. You will have to work for a good grade!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Yelinek's class was very straight to the point and manageable. She allowed all the quizzes to be open book/note/powerpoint. The grading was made up of 6 100 point quizzes and offered an 80 point extra credit quiz! You can tell she wants her students to earn a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: I have absolutely no respect for Dr. Yelinek after taking her class. She promotes stigma against mental illness which I ultimately had to go to the dean about. Requires cameras to be on and screenshots you if you are on your phone or laying down. Told us getting covid is not an excuse to miss class. Ironic for this being a mental health class
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I loved Dr. Yelinek! She was very polite and the only grades in her class were quizzes that were every other week and if you payed attention in class they were very easy and often straight from the power points! I hope to take another class with Dr. Yelinek.

Professor Yelinek mainly read from power points for the majority of the class period, which became kind of redundant since the powerpoints were already posted on D2L for all of us to look at. Class participation wasn't mandatory, and I was able to get a 4.0 by just reading the notes on my own. The class should have been asynchronous.

I liked her a lot got over a 100% in her class. Randomly take attendance for participation pts, you never knew which day she would do it. She gave a 50 pt EC opportunity, then she even gave us another 50 pt one at the end of semester. Class is only graded on 5 noncumulative quizzes and the participation points. Doesn't believe in hard quizzes.

Professor Yelinek was super sweet, and she was very understanding if there was ever an issue. She is not a hard grader, and there were no exams or quizzes in the class. Your grade is based off of simple in class assignments and a final paper. I would recommend taking her if you want an easy 4.0.

I love professor Yelinek! If you show up and take notes and look them over before the exams, you will pass every one with an A! Her exams are easy, her lectures are clear, and she is accommodating to students. It is very test heavy but very doable. She also offered us 50 points extra credit (when most exams are worth 100). Highly recommend!

She was not interesting, read directly off her power point slides, but the quizzes were very easy and pretty common sense based. There was never homework. The extra credit offered at the beginning of the year was awesome, but she was boring. No final exam though which was nice. Easy class overall.

I could tell she truly liked what she taught. Her lectures were well organized and relevant to what was on the quizzes. She does a extra credit project to help boost your grade. If you take notes/ pay attention during class you will be fine. She is a very nice lady and makes class enjoyable.

Professor Yelinek is probably one of the best MSU professors there is! She lets you out of class ATLEAST 30 minutes early each time and there is opportunity for extra credit, and it's fairly simple. The quizzes (which are essentially exams) are quite straightforward and the study guides are very helpful. Highly reccomend this class & Prof. Yelinek!

Jodi is probably one of the best professors I've ever taken. ISS 318 talks about lifespan development and it's by far the easiest ISS i've ever took. She is very passionate in her work and her grading is very simplistic. Half the points you earn, the other half you can earn your own way. Would recommend professor!!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: One of the only ISS210 profs to not do open note quizzes. Quizzes are on basically nothing from the notes or lectures. She likes to talk about things that are irrelevant to the class and gets off topic a lot. She's super nice and there's no homework though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: A lot of people complain about him, but if you just show up and do what you're supposed to do you'll get an A. You have to participate to get a good participation grade. He did go completely off syllabus, but he emails you weekly letting you know which articles we will be discussing in class. There are a few group projects which can be annoying

by week 2 he had gone completely off syllabus and free styled the entire semester. puts people on the spot & interrupts them. constantly makes us sit in a circle, then at our desks, then repeat. impossible to take notes. made up an assignment on the spot as an “apology” for telling us the wrong exam time & because it was so hard.

Extremely vague grading criteria for all assignments. Assignment instructions very unclear. He grades participation and attendance but I did not miss a single class and got 80%. The assignments are very easy, but extremely frustrating.

Worst explanations/teaching style. Will cut people off mid-explanation. He has no rubrics for his assignments + makes them due at random times when most people have class. Doesn't give you an explanation as to why you did poorly on an assignment. Took a CSE class in the same semester and did worse in this class… your grade is truly a flip of a coin

While he does lecture, most of class time is spent discussing material from the readings or making group videos summarizing the material. Disorganized D2L setup and confusing discussion prompts. I understand why he has mandatory attendance; everyone would skip otherwise. Seems like a nice guy, this is just the weirdest class I've ever taken.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: super easy grader, just a lot of writing and reading. interesting class but a bit content heavy, i didn't mind. super understanding and responsive
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: This class is a joke. The professor gives us tons of readings every class but half the people just skip the reading and do as well as the people who read. He gives us *graded* attendance based on videos we have to make outside doing fake coach interviews, running football plays and recreating historical photos. Idk how this guy has a job here.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is a professor who genuinely cares about the success of his students. He is kind, accommodating, and definitely enjoys teaching. Exams are straightforward and come directly from what we learn in class. Show up to class, take notes, and you will excel. Interesting content, thought-provoking lectures, I would highly recommend him as a professor.

I had him for a seminar and he was really good. He was really invested in making sure we understood the topics being covered and he really seemed to enjoy what he was talking about. There was a lot of reading involved but most of the time you could skim and would be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He actually did not follow his own rubric, he had a tuition assistant do the complete lecture and grading for the class. This meant that the TA gave people across the board grading and this across the board grading was low. Pescador for his part supported the TA and claimed no wrongdoing or fixed the problem. He is an academic gangster at MSU.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Had him for an honors seminar. Lots of reading but he was enjoyable to listen to and good at starting discussion. He also gave good feedback on papers.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Lectures difficult to understand, posts all lectures online. Seven 1000 word min. essays (70% of grade). Final research paper (3500 words, 20% of grade). Participation taken every class (10% of grade). The 7 essays are tolerable, all based on readings (approx. 3 per week) and lectures. Difficult if you don't like writing, easy if you like history.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: The guidelines for some assignments were different on D2L than what he would say in class. He only took attendance whenever he felt like it, and gave me a 100 on participation because I talked sometimes even though I barely showed up. Exams were all written, and there were a lot of essays which he doesn't talk about in class so look online!

This class was completely terrible. Totally not what I expected. I expected to learn and to enjoy this class but I was completely let down. He is hard to understand, does not give clear instructions, and gives horrible feedback to the assignment that you do complete. When answering your questions, he is vague and gives no context.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: There are a lot of readings outside of class and a lot of review essays that are due. Very hard grader and doesnt explain why you got your answers wrong. Reads off the slides!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Prof Pescador is seriously just awful. Reads off the slides. Takes attendence. Forces people into groups in a 300 person lecture hall and makes people talk in front of the class. He takes attendance which is 10% of your final grade. The class and content itself is easy. Your assigments are just weekly essays with one group project in the middle.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Good professor. He assigns a lot of reading but you don't really have to read that much of it but you will need them to use them as sources for papers. The papers can be tedious and require a lot of sources but he's pretty lenient on grading them and grading in general. Overall not a bad class, just make sure you actually are into history.

Horrible professor. He does not respond to emails and provided us with contradictory information in the guidelines of our first essay. He also reads directly off of his slides and then forced ~10 students to come to the front of the class and regurgitate the details of some article concerning an unimportant time period in athletic history.

Pescador's section is much more difficult than Fisher's. Fisher's class has 1 paper, written in a group. Pescador's has 3, 4 page papers and a final 16 page group project. TRUST ME, just try to get into fisher's, if not wait until another semester. He also grades pretty tough also. nice guy.

this is by far the worst class i have ever taken. everybody in the class fails the exams and he curves your final grade based on the average of the class. says his goal is to make you switch your major to economics but if anything this class has taught me i never want to even touch this subject again.

Do not take Econ with him if you're someone who struggles with the subject. He overcomplicates things and uses confusing examples. He is a nice guy and is clearly passionate, but because of that he lectures as though everybody in the class is ALSO an expert. Better off taking this class online.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Didn't look both ways before crossing the street on my way to this class. He uses top hat but you can do it outside of class and get your attendance points there. He only reads off slides and when he does write down examples his handwriting is tiny. I would gain the same amount of information by reading the slides on my own.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This was one of the worst classes I took. If you aren't going to be completely present every class it's very hard to succeed. He rights notes on the board that you can't read or even understand most of the time. lowkey gonna fail so i wouldn't take ts

Prof kang is clearly a very passionate and nice professor about econ but he tries way too hard to make you an econ major and his accent is super thick. He goes only reads off the slides and goes through them quickly. Overall has good intentions but doesn't go about it very well.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He gives super boring lectures, really wants you to switch to econ a a major (which i never will). Also mandatory quizzes every class for attedance. But tbh you can just pay attention during the review sessions and he gives you thr awnser to like 80% of the exam. Just study those

Professor Kang is alright. He doesn't really teach the concepts he just clicks through his slide presentations very quickly and gives confusing examples. He is a very sweet guy that's passionate about what he teaches, but he just doesn't teach it in an effective way. Exams are worth big chunk of grade, but almost identical to practice tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Kang is a very loving and enthusiastic professor. Unfortunately he has a very thick accent and it was nearly impossible to understand him throughout the lecture.

EC 201 is an econ class, so what did you expect. As an intro class, though, there's a lot of work and it's heavily graded on exams. The in-class check in's make sure we go to class, and the homework doesn't really contribute to the final grade, although it somewhat helps. Prof Kang loves what he is teaching and it shows, it's just hard sometimes.
Just started taking this class and already know it's gonna be good
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Very passionate, however sometimes hard to understand. 3 mid terms throughout semester with cheat sheet weren't too hard. Is not lenient with rounding up grades. Attendance isnt required but you get a lot of points from pop quizzes that happened almost every day ranging from 2-5 questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Kang is great! He covers a lot of content in his classes, so if you take notes, you're good, class syllabus he covers is a total of about 17 chapters. He takes pop quizzes in class as he teaches and uses those to mark attendance. Exams are not hard, they are multiple-choice, he takes questions directly from the end of chapter questions.

The class isn't too bad, and as long as you take notes during lectures, you shouldn't have to study too hard for the exams. The questions and answers from the practice exams are used on the actual exams, so pay attention to those. He uses TopHat for attendance points but you can answer them from your dorm.

As long as you pay attention during lectures and do the top hat homework you will be fine. Homework is graded for completion (super nice compared to other 201 classes). You get a full page cheat sheet for each exam. The class before each exam he does a review session and if you write all of that down and put it on your cheat sheet you'll be good.

He is a very caring professor if you go and talk to him, however during the lectures, he is hard to understand and there are many pop quizzes that are worth a grade. I would say that the class average for exams are in the 50-60 percent range. He uses top hat to go through the lectures and doesn't really teach the content just reads it off.

He has a very thick accent and moves quickly in class. There are in class quizzes that are taken for credit and based on accuracy. All the homework is on TopHat and it's for completion. Exam's are a big part of your grade, but they're almost identical to the sample tests. He tells you exactly what will be on the exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor uses TopHat for textbook and quizzes in class. You can do tophat from anywhere and thats how he takes attendance so keep that in mind. Gives you a cheat sheet for all exams including the final.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: just pay attention in lecture and know the material for the three tests (two midterms and final)

Do not listen to the negative reviews. They are made by people who sit on their phone or talk the whole lecture. It is the easiest class to 4.0 that I have taken at MSU. The expectations are clear from the start and he constantly gives away exam answers. If you pay attention and take notes you'll have a 4.0 before even taking the final.

Prof. Kang sets clear, basic expectations with TopHat quizzes and homework. Each exam has a review session where he literally spells out what questions will be on the exam. He literally tells you the questions and answers them... It can still be a challenging class, obviously, but that's the content. Don't hate the player, hate the game.
A good professor overall. Can be a little difficult to hear/read his handwriting sometimes, but not too bad. He did a good job of teaching most of the content, but he relies heavily on TopHat.

I absolutely loved Professor Kang. If you are willing to pay attention, I can assure you will actually retain the concepts. Really take the review sessions serious, they are beyond helpful. His office hours truly made me succeed in this course.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: The exams are a lot like the practice tests that he gives you, as a lot of the answers are the same. As long as you take notes and do the participation points, and homework you should be fine. I really liked him as a person too.

Genuinely don't know how he's still allowed to teach at MSU. Only cares about 15% of class getting 4.0. Grades on a curve instead of curving the exams themselves. Doesn't teach, just clicks through slides fast and does confusing examples. Class is way harder then it needs to be. Everything is online, even exams. You're better off taking online.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Teaches at an extremely fast pace and is very difficult to understand. Exams are very lecture heavy and attendance is required.

Professor Kang is a simple professor who expects the students to understand the material taught in the lectures and then apply the knowledge on the exams. If you pay attention in his class it's not that difficult of material being taught. He is difficult to understand at times and his handwriting isn't the best. Take good notes and you'll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Do NOT take Econ201 with Kiwon. Very difficult to follow due to his monotone voice, fast-paced content and horrible handwriting. Class avg my semester was below a 60% on exams and progressively dropped with each exam. Attendance is very important as he has live questions online that are directly related to content that is projected on the screen.

Kiwon Kang is by far the worst professor I've ever had the misfortune of having. His lectures are dull and his voice exceedingly monotone. A majority of my fellow students in this course are boasting 50 percents or lower, with the class average even being below 50%. Homework usually consists of over 40 questions every night. Do not recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Kang knows his stuff, but he goes really fast in his lectures because he covers a lot of things, especially on exam reviews! Attendance isn't mandatory but he always has pop quizzes in class which are graded. Overall he's an ok teacher if you're good at econ and focus really hard and pay attention. The 3 exams are major parts of the grade
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Do not take this class. Take EC 201 with ANYONE else. I have never in my life failed a class, let alone one that I did every homework question and attended every lecture. The exams are impossible and the TopHat is extremely hard to study. He said that even though he had HUNDREDS of emails asking for extra credit, there was nothing he could do.

Literally the worst professor I have had at MSU. I can get past his accent, as most of my professors have thick accents, but he is so monotone when talking about an already boring topic. He also has a very low score average, making 65%s curve up to 80%s. If you can take a different econ professor, I highly encourage it.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Goes at fast pace, gives pop up quizzes on things that his lecture doesn't really target.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Kang is a good professor who has a lot of office hours. In large classroom settings, it can be hard to hear and understand him in the back so you will need to sit closer to the front.
Tests are very difficult, but he will work with you to help you improve your grade if you reach out for help. One chapter of reading assigned per class. Very nice guy.

I cannot pass this class and I'm usually good at econ. The tests are impossible, with the class average being 50% on both exams. I currently have a 47% in the class, but he never made it clear where i would end up after the curve. I wouldnt recommend him to anyone, and I wish someone warned me not to take his class. My GPA is going to plummet!
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Tests are insane with class averages below 55%. So much content with barely anytime to comprehend it. Just regurgitates the text book for an hour and 20. You can not pay attention even if you enjoy the subject matter
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is miserable to listen to. You cannot pay attention if you tried. Tests are curved but whole does so poorly it doesn't change anything. Grade scale is by class percentage not by points so your will be a surprise . Tests are hard and not enough to buffer any bad grades.
How does one pass this class without needing to cheat this bro aint teaching nothing

He lectures two chapters ahead of the weekly homework's and moves through the lecture so fast that it's nearly impossible to take notes. The tests are incredibly difficult and your score is pretty much irrelevant, all that matters is the curve. Also be familiar with TopHat because that's all he uses.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Had him this semester and this is the hardest class I've ever taken. Tests are very hard and make up most of your grade. Homework and popup quizzes will help your grade. You have to teach yourself and honestly pray you do well. Final grade is curved, based on percentile of the class. This class makes me wanna cry ngl. Lucky if I finish with a 2.5.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Cannot read the room to save his life, and blamed the students for not understanding a concept that he spent less than 5 minutes on. His teaching style is probably fine for some but it wasn't for me. Really important to listen in lectures but prepare to teach yourself out of the textbook.
But it's an econ class so it wasn't hard
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Kang seems like a humble, sweet guy, but his class was just not for me. He is available outside of class and is often flexible with make-ups. If you don't read the textbook or actively pay attention in class, you will do horrible. Even if you do pay attention, its very possible that you won't do as well as you would hope. I think we all struggled

Professor Kang really cares about his students and wants to make sure you succeed. Overall the class is tough and you will definitely need to put in the work to do well. You have to attend the zooms because he does attendance questions, and you will also want to make sure you take good notes to do well on the exams. I would definitely recommend him
Grades on a curve, also he let me redo the final so he's very flexible. Good man.

He is a great professor and cares about his students. He allows extensions and gives help during his office hours. Quizzes are given during class, so going to class is mandatory. The exams make up most of your grade and are hard, so you need to study hard. He curves your grade at the end of the year. You need to take lots of notes in class.

Kang cares about his students and makes himself available for extra guidance. Everything is based on TopHat, so you don't technically have to be in class to get credit, but I would recommend it. The exams are tough but your GPA is based on a curve, so even though I got a B in the class I still finished with a 4.0. Overall, a solid professor.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was fine, but Professor Kang is hard to understand sometimes. It's nice that your exam grades can be balanced out by homework and attendance points. He's always willing to help and is accommodating.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This man changed my life in the world of Micro Economics, he is an amazing lecturer as well as a respected faculty member, 11/10 would recommend.
His lectures always contain humor. As long as you do ok on the midterms, he gives a pretty generous curve.
being not a business major, this class got very repetitive and boring. just go to class and read the notes and you'll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Professor Kang tries really hard to make sure everyone understands his lectures, but microeconomics is just a tough class. Make sure to take the quizzes on TopHat seriously, because those will affect your grade and they help prepare you for exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I can tell he's good at what he does, but some people may find it hard to understand him. Therefore some may find the class to be difficult. Overall he's a decent professor.

Prof. Kang has a very thick accent and if you have trouble with accents I'd recommend finding a different professor. Attendance is necessary for lecture notes. Exams are graded on a curve, a total of two midterms and a final exam. Homework is done through Pearson MyEconLab with a total of 22 assignments.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Greatest teacher I ever had.
Do not need to show up to class Cant understand him Nice person Big curve Need your book and do the online hw

Good teacher, at times hard to understand, but text is clear, and easy to understand. Generous curve. Recommended if your motivated and interested, if not  his accent may make the class difficult for you.

This class was extreamly difficult to stay focused in becasue Kang is not so easy to follow in his venacular. But on the up side she is very helpful and intellegent. You have to keep up on readings becasue he is hard to understand at times. He basis he tests of his lectures so although attendences isn't required I would still go to class.

The best professor I have had yet at MSU.  I hate econ, but he actually made it tolerably.  He knows a lot, and if you go to his office hours he makes it easy to understand.  His teaching style was highly effective for me, so I recommend taking this class with him.
Speaks good english but sometimes I had a hard time understanding him, he was very helpful to those who asked and paid attention

I would rather chew off my hand than take this class again. I have always done well in school, and I went to every lecture and studied after every class, and even had a tutor I met with twice a week for two hours, but it still barely helped. The problem is that the stuff in the homework and lectures is NOTHING like the content on the tests!!!!!!!!!

Dr K was a good professor and she explains the concepts of general chemistry very well. Most of the grading besides testing was based on completion and often times I found myself struggling on the tests because I felt that I did not receive much feedback in general. Use your peers and ULAs in recitation to your advantage!

i am taking her class again for chem 142 but she does explain things pretty well and explains things very well when it comes to the time of having to take a test and or what homework we have to get done the ta's were pretty good but overall you have to go to lecture if u want to do well.

She explained things very clearly and concisely; however, upon talking to other students, they said they either really liked her or really didn't. I myself liked her, but just be aware of the polar opposites.

I was nervous about taking chemistry for the first time, but it became my favorite subject. This professor explains concepts clearly, and what she teaches matches the exams. The iClicker questions each lecture are engaging and count as attendance, with only two drops allowed. I wish I could take her for every class—she is the best professor.

If you pay attention and study outside of class, you'll be absolutely fine. She clearly cares about her students' success. There are extra credit opportunities and your final grade replaces your lowest exam if beneficial. All the people that hate her just didn't try, its easy if you read the textbook and do the homework, which a reasonable amount.

It's a miracle I got a B in this class. It made me miserable and I hated going but attendance is mandatory and I needed every point I could get. Set up reminders for the homework, there is LOTS of it (it's easy and graded by completion.) Unfortunately i've heard bad things about the other MSU Chem teachers so good luck.

She was very articulate and good at explaining everything in lecture very well. The slides were very accessible and easy to follow along with in class. She also recorded each of her lectures so you could go back and watch them if you missed class. The only downside was iClicker.
She's really nice, but the class is so large that you won't interact with her much.
This class is so hard and it's very exam heavy. The professor has an accent so it's really hard to understand her. She's really nice though.

Dr Kijewska was very caring and understanding but exams were so much harder than lectures, the lectures only briefly mentioned a topic and there would be a question on the exam way more difficult than we ever discussed, there was a lot of material to be covered but slides were just read off of. Don't take it with her if you can't learn self taught.

cem 141 is a course with LOTS of material to cover. I liked this course, but she may have to rush through a few lectures because of how little time she has. She records all of her lectures, so it definitely helps to go back and watch them and take notes for anything you missed. If you pay attention, and study every day I promise you will be fine.

The one piece of advice I would give for this class is to listen to her when she tells you to study after every single lecture. This is a very difficult course and there is a lot of information you have to learn. Looking back on the BeSocratic assignments and the recitation activities is also very helpful before exams.

Lectures=life. lectures quick and doesn't waste time, stay awake and alert. study after each lecture- and do all homework (quite a lot) do practice exams, show up to class, go to recitation and you'll be fine. textbook is easy to read and connects. besocratic was annoying, but was graded and ec opportunities were given.

missing a day of lecture can set you really far behind. The CLUE online textbook was very easy to follow, and a quick read once you know what you're supposed to be reading. (you can skip a lot of the examples and equation stuff shown, it gets repetitive with the lecture notes)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class was super easy to pass if you put in the work! She explains things well, but you do have to study a little outside of class, but tests are very fair. She is a caring professor and I def recommend.

I hate CEM 141. I can't understand anything my professor tries to teach us. I thought I just didn't understand chemistry, but when a fill-in professor taught us during our professor's absences, I understood so much better. Try to avoid her.
she isnt as bad as people make her out to be. when she says anyone else for the iclicker questions its a little annoying but you get used to it
