She talks extremely fast and goes through the lecture slides before you can get the information down. The practice exams given are not very helpful. Take detailed notes and spend every day outside of class reviewing notes and you'll be fine.
She was fine. I think the class is fair. I have never taken chemistry before, and I did good, if you try youll be fine.

I think that chemistry is seen as a difficult topic to begin with and this professor made is much easier than without her! No matter which professor you have this class is going to require you to study but she gave us amazing resources and outlined things to know for the exam.I am actually taking her next semester!

She is a very kind and passionate professor. Her class is very well organized and her lectures are very easy to grasp. I didn't feel the need to study and aced the exams (though everyone is different!). Attendance is mandatory though and is done through iclicker so make sure that your iclicker is reliable (unlike mine).

She is the sweetest, she records lectures and tells you exactly what is needed to pass. Unfortunately for me I will be retaking this class by no fault of hers. You need to pay attention and study everyday, this class it is very important to understand everything you are learning.

With iClicker attendance only being 5% of the final grade, I found it easier to simply watch the lecture videos on 3x outside of class, as she moves very slowly. This class has many resources to help you succeed, should be easy 3-4.0

The lectures end up being very short because she spends a large amount of time at the beginning of class reviewing homework and restating prior lessons. Usually the concepts are simple to grasp and the homework is easy

Prof K as a prof is good. She tells you how to succeed. My only issues are with the class. The material is pretty straightforward in class and on homework, but the exams are just stupid. Very unclear in the wording, makes me think this is a weeder class.

Goes a little fast in lectures but posts them after so you can go back and fill in your notes if you missed anything. You have to pay attention in class but if you do it's pretty easy to follow along. The exams will be hard. Study, study, study. If you put in effort, you will do fine.
Amazing lectures, super great at explaining concepts and makes sure class understands topics before moving on.  Super kind and helpful if you ask for help.

Finish the ALEKS 100% before the deadline and the class should be a decently easy pass. Not super accommodating past the allotted absences so save them for emergencies. Lectures were actually helpful and I highly recommend going, even if you don't have to. If you survived AP Chem, it's basically a guaranteed 2.0. Chemistry is awful, she's great!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Super nice professor, some people can't get past her accent but she was super nice and her lectures were straight forward. She's a good teacher and her TAs/ULAs are amazing and super helpful during lecture. Attendance for credit is annoying but all of her lectures are recorded and up to date if you happen to miss any of them.

Prof Kijewska is super patient and caring with her students, even when course material can be overwhelming. She is always willing to go through and explain things during lecture and it makes learning super easy.

I found this class to be paced well with the professor not wasting any time during lectures. She provided ample practice activities that helped with learning. Some mandatory aspects of the course felt pointless and a waste of time that could have been spent studying and some exam questions were too vague for chem. Decent, but could be better.

Chemistry is like math, you have to try in it. I thought gen chem was fine as long as you understand what is going on and study. One of the exams can be replaced by your final score in case you do terribly or miss an exam. I missed one due to an ER visit lol but it ended up working out due to this. Study after each class.

Prof. Kijewska is an incredible teacher! Very knowledgeable w/ chemistry, explains concepts very well. Willing to explain things in detail. There's homework, recitations, EC, and exams. Prof uses iClicker for points, but the concepts help you learn more. Class is easy if you know how to apply concepts instead of memorizing facts. Dr. K is so great!

This professor was reluctant to help students who were confused, wasn't good at explaining the material. I relied on the TA's for help and they were so much better than the actual teacher. I was less than 1% away from a four point because of a grading error and didn't bother to round it up. She's a little rude, if you have her ask the TAs for help
You think you know what's going on then you open the exam and you're cooked. Tbh she gives a lot of opportunities to raise grade and I got my A so no worries! Hope this helps.

People rate her badly because they didn't show up or do homework and they're salty about it. She explains things very well, and if you don't understand her there are so many resources available. If you do the bare minimum (so what she tells you to do) you'll get a 4.0. Minimal homework too, just don't cheat on it and you're fine.

Very enthusiastic about teaching and success of her students. Attendance is required and very lecture heavy. Dr. Kijewska can talk fast at sometimes, but is very nice and will go over again. Many hours of studying for this course. Exams are only in the late evening or early mornings.

Lectures are fast paced, but if you take good notes, you'll do well. She's clearly very passionate about chemistry and wants students to do well. Exams are difficult, but the class is very organized and gives you plenty of ways to get help if you need it.

I think she's a great professor. She'll spend the first 20ish minutes going over the homework from the night before and then go into the lecture. She does talk and goes through slides fast so make sure to keep up. Also on some days, if you're lucky, she'll let you leave lecture early. There's about 3-4 TAs that are there to answer questions also.
She's honestly just your average chemistry professor. If you really put in the work and study the objectives and read the textbook, you'll be good. The TAs help answer most of my questions.

In the lectures it seems so easy and simple to understand, and I feel confident I have a grasp on the material. When i get into the exam, it's like it was written for a completely different class. The fact that this class also isn't curved when students tend to get like a 50% on the tests is beyond annoying. Also barely posts any study material.

she'll often spend the first 20 minutes or so going over homework which sometimes throws off the pacing of the lecture and it can get very fast. 
i liked that she talked the class through more difficult iclicker questions. 
homework is very very easy but can sometimes take some time. you definitely need to study for the tests though

Easy professor. Everything is graded off participation- study for exams and you're fine. Problem is her lack of empathy for students and lectures you that you don't understand the concept, but won't go back and explain it. Also, every. single. iClicker question she says, "Anybody else" in a really annoying way. Easy A, but annoyed every lecture.

When I had a death in the family and arranged grief absence, she gave me so much work to "make up what I missed" which was right before an exam and 2 weeks before the final with a stupidly short due date. I was not told of this until after I came back and saw all the zeros. Next time, I'll ask my sick family members not to die, I guess.
hated this class so much and she made it 1000x harder

Professor Kijewska has a really strong accent which can be hard to understand. She talks very fast and goes through the lecture slides very fast but there is always a lot of slides with a lot of information to retain. Must study after every class if you want to be on top of the work.

Professor Kijewska's class is challenging but rewarding. Attend lectures and recitations, and use resources like the free textbook and unstuck ai to really grasp the content. The tests are tricky, so stay engaged and you'll do well.

Lectures are pretty fast-paced, but if you develop a good note-taking system and just write the things said you'll do great. Great explainer, clearly loves chemistry, regularly surveys class with iClicker, then re-covers topics that a lot of people were (anonymously) confused about. There are no trick questions, just pay attention, loved this class
lectures were engaging, she breaks down the material well and is good at fully answering any questions asked
Lectures are easy to follow but she moves fast. Almost everything is graded based on completion.

Professor Kijewska very clearly cares a lot about chemistry. Lecture heavy with iClicker attendance, easy homework after most classes, a few extra credit assignments. If you study the material you'll do well on tests. Easy to get an A if you care to try.

She was very kind and clearly very knowledgable on the subject. If you take notes and pay attention in the lectures, you will do very well. I really appreciate that there are always multiple TAs walking around the lecture and it feels very easy to ask questions and get quick answers. Overall an enjoyable class.
Dr. K was incredible at explaining topics, very patient, and was easily reached when you needed help.

Professor Kijewska is very knowledgeable in chemistry and oozes passion, professionalism. It is important to listen to her closely, and write notes as she's speaking during lectures. If you re-write the lecture jutes in your own way after each lecture and study them for at lest 15 minutes twice a day, you'll be set for the exams.

She's a great teacher who covers a lot of material in a way that's easy to understand and manageable. She's always available during office hours to help, though sometimes she goes through the slides a bit quickly.

This class can take a lot of effort (depending on how well you grasp the subjects). Granted, CEM 141 has many students. So, this course is designed to teach roughly 300 students at a time, making it rather impersonal. SEEK HELP FROM TA'S IF NEEDED! Prof. Kijewska is not bad, but you need to put in hard work too. Her provided checklist was helpful.

Hated being immediately bombarded with Aleks topics but I know she doesn't control that. Be ready to spend a decent amount of time on those topics if you haven't taken chemistry in a while.

On a positive note, she seemed genuinely caring but did have her days. Tons of material on lectures so she did go at a very quick pace.

7/10.

Most of the lectures are her reading off of the slides and asking questions in relation to the slides, not a bad style but if you prefer more in depth learning I would choose a different professor. Exams are either 6 am or 7 pm ish which can be annoying but overall a very easy course.

There are 4 tests in the entire course and they are worth 60% of your grade, all of which are taken at 7:30 at night or at 6:00 am. She's a brutal grader, a great lady, but getting all of my points taken off for a question because I "Didn't draw an arrow long enough" in a diagram just leaves a bad taste in my mouth. Weirdly difficult for a starter.

Dr. Kijewska is a tricky little bugger. She is the sweetest human ever but is simply not fit to teach this class. She is a little feisty and appears to not enjoy her job very much. The exams are very difficult and don't even reflect a lot of what we have learned. I would not take her as a teacher again, but she seems like a lovely individual!

I was so worried going into chemistry as I've never been a science/math person but Kijewska is incredible! I actually really enjoyed her class and she did a great job at presenting the material in a way that helps you to actually understand the concepts and piece it all together. She doesn't give too much homework and has extra credit assignments.

Dr. Kijewska seems very passionate about the material. She goes a bit fast on the slides, and if you never took chem before in high school, you may struggle to keep up during class. The tests are tough and very detailed, but they match the lecture slides. Haven't read the textbook and I did just fine. Just go to class and study for the exams.

I would rather gougue my eyes out and eat them than take this class again. I had reached out multiple times to her with questions via email and gotten no response. She goes through lecture slides very quickly, and as someone that has taken a chem class in HS, I was still very confused. I attended every class and did every assignment and exam.

Lectures are super important and are graded for participation. Exams are hard and pull from lots of different sources (textbook, recitation activities, homework, and lectures). Make sure to read the textbook and go to recitations.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Anyone who has gotten below a B is probably not that bright. The content covered is honestly so easy to understand, the only possible way for you to not get a good grade is by not coming to class. The teacher is also very good and gives extra credit from time to time.

Dr. Kijewska is an extremely kind, intelligent, and skilled lecturer! She answers questions thoughtfully, and always explains things extremely slowly and intuitively in a way that guarantees everybody in the room understands the content perfectly. She scaffolds her lessons very well, and her kind disposition and passion for teaching are apparent :)
Professor Kijewska was a great teacher and all i could have asked for for CEM 141. Attendance is mandatory but she knows her stuff and will make sure that you understand the material.

This general chemistry course is very straightforward. The exams are highly dependent on the learning objectives (which students have continuous access to) as the professor mentions numerous times. YOU WILL PASS AS LONG AS YOU ATTEND CLASS, PAY ATTENTION, AND COMPLETE THE RECOMMENDED PRACTICES. The time-commitment is average for a 3-credit course.

This class is a lot of very general content all at once. Go to class, get the free points for homework and attendance and go to the help room. My best advice is to never assume you are prepared for exams, and don't wait to go to office hours or ask for help. She is super thorough and willing to help if you ask!

I think the lecture is way too info heavy and we move past things way too fast. There's no time to process what you are learning. Sometimes the homework doesn't even match up with what we're learning as well. I know some students think her accent is a little heavy and makes it difficult to understand, so be aware of that.

Dr. Kijewska gives very good lectures. She explains topics very clearly and she seems to enjoy teaching the class. she could do slightly better with pacing during the lectures as it sometimes the end of the lectures feel rushed.

Honestly, she was one of the best professors I have ever had for science. Attend lectures (preferably 3 times a week, not 2 since they can feel dragged) and she was very clear on what she grades. The exams felt aunting but when you took them they were not bad. All are based on mock exams and lecture slides which are easy to follow.Take her if u can

If I had a few hours left to live, I would spend it in her lecture. Due to the fact that each lecture seems to last an eternity. She isn't passionate about this content due to the fact that it is work that is below her knowledge level. Would not suggest this class or professor. Pay attention, do the work, and don't forget to study for exams.

This professor covered very interesting material and is passionate about the things she teaches. She sometimes dumbs-down material if you aren't good at Chemistry (I'm not,) however I had to teach myself a few topics. She often made me feel rushed and seemed passive aggressive to those who didn't understand the material, which made me uncomfortable

She posts Lecture Slide videos so if you need to review these are very helpful, Do your ALEKS as soon as possible! She seems snarky at times,sometimes she'll be like "I can't answer that, but a TA can." with a sarcastic smile. This can be frustrating. She also has an accent that isn't too hard to understand but definitely has you listening closer.

(Taken Spring 2023) 

Good lecturer, can sometimes go a little fast but is very nice with clear explanations. Her exams are easy to score high on if you follow along with the material intently. The people who scored poorly didn't study well or didn't care to set time aside to actually learn the material.

Dr. Kijewska was a fair grader and was accessible outside of class, but she often went through slides a little bit fast and some of the content was difficult to understand. The tests and important assignments are full of trick questions designed to catch the slightest slip-up, which I guess is good sometimes, but it felt excessive for this course.
This teacher was a tough grader and a poor lecturer. You will have to teach yourself. Additionally, she would make fun of in-class questions and then become upset when no one would raise their hands.

Super nice but she goes way too fast on her slides. The lectures are so important but there is not enough overlap between lecture and exam. It is hard to stay engaged but she explains very thoroughly and is great at answering questions.
All exams are terribly worded and designed to be confusing. Alecs is annoying make sure to do it.
extremely lecture heavy. goes through slides way too fast. however, if you pay attention and attend recitation you will be fine
Attend lectures and do the practice exams.
This class was enjoyable. As long as you go to lecture and recitation and pay attention you will do fine. The tests can be tricky so make sure to have a good understanding of the contetn

I went to every lecture, did all the homework, went to help room and office hours, and studies 3 weeks in advanced for exams exactly how she asked us to, and I still did terrible on every exam. When I went to her office hours to talk to her about it, she was so rude and condescending. I'm considering a career change because of her

I received an ADR from her and she would not give it up until I took it to court and got it reversed. Giving a falsely accused student an ADR and having me do all the work just to show that I did not cheat was enough for me to rank her 1 out of 5. I highly recommend switching out of her class if possible. I did not like the way she treated me.

She was a good professor; she knows how to do her job and was always open to answering questions during class and break down IClicker questions that students got wrong. Definitely wasn't an easy class so make sure you do study, and you should be fine.

This class is not at all difficult thanks to her excellent lectures. Without a doubt I would take this class again. Everything you need to for the exam is in her lectures. Just paying attention and studying guarantees a good grade. I highly recommend asking her question ASAP if confused. She always provided excellent explanations. Love her!

Dr. Kijewska was great! CEM 141 is super lecture heavy, but she had us do iClicker questions throughout the lecture, and if it seemed like people weren't understanding the questions she would break them down and go through them. She was open to questions and wouldn't judge, there was even an online anonymous form for questions.

This professor was horrible. She was super condescending. I had COVID with a doctor's note and she told me to come anyway or take the 0. Not available out of class, not helpful in class, really just a professor who made me cry a lot in a course that I needed support in and never could get.

Clear expectations. Lecture heavy, weekly homework, and 4 total exams..  Exams are based off of lectures and readings.  The content builds on itself throughout the semester so if you miss a lecture, I recommend going back and watching it - it is hard to catch back up if you fall behind.  Study after every lecture and you will do fine.

She definitely cares about her student's success and provides many resources. The first exam is by far the hardest of them all. I never read the textbook and did well in the course. Attendance is mandatory since there are iclicker questions. There is a lot of homework but it is easy and only graded on completion. Very hard to fail this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Kijewska is clearly passionate about what she teaches, and wants her students to succeed. She made sure that when students weren't understanding a certain topic to spend additional time going over it. The exams were reasonable for the content, and the homework is incredibly easy.

The professor seemed like she did not have any interest in the subject material that she was teaching and always seemed condescending towards our class like we were stupid for not understanding the material. The class itself is not too terrible, but it's easy to do somewhat poorly on exams just based on the number of questions and their worth.

Very kind professor, incredibly helpful and Cleary wants her students to do well. Homework is unbelievably easy but the content is of course difficult. Class is hard but she definitely helps with the terror.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Exams are really difficult so make sure to really study for them. she doesn't really bother to review 141 material when you need to know it for some material in this class. Class exam averages are quite low. Attendance is mandatory as there are clickers for which you need to get at least 75% of them right each lecture. Take Cangelosi, shes amazing.

Each lecture is unbelievably slow, and the professor's monotonous way of speaking does nothing to help. The concepts are explained somewhat thoroughly, but oftentimes it takes a rewatch of the lecture afterwards to properly understand it. Any examples given are extremely basic, and don't do much in the way of helping to explain the concepts.

She is very nice and good at explaining concepts. Do the homework + extra credit, go to class, go to recitation, go to office hours w/ questions, and you get a lot of free easy points. Study for the tests because they are the hardest part by far, but do well on 2 of them and you should be fine for the final.
I love chemistry but these lectures were so boring and monotone, I almost fell asleep every lecture.

She is kinda hard to understand due to accent but a good teacher. The class is VERY lecture heavy but you can easily do good in the class if you take good notes and study hard for exams. She is super nice and understanding.
Dr. Kijewska is great at lecturing and is really good at getting the point across and making chemistry easy to learn. Attendance is mandatory, and after every class there is a homework assignment.

you guys are LYING. she is not a good professor. she has never explained or written down absolutely anything in our class. please i beg you, do not take her. go for virgina cangelosi, please!!!!!!!!!!!!
She is great at giving the information you need and is willing to help in office hours out of class. highly recommend

Krystyna was the nicest professor I had my first year, she is so caring and willing to help. It was very easy to make a connection with her. If you pay attention in lecture and put in the work to understand the material rather than memorize it, you will easily ace the class.

HW is based off completion and most of class grade is exams/online ALEKS work. That said, most of exam questions are repeated HW/recitation/class activities so if you study you should get an A. Records lectures + gives EC. Love Kijewska + am taking her for CEM 142, highly recommend cause she's a sweetheart even if her class can be tough at times.

Dr. Kijewska did a great job at explaining content in a way that is easy to understand. There are a lot of easy points in her class, and you can drop one of the exams so it isn't hard to pass. She is an absolute sweetheart and I will be taking another one of her classes next semester.
She gives boring but very informative lectures, and the class is fairly easy to do well in as long as you focus or have some prior knowledge of the course.

I didn't think i would do good in chemistry and somehow passed with an A pretty easily, her lectures are very easy to follow if you just print off the notes and follow along, the homework isn't long and always goes along with what you learned. The exams are fair but you have to study a good amount, but don't read the book, got to drop a exam too
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Kijewska was lovely. She is brilliant and really cares about participation in class so that she can see how well we understood things. This class was difficult for me, but I passed with a decent grade and would honestly take a class again if she was the one teaching it. As long as you pay attention and use help rooms, you literally can't fail.

She is an amazing professor. She explains stuff clearly and explicitly while trying to connect different knowledge points. She uses an average pace to teach, which gives me enough time to absorb the knowledge. The test is really well designed since we were allowed to have 1 test grade dropped for both MC and short answer questions.

Do not read the textbook. Only use her slides and the practice tests (almost the same as the actual one). As long as you complete the ALEKS (do it asap trust me), do the homework, and pay attention in class, you will succeed in this class. She is always kind and available outside of class.

Chemistry itself is what is the hard part of this course. The professor is great. She wants you to understand the content and goes over how to succeed in the class every day before the lecture. The homework is graded based on effort and is fairly easy and the exams are graded harshly depending on the TA, but overall they're pretty easy.
Very good lecturer! Going to class is really important though and not getting behind
The structure of the lectures and the course as a whole is super followable and well-thought out. She clearly discusses the expectations from the class and as long as you follow that you'll pass!

This class is just unnecessarily hard for being the lowest level chem course available at MSU. It is the definition of a weeder course. Dr. Kijewska is a fine instructor, the course difficulty isn't her fault. The whole chem department needs an overhaul, but she is a fine instructor and friendly to students when they have questions or concerns.

I loved having Dr. Kijewska for CEM 141. Her lectures are thorough and interesting. The learning objectives of each lecture and the required knowledge for each exam are clear. She is caring and very nice.

I had her for both CEM 141 and CEM 142, and she truly cares about her students and wants them to succeed. However, the transition from an online setting where I was able to get a 4.0, and then to an in-person setting was very difficult. The multiple choice exams are very difficult and you have to study every little thing if you want to succeed.

Professor Kijewska is a very nice professor to have. She is friendly and easy to talk to. You're required to participate during lectures but it does help keep you engaged. You have two short assignments each week, but you have to study outside of lectures to do well. It is a hard class, but the chem help room is a great resource to use.

Dr. Kijewska was okay to me. Her HAs were more of the problem than she was, but she still wasn't great. I feel like she either over or under explained everything. Quizzes in this class are hard and the graded assignments are even harder.

Favorite professor!! She cares about her students and will constantly check to make sure everyone is on the same page in lectures. That being said, in order to get a good grade you have to put the time in, study, and ask questions. I took Dr. Kijewska for both CEM 141 and 142, and I will miss not being in her class in the future.

The TAs are very hard graders when it comes to the graded assignments. She gives amazing lectures and makes the material interesting and tries to keep everyone engaged. She always checks up on the students and tries her best to make sure that everyone understands the material.

This is a non-bias report. Dr.K is an amazing professor. However, her TAs will butcher the course by being difficult graders. The quizzes are difficult in this course, each question will have 4 or more multiple choice answers being difficult to differentiate since they're similar but a little bit is changed. Graded assignment grading criteria sucks

She is super nice and overall a good teacher, but the TA's are really strict on grading and that was ultimately why I didn't get the grade I wanted. Studying is very important or else you won't get a good grade on the exams.

Lectures presented material clearly, participation is encouraged, and there are plenty of opportunities to ask questions. My biggest frustration with the course was the inconsistent grading of the TAs and the ambiguous grading criteria. Dr. Kijewska is a talented lecturer, however, and was always willing to help.

It's hard to get very personal with her because her classes are so huge but that's not her fault. She explains content very well and clearly. The TA's do the grading, so any issues with that is on them but it was never an issue for me.

The professor is probably one of the best and easiest cem professors you will have. She explains things so easy to understand. However, the only thing holding me back from giving a 5/5 rate is because of the TA's. I'm unaware of which ones, but they are extremely difficult graders, more difficult than the professor. The course would be perfect.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: The best cem professors Ive ever had. Many find her lecture videos boring. however, her lecture videos are so easily explained to where its almost hard to not learn. Shell do a poll quiz during lectures to see if the class understands, if majority doesnt, she explains it again and better. Take her class, you wont regret it.
Taking this class online sucked. The lectures were long and boring, the material was pretty hard to ynderstand through zoom.

please don't use Chegg in this class or all other classes, I wish someone told me this before I viewed the chegg when a friend ask me to do so during the finals. All the answers for the assignment could be found on the lectures. The prof was very nice and caring when you have questions. And it is not a hard class overall if you watch the lecture.

My lectures were through zoom, but Dr. K is an AMAZING prof and really knows what she's talking about. Lectures are very long, but recordings are available if you need to look back. She takes time to explain things very well and constantly checks to make sure we understand. Homework is graded for participation which was lovely. Quizzes were harder.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Im not particularly a fan of her lectures. Shell go over a topic really quickly, than will come back later to explain it. It personally made me confused. Theres a lot of homework, but she does offer good extra credit.
Dont miss lectures or fall behind, itll be bad. Overall, wasnt a bad class, but it is a self-taught class, with lots of lecture material and LOTS of reading, but quizzes/GAs are open note/book.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This professor was really sweet! However, the lecture material did not really match the material that was on the quizzes. Homework was easy as it was for credit, but the Graded Assignments and Quizzes were more difficult. I felt like she didn't really teach much, and I did a lot of self-teaching throughout the semester.

Professor Kijewska's lectures move quick and she was sometimes hard to understand. I liked watching her recorded lectures instead of the live ones. There was about 2 homework assignments/ week, 4 graded assignments, and 3 quizzes including the final. You have to work to understand the material and take good notes because the grading can be tough.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Switched out of her class after she wrongfully accused me of cheating on an assignment. I talked to her and she refused to look at any proof, wouldnt listen and was simply mean. I switched out of her class and am 4.0 ing the class, take any other prof but her.
Open note tests and quizzes
Her accent was hard to understand at times. Have to attend lectures. Had two quizzes each time, one short answer and the other multiple choice.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: She is a queen. Not only is she always is extremely nice and respectful, but also she doesn't give too much homework and makes sure her student get the material through polls.

I truly never thought Id understand chem but this professor made it so clear and easy to understand. I took this course in the summer so it was accelerated, but she made sure that we understood the topic. Quizzes and final are open book/ open note so taking notes during lecture is def what helped me pass.
Amazing prof. Made chemistry easy to understand and made it clear what we needed to know for tests. Very kind and understanding especially during covid times.

I had Dr. Kijewska for CEM 141 in Fall 2020. She was an excellent professor. She explained the material very well and I understood it pretty well, even with her accent. At first, I had trouble with the class because I was a lazy listener during the lectures. But as I adapted to the class, it got better for me. I managed to make it out with a 4.0.

Lectures are kind of boring but have to pay attention and keep up or it gets really confusing. There's assigned readings but I didn't do any, just watched the lectures and got a 3.5 so not too bad. There's 6 MC quizzes and 6 SA quizzes throughout the semester, some of which are pretty tough but you can drop one by doing an EC project.

Lectures were structured well. During the lectures, she would ask questions and would explain why the answer was what it was. The textbook was free and online, so easy to use. Questions asked in test could be directed straight back to material covered in recitation worksheets and the lectures. Definitely attend recitations. They provide extra help.

She's a very sweet and caring professor, I would love to have another class with her! Make sure to complete all hw assignments and go to office hours (very helpful)! Attend the lectures for sure and review learning objectives for quizzes. The extra credit hw's and optional final project boosted my grade a lot at the end.

If you do the homework and watch lectures you will be fine. Attendance isn't recorded and she posts recorded lectures on d2l. There is an optional final project that replaces your lowest quiz score and a few extra credit homework assignments. Do the work and you will be fine!

I was in the hospital on morphine and she wouldn't give me an extension on a quiz that was available for two days. Even if you go to lectures and do the homework, you're not going to be prepared for the tests. Keep in mind I took the class during COVID and I have heard good things about her before. The homework is easy but the tests are super hard.

This class overall was fine. Quizzes are hard even when you pay attention in lectures. Never used the textbook. Professor has an accent, but was very understandable. She is very nice, but doesnt do well explaining material in my opinion because it barely relates to quiz material.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Taken online due to Covid. You have quizzes every few weeks which consists of a short answer portion and a multiple choice. She does have an accent but is easy to understand and follow along with. The final was an optional project which can replace your lowest score. TA's answer a lot of questions and there are lots of office hours for help.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Great professor, she know what she's teaching and how to teach it. Everything she talks about in lecture is on the quiz, so make sure you take notes on what she says aloud and not just the slides. Didn't use textbook much myself. Homework is helpful as well. She keeps students engaged in lecture's which helped me stay focused much of the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Go to lecture, download the slides after lecture and watch recorded videos and take notes on what she says (THIS IS KEY). Go to reciation and look at correct answers after in D2L. All the material said and written down help with quizzes. Don't slack on units all of them connect to each other. Great class, very fun to learn. Ask for help!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: 1. Lectures are the best material to study. 2. Textbook only supplements lecture material. It wasnt useful to me. But it is free. 3. Take notes. Each unit builds on the next. (Watch out for Unit 4!) 4. Do the HW. Shes really kind. While she may not be able to answer all questions in-depth during class, office hours are always available.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof K is very smart and cares about her students success. Although I wouldn't recommend her to anyone who is an auditory learner. Lots of visual examples in class that didn't help me much and would very quickly explain complex concepts. Her class isn't easy, but college isn't easy. Your grade depends on the exams, be ready to study!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: You need to go to lectures to participate in iClicker questions and get credit. The material is presented in a very organized way and if you pay attention in lectures and do the exam reviews, you will do well on exams. Handled the transition to online really well, she wanted everyone to succeed and adjusted properly to do so. Very nice person too!

You can tell she cares about her students and likes to teach the course material. She is very organized and takes her time. She always pauses lecture to ask if there are any questions. The only thing is that you need to go to lecture to get credit for attendance (iclicker questions), but they are beneficial to understanding the material.
I had her for CEM 141. She was great. Homework isn't hard at all, and you don't have to study too much for the tests. Highly reccomend.

I had her for CEM 141 and she was an awesome teacher. I would definitely recommend going to class and paying attention. Homework is basically participation and really quick, and I didn't need to study very much at all. She's very nice and pretty interesting to listen to.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I had her for CEM 141 and 142 and absolutely loved her lectures. Her lecture slides are enough to study for the exams. Makes the student's practice problems in the class. I encourage to take part in all the in-class activities. The book is not required. Uses Besocratic which can be frustrating at times but I still think it is worth it.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: The class was extremely frustrating. While the professor is clearly smart, I frequently found myself questioning her teaching abilities. Information is constantly thrown so fast at you that it becomes difficult to even remember the overarching topic. Ended up doing well in the class solely through constant hours of studying and a tutor.

This class is one of the worst classes I have taken. Half of the material on the exams was barely even mentioned in lecture. She gave us learning objectives before the exam and a majority of them were never even touched on so I had to teach myself. Do not take this class unless you want to spend 20+ hours studying just to get an average grade.

she may not be the best teacher, but this class is EXTREMELY easy. don't worry about it at all. all you have to do is study learning objectives and the recitation packets before exams and you will get a 4.0.

She is super sweet and an excellent professor. Makes every concept crystal clear, and as long as you pay attention to and study her lecture slides, you will do great on the exams. (Exam 2 was the hardest in my opinion). You also have other grades, which help. Don't skip class; lectures are SO important! Wonderful prof; would take again!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Great!

Prof Kijewska is so nice! She does an excellent job explaining the concepts in lecture classes, and does a thorough job. You don't have to attend class (you can skip up to 3 w/out an excuse) but it's not worth skipping. If you come to class, hw, concepts, & everything else will be super easy. would take this prof again
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: A good teacher. She writes in kind of funny cursive but if you ever ask her a question she will explain the answer clearly.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I had a very big class so her style of teaching was only because she had 400 students per lecture, she does a great job and telling you what you need to know, and reviewing key concepts
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: You're going to have to study but the book (they give it to you) isn't even worth reading. Lectures won't be super fun but she tells you what you need to know to pass.

She is very smart and very kind. You can tell she just wants the best for her students. If you ask her questions she is always happy to help and is accessible outside lecture. She does have an accent but that shouldn't stop you from taking her class, as she does a very good job explaining difficult concepts. I found 142 to be much easier than 141.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She is a smart professor and does know what she is talking about, but she just lectures by reading off the slides that she posts after class anyways, but there are clicker questions everyday so you have to go to class and sit through a dry lecture of her lecturing right from slides on the board. Sometimes hard to understand too

She always posts assignments after lecture and they're due that Friday at 9am and it's usually about the lab which we have during the week.  We never know what to look out for during our lab and often barely mentions the assignments due Friday during lecture.  Changes assignment due dates often.

Syllabus and schedule change often. Late work gets 50% off—even if it's 1 hour late—though assignments may be graded weeks later. Due Fridays at 9 AM. Little communication with mentor teachers and minimal feedback before the next assignment. Templates are repetitive. Only .docx accepted, no resubmissions. Do work early.

The professor is super nice, always answers questions, and did online reviews before the exams — sometimes giving hints for the tests. Lectures were a little boring, with a lot of info, and a good amount of work. Do the extra credit, it really helps your grade!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: i loved professor wood! she was really sweet really cares about her job the extra credit really does make a impact on ur grade so make sure u do it! she was understanding when it came to any online issues and her lectures were pretty interesting some were boring ans she do online reviews and give hints for the tests i would take her again!

Not recommended. There are only 10 in-class assignments, and missing two due to illness meant no flexibility or makeup allowed. I earned an A, but only by teaching myself. Lectures mainly involve reading directly from PowerPoint slides.
This class included a TON of work, but if you do the work, you will be fine. Do not get behind! This particular lecture is long and boring but if you put in the work, it will be fine.

very nice prof. lectures are super slow and boring, but she means well and explain everything well. I never pay attention in class, no exams, everything is based on connect quizzes and d2l quizzes (no lockdown), 2 larger assignments. Changes deadlines to help people. has difficulties with technology which is hard when ICA dont work.

Her class was mostly connect assignments and weekly quizzes. There were no exams. You barely need to go to her class and she posts before if there is an in class activity. I missed 1 class a week and I still ended with 540/515. Plenty of EC. Her lectures are very boring but I would just finish all of the hw during class.

idk why Dr. Woods rating is so low. She is an older lady so the lectures can be boring but you don't even have to pay attention to the lectures to pass. There is no exams, multiple extra credit assignments, and 1-2 short open note tests a week. literally so easy. I passed with a 535/515 points because of all the extra credit.

Class is beyond easy. Graded by weekly quizzes instead of exams, homework assignments, in class activities, & 2 application assignments. Everything was easy, we only had in class assignment sometimes. She's very sweet & caring. If she's not answering your email, go up to her in class, her and her TA are understanding & more than willing to help.

Kellye is very sweet and kind. She is willing to answer any questions you have in class or look over assignments for you. The class is long, but we end early a lot of the time. There is a good amount of work outside of class, but if you budget time, it's doable. It is difficult to reach her via email, but she does hold office hours.

You can tell she means well, but lectures are so insufferable. Class is structured poorly, there's weekly assignments and quizzes. Overall, work is not difficult, but she is such a boring lecturer and you will learn nothing. She will randomly assign in-class assignments, so even though attendance isn't mandatory, you usually have to be in class.
Insinuated that I needed therapy because her unorganized, unstructured, lack of proper instruction she likes to call lecture caused me to be confused. Needs to be fired.
Lectures are insufferable.

please please please answer my emails ;( 
ive sent her at least 30 emails in the past 2 weeks and not a single one got a response.

never EVER answers emails and will set you back 3 weeks struggling to try and figure out how to still pass the class being 3 weeks behind without a clue on what to do. debating on switching my certification so i dont have to take this class. help.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Super tedious about small things that don't matter, yet forgets to open up drop boxes every week....
Lectures are boring, makes it hard to engage... especially since they're so late in the day. Emails left un-responded to, don't take this class with her
Overall seems like a nice lady, but if you're not going to respond to emails, don't teach.
All busy work, lesson plans on top of in class quizzes on top of field assignments on top of in class assignments on top of anti bias assignments on top of placements. So unnecessary
Just don't do it, truthfully. If you can take it with anyone else, do yourself a favor and do that.

Take it with her if you want to pull your hair out due to confusion and lack of structure in the class. 
She also doesnt respond to emails

Legit does not respond to emails. I emailed her a total of 23 times over the semester and she didn't reply to a single one. D2L is confusing and the class is unorganized and chaotic. DON'T TAKE IT WITH KELLYE

If you don't have to take this class with her, don't. She claims we can reach out to her anytime, but does not respond to emails. She assigns 100 different assignments per week and is such a tedious grader that one little typo will cost you -3 points. Lectures are pointless, but required. Unhelpful and overall needs to just be done teaching.

Kellye was a fantastic professor. Lectures could be a bit boring but were informative. Great feedback on assignments. If you go to class, go to the lab, and do the work, you should do fine overall. Many complaints come from people who skip class and get frustrated by assignments explained in class. Not the best tech wise but TA is very helpful.

Shes a nice professor and does her best to answer questions. The course itself is very overwhelming, not super organized, lowkey chaotic. Am I learning a lot, yes? But there is so much information being thrown at us at once that it's hard to remember everything and it feels like you HAVE to remember everything. Lots of overlap of assignments.

Do NOT take this class with Kellye Wood!  Her lectures are hard to follow and rarely have anything to do with the homework assignments.  She will NOT reply to your emails, no matter how many times you email her.  She is unhelpful, has no clue what she is doing, and really just isn't cut out for teaching this class.  The final paper is 20 pages long
Good 8 am class to have.

This class is very easy but there is a lot of homework that counts toward your grade. Quizzes and exams are taken in your own time so they are open book and straightforward as long as you take notes from the textbook and attend lectures. She uses an app to ask questions for class participation which also goes toward your final grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Very easy class that can be done online, and all the tests and quizzes are open note. The weeks you have a test she cancels class for the week. Also she would end class at least 30 minutes early all the time
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Dr. King's class was very fun and eye-opening. Lecture participation questions via TopHat are worth points, so please go. Readings could be a bit heavy (several articles/papers each week), but once you get the hand of efficiently reading them, they aren't bad. Quizzes and tests were very manageable and online. Great class overall.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: All online open note tests and assignments were easy.

She was a pretty good professor! I will say her lectures did feel like they were dragging on, but she seems very passionate about what she was talking about. there was one writing assignment, but she gave you a bunch of options, and it was pretty easy. All of her tests were online/open note.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class is based on weekly PackBack assignments (1 drop), 4 exams (1 drop), 4 quizzes (1 drop), one essay (less than 200 words), and TopHat in class questions for participation. Exams/quizzes are online and open note. Weekly readings were sometimes a lot, but you could skim them and still understand the material. Lectures were boring at times.
Loved her, I'd take her class again. She's retiring soon and that makes me sad.

Dr. King is very sweet, but if you have no interest in this subject, please do yourself a favor and find another ISS. The lectures are long and can be boring if you aren't genuinely interested in anthropology. Still, I managed to get a 3.5 in the class not being very interested. She uses TopHat and online assignments.
Turns in grades very late, she is pretty laid back and attendance isn't mandatory but gives bad feedback on projects.

Dr. King is a sweet person and she teaches very nicely despite not a lot of people participating. She's relaxed in all the good ways. You can miss the participation and still 4.0. Her work is doable, or else I couldn't have added this class on. It's the weight it should be. Exams are doable, class is canceled. HW is HW but she sets up for success.
Chillest prof ive ever had. It can be boring but no more than any other iss classes.

King is maybe the most chill and laid back professor at state. All quizzes and exams are open note and technology. class is cancelled the whole week for exams. Homework every week, and a big top hat assignment due before each exam, not hard, but time consuming. attendance isn't required but there are participation points you will miss if you skip.

Simple weekly discussion posts, online textbook work completion that guides you through it, and the fact I didn't show up to a single lecture and still got an A is probably why you should take this class. Professor responds to emails in a timely manner (rare at MSU). Overall nice person
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Second time taking Dr.King. Super easy, online heavy course. There are in person participation points but as long as you do good in everything else, you'll be okay. Interesting course and Dr.King is super nice!
If you need ISS 2XX credit, take this class with her. Easy grading and somewhat interesting lectures.

It is not required to come to class but you must participate by answering questions on Top Hat to get participation points. You will have 3-4 modules to read per test, if you stay on pace they are very manageable. Quizzes and tests are online, you can take them anytime in the week. She is very helpful, easy to approach, and wants the best for you.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: ISS220 with Professor King has been great! She is extremely knowledgeable, caring, approachable, and incredibly clear on the class structure. Attendance isn't required, but there are in-class points that you'll miss if you skip. The textbook is online. Highly recommend her class. Definitely an easy A if you put in a little bit of work.
easy would recommned

There's a moderate amount of reading and work but it's not at all overwhelming. There is an attendance grade but it comes from answering lecture questions virtually. You can answer them from home if you can't make it to class. It's really easy as long as you show up for most of the classes and don't procrastinate too much.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Take her course if you are looking to compete your 300 level ISS credit. She is such a great professor and it is very easy to pass her classes. Quizzes/exams are all online and you can work on each unit at your own pace. Very great professor and even though it was an 8 am course, I enjoyed going to lecture.

Overall an easy and enjoyable class, she gives plenty of extra credit opportunities through homework and attendance! Exams were straightforward as long as you went to lecture, not too much reading, and plenty of documentaries viewed in class!

Dr. King is a great teacher! She has interesting lectures. The readings are not usually very heavy. There are weekly packback posts, but those usually don't take very long. The quizzes and exam were all online, and you have the week off to complete them. There was a final paper that took a while to write, but if you start early it's not bad at all.

King gave an honors option for the class along with a volunteering extra credit opportunity. She wrote the textbook for the class which is very informative and insightful. Attendance is taken through TopHat, so you can get your attendance points from your laptop if you can't go to class. Not a lot of workload and the best ISS class in my opinion.

Be carful with this professor. She would get upset multiple times at students for little things like coughing and actually threatened to kick the student out of class for it. She has a bad temperament and I would really advise other students to consider other courses unless you want to deal with someone like her.

I had to take this class at 8AM, and I went anyway because I liked this class so much. Great professor, super interesting topics. The best IAH or ISS class I have taken. There are pack pack discussion posts, which are a nuisance, but everything also I really enjoyed.

Overall the class was super clear and organized. The exams were all online and straightforward and the homework was just one packback and some tophat work every week. Professor King is also very understanding and kind which was nice.

Our homework was a TopHat assignment for each unit and a Packback discussion each week. It was relatively easy. You have to buy both TopHat and Packback, but I don't think it is too big a purchase. The exams are pretty easy and all online. For every exam week, she didn't hold class (which was obviously very nice). She is a very sweet prof.

I loved taking her class, she is a very inspirational person and her course was eye opening. For pre classes and homework it was usually video based which I found to be helpful, but time consuming. Overall I recommend!!
She is great!

Prof King is great at helping students understand class material, but her class uses top hat, packback, and multiple textbooks, so be prepared to spend money on those. attendance and participation are both mandatory as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dr. King's lectures were helpful for understanding course material. She used Tophat to measure participation. There were readings for most classes, but she usually went over the main points in class. There were 4 open note quizzes and exams, with 1 drop (including the final/exam 4 and quiz 4). This class was interesting and easy to succeed in.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. King is super sweet and really takes a liking to the content that she presents on. I found that her lectures were super knowledgable for all that were present to listen to her speak. Her exams and quizzes are straight-forward. As long as you take the notes, come to class for the TopHat questions, and do the pack backs, you're golden.

Prof King is super nice and provides an interesting insight into the medical field through this course! As long as you do the PackBacks and participate in the TopHat, you will do just fine. She gives you a week to do the quiz and exam, both of which are open note and easy. I recommend this course to anyone with an interest in anthropology!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor King was great. I had her for ISS 310, and besides the lectures we used packback and tophat. Both of the two were super easy to navigate and not too content heavy. All the quizzes/exams were open note, though they were a little difficult. It was overall a good class, and Professor King was great and I would recommend.

She is super sweet and very easy to listen to! Exams and quizzes are easy as long as you take notes on her lectures. Likes to give personal stories related to the topics in class. Would definitely take again! She does have weekly pack backs, but they are super easy.

Dr. King is super cool. This class was really easy and really opens your mind if you want to pursue a career in the healthcare field. I wish anyone who wants to be a doctor, nurse, PA, etc; to take this course, we would have stronger leaders. As long as you go to lecture, do the packbacks each week, and do the study guide you'll 4.0

I didnt get the opportunity to take this class in person due to Covid, but even so, prof. King was easily the most uninvolved professor Ive ever had. One of the first things we reviewed on the syllabus was a list of types of emails/questions that she just wouldnt respond to and it felt like every module was just copy and pasted from quizlet.

Not very tech savvy, used Tophat for everything besides some discussion posts. Very sweet person, but had a hard time meeting her own deadlines. The module was always released a day or two late. My class was completely asynchronous and I could get the work done weeks in advance pretty much. Easy, but you get out of it what you put in.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This class caused me a lot of stress for sure. A lot of things due at the end of each week, and she's hard to get a response out of. Make sure to read the syllabus so you don't miss any important info about how things are graded and the criteria you must meet. Since the class is online, she uses a lot of different websites for different assignments
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. King was nothing but unprofessional, unethical, and frustrating. She responded to a student's concerns about lessons being released late with "This put me into therapy," and at the end of the semester, she changed the syllabus in such a way that destroyed several people's grades, and when confronted, claimed it never happened.

I took this asynchronous class online in Fall 2020. We had to use a platform called Packback to post and answer questions every Sunday. These posts are worth 20% of your grade, but theyre really easy. Homework is reading the online textbook and answering questions. Quizzes and exams are open note. The class is really easy. No problems w/ prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This course in theory was quite interesting, but the professor made it near impossible to connect to any material. A total waste of time going to class, yet attendance is practically mandatory if you want any cushion to your grade. Says she "cares about our wellbeing" but is cold and blunt when you go to her for  anything. Weird grading system too.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: she called me out in front of the whole class (200+ students) for having my headphones on while she was lecturing... never in my 4 years of college have i ever had that happen to me.

She offers 0 help, makes rude comments, unapproachable, and pushes her poltitical agenda on others. Anytime I ask for help I get turned to a TA that doesnt respond. She will treat you like youre 5. Has some very hot takes and criticizes religion. She is very knowledgeable, seems like a smart woman, but uses it 2 make u feel bad about yourself.

The ratings are too harsh. She can be a little sarcastic at points and she can go on tangents, but I thought the tangents were overall helpful and pertained mostly to class material. If they didn't, they were interesting. I thoroughly enjoyed class material and the way she lectured. Just go to class and complete the smart book assignments.

Class was taught well, good resources for learning and studying. Exams test your understanding of topics more than just memorization. Biggest issue is that she frequently goes on tangents and wastes lecture time leading her to rush through the content towards the end (this also leads to the time for in class activities being very short).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Teacher let outside views and political opinions drive how she taught most subject material.

Clements is one of the worst professors I've had. She says she cares about us doing well, but she just reads off her lecture slides and the exams are unreasonably hard. Thankfully the exams aren't a very big part of your grade. You have to go to class to get attendance points and participate in discussion.
She will literally provide you no help and is really rude when you ask for it. AWFUL lecturer. Avoid this at all costs. Just take it online.

If you get stuck with this professor I hope you like solving riddles, because that is what the exams are like and she treats her students like they are in middle school. Lectures are basically useless, only way I passed with a decent grade was by teaching myself everything using the text book.

this professor does not know how to treat her students. As if were still in 5th grade she repeats the same reminders a thousand times and while the rest of the psy classes have 70% for final exams she gives 80% others give online exams while she does hers in person. She likes to do things her way and it hurts the class and everyone in it. AVOID HER

The only downsides to this class were the exams that required thorough knowledge of each concept from the chapters. If you go to lectures, take notes, and review those notes you will be fine. Homework and research is very simple and not a large amount. Professor offered a generous curve at the end of the semester too. Very accessible and friendly.

This is by the far the worst professor I have ever had in my entire academic career. She genuinely does not care about educating her students in any shape, way, or form. She adopted the worst possible learning techniques and it would be more beneficial to just not go to her classes. Exams are hard, even if you memorize the textbook.
Worst professor don't even take the course

WARNING: DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS. She will not educate you under any circumstance. She treats your education like a joke and she smiles while we struggle through our exams. She could care less about her students mental health or our exam grades. She thinks that its our studying that's bad, but she really does NOT have resources to help students. BADD
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: If you have her, drop out immediately. Take the course online or with another professor, if possible. My first worst professor at MSU and it's a psychology professor... Even the TAs look miserable around her.

This teacher has no respect for education and nobody should ever take her. She teaches this class like we are all a bunch of robots and is very lazy with her teaching style hence her whole class being taught by a slideshow. Not even the underpaid teachers at my public high school could compare to the extreme levels of ignorance this class embodies.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Test heavy

reviews.comment: if you are international student. don't take this class. you will have 5 exams per semester which means 1 exam per 3 weeks sometimes is 2 weeks. exams score occupy 80% of the final. you have a lot of homework such as packbag (usually just yapping). too much work to do.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: While giving lectures she would just read the slideshows to us and it became very boring and she wouldn't post the lectures. For me and my friends, we didn't think that the lectures relayed any of the same material as what the lectures presented us even though she said the exam was basically the lectures. This class also has a TON of long homeworks
The class was easy, just personal issues with the professor.
his new hst 150 is based off a 1,000 page book about capitalism. not what i expected for "global history".

Professor Keith was great! His lectures were interesting but can get drawn out, and he's a passionate guy. You're only graded on 3 out of 4 hand-written papers, a midterm, and a final. Your TA does all the grading and they grade hard for a supposed short answer essay. Would definitely take another one of his classes!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Considering this was a level 100 course, it was super difficult to get a 4.0 in and I am an honors student. The TA did legitimately all of the grading and it was all written assignments. Your grades are not in d2l so you have to calculate everything yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Keith is one of my favorite professors at MSU. He teaches some of the best history classes I have taken here and he makes it very easy to pass. As long as you do the readings and watch the lectures you will pass. All the tests are in class and written essays but you get to have your readings/notes with you.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Wonderful professor, perhaps the best teacher I've had personally. Proffessor Charles is down to earth, approachable, and deeply passionate. Accordingly, it is very clear he cares alot about his students learning expierence and he is very careful in how he presents his information to not give a wrong impression. Extremely respectable guy. 5/5

Professor Keith has been one of the better profs I have had. He is extremely considerate of all his students and is flexible with due dates (if need be). He is very understanding of student life outside of class and is flexible with scheduling. This class taught me a lot about the war. Although, you have to work to get a good grade. No free pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Great professor. Engaging lectures and cared about the material taught. Graded by random attendance, papers and quizzes in recitation. Not super difficult but need to go to class to succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Keith and Patrick Buck were great! Buck led the recitations. Very willing to work with you outside of class. You have to work to pass. Don't miss lectures and readings are a MUST. You get 3 absences, and Keith took attendance randomly. Attendance is always taken in recitation. Papers have complicated content but easy grading.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: He is a very good, nice prof. He is extremely knowledgeable and knows how to convey information very well. He was interesting and was always more than willing to answer questions and have discussions in or out of class. The grade was based on midterm, paper, and final paper and attendance/participation. Going to class/reading are musts. Great prof.

He was really great, and you can tell he knows what he is talking about. I always enjoyed being in his class, and am looking forward to taking another one of his. It was nice having such a young professor too, and he was very silly and always joked with the class too.

He really knows his stuff, which helps make this class a lot more interesting. He was very helpful and easygoing. He liked to throw some humor into class every now and then too. He's lenient with papers, you don't have to say what he wants as long as you support your point well.
Awesome professor, really knows his stuff and has a passion for what he's saying.  TA's grade papers, but final is easy.

Probably one of the best professors I have had so far at MSU. this guy really knows his stuff when it comes to Asian history and it shows in his lectures. He keeps class interesting, has a good sense of humor, and is always willing to help..definatley reccomended!

He is very mumbly and uses broken language so its difficult to follow when he "explains" programming, leaves class to take unnecessary calls, redundant advice which feels more interrogative than helpful, weirdly aggressive which I've never seen in another professor, and he couldn't solve his own problems and tried to hide his AI use at one point.

He's an amazing instructor and answers questions. If you did ANY programming in your life, the exams will be a piece of cake. He frequently looks over at your shoulder to see your code and tells you what is incorrect. The other reviewers who say that it's intimidating are saying it because they think their code is perfect and it's not.
Bro is always all up in your space during quizzes and exams. He will come up to you during a quiz and be like “what are you doing” then make your code worse. Do not recommend this professor.
brother was all up in my space and he's very condescending, loud, rude, everything. not helpful at all and everytime i listen to him talk i'm on the verge of having a stroke.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: makes lots of programming and spelling errors in his labs. class was all self taught for me. His speaking is mumbled, unclear, and very broken English. Also quick to anger and gets into your personal space. He lectured me about paying attention in class for 5 minutes during a Timed quiz because I had one simple question to clarify instructions.

Dr. Khalifeh made the course very fun and interactive. He truly aims to make it a seamless experience for students. He explained the labs very well and walked us through demonstrations, using trial and error to help us understand each concept. Overall, it was a phenomenal experience. Highly recommend!
is not a kind person, struggles with technology, gets very close to students (especially females) and has very bad things to say about certain groups of students
not helpful at all
The course basically killed my motivation to learn programming. Khalifeh is very slow, and has some speech problem (he keeps repeating same word/sentence 10 times). He is also very insensitive.

Really nice guy, but the class structure and assignments are really complex and confusing, and exams are HARD. Also this course in particular is very linguistics heavy with him as a prof, so keep that in mind.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I'm taking IAH 231C and I would not recommend at all. None of the course information makes sense. He is a very nice professor, but the course material is dumb and confusing. DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE

I loved having Professor Ke, he was funny, engaging, and approachable. His lectures were clear and he was always willing to go over confusing information. The content itself can be challenging at times, but if you pay attention in class and ask questions during or after class, it's not difficult to do well in the course and genuinely learn a lot.

Professor Ke is an extremely kind person. Linguistics in general is horrible and hard to teach, but he tries to make up for it by being an easy grader. For assignments you have to record yourself to explain yourself, which was annoying, but trying to explain it out loud helped for understanding. Attend class or you will be confused.

IAH 231 not lin
Genuinely one of the worst professors I've ever had.I should've dropped this after the first class. Super nice professor when you talk to him after class, but truly horrible course overall. The grading is super messy on D2L, in class quizzes can never make them up either. I really hate this class and  it has been a waste of my time.

Alan is very nice and a great teacher, this is the second class I have taken with him and I still really like him as a professor. Linguistics can be confusing, but really just pay attention. He gives you extra credit for asking questions in class, so if you're confused you have a big incentive to ask!

Probably the nicest professor I've ever had. Unfortunately, most of the semester I was confused because the material is extremely difficult. I missed one class and I was pretty much a goner till after the midterm. If you take this class, make sure to never zone out, and keep asking him questions until you understand.

Alan is very nice, and really cares about his students. Students often walk away confused, although he tries his best. Linguistics is a hard subject to understand all together, and his teaching style isn't great for it. A good amount of homework. Midterm and final is online asynchronous, open note. No in class tests. Extra credit is offered.

Professor Alan was very friendly and he knew his stuff. Unfortunately, his students do not. I paid attention to lectures and still found myself confused often. Difficult topics were brushed over. There are 6 big assignments along with daily 4 questions lecture quizzes. The assignments were very difficult. A pro: he curves grades and offers E.C.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I personally found this class really difficult to the point where I couldnt finish. However, take that with a grain of salt. Ive personally heard amazing things about this course from other people but this class was just NOT for me.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Super nice, approachable professor and generally very open to suggestions and feedback. However, he wasn't always the clearest when explaining more complex concepts. The book wasn't really needed, but could be helpful when Alan's explanation just wasn't cutting it. Grading was generous and TONS of EC opportunities. Almost everthing was open-note.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Alan Hezao Ke was very nice, yet the class was very difficult. Prepare to be confused during lectures. There are six major assignments, one paper, and two exams. Lots of extra credit opportunities which is nice, but there are also a lot of confusing lecture quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Class was difficult and not recommended for freshmen , even though many take the class. Textbook use was optional but included EC readings. In-person lecture quizzes and participation is part of your grade and be prepared to spend a lot of time on assignments and preparing for the difficult midterm. Prof was nice, not super clear on things...
I like his class structure, especially the individual conference where I usually receive very helpful feedback.

He seems super nice but he gives minimal criteria and zero guidance. His comments when grading an essay were “good” and that's it. He's nice but he doesn't teach you anything about writing and repeats himself a lot during the lecture.
He's easygoing, but more importantly, he's a truly original professor. I really appreciate his efforts to make the class engaging and the many creative ideas he brings.

Took his writing 101 course for this semester. He's a cool and super friendly teacher! Always willing to help you when you have questions. A lot of small homework but they only counted as the participation grade. 3 essay, 1 research paper, and 1 remix Video throughout the semester but they were really easy. Easy grader.
