I enjoyed all of the topics we learned about but definitely put in the work to know it. Put in the effort and you will love this class!

Dr. Spranger is an excellent professor who clearly has a passion for physiology and working with his students to help them best learn how to study. His course can be challenging by nature and topic but that is fully supported by his lecture content, the resources he provides students, and his accessibility in office hours.

Dr. Spranger is an incredible professor. He truly cares about his students success, which is evident by the amount of help sessions that he has dedicated to succeeding in his class. His lectures are engaging and informative, and he made physiology super enjoyable to learn! If you put the work in, he will set you up for success for sure.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: The material helped me in understanding the cases I would see while shadowing in the hospital. I believe this class helped me in other courses such as biochemistry and immunology, as it provides a foundational understanding. You will spend lots of time studying!! Great class for pre-med/health. He also offers test review and lots of office hours!

Not an easy 4.0, but if you're looking to actually learn, 100% take this course.

Spranger may get a bad rap from students who didn't want to be challenged, but he's been my favorite prof. He truly prepares students for the real world, offers every resource imaginable (+ a ton of extra credit) and really cares about his students' learning.

PSL 310 is a content-heavy course, but a necessary one for all pre-health professionals. Professor Spranger truly simulates the material that you will encounter on the MCAT and equivalent entrance exams, so engaging with this course and striving for long-term retention of the material is fundamental. Embrace the academic rigor and you will succeed!

Dr.Spranger is clearly dedicated in teaching material that will stay with you. Yes, he expects a lot from his students but he gives you the foundation for medical graduate programs. The course required a lot of effort but there is a ton of resouces to make sure his students succeed. I have never learned so much from one course at MSU.

PSL 310 certainly is a challenging course, however, Professor Spranger does a great job of providing his students with all the resources and support they need to succeed. If you dedicate time to watch all lectures, take good notes, and go to his office hours (or the TA/LA help sessions), you will excel in this class, and find it very rewarding.

Professor Spranger was a great professor who set me up academically for PA school. He made the content interesting and engaging. I enjoyed coming to his lectures and office hours. You can really tell that Marty enjoys being a professor and only wants the best for his students.

PSL310 was so content dense but it's expected. Can see students saying Spranger has a big ego, but he gives EC and exams represent the content from lecture. If you have questions, just ask him after class or in office hours. He can be blunt, so heads up. You won't retain or do well on exams by memorizing... you actually have to apply the concepts

This class was easily the hardest class I took in undergrad, but now as a PA student I can't imagine how behind I would be if it weren't for this class. This class will prepare you for grad school!! Yes it is extremely challenging BUT prof  Spranger genuinely wants every single student to end with a 4.0 and will always help, just ask!

Prof Spranger's physiology class was a tough class but as long as you put effort into the class and he see's you trying, he is a fair professor. He offers a lot of help resources outside of class and a good chunk of extra credit. The lectures are very information dense, but it is clear he cares about student success. The lectures are entertaining.

Spranger is very fair as a physiology prof. This is one of the core classes that preps you for the MCAT and tests if you are capable of pursuing a pre-health profession. Unsurprisingly, the lectures are tough and content is hard to digest, so you have to spend time out of class. Exams are easy (hard if you don't watch the lectures). Offers EC too
Put in the work and you'll get a 4.0. Class is all lecture based but Dr. Spranger gives interesting lectures and keeps everyone engaged.

This class was very difficult and requires time and effort to be successful. Dr. Spranger was so supportive. He was responsive to questions over email and records all lectures. Rewatching them is the key to learning the material. He has study help sessions, review guides, and TAs do review sessions for every exam. Put in the work and you got this!

There is a lot of material to learn, however, the professor makes the lectures intriguing and very understandable. Professor Spranger cares for his students and provides many different kinds of resources to help them succeed in this course. If you use any of these resources, it is very possible to achieve a 4.0.

PSL 310 is a true 4 credit course, it will undoubtably be your most challenging class and you must stay on top of the content. Spranger is an amazing professor who is truly passionate about what he is teaching, and he provides many resources for you to succeed. Put in the work, ask for help when you need it, and you will do well.

If you plan on taking the MCAT, this class is an absolute must. Spranger is a very good lecturer and this class goes through everything about the human body. This class is definitely difficult, but if you put in the time in studying for exams, it is very realistic to get a 4.0 as theres a lot of extra credit offered. (exams are also not that bad)

Dr. Spranger is one of my favorite professors at MSU. Physiology is a tough subject and many students conflate difficulty of a subject with the difficulty of a professor- which is inappropriate. He made physiology as simple and easy as he could. In fact, I refer back to his simple slides regularly as a medical student. He's also kind and patient!

Mr. Spranger is an amazing professor who provides detailed lectures and resources to help you succeed in his class. He's approachable, easy to reach, and always willing to explain topics more thoroughly if needed. As long as you manage your time well and put in consistent effort, you'll do great.

This class was one of the harder classes for my major; however, it was reasonable. He offers lots of extra credit. I went to all the lectures and studied a lot for this class and received the grade I wanted, with no prior knowledge in physiology. He is a fair teacher and I respect his dedication and the resources he provides for his students.

Professor Spranger takes pride in what he teaches, he knows the material extensively and teaches it in a way that is very graspable if you also put effort in. I 4.0 his class because of his teaching style and his effort he puts into his lectures and students. I recommend him if you want to actually learn and not just memorize and forget.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class is extremely challenging and requires a LOT of work outside of class. Many like to complain about the class, but do not want to put in any extra work to make the class more doable. Marty is a good professor that challenges you to be ready for grad school, and I feel more prepared now for the work load required in grad school. Take it.

PSL 310 was the first class at msu where I felt the lectures clearly tied into the medical profession in an engaging way that prepares premeds. Lectures are active with top hat, and while this makes attendance mandatory, helps with applicable test content and grade boost. The content is clear with posted review questions and help always available.

Sprangers course IS difficult. It takes work. This class shouldn't be easy, it is important. If you're taking it, you're taking it for a reason. He is an awesome prof whose course is made to challenge you WHICH IS GOOD. He gives lots of extra credit and does set you up to succeed. This class is completely doable if you put in the work. He's awesome

I took the class last fall and it covers a lot of material and lectures can be pretty dense. I studied by going back and reviewing the recorded lectures the next few days to really soak in all the material. He's a good teacher and his personal stories really stuck with me. If you put in the effort, this class can be rewarding.

Pre-Health Physiology is a tough course. Although a professor cannot make the content smaller or easier, Spranger provided an abundance of resources on both the content and how to study to make this class easier. Spranger made me feel like I could do well and he really wanted me to do well. In a tough course, this support meant a lot.
He is an amazing professor and keeps the lectures interesting. I was able to apply the methods he suggested to other aspects of my life. I seriously recommend taking his classes they are awesome!

Professor Spranger was one of the best professors I had at Michigan State. He cared for every student, and gave a lot of opportunities to have one on one meetings with him to discuss succeeding in the class. His teaching style made the material easy to learn. The study skills allowed me to succeed in his class and other pre-med classes.

I loved this class. I am writing this review 3 years later as a current medical student and to this day, Dr. Spranger is one of the best professors I've ever had. THE MATERIAL IS HARD, but he will assist your learning in so many ways if you PAY ATTENTION. Remember that he is teaching physiology, which is one of the most difficult concepts to grasp.

Professor Spranger's physio course is definitely a more challenging one. That being said, it has been one of my favorite courses I have taken in my undergraduate experience so far. He always tries to squeeze in stories during his lectures to keep everyone engaged and offers a decent amount of extra credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Physiology with Spranger is an infamous class at MSU. To be blunt, it is not an easy class, and the topics are challenging and cover a wide variety of fields. Spranger, however, is a more than understanding professor who gives numerous ways to improve your grade and is constantly helping students improve their notes and studying skills.

Dr. Spranger is hands down one of the best professors at Michigan state. PSL 310 is a great class for anyone premed. He's a cool guy and makes himself available when you need help. As any premed class, it is not easy but anyone that tries will get a 4.0. Do well and he will give you a job as a ULA.

He knows how to motivate a class on such a hard topic. His class changed the way I study and I was able to apply those methods to other aspects of my life. I would recommend anyone to take this his classes, he is seriously amazing.

Tough class, but very doable. Spranger is very honest about his expectations for the students and goes out of his way to help. The lectures are SUPER helpful and recorded for later review. He sets up help sessions with TA/ULA almost everyday, so help is everywhere. You will have to apply yourself, but he is very supportive. Rewarding class overall.

Spranger is hands down one of the best professors at MSU. He gives you every single resource you could possibly need to succeed in this course. The content was very dense and difficult, but as long as you pay attention and listen to his study method, you will excel in this course. As difficult as the content was, you can do well because of Spranger

Overall great professor and taught the course well! This is a class where attendance is very important as a lot of the exam content is directly from the lectures itself and not from the textbook. He puts the lectures up online after class so I recommend always going back and reviewing those. The class content is hard but teaching wise he's great.

Dr. Spranger is an incredible professor. I found him profoundly smart and he went above and beyond for us as students (ex. making additional practice questions, etc). You can tell that he deeply cares about his students' success, and he truly was my favorite professor during undergrad. Highly recommend you take his class- you'll learn SO much!

I took PSL 310 with Dr. Spranger and it was one of my favorite classes. Dr. Spranger does a great job explaining important concepts and was always available for any questions. He provides dozens of sessions each week to catch up on content, go over notes, and review previous exams. Spranger does everything he can to make sure his students succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: It seems easy until you bomb every test. So discouraging when you do the study skills and continue to perform poorly on exams. I am typically a 3.5-4.0 student, but was ecstatic when I got a 70%. He thinks this class should be nearly impossible for no reason. There is truly no good way to study for his exams, just memorize the AQs and pray.

I retook this class to obtain a letter of rec from Spranger, and excelled in this course besides my final. I scored lower than what I had been scoring, and he knew I wanted a letter of rec from him. He saw my score, he looked at me and said “no letter of recommendation” and went onto a rant in front of 4-5 other students. I lost respect for him.

This class is tough, but not impossible to get a 4.0 by any means. Spranger is an intense guy, but he will always help if you ask a question. TAs, SSHS, and CRs help immensely. Don't understand why so many people claim the exams are impossible. The questions are extremely similar to the PEQs, AQS, and Tophat quizzes.

Good luck in this class! Was told by TAs that he makes this class busy on purpose. Heard he is also reducing extra credit from now on which is crazy. Exam averages are always low (60s) but tries to make it seem like it's not bad. One person did not score an 100% on the final exam. Had a quiz on finals week which I don't even think is allowed..

Hardest class I think I will ever take here. Lectures take about 5-6 hours to rewrite. Have to do smart books which should be for extra credit since class is already heavy, and I've been told by ULAs textbook is no help overall and not to even read it. You will have no life outside of class if you want a good grade. Class is very interesting tho.

An intense personality, feels bad for next semester as he is reducing extra credit, as someone told him that they didn't need to study for the last exam and could still 4.0. I have never seen a prof receive so many gifts during office hours. he does not take the blame in almost a quarter of the class failing. The averages on all exams were failing

Prof. Spranger ruined my love for physiology. I was very excited to take his course, but Prof Spranger made his exams way too difficult for the average student. He expects you to put 15+ hours of studying in during the week, as if his students don't have other difficult pre-health classes, a job, clubs, and maybe even a social life.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Exams are tough. I studied for 20+ hours a week by rewatching lecture videos and rewriting concepts in my own words. Recommend making flashcards of AQs and memorizing them. I'm a terrible test taker and got 85-95s on most all my exams doing this. Super hard class but definitely possible to 4.0. Spranger is egotistical but its not outrageous.

His lectures are interesting, and the homework is manageable. However, the exams are extremely detailed and harder than expected. These are not a fair measure of the information students know.  If possible, take this class at a community college instead. He is very condescending to students, so be prepared.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Spranger is exactly what everyone says he is. When preparing for this course I thought "this cant be that bad" it is. Your exams are not an accurate representation of your knowledge. The amount of homework is diabolical. Spranger steps over the boundary of a teacher student relationship. do yourself a favor and take at a community college
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is my favorite content-wise but takes up the most time outside of class. I rewatched every lecture taking notes then rewrote them into my own words + review. Ended up with about 85% on each exam but 4.0 with all the extra credit he gives. Spranger himself is a little full of himself but genuinely believes everyone can succeed.

This class is very challenging and takes a lot of time outside of lecture just due to the nature of the content. Spranger does teach well and he is very good about only testing on content he actually talked about in class. Tests are very hard though, so be prepared to spend 15-20 hours per week outside of lecture if you want to do well.

Came to have a love hate relationship with professor Spranger. While his lectures are some of the most interesting I've ever sat in, it was a fight to survive from start to finish in his course. You will be spending so much time on hw and review and extra credit if you're lucky, while losing the plot. Then turn around and have a tophat quiz due :,(

I have mixed feelings about him. I feel like he is a good dude, but at other times… I can agree with what others are saying. Very grateful for the extra credit, but the fact that people clap for him for giving extra credit1… big ego boost for him lol. I will say that he is great at his job. Be prepared for heavy lectures and tests!

This course was the best class I've ever taken, it made me fall in love with learning again. However, the professor is cruel and egotistical. He gets off on watching students struggle. He lacks empathy for students and is condescending. Take the course, but do not expect to like the professor.

I think he is a great teacher and truly passionate about his teaching. He gives many study tools for the exams, which help if utilized. He also gives a good amount of extra credit, which ultimately helps. This class is not easy, but if you put in the time and effort, it will pay off in the end.

I really enjoyed Spranger and pretty much everything about PSL310. He's the type of guy who has high expectations for his students, but makes an effort to give us a bunch of resources to succeed. This class requires an adequate amount of time and effort. Study smart and stay focused.

This class is content-heavy and time-consuming.  It requires you to rewatch lectures and take detailed notes. To help organize your notes, use the Aqs as an outline. This was helpful to me. Quizzes are assigned randomly, and you have only two days to complete them, so watch out for those D2L emails. If you do all of this, you should succeed.

This is hands down my favorite professor. Gives amazing lectures and is hilarious. Although this class was very very challenging with a heavy course load, he gives you all the tools you need to succeed in the class. If you follow his study method he teaches during lecture, you will succeed in the course.

Everybody seems to love or hate Spranger but I'm pretty neutral. Yes his class is hard but he gives you everything you need to succeed. He's a weird guy though- very egotistical. Just rewatch the lectures and include everything he says in your notes and you'll be fine.

Spranger assigns quizzes on weekends and makes them due at ridiculous times. Good luck with his class, because he says he's giving less extra credit.. as if the course isn't hard enough. Kiss your life away if you take this class; every free second will be for this course. The AQs and PEQs don't help, and everything he says will be on the exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This man gets off on being cruel. This class is so much material while trying to stay afloat in other classes. Random due dates for quizzes, 5 hard exams, thinks he's a god for giving 20 EC points. He's rude and condescending.

Overall PSL 310 is a difficult course. Prof. Spranger knows this and provides all the resources necessary to succeed. You will do well if you put in the effort (which is a lot). His test replacement policy is also a nice plus in case you have a bad test.

This man stressed me out. He sent out quizzes that were not in the syllabus and were due at random times a day later. He made weird comments every lecture and gets off on students who suck up to him. He'll give extra credit and just stand there to listen to everyone praise him. Did not need to know details about his colonoscopy!

Literally the best professor I have ever had. While this was the most difficult class I have taken, I have never been more engaged in a course. Spranger WANTS you to do well, so many help/review sessions offered. Plenty of extra credit opportunities as well. Exams are difficult, but if you rewatch lectures and study hard, you will be fine.

Spranger seems like a chill guy, and his lectures are pretty good. It's just so much content and constant studying. I think it's just a hard class anyway, but the exam questions are sometimes ridiculous, like "If I were microscopic inside of the heart during systole" You really have to memorize everything he says in each video in order to do well.

I have NEVER in my 3 years at MSU, had such a hard class. I spend hours each day and have never gotten a passing grade. Every time I reach out for help, I am told that I am not doing enough, though I have tried all of his study methods. When I used to go to office hours, they were always packed with suck-ups, one girl even brought him chocolates.
Extremely easy class. Watch the videos, pay attention to what he says, and you should easily get an A on the exam. I did this about two days before each exam.

Really tough class, I've gone to every lecture and after I rewatch it n write notes/studying it took me ~6hrs each, I stand at a 3.5. He is a great teacher n is willing to help, his pettiness/side comments lowkey crack me up the sensitive people don't like that tho. I like the content but its just sm I filled 1.5 notebooks, just don't get behind.

Literally used the first day of class to trauma dump. He is extremely uncomfortable to be around and has the biggest ego ever. If you do badly, it's your fault for not doing his study skills, even though I did do them and still did badly. He goes on tangents in his lectures, so you never know what will actually be on the exam.

I never write reviews on this website, or any forum for that matter. But I am taking time out of my busy day to write this because this guy actually sucks. I do well in his class dont get me wrong, 90 exam average, but the number of times this guy assigns a quiz (he never mentions it in class) on a saturday or sunday and you get 1 and a half days
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Manner of communication with students is unacceptable & unprofessional. We're all sorry he didn't get into med school if it meant this personality. He lectures well, and I enjoy the content, but the attitude is incorrigible. Authority comes with responsibility. TAs except for Helen are mini Sprangers. Bullies students, tone, & is unapproachable.

This class was easily the hardest class I have taken for my major. I would feel really good about one exam and bad about another but my scores would be better with the one I felt worse with. I made a mistake of not asking for help soon enough, so when people say go to office hours, I would be going to AT LEAST one every week.

This class has almost deterred me from continuing with my major, and it's not because of the content. If other pre-med professors are as egotistical and rude as Spranger, I don't want any part of it. The fact that he truly thinks he is so good at teaching that if you fail it is because you are lazy and not good enough is demoralizing. DO NOT TAKE!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This material is hard but Spranger is amazing. The class gets a bad rep because it is content dense, but he genuinely is an amazing teacher who gives you all the resources needed. The class is a time commitment, but if you put in the work the material is not actually complicated. He is super approachable and is super fair to those who actually try.

Professor Spranger has a specific teaching style that takes some getting used to, but if you engage with it, the class can be very rewarding. Attend lectures, study the textbook, and use resources like unstuckstudy AI to prep - it'll pay off.

Same reviewer (ego/UMich before). He announced to the class several times he's read his ratings here--+ takes pride in it. Unbelievable/delusional to me that he sees sm justified frustration + thinks failing his students = a sign of success. Classic cherry picking (validates only ass kisser opinions). Thinks his trauma is a personality trait/excuse
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Few weeks into this class--solid 4.0 student, dual degree, hs valedictorian + firmly believe in kindness in academia. One of the most inflated fragile egos I've ever seen, from day 1. Unbearable, aggressive towards students (including public humiliation), + towards those who are alr shy/uncertain. Pits students against (this is not UMich grading?)
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: So I took Sprangers class asynchronous over the summer and the shear amount of work I did for this class was ridiculous. We had a deadline almost every 4 days and I lost majority of my summer taking this class. The class had its own personal grading systems and exams where something you really couldn't afford to miss and the questions were specific
This class was super hard I felt like I was drowning in it. I want to cry.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Just watch the lectures, write down the nuanced information from each lecture, review the AQ and answers after each lecture, and do the practice exams, and you should do well.

Mr. Spranger straight up made one of my friends cry in office hours.  She went in to get help, however Mr. Spranger ripped her entire life apart, because she wasn't 'trying' enough.  I put in 25 hours+ a week, but only got 2.0 in the class.  I hope someone sees this as a strong warning to not take PSL 310.

Man is a straight-up egomaniac.  Look at the below comments, half are about how funny Marty is and how great...  Way to write your own reviews, Marty.  He would email us during football games to remind us of exams in 3 weeks and how we were failing if we were at the football game and not the library.  He would try to be motivational but was not...

Worst professor by far in the physiology department if not the university.  Marty I am sorry dropping twice from medical school has this badly damaged your fragile ego, however, do not take it out on your students.  To anyone thinking this class is helpful, it is not.  He expects at least 30 hours a week for a 3.0.  I wish I had more than 350 words

I loved this class. It has been my favorite so far at MSU, and Spranger was awesome. Really funny dude, strict but not unreasonable. I think people who rag on him all the time just don't study enough. Go into the class with an open mind and don't listen to reviews. Not an AI written (no idea why there are so many), this is honest.

You have to sacrifice some of your time to do well in the course. There will be times where it feels impossible to learn everything that is needed, but with enough time and discipline, you will get it. There are many opportunities to get help with understanding content, and if you can explain the concepts in a conversation, you'll be good to go.

I personally loved this man's lectures but they were not reflective of the exams. For some he'd primarily focus on one or two slides during the lecture and then have it be the bulk of the exams especially with the drugs. Very engaging lectures and a funny man.

I have never felt so disrespected in a class setting by a professor. Attendance was mandatory (which is fine) but he would actively make fun of students who showed up late and would even publicly shame them for it, discouraging people to show up. The tests were difficult and I did not feel he prepared students for them. DO NOT TAKE AT ALL COSTS.

This was a monster of a class. Literally SO much content youll learn, so be ready to put in a significant amount of time to learn. Spranger is super knowledgeable and has high expectations but gives you lots of opportunity. I thought the class was too much to be honest but what can ya do. Put in work and youll have a good background for med school

I had just finished taking this class again. I passed with a 1.0 barely. He is an amazing person very inspirational- not an amazing professor. He has a large ego, and underestimates the difficulty of the content and assumes students are just lazy if they get a bad score. I seriously thought about changing my major just so I didn't have to retake it

He lectures great BUT he has a very fragile ego and will not take any complaints or criticism. He is quite condescending. He is inconsiderate of students time and also intentionally makes the last exam much much harder than the others. He will say he wants all students to succeed but he purposefully makes that hard for people.

Spranger is a good professor but prepare for the workload of this class. I found myself spending the bulk of my studying time on this class. Attendance required and he will put an attendance code up, so don't be late. Lectures were very confusing to sit through and you will need to rewatch all of the lectures to study. Offers many opportunities

Nice guy. He lives up to the hype but not as much as you would think. Prepare to feel very behind if you do not understand course content. Just study correctly 1 week before your exams and you're guaranteed a 95%

Very lecture heavy and you're graded with a lot of exam points. Lectures are long and recorded, but they take a long time to watch. Likes interacting with students before and during lecture. Utilize office hours because they're very helpful. Knows a lot of physiology and loves what he teaches. A lot of content, but it isn't impossible to do well

Spranger is very knowledgeable, but has high expectations. Lectures very quickly and expects you to rewatch/take notes on 1hr 50min lectures, which takes ~ 4hrs. This is the most content heavy class I've taken. It's not impossible to succeed, but very time consuming and tiring to do so. Don't take this with other hard classes if you can avoid it.

Amount of content given in this one-semester class is not reasonable. Have to be ready to commit to learning the material like a 12-hour part-time job to have a chance of getting a decent grade. Instead of an assessment of each system, three units are grouped into one large test. Knowledgeable professor but teaches like we're all physiology majors.

Professor wants you to learn and is very knowledgeable, but gives no leniency in grades or deadlines and can be rude. Rambles for the whole 2.5 hour lecture and expects you to go home and rewatch the whole thing which takes about 4 hours. Provides many resources but get ready to have no life bc you're always studying for this class. Good luck...
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Spranger is the best prof I have ever had. There is an extreme amount of content and exams ask very specific questions, but with the study skills he provides you will succeed in the class. He also gives out multiple extra credit points during the semester.

Prof Spranger was a great professor- cared about his students, made lectures engaging, and provides a lot of resources for students to succeed. The class is super content heavy but you just have to put in the work to succeed.

I think if you're willing to put in the hours - about 4 per lecture - to watch the videos, do practice problems, and take notes you will end up with a 4.0.  He is willing to help but he can't hold your hand.  I never went to office hours, but his class is thorough and he has tons of resources.  Don't take this class if you are not self sufficient.

Dr. Spranger is one of my favorite professors! He would often sit with students in the study area outside of class and chat, so I got to know him pretty well. I loved that the class was more of a conversation than him lecturing at us. I learned a lot from him. I didn't think his exams were hard. I just studied the day before for a couple hours.
Great professer as far as preparing you for for future classes but difficult. Be prepared to spend lots of time outside of class studying for exam but he sets you up so well for future classes.

The workload for this class is severe. Attend 2 1/2 hour lectures, which are incredibly long as he talks at you for the full time, then rewatch the lectures when you get home to understand the material. He is available for office hours, but is not respectful of people's time and was personally rude towards me. TA's are extremely helpful, use them!

This class is incredibly detail-oriented and heavily graded on exams, so it is important to pay attention. There are many resources to help you succeed in this class, however, Professor Spranger does not take criticism well and will be defensive if questioned. He is incredibly generous with his extra credit and wants everyone to succeed.

You have to attend in person lectures as he writes on the slides he teaches off of. If you pay attention in class you will know whats on the exams and he has a fairly fun personality. However, his exams are very hard and he doesnt mind criticism well.

I have never met a professor who is so full of himself and yet students continue praising him. It does not make sense. He is genuine and a decent professor, but it is quite annoying to constantly hear his achievements and him praising the top students. I'd rather learn than spend 30 minutes listening to him talk about dropping out of med school.

Spranger is my favorite professor I've had at MSU. The class is difficult and there is a great amount of information to retain for the exams, but you will not meet another pre-health professor that cares more about his students and is so enthusiastic about what he teaches. I would take any class taught by Spranger.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: lectures were long, and the tests were much harder than the content taught. there was way too much content for the amount of time in the semester. atmosphere was uncomfortable, professor was extremely unapproachable, had an attitude and was disrespectful of student's time.

This prof is GOATED. Such a great lecturer and quite knowledgeable about the subjects he teaches. He is ready to admit to any mistakes he makes (such as a lecture slide error) and he cares about his students doing well. His exams are significantly harder because they make you think, but they are fair in regards to what he includes in the slides.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger is a wonderful professor that truly cares for his students. Even through his health difficulties, he provides the opportunity for us to be in-person for lecture! While his exams are difficult, they only cover lecture material and are completely fair. His subject knowledge is incomparable, and his examples are always helpful! FAV PROF

He is very knowledgeable about physiology. I don't like the environment as he is unapproachable and doesn't seem to respect students' time. There is a ton of material but focus on taking good notes and going through the AQs. Don't let him pressure you into using his study skills bc you can do well without changing your methods to suit his ego.
He puts the p in poop professor
Poop professor. Im pretty sure he writes his postive reviews.
Bad professor, Im pretty sure he writes these reviews himself

Very disorganized. Rushes students, & gives lectures because he cannot teach his content at a good enough pace for students to retain it. This professor has to highly inflate his grades ( 40+ of extra credit ) for reference exams are worth 60. Hes disrespectful to students time, and frankly out of touch. He has assigned homework over breaks. Creepy

Marty cares about his students. He makes many arrangements so that students can get the help they need to succeed. He does talk about himself a lot but it is more interesting than just reading off slides for two hours. The exams are very hard and he does pile on quizzes. He does a good job with such a hard and content-heavy class.

Best prof Ive ever had. Students will line up first week of class vying for a letter of rec. Not recommended. Study hard, be active online, and prove yourself to a point where he knows of you before even meeting you. Amazing lectures, easy to understand and catch onto. Uses TopHat. Very engaging content, re-ignited my love of health.

Genuinely my favorite professor I've ever had. Show him that you care, and you will be rewarded. People line up to kiss his shoes for a letter of rec first week of classes-don't do that. Put in the work, be active on discord, and prove yourself so he knows you before he ever sees you. He has an ego, but as he should. He re-ignited my love of health
Marty is the GOAT. He teaches one of the toughest courses at MSU and does literally everything that he can to help you succeed. If you do not do well in PSL310, then it is on you.

Dr. Spranger did a great job teaching intro level physio. He organized the course very well and it was a pretty tough class context-wise, but there are many opportunities for extra credit. The lectures are important to attend so while they weren't mandatory I attended all of them. He does get off track throughout lecs sometimes for funny stories.
Spranger is one of the most caring professors I have ever had at MSU it is a tough class but if you put in the effort a 4.0 is achievable.

Spranger is by far my favorite professor I've had at MSU. He truly cares about his students and does his absolute best to prepare us for our future as healthcare professionals. He is very clear about his resources and how to exceed in his class- do them! His lectures are engaging and I will miss this class. I truly felt I learned the content.

Can't say enough good things about Spranger. He is so dedicated to his students, and provides a plethora of opportunities for you to succeed in his class, it's up to you if you use them. He's a fair professor, the content on his exams is what is on the exams. It is very content heavy, but it is also preparing you to become a health professional.

Prof Spanger was extremely caring and considerate. He genuinely cares about his students' success. I thought he tried his best to make the lectures interesting even though they were content HEAVY and the concepts are difficult. Highly recommend. PS: you WILL have to study even if you have never had to before

Spranger is by far the best professor I have EVER had!  I'm not going to lie, this class is pretty difficult, but he really does care about the students and does the best he can to help them succeed. Attendance is mandatory but it really is so worth your time. Lots of extra credit opportunities and just a super enjoyable class overall!

Spranger is the best prof I've ever had in my life. You can tell he enjoys his job and tries to make lectures interesting. Most of all, I appreciate how direct his tests are. Memorize and understand the slides, and you'll get a 4.0, no question. He also does well to prepare you for med school, etc. Also a lot of extra credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger is easily the best professor that I've had my entire 3 years here at state so far. He's a genuinely caring professor, and so outgoing. You can tell he loves teaching the content of this class. This class is in no way easy, but if you take notes and study correctly you'll have a fulfilling experience in the class.

Spranger is easily the best professor I have had during my entire college career. Not only does he teach the content of the course extremely well, but you can really tell that he cares about each of his students on a deep level. He has also structured the course well, and help is easy to find. I cannot say enough good things about him.

I have never felt compelled to write a review for any professor (I am a Junior), but I feel I should for Professor Spranger. Put simply, he has gone above and beyond for me and my classmates. In a class of 600 students, he has the unique gift of being able to connect with me in a way that feels personal. He really cares and brings his all everyday!

Professor Spranger was a great professor! He is passionate about physiology and gives many resources to find success. I would accredit some of my difficulty to not having taken anatomy prior to physiology; I'd do this if I could go back in time. Regardless, I learned a great amount and learned how to put meaningful effort into a class.

Spranger was passionate about what he was teaching and asked lots of application questions to make sure students were picking up on the information. His practice questions and practice exams were reflective of the actual exams and his review sessions were VERY helpful. If you actually pay attention in class and study well then you will do fine.

All I can say was that his lectures are very good and interactive. In terms of how he tests that material, you don't really know what to focus on because his lecture slides consist of images that he annotates multiple times before clearing them and moving onto the next slide. As a result of him pulling from memory, conflicting facts come up often.

Professor Spranger was by far THE BEST professor I've ever had here at Michigan State University. I find myself taking other classes and referring back to my notes from PSL 250 because he was the only person that could get me to understand anything!! His study habits he suggests help so much and he provides SO much extra credit. I love him!!
Warning do not take you have been warned
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Spranger is a great professor who really cares about his students education. He offers a lot of resources to help students succeed which includes a lot of extra credit. The workload is very fair; not too much but not too little.  My only complaint is the exams are very, very difficult. You must completely master the material to do good.

Prof. Spranger is very fair and wants to see everyone succeed. He offers ample opportunities to receive help outside of class. There's lots of content covered in small amounts of time, but you're given the resources to succeed so a 4.0 in this class is very doable if you put in the work.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He is very intelligent and really loves teaching. He won't baby you through the course but he'll provide help if needed as he really wishes for his students to succeed and learn the material. The material is difficult but definitely reasonable. Practice questions and practice exams are provided and highly reflective of exams.

Super fair. ATTEND SSHS. I got a 4.0 on every test in this course, but my friend 2.5'd every exam and still got a 4.0 because of the amount of extra credit he provided. He gives so many chances and has so many resources to succeed. This class is a lot of work, but with basic work ethic, you will probably 4.0. Spranger is amazing, I learned so much!

Spranger is extremely knowledgeable about this course. The lectures are super heavy and the exams are heavyweight but I mean this is Physiology, it is meant to be hard. However, despite that, he gives extra credits and resources to help you prepare for exams, use them. You gotta grind on this course but it is not too much to handle. P.S be regular.

He gives a lot of extra credit but the class is extremely difficult and your grade is based heavily on exams. Make sure you show up because attendance makes up a decent portion of the grade and he sometimes gives extra credit when you say good afternoon. Make sure you study and you will be fine

Exams was super hard 60 questions per exam no opennote. He doesn't listen to students request. It's his way or the high way kinda attitude. Most definitely the worst professor at MSU. Wish another professor was available
Stay away from this guy at all costs. I cried so many times taking this course and he made me feel stupid at times

He made my semester a living nightmare. if you're taking his class beware he gave us lectures about bad students was doing. He expects you to breathe, live and eat physiology. Also he has a smart mouth when its comes to email and replying. Only thing nice about Marty is he provides extra credit buts that's it . worst professor at MSU hands down.

Dr. Spranger is very knowledgeable, but also quite understanding and kind of many circumstances. He approved my RCPD form and worked with me to make a very scary class quite manageable. FOLLOW HIS STUDY SKILLS! You will need to devote a lot more time than you think to this! ALWAYS keep an eye on your D2L for TopHat quizzes & update posts!
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Spranger was by far my favorite professor I have had throughout my 2.5 years here at state. The content is dense which makes it difficult, but Spranger is extremely motivating, helpful, and fair. He curves exams and offer many opportunities to get points back. I have never learned so much in a class before content wise and how to study! He's great!

This has probably been my favorite class so far. It's hard, but it's for pre-med students, so it should be hard, right? The professor gives you so much extra credit and so many things on the side to help you, and his lectures really show his passion for teaching. Again, best class in the world, do the study skills.

He was a great lecturer but the class itself covers a lot of content. He warns you that if you do not take time to study you will be screwed on the exams. He will give you a lot of resources and homework to do for grade but they are worth doing.

He truly cares about his students. He is a super fair grader and his exams are reflective of his lectures. By no means is the class easy, but he makes it manageable if you come to class, participate, and re-watch the lectures. Would 100% take another course with him in the future. Spranger, you will be missed!

Professor Spranger is awesome. Easily one of my top 3 professors I've had at MSU. It is obvious he cares deeply about the class and the students. He grades fair, listens to his students for advice, and gives multiple extra credit opportunities. On top of that, he's just a really cool guy. Great professor and I would take his class again 100%.

This was my favorite class! I can't stress enough how important it is to draw out every picture he puts on the slides- it is the key to the easiest 4.0 for this class. Dr. Spranger was super funny and an engaging prof who was very understanding with grades, giving multiple opportunities to increase exam grades. Lectures are a bit long, though.

Dr. Spranger was seriously an amazing professor. Spranger really cares about his students. The class is for sure difficult content-wise, but he gives multiple opportunities to improve your grade if you have bad moments. We got EC for saying good afternoon.. Prepare in advance and study over time for exams. Use outside resources that are offered.

Dr. Spranger is an EXCELLENT professor and the best professor I have had at MSU. The content is hard- there is no getting past that so that is why the class averages can be low on exams. But Dr. Spranger genuinely cares about his students and wants all of us to succeed. He outlines EXACTLY what to do to succeed and is passionate about the material.

This class is difficult but Dr. Spranger knows what he is talking about & makes the lectures engaging. I found that in order to get a good grade in the class, rewatching each lecture was necessary. However, this is due to the fact that the CLASS itself is difficult. He offered lots of opportunities to get extra credit & overall is a funny guy.

PSL 310- Spranger is a great professor and is very knowledgable! The class is very difficult itself but Spranger offers many resources such as Study skills, content review and extra credit. He may be intimidating but he truly wants his students to succeed and truly appreciates feedback! Arguably one of the best professors I've had in college.

PSL310 not 240. This class is hard. I think that's why he has a poor rating because he's great at his job. He records the lectures and posts them so you can go back and watch, his TAs and ULAs hold content review and study sessions multiple times per week, and he offers a ton of ways to get points back that you lost on the exam. Highly recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He's passionate about teaching and when in class you can tell. However, the amount of content he covers in lecture is insane. Its 40-50 slides per section and 3-4 sections per unit (aka per exam). I would say this class is way more than an intro, relisten to lecture and take notes to 4.0. Quiz, participation, attendance, exam, flipgrid make grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger is easily the best professor I have had to date. His lectures are really content heavy and he does expect a lot out of his students, but he also provides so many resources that will help them succeed. If you are ready to put in A LOT of time and effort, you will get a really fulfilling experience out of his class! Highly recommend.

BEST prof I have ever had at Michigan State. If you care about learning and love the human body, his classes are for you! Tests are VERY fair, only tests on what is said in lecture. Follow his study skills and go to review sessions and you can 4.0.
This class is very difficult, but Professor Spranger is an incredible teacher and makes class fun. The exams are challenging but he is very good at giving back points that the majority got wrong.

honestly it pains me to give this man a good review, but his lectures were amazing!! personally, he's annoying and arrogant. HIS EXAMS ARE HARD AND THEY MAKE YOU THINK, but you need to put the time and effort into the class. he gives you everything you need to succeed but its on you what to do with the info. HE GIVES EXTRA CREDIT AND CURVES EXAMS

Had a very bad experience and interactions with Dr. Spranger. Can be quite rude and unprofessional. He is good at teaching and explaining material but the amount of material is outrageous. Class is a full time job. The lectures and study skills do NOT reflect most exams. You can study for 4 days straight and get a 82% exam at best.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger is great at lecturing - he is one of the hardest professors in PSL 431, and his tests are a challenge but it really prepares you for real world situations. It is completely possible to do well in his class if you put in the time and work. I think Dr. Spranger is one of the professors I have learned the most for at MSU.

He is one of the rudest and least personable teachers I have ever had. He has his confused TAs run the help sessions and they cover little to no material that will actually help you with the exams- that are unfair and have question pertaining to a 30 second segment of the lecture instead of the main topics he should want you to learn. Terrible.

Just a wonderful journey. This course put me in desperate at the beginning of the semester but he soon gave me hope by letting me know there are Dr. Spranger, and so many TAs and LAs who are really willing to help. I was moved by them (I haven't felt that warmth for a while). You really have somebody to help when you have troubles with the course.

One of the worst professors I have had at MSU. He is rude and doesn't truly care about teaching students or about students' success. He goes way past his lecture time and his exams are not a fair evaluation of your knowledge. Class average on his exam was around 50%. He wants to challenge students, but being unfair is not challenging students.

One of the worst professors Ive had at MSU. Hes a nice guy and he knows what hes talking about, but the content on exams is much more advanced than anything we are taught in this class. Exam averages dropped significantly when he started teaching. Does little to help you prepare for his exams. If hes assigned professor, dont take his class.

If you have the choice between PSL 310 and PSL 431, go with 310. I took 431 this semester because I believed it would challenge me more, but not the point of despair. Spranger incorrectly teaches concepts, goes overtime, and does not respond well to criticism. Upper levels should be difficult, but not to the point the whole class is failing.

For all that is holy, do not take PSL 431 if Dr. Spranger is teaching it. He is an alright teacher and explains concepts well, but he will absolutely obliterate you with his exams. To compare, I have 4.0 all major sciences (orgo, biochem, etc) and cannot put into words how unfathomably hard he makes his exams. If you want to go to med school, dip

This class is definitely challenging don't get me wrong, but Spranger makes it easier than any other professor probably could. He makes it so that the amount of effort you put in is reflective of your grade, so if you want to succeed just work hard. He is a pretty accessible guy out of class as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Good professor but makes this class much harder than it needs to be. Can't access new material until you get 100% on previous, entire course grade is exams (average is usually less than 70%), quizzes, and a small amt of extra credit. Would be manageable if not online. Hardest class I've ever taken, and truly doesn't need to be.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is the most content-heavy class I have ever taken. Spranger really makes you work for a 4.0 but you will not be disappointed if you put in the work. I left this class with more knowledge than any other course I have taken. Superb professor who cares about his students and their learning.

Honestly, I dont have anything to say negatively about this course besides the flipgrids. I just found it difficult to teach a concept well under 4 minutes. Nonetheless, I thought Dr. Spranger did exceptionally well with his explanations. Yes the course is challenging and requires attentiveness but he gives you all the tools to succeed.

Professor Spranger's course required a lot of time and effort, along with a solid understanding of the concepts. The lectures were long and in-depth and there were many check-in quizzes afterwards. The tests were not easy and the video projects took a lot of time, however a good grade can be achieved with enough dedication.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Hardest class I took Spring 2021, Structure of class online was superb making it easy to navigate and understand assignments/due dates. Gave many resources to help supplement learning, however found the amount of material we needed to cover for each exam to be overwhelming. Graded only in few categories, but gave out extra credit to help out GPAs.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger was great. He expects a lot because he really wants you to learn the material, but he's really good at explaining difficult concepts and he gave a lot of extra credit. The course is super challenging, but I was able to 4.0 it with a TON of studying. Take his study skills seriously because they're honestly the key to a good grade.

Okay, I have never had a worse professor. If you do not spend 4 hours a day on this course, then you will not 4.0 and maybe not even 3.5. He will briefly mention something in his lecture, there are 83 average 40 minutes throughout the semester, and that concept will be 3 test questions. His tests are there to trick you. Take at LCC if u can.

I think he is a good professor and I did learn a lot, but there is way too much material for this class. It was extremely overwhelming and very difficult to keep up. This is the only class I took this semester, so I did well but I can't imagine how it would be possible to get a 4.0 with a full class schedule. Take a different class if possible.

Great professor! If you do his study skills that he creates for you from the start you will pass the class no problem. His lectures are interesting and he gives you so many resources for you to do well. No textbook needed, you have topic quizzes that you get 2 chances on but your grade are mostly the 5 exams. Take this class you will learn a lot!

Spranger is the most uncaring professor I have ever had, although his lectures are interesting. His ego is too big for his well being. The work load in this class is crazy, it is possible to well as long as you keep up with the lectures and study skills.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Spranger is a sassy man and does not take any sort of criticism well. He uses 4 different platforms for this class: D2L, TopHat, FlipGrid, and a Facebook group. Too much to keep up with. However! He does give at least 5 points back on each exam and offered a 10 point increase if class average was 70% on an exam (it was 63% but gave 6pts still)

He seems very intimidating at first but is very nice and wants you to succeed. He will tell you that you need to do his study skills to pass the class and believe him when he says that. The study skills help tremendously and the review sessions before exams help as well. Make sure you keep up on the videos or they will pile up quickly. Curved exams

Dr. Spranger is a kind professor. I admit that the class he teaches covers a lot of knowledge and is kind of difficult. But, Dr. Spranger used a lot of methods to help students learn more in distance study. His class is worth taking.

Spranger's PSL 250 class is probably the most difficult course I have taken; however, I have learned so much and he is truly an excellent professor. In exams, he is always generous with giving the class extra credit. As long as you keep up with the work, Spranger provides all the resources you need to succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: This class is defiantly a challenge but it is not impossible. Content load is heavy but very intriguing and fun to learn so its not unbearable. This class truly does reflect How much work you put in because that is what you get out! If you put in effort and show respect then you are all good. Take this class with this professor its worth it!

Professor Spranger seems intimidating, but if you just work hard and put the time in (like he says you will have to) then you will do great. He also cares about his students success, and is very fair when providing extra credit and extra opportunities. If youre a hard worker and dont go looking for excuses, you will enjoy his course and style.

Spranger curves every test and gives opportunity for 20 points of extra credit. While the course is challenging, he provides many resources and includes office hours, TA-held review sessions, and a Facebook group chat for students to constantly have access to help. The course is challenging but doable with hard work.

Prof Spranger was a nightmare. He gave the same amount of work as he always has, with no consideration toward the pandemic as "his past classes did fine so we should too." Everyone was struggling and he just pointed us toward his TAs, he didn't seem to care about how we did. There were also lectures and quizzes almost every day.

Idk how he was in person, but online he is awful and rude. PLEASE trust me and pick PSL 310 instead if you need this course. The pace of this class is unbelievably fast and organized poorly. Assigns work over break. Many people brought their concerns to him and their comments were either deleted, dismissed, or never talked about. Hardest 4.0 ever.

DO NOT TAKE. His lectures are extremely long and in depth, you will not be able to take this class with other classes. extremely rude and condescending. I asked questions and he wud refer me to his TA. DONT EVEN GET ME STARTED ON THE GRADUATE TA. he will talk about anything BUT the material after asking for help multiple times to learn.

Professor Spranger really wants his students to challenge themselves yet he should understand that this is only an introductory class. I appreciate his effort in organizing the class but in his lecture videos many terms that he's saying remains unexplained and it's really hard to keep up the pace when you have to pause so frequently to google smth.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: THIS IS FOR PSL 250. Does not understand that this is an intro class. Treats it like you are in med school, but even then a 3rd year medical student could answer his questions. Doesn't explain topics to the extent that he expects you to know them on the exams. Overall, he does not his info, but cannot translate that to an INTRO level class.

I took this class twice. In person freshman year, failed the class. The lectures are really tough to keep up with and you probably have to watch them a few times to get all the material for the exam. Second time around was online going into senior year, much easier to keep up with lectures. The exams are really hard,he makes sure you really know it
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: By far the most challenging class I've ever taken. He expects you to attend every lecture & then go re-listen to every single lecture, as he talks through material too quickly to take any good notes, and if you don't follow all of his study skills, he assumes you don't care about succeeding. Don't expect to do well if you have a full course load.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Despite everything that you may read about him, Spranger is a great professor! I got a 4.0 and in order to do that, you have to put in some work. It seems intimidating, but I promise that if you stay engaged and go to class; a 4.0 is definitely possible! I went in thinking the worst because of what I heard, but walked out happy. Put in the effort!

He is "caring", only if you abide by and study the way he says. The class was very unorganized, he spends 3 class periods on one section & rushes through the rest. Most frustrating class ever, EC is the only positive and the only thing that saved my grade. Keep in mind, he asks every student that 4.0s his class to write a review :) best of luck....
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: This was the worst professor I've ever had

Spranger had all good intentions, but he cared much more about forcing a certain style of studying (study skills) on his students than his students actually learning the material. It seemed as if he did not care about you or your grade if you didn't do study skills, which didn't work for me and many other people.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Spranger talks extremely fast during lectures and has slides that are difficult to follow along with what he is trying to say. I will say that his lectures are recorded and he post them online, but if you are taking more than 3 classes in a semester good luck trying to keep up.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Attend lecture, go to office hours, use the study skills help sessions to your advantage! He is not kidding when he says you need to use the study skills, very helpful but time consuming. Do them ahead of time, don't get behind. He is a very good lecturer and extremely smart, he truly cares about what he is teaching. He is always willing to help.

He is caring and wants his students to do well. He is always asking positive feedback on how to improve the class. He LITERALLY tells you how to succeed. I was reluctant at first, but after following his study skills, the grades I earned made me very happy I put forth the effort.

There's no doubt that you''ll have to work for your grade, it's physiology. There's no way to simplify it. In all honesty, his class is fair and he truly does care. The amount of opportunities he gives to succeed in the class, both during and outside, is incredible. You have to want your grade, and you will get it. Keep up with the content!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class was defiinitely the hardest class I have taken at MSU and requires effort daily, however the material is relevant and is super interesting. Professor spranger does care about his students but does expect there best in return. He is always available outside of class to help as are previous students too. Overall I enjoyed this course.

Great professor if you choose to study the way he shows you. Theres a specific method he explains in order to study for his class and 4.0 it. I used his method for every exam and never got below a 90%. Exams are very challenging but if you use his method you will succeed. He also gives a ton of extra credit and curves each exam!

Sprangers PSL250 class is far from a walk in the park, BUT he gives countless opportunities to allow for success. His study skills help sessions, content review sessions and exam reviews are all excellent ways to succeed and he outlines this for his students. You will learn so much in this class, show up, get the help you need and youll do great!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I'm not sure why people hate on Marty. He is truly one of the most intelligent teachers I have ever had in my life and he truly cares about your success. Don't get me wrong, the class is really hard but any science class is hard. If you follow his instruction on how to study for the tests you are bound to do a great job. He also gives a bunch of EC

Marty teaches like he doesn't care about his students. He is very arrogant and thinks he knows everything. Overall, if you stick to listening to his notes and doing the learning objectives you should be fine. I would also say go to the TA's review session he gives hints as to what questions will be on the exam.

Marty is one of if not the best professor I have had in my college career. His class is in no way easy however he tells you exactly the steps to take in order to succeed. If you follow these steps and continue communication with him on how you can improve you will be golden. There is some incredibly interesting material that you will learn!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: This was the hardest class I've ever taken. Id say I spent at least 17+ hours of studying a week outside of class for this course and would still fail the exams. teacher talks really fast and gives terrible notes. he does post videos of the lectures but they take about 4 hours each to listen to while trying to write down everything he says
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If you plan to take the DAT/MCAT, Professor Spranger is your guy. I am currently studying for the DAT and know that his class made studying for it easier. Follow his study tips. I am a better student because of the way his class taught me to study. Understand the material and don't memorize it. His class will help you SO much in the future.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Spranger is by far the hardest teacher I have ever had BUT he is also probably one of the best. He TRULY prepares you for the rest of college and teaches you how to study. Lectures are SO boring and long and I honestly studied 20 hours a week just for this one class. Be prepared to study but be prepared to learn the most you'll ever learn.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Spranger is the type of professor that cares only if you care. If you dont put in the time and effort he wont be sympathetic, but if you show that you really want it he will work with you to make sure you get the grade you want. Would absolutely take another one of his classes, one of the best professors Ive had at MSU.

If you can write notes based off of someone just talking at you about something you have no prior knowledge to this is the class for you. Me personally, nah, I hate when professors talk at me and hope I just absorb the information like it's going to make any sense.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: The lectures tend to move at a quick pace but if you follow the special study skills he teaches then the class is very accessible and any is able to get an A. He is very fair when it comes to grades and extra credit so dont take him for granted
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: PSL 250 was by far my favorite class this semester. Although the class is as much work as people say, Dr. Spranger offers numerous resources, including his developed study skills process, to ensure his students are successful. Dr. Spranger is very approachable and cares about his students, therefore attending office hours made learning manageable.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

reviews.comment: While this class is one of the most difficult courses I have taken thus far, I think Professor Spranger is the best one to teach it. He has a passion for what he teaches. The course and its tests forces you to learn the material completely as it will be beneficial for future classes. If you want to do well in the class, you have to work for it!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Its a tough class with lots of material, but Spranger makes it doable by providing students with endless opportunities to get help and learn the subject. Its all about how well you seize these opportunities. He might look intimidating, but hes not and hes always willing to help you learn and work towards the grade you want. Great professor.

This class is going to be DIFFICULT, but only if you dont want to work for it. A lot of kids dont like Spranger because they dont do well in the class, but he gives you everything youll need. Records lectures, study methods, TA info reviews, practice questions, & lots of extra credit if you go to class. Use these & put the time in & youll 4.0
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Spranger is an awesome professor!Yes, Physiology is hard but its definitely doable considering he provides a multitude of resources to utilize, and he teaches extremely well. I have dealt with a multitude of professors that just read off powerpoints, but professor Spranger always expands and gives examples of the material he is teaching
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: PSL 250 is not a easy class by any means but its not impossible to 4.0. Spranger is a amazing professor that wants you to know your stuff when exam time comes around. The material he lectures over is super interesting and requires you to work through it, you can't take the easy way out. He is straight forward, do what he says and you will 4.o easy.

Spranger gets a bad rep but its only because it takes a lot of hard work and time to do well in his class. Yes hes a tough professor, but he its not like he wants his students to fail. As long as you arent lazy and use his study method, you will do just fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: PSL 250 is definitely one of the hardest classes I've taken at MSU, but Spranger is a really good professor to take it with. If you work for it and put in effort, you will get a good grade. His tests are straight forward and always gives 2-3 points back on them. Tons of extra credit, too. This class is so worthwhile if you TRY, give it a chance

I obtained a lot of information from this class but most importantly this class taught me how to study. A lot of people say they don't like professor Spranger and that is because they do not put the work in and end up getting a bad grade. Professor Spranger is actually a very knowledgeable professor and I really enjoyed taking his class.

Dr. Spranger is one of the most intelligent and favorite professors I have had yet. He provides countless office hours, easy participation points, extra credit, and reposts his lectures. This class takes work, like any class at MSU, but if you show up, listen to the lectures, and WATCH THE STUDY SKILL VIDEO, he will make sure you get a 4.0.

A lot of people have preconceived opinions about Spranger and this class, but he is actually really nice guy who really cares about his students and wants them to succeed. This class is tough and requires a lot of time outside of class to listen to his lectures, but if you do that and go to class, the hard work pays off.
Definately is a tough class, but the professor does a great job making it interesting, and diving deep into the material so you can get a real understanding on how the human body works

Contrary to a comment below, Spranger is anything BUT, "a laughing stock on campus". Prof Spranger is incredibly intelligent & altruistic. Many people enter this class w/ predisposed attitudes picked up around campus & don't even give the course an honest effort. Yes, this course is HARD WORK; however, the knowledge you leave w/ is unparalleled.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger is a great lecturer. Although the material is difficult, if you show up, take notes as he speaks, and do the work required outside of class, you will do fine. He is always giving extra credit for attendance and makes himself very available for question asking. He seems intimidating, but really isnt at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Going into this class people told me his class was impossible to pass and he wasn't nice but that is COMPLETELY WRONG. Spranger was an amazing teacher and he gave you all the tools to do well in his class, he cares about his students doing well and is a really great teacher. If you apply yourself and take this class seriously you will do just fine
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Spranger is one of the best professors I have had throughout college. The material of his class is very hard, but it is a physiology class, so that should be expected. He offers lots of extra help outside of class, and provides ton of resources for you to succeed, as long as you take advantage of them.

Dr. Spranger is a great lecturer. Although the material is difficult, if you show up, take notes as he speaks, and do the work required outside of class, you will do fine. He is always giving extra credit for attendance and makes himself very available for question asking. He seems intimidating, but really isnt at all. Highly recommend him.

Yes, Professor Spranger is very real guy! He can come off as hard head, but he teaches so that you actually learn the material. I have always struggled in school, but I put in the work for Spranger and I did just fine in his class.
Gives a lot of extra credit on random days but this class takes a lot of work to pass. He outlines exactly what to do to get a good grade and theres no way around it.

This was the hardest class I have ever taken, only because of Marty stranger. It has come to my attention that his name has actually become a laughing stock on campus due to his reputation of being so awful. Do not take his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger may seem indimidating at first, but once you get to know him he is a very considerate person. His goal is to challange you to create a self sustaining drive towards academic excellence. Yes, the class covers A LOT of material, and can seem a bit overwhelming, but it is worth it in the long run.
Gives great lectures really cares about his students gives great advice about studying you learn a ton makes physiology super interesting only negative is the class is time demanding

Dr. Spranger genuinely cares about the success of his students. He frequently adjusts exam scores to account for questions missed by the majority and offers extra credit. There are attendance points and easy online homework which helps your grade. He will give hints about what class will have the tophat quiz so youre prepared He is very encouraging
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Spranger ensures that his students learn the material of the class. You may have to work a little harder in this class, but it is so rewarding by the end. USE HIS STUDY SKILLS TECHNIQUE! I lived by this in his class and 4.0. It is very possible and I use his study skills in my classes now.

Professor Sprnager has a reputation for being tough, however, he is extremely easy if you just follow his guide for passing the class. Some people say he is tough but thats only because he wants you to actually learn the material, not just memorize the words on a slide. If you put in the work, you will be rewarded!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger is by far one of the best professors I have ever had and definitely one of my favorites. He gives you all of the tools and information that you need to do well in this class, you just have to put in the work. He truly cares about people succeeding and is always willing to help students understand the topics to do well in the class.
