One of the most rewarding classes Ive taken! However, you will DEFINITELY have to work for it. Listen to the lecture recordings and take good notes on the slides. I learned more in this class than most classes but you will have to study a ton to do well.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger's class is a tough class because he is giving out so much material. Unlike some professors that don't teach the material very well Dr. Spranger goes over all of the material you need to know for the exams its just up to you to go over it. He can seem intimidating at first but he has a big heart and genuinely cares about his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: You will have to work hard for your grade, but it will be worth it. He makes sure you are actually learning, and not just going through the motions. He gives you all the tools and tips necessary to succeed, and if you take his advice, you will be successful. He is very willing to help you succeed if you ask. I now use his tips for all my classes.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Professor Spranger is very knowledgeable and he is a great lecturer. If you go to his class, you will really learn a lot in an hour and half. He is willing to help students outside the class as well. He is fair and caring for students. Put effort in, and you will get great grades!

Marty was one of the best professors I've had. Although the class may seem difficult at first, if you are willing to put in effort and try to learn the material you will see the results you want. He is fair when it comes to his tests and if you think that something was unfair he will give you the opportunity to overturn it. Great professor overall.

Professor Spranger was one of my favorite Professors at Michigan State University by far. He is an intellectual and is beyond knowledgeable when it comes to the fields that he teaches. He was more than happy to help me outside the class room during his office hours and provided many opportunities so students could do excel in the class.

Marty is a tough grader with tests that can be difficult, but for good reason. He wants his students to be successful in upper level biology classes. Anything worth doing takes effort. This class prepares you for ever other science class on campus. It will pay off
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational

reviews.comment: For an intro level course, goes way in depth. Spends about 4 lectures on one body system and then ends up falling behind and not covering lots of other body systems. Tests are insanely hard and averages were around the 60-70s, but gives extra credit sometimes. If possible, take with some other prof. This class will dominate your schedule.

Spranger is my favorite professor I've had in college. You have to take a lot of your time out of your semester to study for his exams but it's worth it. Make sure you follow his study skills videos. Office hours will definitely help and he has an awesome TA that takes a lot of his time to help students. He holds a lot of review sessions that help.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Spranger is by far the best professor i've had. He puts in a lot of work to help students succeed. GO TO OFFICE HRS. Go to class for participation pnts and occasional quizzes. His exams are EXTREMELY hard. This class takes up your ENTIRE life. But material is super cool and you learn an insane amount of material. Favorite class i've taken
Terrible professor overall. Tests were very hard, he's very rude and he took pride on students receiving low grades. If you want to succeed, you will avoid his class at all costs.

I enjoyed this subject before I took it from this guy. I've never struggled so much in a class, and I was an all A student before it. Exams were very difficult. To actually do well in his class, you'd have to spend every waking hour studying for it. I would avoid taking a class from him at all costs.
He recommends listening to his lectures again and taking notes on that. It's DEFINITELY necessary

reviews.comment: The tests are extremely difficult, although he does usually curve it by a couple points. Has pop quizzes at the beginning of lecture so be on time. He does offer a decent amount of extra credit though.

reviews.comment: Spranger is a genuine guy who cares about the success of his students, however, he is forced by the BIO department to teach his class in a way that is ridiculously hard. reviews.comment: Hard class, but Spranger is a cool guy. Willing to help you succeed if you go and talk to him in office hours. Lots of time required outside of class studying. reviews.comment: Very smart prof. However, he is also very full of himself. The class overall is not hard with al the extra credit he gives. I received over 100% in the class. I did relisten to every lecture though. which takes at least 7 hours extra a week

reviews.comment: Dr. Spranger is very knowledgeable, but he expects WAY too much from students in an INTRODUCTORY Physiology class that is a university requirement. He expects you to set aside a ridiculous amount of time for a class that may not even be relevant for your major. Not impossible, but if his class isn't your main priority, it's so difficult.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Prof Spanger is a really good teacher, however he does cover a lot of material and it can be overwhelming. You need to set aside about 6-9 hours each week. He posts his lectures online which is very helpful. Tests are only based on what he lectures so you don't need the book! I learned so much from this class but I had to put in a ton of work.

I think Prof. Spranger is a really great lecturer - he really knows what he's talking about. Unfortunately, his voice is really dry so be careful not to space out. His exams are pretty fair (he just tests you on what he says in lecturers). So you have to be at his lecturers. You'll be able to get a good grade if you put in the work.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests are tough--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Prof. Spranger is a nice guy, gave a good amount of extra credit, told you almost exactly what topics would be on the exams, and used a good amount of examples and real life scenarios to help you understand. But the material is very hard and he makes the test incredibly hard. If you want to get above a 2.5 you have to put in A LOT of outside time
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Marty is a very good professor. His class is very difficult because there is so much content that he needs to cover in such a little time. You must put in a lot of work to get a good grade in his class. He puts his lectures up on D2l for you to re listen to. If you go to his class you have a chance to earn up to 12.5 points of extra credit.

I've had the pleasure (not) of having Spranger for biology AND physiology, and only took psl with him because he was the only person teaching it. He forces you to study how he studied in college which is cool if you have 10+ hours a week to study for only his class. He purposely makes his tests extremely hard and nit picky. Avoid him if possible.

Truly a professor who couldn't care less about his students. I had originally enjoyed his lecture style and how he put emphasis on real world examples. But, the exams proved that to be completely irrelevant. He words questions weird and also thinks a class average of 60% is good. Avoid his class at all costs.

Insanely hard and tons of info for an intro class. Class is all lecture and tons of very complex info like its med school. Go to class for participation questions and get attendance extra credit. Curves exams and has one drop but still the exams are insanely hard to do well on. Fast lectures make it hard to take notes. Generally very boring.
This professor should not be teaching beginning level classes. Way too advanced. Way too unreasonable with how he writes and averages his test scores. Will ruin GPA.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This was easily one of the hardest classes I've ever taken. Be prepared to spend hours outside of lectures studying. Exams are very difficult and don't make a lot of sense. I recommend taking a different section.

Dr. Spranger is very knowledgeable, & he  wants you to be too. He records all of his lectures so that you can re-listen to them & take more notes later. I printed off all of his lecture slides ahead of time & took a few notes in class & then re-wrote them later.. Be prepared to devote a TON of time to this class. You have to study a LOT!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: This guy is a great lecturer but the class is really hard. Be prepared for hours of studying and make sure to relisten to all of the taped lectures. He words the questions on his exams weird making it hard to understand what the question is asking. The class is only 3 exams, participation points and the final. He does give lots of EC though.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Prof is entertaining at times but is very light on lecture material. Had to read and re-read the book he assigned all the time and I still barely scraped a 3.0.  In addition to that, the second exam is after the final drop date of the course, so be sure you want this class. He will schedule a quiz using TopHat and take it a different time. Prepare!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Do not take this class unless you have 16 hours a week (8 hours after each lecture) to spend studying. Despite putting in the hours, you can study the review questions he posts, and re-listen to lectures, and still fail an exam. Class averages were 50-60% on each exam; he attributes the low averages to students "not trying hard enough."
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: He really cares about his students. Gives pop quizzes sometimes. Also gives extra credit. He has a lot of cool stories, but this class is very hard. You have to put in A LOT of work in order to pass, so I don't recommend taking this class if you have a lot on your plate because this will take up most of your week.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: This guy is really knowledgable on biology, but he's not the best teacher. Also, his exams are very nitpicky, which got very annoying. He would often ask questions on the exams that we barely touched on in class. He gets a one star in my book because I had an 89.6% following the final and he wouldn't round up to a 4.0. Avoid his class if you can!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Okay, I have a lot of things to say, but to keep it short and sweet, all I can say is that if you don't have enough time on your hands, DO NOT take his section. He knows what he'/ talking about and is passionate, but there's a lot of work to put in and a lot of information he covers. And it was hard to balance this with my other activities.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: This class is a lot to take on if you don't know what you're getting into. I left this class understanding physiology very well but had very little time to even breathe all semester. You have to want to learn the material or else you'll just drown. It's a ton of work but anything you'll be tested on is in the lecture (which he records).

Insanely tough prof. My semester was already hard, so this class on top of it all made it impossible to pass. Test averages were extremely low (50-60%). If you wanna do good, be prepared to study 3-5 hrs every night (which nobody has time for). Nice guy with low BS tolerance but avoid him. You WILL fail if your life isn't dedicated to the class
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Marty whata guy; he once bought the whole class brownies. Really likes to hear himself talk (be prepared to spend the whole hour in office hours) but has really interesting things to say. REALLY hard exams but he curved all of our exams& gave a lot of extra credit. Take immaculate notes &you'll be fine. Offers lots of ways for students to succeed.

Although he is a decent professor and very passionate about this class, the class is very tough. He purposely makes his exams extremely hard because he wants you to learn the material that he briefly covers in class in great depth. Also he expects you to do 8 hours of homework or studying per lecture which is not even possible to accomplish.

Honestly I don't know why he has such bad reviews. This class is not impossible. I study the night before each exam and I am going to most likely 3.5 or 4.0. I put in minimal work this semester. Marty is a GREAT professor, one of the best I have had. You can tell he really loves what he is talking about and it's scary how smart he is.

He's very smart and I love listening to his lectures because I have learned so much. It's just hard to juggle everything because his exams are tough. He tries to help though and posts the lectures and recordings online. He's a cool guy, it's just a lot of info all at once. I'm glad I got to take the class still.

Easily one of the worst classes I have ever taken. I came into the class the first day and he seemed like a cool professor, turns out he was terrible. He makes the tests almost impossible to pass, and in class he just reads the powerpoint without explaining the concepts. Would never take one of his classes ever again.

Terrible. hardest class you will take, not due to the material but due to his goal to fail his students. HE stated he was happy w/ the performance of our class after a 58% class average. The man designs the exams for you to fail, he admits he puts little thought into his tests which is why only he would be able to answer the question correctly.

I love biology so I thought this class would be easy. I was wrong. I was taking 3 other classes and working 2 jobs and I didn't have extra time for this. He gives quizzes on days when you don't have class, regardless of your schedule. His tests are impossible - I studied three weeks for every minute I wasn't occupied and I only scored a 70%.

I am taking 15 credits, working, and also volunteering. I do not have time to spend 3 hours a night studying for this class. He says his exams aren't hard but then makes them incredibly difficult. Class averages are about 50%. His extra credit he gives for attendance to make his averages look good is the only thing keeping me afloat.

This class is hard as hell but hear me out. He makes you understand the material to do well. I got a 67% on the first exam and then I did what he said and got an 86% on the next one. You will have put a ridiculous amount of work in to do well but its possible. Take easier classes the semester  you take this, you will not have time for other classes

First, during the lecture the professor goes off on tangents much too frequently, and then will breeze over slides to try and make up for lost time. Secondly, his exams are poorly written. For the first exam he told us that he wrote the exam the night before. Thirdly, you will not get a study guide until the night before the exam. Prepare to study.

I thought I was smart, then I took a class taught by this guy. Unless you have three hours a night to spend studying for his class don't take it. Seriously, I'm an honors college student and I'll be lucky to get a 3.0 in this class. Emphasis on LUCKY. Do yourself a solid and avoid this professor.

I liked his take not crap attitude.  He would call out students that are disrupting the class.  He explained everything well and was easy to follow along with.  He was always available to students if they needed help.  However his exams were incredible hard.  He would test us on the smallest of topics that he barley covered.  Expect to study a lot.

Well the exams are incredibly difficult, even after spending countless hours reading and UNDERSTANDING the concepts. I took this while also taking organic chemistry and the orgo was easier. He adds points to everybody's exam, which sounds great but it fluffs up the average so that he looks like he's a really good instructor, but he isn't.

His tests are ridiculously hard and the questions always have more than one right answer. Even after relistening to levitates and rewriting notes, I found that the exams usually cover stuff that isn't emphasised in class. He also claims that he'll never test you on something that isn't mentioned in class or on a PowerPoint - it's a lie.
Hard class. He does not have a pleasant attitude at times.

Talks very fast and the tests are extremely hard. I took three years of biology in high school and struggled to get a 3.5 in this class. He really does know what he's talking about and he mostly tells you what will be on the test.

Prof. Moreno is very sweet and kind, and open to discussing social justice issues today. She is also very funny and loves to tell stories. This class is very writing-heavy, so just be prepared. She can be a harsh grader, but she does give good feedback so that you can get a good grade on the final draft. Overall, an amazing professor personally.

Profe Moreno Mosqueda is an extremely down-to-earth person. She is very kind and not afraid to give constructive criticism. This class can be very tough, but as long as you follow her clear guidelines and expectations, this class can be very enjoyable.

Professor was very nice and kind, but could be super professor-y at times. Had clear rubrics and as long as you followed them it was good. I missed a month due to illness and for the most part she was accommodating, up until the last week of my illness. I definitely learned and thought she was funny, would take her again!

She was funny and nice to talk to, but was difficult when it came to grading. You need to be participating a lot during each class as she will take away points every lecture if not. The essays are worth 50% of your grade and the 2 tests had no study guide and we had no idea what would be on them. When I was sick I could not make up work missed

I would never take this professor ever again at MSU. Has no desire for her students, and is rude to many. Participating in class and asking questions would never get you the full attedance points. She believes study guides are a form of cheating, so be prepared to fail your exams. If you have her I suggest dropping out immediately.

Absolutely dreaded this class every week because of her. Consistently complained about her job, was impossible to reach via email and for office hours, and docked attendance points even if you participated and were paying attention. Showed no understanding for her students, lacked professionalism, and was condescending and rude multiple times.

Professor Moreno Mosqueda was the worst professor I've experienced at MSU and many other people in our class agreed. It was almost impossible to get a 4.0 in her class unless you are a native Spanish speaker. She graded so harshly and then offered no further help or feedback. She was rude at times and very inaccessible outside of class for help.

I love Profesora Moreno! She is very kind and makes her class very interesting and fun. We did a couple essays but if you put your best effort you would get a pretty good grade. She always spoke in Spanish and interacted with the students no matter their skill level! Such a great person too and very funny!

The worst prof i've ever had. Even attendance was graded absurdly harsh, being early, asking/answering questions & group participation wasn't enough to get full attendance points. Lectured forever with no visuals, anti-technology, & has ridiculously difficult exams WITH NO STUDY GUIDES. I would never ever recommend this professor, I barely learned.

Do not select this professor. There are classes that fulfill the requirement that are way easier. Midterm was 2 essay questions with no rubric to follow and is graded based of what seems to be opinion, not to mention most of the class failed it. Prof did not make it easy to work with her leaving students feeling hopeless in terms of grades.
This professor does everything possible to make an IAH difficult. She is not understanding, forgiving, or helpful when students are struggling. Avoid taking her classes at all costs.

Had zero care in the world whether her students did well or not and didn't seem to care whether or not anyone understood the material. Over half the class failed the midterm paper she assigned and rather than admit she didn't adequately teach the material she belittled us and told us how disappointed she was. If possible DO NOT take her class.
lol you better hope your mom doesn't die while you're in this class because she will give you a 0 for missing class to go to the funeral.

She never taught, she just had us pay for a $75 online Spanish learning program and called it a day. She'd grade projects, but everything else was automated by the website. When I reached out to her with a problem, she would be very rude and blunt when emailing back. Do not take this professor.

This professor has no care for her students. Since the first day of classes you could tell she had no heart to see her students succeed nor was clear with her complex curriculum and the expectations she had for all assignments and projects. She is flat out rude and does not have email etiquette or skill of respecting those "below" her. 0/5!

Overall, the class content was not difficult. Her grading was harsh and she was condescending. For a language class, we should be given room to learn from our mistakes and improve them. However, Professor Moreno is unforgiving. Did not seem like she cared.
Do not take this class, I regret it so much. The professor grades so hard and the lectures are very difficult to follow. You're better off taking any other IAH course.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: I've never seen a prof non-stop talk for an hour and 50 minutes like she does. Lectures are incredibly hard to follow. Unforgiving if you are sick/have any issues. Assignments are due at 5 pm twice a week which is VERY inconvenient. Grades way too hard for an IAH. I've never had a worse professor here

Worst professor I've had at my time here, hard to follow along, does not care for students issues with homework, grades unfairly in every possible aspect. You could be have the worst illness of your life and she would still expect you to turn everything in. Avoid.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Was a very bad grader, also very hard to follow along in class.
Hard to follow along in class,  extremely tough grader, would not provide sympathy at all.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Grades very diffcult.

The class was easy and I did fine but she didn't really do any teaching. The class was asynchronous and she just had us buy (expensive) spanish teaching programs online that did the teaching. When I did interact with her she responded quickly and also sent emails to make sure we were on track.

The class itself wasn't that hard. There was an average amount of homework but it was all pretty repetitive. Her as a person is where I grade her low. She was very uptight and condescending. Would interrupt you before you could finish your question, refused to give extra credit and was overall inflexible and stubborn. Passable, but bad attitude.

I absolutely loved Profesora Mosqueda! I was genuinely excited for my 8 am classes every Friday with her. She seems like she really cares if you are struggling with any concepts and is great at giving very helpful feedback.

She is such a great professor and makes class really easy and fun. She likes to get to know her students and makes class interesting. She teaches the class entirely in Spanish, which I find to be very helpful since I am minoring in Spanish. I would definitely take another class with her again!

I would recommend going to one of his office hours to introduce yourself or ask questions, he's a really nice guy to talk to. I would not say that this class has a heavy workload. We usually had 1-2 assignments a week along with some reading. Lectures are easy to understand and the exams are directly from learning actives in class.

You're on your own in this course. Despite emailing Professor Satti for help multiple times throughout the semester, his responses were short and sharp, and it is clear that he is unfit to be teaching an asynchronous class.
He is a strict grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: While this class is all online, Dr. Satti was truly a great professor. Many of the aspects of this course are outside of his control, however, when asking for clarification or you need a better understanding of the content, he truly was knowledgeable and cared about whether you understood the concept. Strongly recommend Dr. Satti!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: So my roomie took this course, and bro legit stole her from me. They were losing sleep trying to put in the most insane amount of time ever requested by a teacher for a non-honours, online 100 level course (literally multiple hours a day). It's absurd. They read this whack book that had some really disturbing content and no content warning???
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Get ready to question your sanity as you embark on the horrendous journey of PH101. You will spend hours every day if you wish to do the required readings (which you should take notes on if you are seeking a good grade on quizzes and assignments) and trying to complete some very complicated writing assignments. You will be so frustrated every week.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Not hard but not too easy. The content is not even that hard its all pretty basic. It's definitely not a super easy class, but it's very doable if you set aside a few hours a week to get it done. Buying the textbook is kind of useless you really only need the lectures and you'll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Not sure if it's Satti's fault but HM101 is the worst class I've taken at State. The lectures are read from a transcript and are insufferable to listen to. The quizzes ask the most arbitrary questions and are not indicative of the material. Something happened to this class over the summer...used to a be a blowoff class, now its quite time consuming

Professor Satti is knowledgeable and clearly passionate about public health as a whole. However, the assignments were sometimes difficult and very time-consuming. The quizzes focused on tiny details and were often difficult to do well on, but the majority of the grade comes from them. I wouldn't take this class unless much time could be devoted.
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor lacks communication for an online course. It is up to you to read the textbook and teach the content to yourself, then take a quiz at the end of every week that picks out small details from the textbook. Quizzes are not straightforward and almost seem as if he is trying to trick us. I do not recommend this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: He was the best professor that I have ever had! If you are looking to take an easy class to get your elective credits, this is NOT the class to take. There are a lot of assignments and the weekly quizzes are not easy. I would only recommending taking this class if you are interested in healthcare or the public health field. A great class!

Satti constantly offers Zoom help rooms outside of class, especially during the last months of the semester. In the times I reached out to him through email he was very helpful, and so is the feedback he leaves on your work. The class is based almost solely on essays and quizzes, but the answers are ALWAYS found in the content posted on D2L.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I've taken two classes with Professor Medina and would gladly take another! Her lectures are fast-paced, which I personally like. If you do the readings, you will score well on the weekly quizzes. Attendance is mandatory, but you get 5 freebies before it affects your grade.
The class wasn't too bad, but it could be boring at times, as it's nothing interactive. The class is really easy to pass as you're only graded by attendance and one assignment per week, no exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Easiest 4.0 ever. Apparently in previous semesters she had midterms and finals, but not this time. This ISS 300+ level class was graded by online quizzes and attendance, and that's it. Easiest ISS 300+ credit ever. But it's possible she may make midterms/finals in future semesters. Class topic was mildly interesting as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She is quirky but knows a lot about Latin America which makes it easy to ask her questions. She really cares about students and will help you succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Objectively easy course. The lectures are boring but there's no exams, you just need to show up for attendance, and you can skim the powerpoint to get an easy 3.0+. She's very understanding and the absence policy is pretty lenient. Great GPA boost/course to add onto a busy schedule. Would take again!
i personally love this teacher she cares about her students so much and the class that i had her in was really easy it was pls 201.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: She really cares about students and made the class interesting, she not only taught about stats but also how to read academic papers which is very useful.

The class was pretty easy 5 classes are excused before points are deducted from attendance points. 20% attendance and 80% assignments/quizzes which are based off of lecture/readings. The only real issue I had with her is she changed the syllabus about 2-3 weeks before the end of the semester which changed the grading scale to make it higher.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great prof who really cares about her students! Attendance policy is super lenient, and I found that it wasn't hard to catch up if you missed class. Quizzes were all open-book, open-lecture and open-note (I got a 4.0 just from Ctrl + F searching key terms), and there are no midterms or finals -- your entire grade is 20% attendance, 80% quizzes.

This is one of my favorite professors, im taking another class with her and i am excited. she truly cares about students and makes classes so easy. lenient with absences we had 5 classes we could miss and still get full credit. overall, a good teacher and person.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I took the honors section and there was no extra project or anything so it was a super easy H. The class has no exams or projects, only weekly assignments that are 10 questions based on the readings (which you don't even really have to do) and attendance. You can only miss 5 classes but she usually ends class early anyway. Super nice and easy 4.0!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures were not too boring, although a bit complicated! No lab, no exams; weekly online quizzes worth 80% of your grade and 20% attendance (you can miss 5 classes). Nice professor that explained things well and easiest 4.0 ever. Would totally take again

Mariana is a great teacher. She is very knowledgeable about Latin America and tries to include current events and relates them to the class topics.
She is kind of quirky but really cares about students.
nice professor and easy class, however attendance was mandatory and we needed to buy a textbook to access homework

I took PLS 160 not 201! Really easy class, only two exams and most your grade is based on weekly homework assignments that are super easy. Do need a textbook for this class, but it can be found online at this time. Overall if you are interested in international relations and you have the option to have her I would 100% recommend.
It is sort of hard to 4.0 this class as her quizzes/exams can be tricky and it is only based on those grades, and her lectures are mandatory.
Mariana was a great prof. She really cares about students and it's an easy 4.0 as long as you do the weekly quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Caring--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: inability to convey material clearly. If it was not for the TA I had during recitation, whole class would have done poorly. Everything you will learn will be during the recitation. Very nice teacher, but lectures are very dull and material hard to understand ESPECIALLY If you have never coded before.

Professor Medina is kind and cares about her students. This class was easy, and as long as you showed up to recitation, you could get a great grade on every assignment. No final or midterms. Although reading was required, it wasn't necessary to excel in the course. Great professor, and would be happy to take another one of her classes!

Class was super easy as there were no tests, just weekly assignments. The weekly readings aren't necessary to do as she'll go over what you need to know in the lectures. Unless you do the readings, don't skip the lectures. I was lucky as my TA told us the answers for the assignments, but I heard this wasn't the case for all TA's.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Take this professor if you can! She is very nice and caring! Grades are comprised of weekly assignments that you do in the recitation and attendance, so no exams or anything. The class was super easy, and make sure to go to the recitation! Really nice prof, great at helping with honors credit as well!
Just go to class for the attendance points and GO TO RECITATION!! The class is easy as long as you go to class

You could tell Dr. Medina was very interested in the subject matter, and knew a lot about it, so this made asking her questions or whatnot a lot easier. You're only graded by a few things, but she makes the grading criteria very flexible, and she's a pretty easy grader. Definitely take the honors section if you need an honors credit!

Skip the lectures and go to recitation. Lecture attendance is online clickers, recitation is in person. Helpful TA. No exams or tests, just one easy weekly assignments every week. Don't buy the textbooks cuz there're useless unless you really want to learn about R coding. Should be easy 4.0. Lectures slides are on d2l.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Easy 4.0 but a little boring. 3 exams each 20% of your final grade, weekly quizzes, attendance.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: It was a boring lecture class. Just readings then quiz due at the end of week

There's an attendance QR code for the lecture, but you could always get someone to get your attendance for you. All slides are posted, and all quizzes (weekly, 10-15 questions) and exams (midterm and final, 40-50 questions) are pulled from the posted slides. I mostly did other homework during lecture, but it's an easy 4.0 if you take your time.

10/10 class. Attendance is graded so you have to go to the lecture. However, all the slides for the quizzes are on D2L, you can read them while taking the quiz. Her lectures are kind of boring and she's a bit hard to understand but she's nice and lenient. Don't listen to any bad reviews on her, just do the work and you'll get 100%.
Very lenient with attendance, interesting material, you get any notes you want on all quizzes and exams. Very easy and enjoyable class that gives you a breather in your semester.

She was very accessible outside of class and responded to emails quickly and had a zoom after the first few weeks. I thought the class was easy, but the lectures did not help with the assignments, the recitation was extremely helpful. Easy 4.0 class, just do the assignments and the attendance.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Mariana is nice, but it's difficult to follow her lectures as she goes through the slides so fast. Don't bother trying to take notes during lecture, she goes too fast. TAs are very helpful in walking through the weekly assignments and explaining concepts during section. No exams. Just do the weekly assignments and check in for attendance = easy 4.0

Mariana was fine, but above all else almost useless. I learned jack squat from her lectures. Yet, the TAs on the other hand were extremely helpful. Seriously. Skip the lecture and just go to the recitation. This class is only graded on weekly assignments, which were easy for the most part.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was very interesting and she does a great job making connections to help students better understand concepts. There are weekly quizzes that are straight forward from the readings and slides. I wish more people went to class because I can tell it made her sad seeing 5-10 students there every day when there were 200+ students in the class.

There are a lot of interesting and useful concepts taught in this course. Professor Medina's lectures are good and easy to understand. She should start taking attendance since most people in my section almost never came to class. She is very nice and easy to talk to. Good class overall, but better course organization is in order.

The material is easy, however, do not come to class there is no point. I do better on the quizzes when I don't go to her class than when I do. The biggest complaint I have about this class is her having the weekly quizzes due every Friday at 12 PM. Yes, you read that correctly, not AM but 12 in the afternoon for some reason. It makes no sense atall
She takes attendance but you can miss a few classes. She grades the midterm and final super easy. She posts the questions ahead of time and you can ask her anything about them.

This professor lectures at an unreasonable pace. You'll want the slides pulled up. Get ready to write as fast as possible. She is not accommodating to student circumstances and apathetic towards appeals from students. Avoid if possible. Grading is relatively lax but she is ultimately unreachable to students and her lectures are at record-pace.

Overall, she is a good professor. There was a midterm exam and a final exam, every week had a 10 question quiz. There were 3 reading summaries dues before the midterm and 3 due before the final, 1 page each. Everything was open note and mainly the exam and quiz questions were word for word from the notes.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor Medina is a really kind person who cares about students, she is very understanding if you have any issues pop up. However if you are a procrastinator be careful because she leaves all the martials open from the semester and you can do them at you leisure.

I don't think that Dr. Medina is the issue, but she is also not the solution. I had her for PLS 201 and that class has made me drop my public policy major. The class is boring, and is mostly programming in R. She is good as far as grading, but the class itself is so unnecessary tedious that its overall not a good experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--So many papers

reviews.comment: Material was very easy, lectures were pre recorded and theres a lot of reading material. Nice lady, awful professor. She wont help students if they arent understanding the material she even guided me in the wrong direction. Her quizzes are like guessing games, even if its in the material for why its right shell justify why its wrong.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Tough grader--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: There is 3 exams and each exam is 22% of the grade. The exams are very very vague - it's very hard to know what she expects you to answer. Yes, she is very nice but we need better grades. She just reads of slides and sometimes it's hard to understand her. Going to class is not worth it at all. I wouldn't recommend taking her but you might like it.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: not much to say. the class is pretty boring & she just goes over the material. she drags through the lecture & it is so boring. really hard to stay awake during class. the grading isnt bad, but you will work a lot with database searches through a computer programming app which is kinda hard. overall not a great class but will keep your gpa up.

She is seriously an awesome professor! She is so caring and understanding. As long as you communicate with her about what you need she is very accomodating. Her exams are super easy and so are the assignments. I would 100% recommend her especially for a requied course!

She was an awesome professor and was always willing to help her students. As long as you show up and pay attention to her lectures its an easy A. She has very easy pop quizzes and her exams are straight forward. She does a review before each exam which is extremely helpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Caring

reviews.comment: Mariana Medina was a great prof! Had her class as an 8am which honestly was terrible and i probably would have done better if i took it at a later time because i could never make it to class. Lots of pop quizzes but she posts the slides for her lectures on D2L a few days before class so you know what the Q's are before hand! 4 exams total!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Mariana is a gem. I took an 8 am with her and we never stayed past 8:50 for lecture. She also posts the lectures the night before, but there are pop quizzes so if you don't go, you might miss out on a grade. You also aren't graded on a lot in this course, so you should probably go. You also have to read the book and write responses on it!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Slow grades (no grades entered more than halfway through the semester), and the course topic is just not what the class is described as. If you want to learn more about Chilean politics, def take this course, but if you're looking for reading writing by women, this isn't it.

McCleese only gave out 5 assignments the whole semester.  She takes attendance randomly so just attend even though it is boring af. Her lessons are so boring, playing minesweeper is more entertaining. She reads everything off of a slideshow, (which that is NOT posted to D2L).

This professor graded everything a week before the course ended. An okay instructor who oddly made the class about food culture. Assignments were easy ten-minute tasks, with only five total grades, so submit on time. Attendance wasn't taken, so skipping didn't matter. Clear grading, but never replied to emails.

One of the worst professors I've ever had. I was expecting the course to relate to the description MSU put out, but she put an "indigenous food studies" twist to it. Lectures consist of her reading slides in a monotone voice. She gives us useless assingments and hasn't graded anything the entire semester (less than a month left). Stay away.

Very careless about student feedback or incidents. Took random attendance for the first month as a scare tactic, then suddenly stopped, nobody knows when she will take attendance next. If you are absent, she gives 0 grace to your situation regardless of how serious. She has a temper, and petty mannerisms. There's not much work she gives out.

Easy 4.0 she didn't really have class a lot and was an easy grader besides that he grading didn't go in till the last day but if you want an easy 4.0 take it with her you also only like have 4 scattered assignments so if you don't really care and are a good writer this class is for you

As an exchange student the class was anything but interesting especially when we had to read a book about a child vampire having relations with adults and we had activities on this without analyzing it in class. Moreover she takes MONTHS to grade homework and if you have a problem don't count on her to help you.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. McCleese lacks clarity in instruction and grading. Lectures consist of reading directly from slides, offering no real engagement. With unclear assignment criteria, it's difficult to perform well. Her teaching style is ineffective and grades are often NEVER returned. It is finals week and I still have not received a single grade back.

Dr.Mccleese is a nice person yet she has no idea how to grade. I have zero grades right now and it is the end of the semester. She will yell at you if you talk and she does not know how to give instruction on any assignment so you have to figure it out yourself. It is an easy class but I wouldn't take it with her.

Dr. McCleese is clearly a great scholar but she CAN NOT TEACH. She only reads off slides the entire lecture and takes forever to grade anything despite promising to grade sooner. She is literally afraid of students and acts very timid.

Other reviews are true, I didn't get anything out of the class, but if you want something easy you found it. I skipped class often and my grade wasn't effected. I didn't put a lot of work into the papers and I just rambled and made stuff up for the final project because I didn't read the book it was about and I got a 4.0 in the class so I'm not mad

My grade on this class was based on 2 300 pnt papers, 1 300 pnt project, and 200 pnts of attendance thats c/nc. Criteria for assignments was unclear and as such they were very difficult to write. The D2L is closed and final grades are due today by 4 but she has not graded a single assignment. Lectures are boring and she reads directly from slides
reviews.rating_tags: So many papers--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Reads exclusively off of slides. If you have any questions, Just look it up bc the answer she gives you will just make you more confused. She seems like a very intelligent person, just a bad teacher. Didn't feel like I learned anything. Also takes forever to grade papers. Almost 2 months for Paper #1 to be returned.

Very poor experience. Very late grader, unclear and confusing projects and grading guidelines. Lectures are monotone, unengaging, and unhelpful. Is not accessible outside of class, email regarding course was never returned.

The course was graded by 2 papers, attendance, and the Final Project. The first paper was due October 6th and we only got our grades a week ago. She doesn't take attendance. The Final is a five minute video and seems like she thought about the project before class that day. Lectures consist of her reading off of a slide in a very monotone voice

Every one of these reviews is accurate. The course was extremely unorganized and Professor Mccleese often times seemed like she had no idea what she was talking about. Completely lacking passion, and has not posted a single grade since the semester started. It was difficult to find the will to attend class. If you can avoid it, I suggest you do.

I would not recommend her class. I feel like I've learned nothing and wasted my time attending her lectures. She just reads off slides and doesn't explain anything in simple terms, so it's pretty confusing most of the time, which made me dread participating. There's no course outline and she didn't grade our papers till a month later.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Teaching style terrible. Didn't give out grades till after multiple complaints. Lots of research papers to read outside of class. Teaches concepts in expert level way despite it being new content to us. Don't bother asking questions because her responses are complex and even more confusing. Participation mandatory. Never took attendance.

The class time was poorly used; Her instructions for assignments were unclear; there was poor structure for learning; no effort was put into the syllabus; she refused to give concrete dates at the beginning of the semester for anything; grades for the first essay have not been released, and the next essay is due soon; she gives excuses;

This professor did not aspire to make class interesting. Somehow, reading about profane vampire relations turned boring. She also didn't make attendance mandatory which was nice, as it was hard to self-motivate to go to her class.

DO NOT TAKE CLASSES FROM THIS PROFESSOR. She takes months to grade assignments, gives little to no instruction for her assignments, uploads drop-boxes for assignments after the due date, does not give a syllabus for the class, and doesn't contribute any of her own thinking or work into her class. I strongly STRONGLY advise you to not take her class

She did not consider attendance this semester due to Covid and tried her best to be accommodating. She is a brilliant woman, however, her language is more appropriate for colleagues than introductory-level students. She takes a long time to grade, and her grading policy is somewhat unclear. Classes are mostly her reading directly from PP slides.

I liked Dr. McCleese. She was an alright professor for a basic class. There were only a couple projects in the class but she put the grades in at the last couple weeks of class. Sometimes class was only 10 minutes long when it was supposed to be an almost 2 hour class. It felt pointless to go. She is a very easy grader tho.

Technically attendance is mandatory, but she hasn't taken it since the start of the semester. Due dates unclear, often pushed back because she never gives any place for it to be turned in at. Came into class sick once post COVID when it was explicitly stated not to do that. The class isn't difficult, but emotionally draining lol.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: SUPER easy class. Her lectures are long but she's funny sometimes. She does questions at the beginning of each class, and it was based off of accuracy. The questions were based off of the readings. Loved her.

This class was very informative, but not too hard. For tests, you are allowed to have a note sheet, and she posts a study guide. You are expected to read the textbook, because she will have an in-class quiz before every lecture based on the reading. There is homework for every book chapter.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: This is the easiest class I have taken. The lectures are important to go to for extra credit, lecture points and for exam material. There is so much extra credit given that I had 20 points of extra credit over the maximum points available in the class. You get a cheat sheet on the test and the beginning of lecture quizzes are just vocab.
Pretty easy class. The only thing that might be time consuming are the textbook readings before classes.

I love Prof Nawyn. There are iclicker questions and daily quizzes but they are easy as long as you do the readings which are a little long but its fine. Inquisitives were annoying but easy points. Very fun and followable lectures, shes funny here and there too. Very accommodating and there are many points in the class. Easy exams w a note card.

Dr. Nawyn is amazing. Wasn't super interested in sociology before this semester but she made every class interesting. She clearly cares about her students and she's absolutely hilarious. Exams aren't too hard if you study with inquisitive because she gives you a one page cheat sheet.

Dr. Nawyn actually wants her students to succeed. Make sure to do the inquisitive/reading before every class because there is a quiz at the start of every lecture. You get a reference sheet for every exam and Her study guides are also very accurate to the tests so make sure to base your sheet on them. Lots of extra credit opportunities
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Nawny's SOC is extremely easy for an introduction course. There are four exams total, along with quizzes for every class period, which are typically 3-4 questions based off the reading and are terminology based. She is down to earth and HILARIOUS. Not hard at all, reading isn't really necessary for these exams, they are also open note.

This class was extremely easy to the point that showing up basically guarantees a 4.0 because the last couple of weeks are straight extra credit. The exams weren't bad at all, with extra credit on them and multiple correct answers. If you try, you will do well. Dr. Nawyn is also great at teaching and is very insightful.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Nawyn is an amazing lecturer who truly cares about her topic. Reading quizzes at the beginning of class with iclicker attendance but other than that very little work. Her study guides make it easy to do well on tests as she tells you what topics will be on it beforehand. As long as you focus a bit in class a good grade is attainable.

Yes, Dr. Nawyn shifted the format of the class, but it was due to the fact that people were cheating on exams by using AI! So, if you are an authentic student who is willing to study and engage in class work, you will be fine! She is extremely generous with extra credit and exams. I took this as an elective my final semester and it was an easy 4.0!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She changed up the class structure, but it still is a super easy class. As long as you show up to class for majority of the semester you're able to easily get an A in the class. Most of your points are from attendance via the iclicker at the end of class. Also you're allowed a double sided cheat sheet for the exam, and she provides a study guide!

Averaging D+ on each exam after studying and having a piece of paper with notes on it. Ratings looked good that's why I took this class in the first place, not at all like the reviews. Professor herself is nice however the exams are stupid hard for no reason. Also a ton of inquizitive and in class quizzes and attendance.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: She's a great professor! She changed the class structure tho... used to be open-note at home online exams but now they're in-person on scantron and you only get a cheat sheet. Exams kinda caught me off guard because of how difficult they can be even after studying. For a 100-level class, the exams should not be that difficult.

Changed the entire structure of the class because too many people were doing good in the past and cheating. Now all exams are in person scantron. reading check quiz nearly every class. shes nice and funny sometimes.

To everyone who said this class was easy, well it isn't anymore. Because of chat, she has made the exams extremely difficult, in person and scantron. This is an elective for most people and is my hardest class for no reason. She seems like a very nice and educated person though.

I really enjoyed Professor Nawyn's lectures. She's funny and explains terms/theories well. She has online open note exams, weekly readings, and iclicker quizzes, but they're fairly easy if you read the textbook and pay attention in class. Would 100% recommend taking SOC 100 with her!

All exams are open note/book, super easy class overall and hard not to 4.0! Lectures can be long/boring, she tends to get off task. The sweetest professor-intelligent, understanding, down to earth, absolutely hilarious. She wants everyone to succeed. I wish I could get coffee with her. Couldn't recommend enough!

Such a wonderful professor to have. She is educated, thoughtful, passionate, and caring to her students. She truly does care, and you can just tell. There were five exams, with the final, and one can be dropped. All are open note, and most were taken online, with an in-person option. No reason not to take a class with such a wonderful person :)

I enjoyed her lectures because she was funny, made it interesting and notes were pretty straightforward. She uses iClicker every lecture for quizzes over the readings you were supposed to read outside of class. Exams are all open note and you have one hour to complete 70 questions, which can be overwhelming. Overall, I had a great experience.

Class is attendance based for iClicker quizzes and attendance. Exams are online 

Teacher is super liberal and biased to the left side of the political spectrum if you do not put up with that stuff.
Amazing professor for SOC 100, she has open note exams and all taken online. Her lectures are very funny and it was very enjoyable. I would recommend taking this course.
Exams are open book and open notes, watch out for in-class quizzes from the textbook, funny lectures with detailed lecture slides.

Super funny and sweet. Lectures can seem boring and drag on for a while even though they're only a couple slides long, but she always let us out early and says important things. Make sure to read the textbook before class because she has in-class quizzes based on assigned readings. Open book and note for exams, and can be taken anywhere.

Class was insanely easy. Exams online no lockdown browser. She is super chill and lecture gets out 30-40 minutes early. Also don't buy the textbook I got a 4.0 and never used it it was a waste of money.

Total queen.  She's very funny and relatable, and she teaches the course material well.  You have to read a decent amount and study some for tests, but her personality and teaching mostly makes it worth it.
Grading is difficult when the test has the same 10 questions and she doesn't give good questions

This professor is very rude and difficult to work with. Her grading criteria is unclear and she even confuses her TA. The tests are poorly made and she does everything in her power to try to get students to stay the whole lecture by tricking us with multiple quizzes even when she says it's a no quiz day. She is not accommodating and disrespectful.

She's a good professor. You don't necessarily need the textbook but there's in-class quizzes everyday that use it and that count for a grade and attendance. You have to either take notes as she lectures or download it before hand because she doesn't provide any note sheets or study guides. Exams are online, no lockdown browser.

Dr. Nawyn is an amazing prof! I wish I could take every class with her. She's so passionate about what she teaches. There will be a few writing assignments but they're not hard. Also do the reading bc she has in class quizzes and sometimes offers bonus points.

Dr. Nawyn is a great lecturer. She keeps the lecture interesting and is really funny. Her class was one of the most interesting classes. There is a short video assignment and a blog assignment, but it isn't too challenging. Dr. Nawyn is the Beyonce of professors at MSU.

Excellent professor, very interested in what she's teaching and wholeheartedly tries to appeal and be sensitive to students of all backgrounds and upbringings. Definitely wants her students to pass & receive good grades.

I loved this class. Super interesting subject matter, and Professor Nawyn kept the lectures upbeat. Professor Nawyn has many real-world examples of the subject matter she teaches, so this made it easier for me to learn. You do have to go to lecture though, because there are (easy) iClicker quizzes everyday.

I was not looking forward to taking this course because I had already taken the equivalent for my other majors, but now I am SO grateful I did. Prof. Nawyn is hilarious, brilliant, and genuinely cares about her students. I have never been to office hours just to talk to the prof until now. Her classes are amazing and her teaching is even better.

She is an awesome professor and I'm taking another one of her courses because I love her style. She speaks to students like equals and has great, real world examples. Her lectures are actually helpful and interesting.

Honestly, an amazing professor. He's fully online and just has quizzes throughout the week that you have like a month to complete. I didn't even have to take the final because he only makes you keep 3 of the exams out of the 4. Also a really funny guy.
Insanely easy 4.0, he wants you to succeed.

I took this along with World of Turf and I actually prefer this class over that one. The topics were interesting, the lectures were engaging and humorous (occasionally), and the professor actually cares for his students. Throughout the semester, he would give us so many updates/reminders. 4 total exams (final included) and 1 is dropped!

Nice, easy-going guy who you can tell really enjoys the content. No additional materials needed besides the posted lectures. My one warning is that the class isn't a slam-dunk 4.0 like the name would make you think, and the 3 unit tests + final (although they're open note) will sometimes touch on some of the more obscure points from lectures.

I recommend that everyone take this class! It's such a good class when you just need a few extra credits but don't want something difficult. Your grade is entirely based off of quizzes and tests but as long as you look over the lectures they are super easy. He makes the class very enjoyable and writes the nicest emails every week!
Very sweet professor, and the class is very easy to do good in. It is based off quizzes and exams and you get multiple attempts to do it. He really cares about his students success
If you were unable to achieve a 4.0 in this class you shouldn't be spending your money or your parents money on college. Genuinely would've passed this class when if I were 5 years old.

This class was extremely easy and Thomas Nikolai is such a sweet guy. His lectures (2-3 a week) are available as powerpoints and videos and information is easy to understand. Very interesting class, take it!

Such a great guy and an interesting class even if it comes off as "boring". Only grading is quizzes and exams which are also easy if you watch the videos and pay attention. Bonus points for his sweet emails about beautiful days outside and so on. Take this class with him!!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: CSS203 was very straightforward and despite being about soil I found it to be very interesting. He really cares about his students and the lecture videos he creates are interesting and I didn't mind watching them in the slightest. Just watch the lecture videos and you'll get a 4.0- Great class and great professor!

One of the best math professors I've had. His lectures are very clear and simple making them not hard to follow along. His office hours were the main reason I got A's on the tests so I highly recommend going to them if you ever need help on the content or just for review before a test. He is always very kind and helpful whenever you talk to him.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He was good at explaining tough concepts. I didn't get the best grade because I missed some lectures towards the end but every time I attended I was able to understand the concepts, very nice guy.
He is really good and the class in really exam heavy but as long as you come to lecture and understand the material the class is light.

Calc 3 is one of the hardest classes I've taken at Michigan State, but the main difference I vastly prefer it over Calc 2 is because of Professor Ovenhouse. His lectures were always super detailed and he always went over good examples, which prepared you really well for homework and exams. 10/10.
He's really nice and approachable, and easy to understand when he lectures. He's super smart but can teach at a understandable level. Also really helpful in office hours.

Nick is a great teacher. He obviously loves his subject and wants his students to do well. He's very organized and will answer any questions you have in a manner that benefits you as a student. He'll go over homework assignments to make sure you can complete them. The only downside is Lab Day - you'll have to work in groups on a large worksheet.
Great overall person, easy to talk to, teaches great, and he has a manly ass beard

If you take any of her classes, be ready to stay on top of things yourself. The D2L page can be difficult to navigate, and assignments aren't always clearly communicated in class. The schedule changes frequently, and lectures often run long with a lot of time spent on extended presentations.

Professor Nashef is honestly one of my favorite professors at MSU. She genuinely cares about her students and their growth throughout the class, and she gives insightful lectures. She also brings incredible experience in the field. I would definitely take another class with her again, overall an easy class.
Professor Nashef is a very nice person who seems to really care about their students. The class is very fun too!

Taking this class was simply annoying, she would lecture for two hours by repeating the same thing over and over and would have a needed thought until the class was ending and tell us to wait and listen to her. She never gave very good advice to improve our work and if we didn't want to do exactly what she wanted she would seem angry and upset.

Writing this review is the reason that I got an account on RMP. There are only so many words that will fit into this review, but my main issue is that she doesn't give feedback that will help one grow as a screenwriter. I rewrote my script from scratch just because she didn't like the story I was trying to tell, rather than the writing itself.

She's fine as a person but her lectures drag on for TWO HOURS on a Thursday from 7-9pm. Luckily the lectures aren't mandatory, just the labs (which are taught by the TA's who are a lot more lenient). The reason for the low rating is because I was never engaged in class, and I don't feel like I learned much. Also assigns so much work.

There are weekly labs that can take a ton of time to complete if you aren't familiar with Adobe Premier. The class has a ton of projects. The lectures are not mandatory. You can tell she really loves what she teaches but the lectures are long. I enjoyed the lectures but the assignments were more than what I was prepared for.

There was a mistake made with my overall grade in the class, and I only found out after all the final grades were put in, after the semester had ended. I contacted her, and she reviewed the situation and helped me get the grade I deserved, even after the next semester had started. Really solid communication and follow through.

Decent teacher, as long as you attend the class and complete the assignments which are fairly easy, you'll do fine. The course was a bit disorganized when it came to grading and what was expected of us. The lectures were personally a bit longer than they had to be and could be diluted through the recitation segments.
Got to be one of the worst ways to teach a class.
Just don't do it.

If you are good at fast memorization, you'll be fine. Content is taught solely online through an interactive textbook (with graded questions), and exams are usually every other week. No final! Take notes only on the learning objectives (skip unrelated content), and memorize them all.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Doesn't seem to care much if students are doing well. 2-week turnaround b/w exams and review sessions are A JOKE. They won't tell you the questions you got wrong, so you're not able to learn from them. This entire course was run terribly, and when everyone was doing poorly, they blamed us and took 0 accountability. Absolutely ludicrous.

I took the anatomy lab with Dr.Geske so I worked with her in person. She was not very instructive during this class. When we had questions, we tried to get help from the other profs because they were much more helpful. This class is nearly impossible if you don't spend every spare minute you have teaching yourself everything. I

All you do is read the textbook and teach yourself. Exams are impossible. There are review sessions but she makes them in the middle of the day so ppl with other classes cannot attend which is not fair. Is not open to suggestions and its petty when you ask if the review sessions can be move so more students can utilize them. DO NOT TAKE!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I loved taking anatomy online, however there is a lot of work! Because this class is online I feel like they just assign a lot of reading and videos. Definitely take this when you will have lots of time to devote to it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The course material is not super difficult but it is very tedious and time consuming at times. Overall mid.

I didn't take the undergraduate course, but the medical school course. Dr. Geske's exams are tough, but I really learned a lot from her. She is very understanding and goes out of her way to help students learn the material. She is a fantastic professor and I wish I had another course with her.

This professor is a saint! The class is hard work just by the nature of the topic but she does her best to make it understandable and accessible. She is available outside of class and is super kind and understanding. The class is fascinating and if you put in the time, they make it totally achievable to do well.

The class format of being online asynchronous is more of my dislike with this course than it is the professors themselves. This class was not terribly hard, it is just the fact that this entire course is somewhat self guided rather than being in a physical classroom and having the ability to ask questions is what makes it hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This class was amazing! Favorite class I have ever taken. There is lots of lectures but they provide a study guide with all the exam content and amazing preparation. The asynchronous option is amazing, allows you to go through each unit at your own pace. Lots of options to ask questions and get feedback. No homework, just lectures and notes.

Dr. Geske is the best! This is a challenging lab but completely manageable if you put time into it. She explained concepts very well and it is evident she genuinely wants every student to succeed. I would highly recommend this lab to anyone considering it.

Amazing class. Learning anatomy on specimen is extremely different from textbook drawing, and Dr. Geske is there to help every step of the way. Tests are pretty hard as they are all fill in the blank, so definitely practice free recall to study for the exams. 100% would take again
Taught a few of the lectures for the class and did a good job. No real complaints.

Class is extremely difficult. 5 exams in the class all on a ton of material. The exams are open book/note, so you need to fill out the learning objectives extremely well to help you. If you want to 4.0 this class you will really need to work for it. Exams are almost all of our grade, so you if you do bad on one the 4 point is gone. Good luck!

She is an amazing professor who will make herself available to help students. She cares a lot about your success and is very knowledgeable in her field. One of the best professors I have had at MSU. She is a wonderful professor, a great person, and also inspiring to learn from.
She seems very nice
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Be prepared to work hard. Dr. Geske is very helpful and relatable. She wants everyone to be successful. This is not an easy class but it is really enjoyable and realistic to what you will be taking in any pre-health graduate program. Its great exposure if you plan to go that route.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Onsay is the bestest professor I have ever experienced!! She is always very supportive to her students to keep track of the progress we have done and she generally is very appreciative of my eagerness towards learning CSE 331 as well as suggesting me to go to review sessions for exams to clear up all the concepts leading to difficultly
Actually cares about her students, probably one of the most caring professors you can have.
bro w professor bro fr

This course feels very repetitive among all the assignments, with everything being fully taught atleast twice for the most part. While DSA is very important for CS, the amount of work for this class definitely felt like atleast 4 or 5 credits worth. Otherwise, if you study and apply yourself in the coursework, you will be successful in this course.

Onsay is my favorite professor by far, and I have spent 2 years at MSU. She is sweet, caring, respecting, and does everything she can to help us understand the material and do well in the class. She does give a decent bit of easy homework, but all of it is HEAVILY related to the material and WILL HELP YOU and your grade - DO THEM. I will miss her.

Passionate and caring professor, calls us all "colleagues" and has us build our "backpack" of knowledge to apply to future projects, interviews, and jobs. There is a lot of content to learn, but class is paced reasonably. Only critique is many things are redundant (lectures, videos, Zybooks all teach the same thing) and d2l is hard to navigate.

Dr. Onsay is an engaging lecturer, which made the content easy to understand intuitively. The course load is very heavy, so it does take time to get used to, and you should give yourself at least a few hours a week on CCs and Projects. She doesn't penalize for AI, but it's hard to do well on exams if you rely on it entirely to code for you.

Onsay is by far one of the best professors i've had. She really cares for the students and structures the course for our long term career benefit. The exams are very easy to study for, the textbook and assignments give you everything to get at least a 3.0 in the class if you study. Theres a good amount of work required but it comes on the exams.

Bro she a GOAT. The course is chill, a lot of working but if you just see her videos and then go to lecture you'll be fine. She understands you got stuff going. She chill and tbh you can really vibe with her if you enjoy DSA like she does. Also she always looking for TAs so if you wanna be one just participate and make yourself known

You can tell she loves to teach and is good at it. If you put in the work, you will get a good grade. Lots of homework in this class but its good because it reinforces the material you are going to be tested. Class material is useful for future success as well. Great class in general, she is very caring. D2L is a mess lol

The workload is that of a 3 course class but the amount of repetition between the lectures, the lecture videos, and the online book makes it feel like a 4 course class. She really cares for her students and their success. The exam content is very fair for the most part but going back through content to study takes a lot of time.

Very poorly structured & disorganized course. Too much material: unnecessarily repetitive with videos, zybooks, lectures covering the same content. Took WAY more time than my other classes combined. D2L is a mess, with content all over the place. Each project is 1% & each exam is 17% of your grade. Def NOT a 3 credit workload, more like 5 credits.

The professor is great, and you can tell she cares about the students. My only complaint is that the class has tons of material, and she does a terrible job of organizing things on D2L. It is such a mess and hard to study for an exam. The concepts are fairly easy but she tends to overcomplicate them when explaining in class.

Onsay is a good professor and cares about students. However, the course itself is poorly organized and sometimes burdensome for no reason. The flipped classroom introduces repetitive work, which ends up being very time-consuming without significantly improving learning outcomes. Students are flooded with a jumbled collection of course material.

It's easily the biggest relief of a professor at MSU for CSE. Onsay deeply cares about her students and wants them to succeed. There is a lot of work within the course, don't get me wrong, but because of that, It requires little studying because the content is drilled into your head. I would rework a class schedule to be in her class personally.
It is a crying shame that Prof Onsay teaches but one computer science course.

I really liked this class. It was painful and stressful, but that comes with the computer science territory. The online textbook & D2L page have plenty of material, and ChatGPT is semi-allowed. Not to write a whole project, but great as a debugger. Just make sure you know the content by heart before exam night though!

Dr. Onsay will \provide you with support and care about your learning if you participate in class and put in effort. Although this class is not easy, she makes a great learning environment for all of her students. Make sure to utilize helprooms when taking this class as the TA's provide lots to offer as well.

She's definitely my favorite professor I have had within the MSU computer science department. She really seemed to care about her students and her lectures were actually engaging. She tells you exactly how it is, even if it's not what you would want to hear which I really respect in a professor. She was great in preparing us for a future in CS!

Professor Onsay is probably the best professor I've had here. She is passionate about what she teaches and genuinely cares about her students. The workload for this class is heavy, but if you run into problems, the helprooms are well run and worth it. Most of the content follows alongside zybooks. Overall an amazing professor.
