Unexpectedly, the professor suggested I switch from my computer science major, despite her reputation for excellence based on positive reviews. The course, biased towards stronger students, missed practical elements and was excessively fast-paced, culminating in a disheartening experience.

Class was poorly structured with project quizzes usually happening 1-2 weeks after the project was due and quiz questions being generally confusing. But it wasn't that difficult, so it's fine. I don't appreciate the exams being outside of scheduled lecture times.

Def go to all the lectures and do Zybooks. There are weekly projects and a lot of the info is contained within both of those. She's excited about the content and I learned a lot about efficient code. Has a temper so don't make her mad. Don't expect her to round your grade up, despite how close you are to the next grade. I was 0.05% away.

Onsay was an incredible professor. After taking 232 with Nahum the bar was set extremely low, but Onsay was outstanding. She really cares about teaching and providing all the necessary material for us to succeed. Super nice and friendly. Overall great professor. Best in the CSE department. 10/10

Onsay was good. You can tell she cared about students doing well in the course and would always give side notes on what's expected in interviews and in your internships/jobs. The flipped-learning style of this class was not the best and there are far too many lectures with most lectures being insanely long. Overall, a very useful class.

Onsay was one of if not the best professor I have had during my time at MSU. She was passionate about what she taught and made it interesting. She was honest with us and wanted us to succeed. Has experience in the industry and offered very helpful and realistic advice about job searching and technical interviewing in the computer science field.
Overall, amazing prof.

One of the nicest professors I have had, very helpful and knowledgeable. Not a good teacher though, lectures are hard to sit through and lecture slides are 100+ slides long every day making it almost impossible to study them or use them for projects.
The TAs teach the course. I've never even met the main professor. If you get a good TA, then you will get a good grade. Luckily, mine was amazing!
Very chill, easy 4.0.
rg34g45g34g435g
Very nice and kind, easy class, very involved and passionate about content.

Such a nice professor!!! You also can tell how much he loves teaching this. Made class interesting enough for people who don't like science. Group projects that weren't awful although some days were more boring than others. Mandatory attendance but he usually lets us go early.
Lectures are tough to sit through but I was able to do other homework. Prelab and postlab due before every class which are easy. I think the class in general is boring but overall easy.

Professor is really passionate about rivers and you can tell based off of the lectures. This was a pretty simple course and I would recommend to anyone who needs the ISP credit. The exams were online and there was weekly homework that wasn't too bad!

In class assignments you can do from home, lectures are SO hard to sit through. three exams that were all taken online outside of class, not proctored. I would recommend to others for integrative studies requirement. very easy coursework that takes less than 10 mins. not a lot of homework at all
Homework is extremely light and take about 10 minutes to complete. Exams are a bit weird and lectures are average. Still an amazing professor who cares about his students and the material he teaches

Lectures were boring as I'm not passionate about the environment, but Ozzy is a caring professor and an interesting person. Attendance is based on short questions you have to turn in online after class, and exams were online for the winter/spring '25 semester. He understands outside circumstances and will be lenient as long as you have reason.
His lecture is great. Homework and class assignments aren't too hard. There are three exams.

Very little workload, and he tried his best to make lectures more interesting. There is an in-class assignment due at the end of each class, but they were super easy. The quizzes (4), coursework (10) and homework (4) were all easy and took less than 30 minutes to complete. Easy grader, just a long lecture to get through. Everything is open note.

This class was easy and the only required work was occasionally homework and In Class Activities for attendance (but those can be done outside of class during the class period). Prof. Hernandez was nice, but canceled class a few times. No tests or finals, only open note quizzes.

This was a super chill class, he canceled lectures a lot so check your email before heading to class. There wasn't a lot of homework or assignments that went along with the class. Overall, super easy ISP credit and Osvaldo is a great guy.

Ozzy was a cool professor ti have this semester. Although he did cancel a lot, the assignments and tests were not that difficult and was easy to complete. ULA was also very accessible when needed. Recommend taking this class as it is very interesting, you just have to like lectures as it is very lecture heavy material.

I loved Ozzy last year when I had him for 203A but in a lab setting he's kinda tough. Like all labs, attendance is mandatory which is expected. One thing I hated is he would keep us in lab even when we didn't need to stay the whole time. 2 assignments due at the start of every lab, pre and post lab. Boring and one huge final project at the end.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: very easy credit for isp. hernandez is a very interesting person but unfortunately his lectures are boring. attendance is technically mandatory because of daily assignments you have to turn in, but you totally can turn them in online without stepping foot in the lecture hall.

He is an easy professor for an ISP requirement. He does have easy homework once a week and four easy online quizzes. He uses an in-class activity for attendance (but can drop 3), posts lecture slides, and cares about his students.
Light work

He is a great professor and thoroughly explains topics. Uses picture-heavy slides and counts participation through in-class assignments that can easily be done outside of class but are due at the end of class. Overall, homework and coursework are easy (look at a graph and interpret or watch a documentary), as are tests which are online open note.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I did not appreciate how many days he took off along with how he announced quizzes. He would hardly announce them and made the final due at a different time than the rest of the quizzes. I went to his class more times than I can count, and he didn't have class. he also didn't respond to emails. Other than his miscommunication, he was nice.

The class was canceled a lot, which made it hard to do some of the quizzes. Quizzes are not worth a lot, HW is easy but, with documentaries, and Coursework is easy. In-class assignments can be done outside of class and lectures are long. I did learn a lot about water and climate change that has stuck with me!

class was cancelled often due to personal reasons but the work he would assign (if any) was always easy. attendance is taken through in-class activities and 6 are dropped at the end of the semester. lectures get boring, but if you show up some of the information gets through and the exams are 15-question quizzes on d2l
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof. Hernandez is a nice person, but his execution of teaching kind of fell flat. He canceled class a lot, meaning we had less time to learn things that he would still keep on the quizzes. The courseworks are easy, but the homeworks often require watching lengthy videos. I don't think attendance is mandatory, just go through the lecture slides.

He's a great guy who is very passionate about what he teaches. He cancelled class a number of times, and the class has a low work load with relatively easy quizzes and coursework each week. No exams. Only bad thing is lectures can get boring sometimes.
Great professor and an easy grader. Not many assignments to complete and no exams, only a few at home quizzes throughout the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Respected--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: All around great professor. Grade is mainly based on very short homework assignments and in class activities. Classes can get boring at times but that is heavily due to the content and not his teaching. Class was cancelled a lot but that was due to personal reasons, and he still made sure everything we needed was accessible online. Easy 4.0.

Professor Hernandez was great this semester. He canceled class a lot due to personal reasons but he always provided lecture videos or homework assignments in place of the canceled classes. There are very few quizzes or tests in this class; your grade consists mostly of homework and in class activities. Both of these were very easy to complete.

He was a great professor and he was very understanding for our course. The only thing that people probably don't like is that he dos in class activities which you have to be in class to get credit for. But don't let that discourage you from taking him because he is a great professor.

His lectures were very boring and hard to understand at times, as he did not thoroughly explain the material. He also cancelled class several times throughout the semester; however, he did give freebies. The material in the lectures, were not always what is covered in the quizzes. Be sure to complete all assignments so quiz scores wont affect grade

Really chill professor. You can tell he loves what he teaches. He had us do in-class, paper assignments every day. He also frequently canceled lecture. Even with taking a previous environmental science class, still recommend paying attention in lecture, a lot of what he says will be mentioned on the quizzes.

Not only was Professor Os super sweet and funny, but his class was also super easy. You really don't need to take notes, you just have to do the simple in-class activities every lecture. The majority of this course is super interesting and inspiring. He is super passionate and he is flexible if you just communicate with him.

One of the best classes for your ISP requirement. Overall an easy 4.0. Attendance matters but lecture slides are always posted so at least missing class wouldn't put you behind for tests or assignments. HW was weekly, but easy and not time consuming. Few tests which were all online, and no midterm or final, which made the class a lot less stressful

He was okay! Very easy going professor the only thing that would that annoyed me was the fact that he didn't answer his emails and get in real contact with him I had to stay after class and literally chase him down. He was specific on attendance. Had him during the winter semester and even when we had a really bad snowstorm he didn't cancel.

Prof. Hernandez is a super easy grader and gives out a few assignments. You have to do a short in-class activity and then 5 homework assignments and 5 quizzes for the whole semester. Definitely would recommend to fulfill your ISP requirement.

Professor H is a gem. We only had 5 homework assignments and 5 quizzes the whole semester and they were all easy. Nicest guy you will ever meet, cancelled lecture a few times. Super cool guy, almost threw us a zoom pizza party.

Dr. H is such a cool prof! Although he is a tad disorganized, it doesn't take away from his passion for teaching. The class covers very basic high school-level earth science, and he makes even that interesting to learn about. He also applies the concepts to environmental issues in our society, making the course material be helpful in the long run.

Prof. Hernandez is clearly passionate about what he teaches, but I did not find the material or the way in which he presented it to be accessible. Gave in-class coursework and the slides are not much help to look at without showing up to lecture, so go up to class.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Prof. Hernandez is a difficult prof to deal with. Does not make accommodations for legitimate reasons and is extremely difficult to get in touch with. Says attendance isn't mandatory but as 20% of your grade is in-class assignments (that he won't allow make ups) your grade will suffer. His tests are very difficult and study guides unhelpful.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Hernandez is very passionate about this class and makes learning about bugs and the environment fun for people who may not have any interest in it. Attendance isn't necessarily mandatory, but most class points are based on the labs we do in class. He makes it very easy to be successful in this class and he's also super funny!!
Awesome, caring guy that truly gives students all the resources they need to do well in his courses.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious

reviews.comment: I had a great pleasure taking an online course with Mr. Hernandez! The course was very organized and if I did have any questions, would get back to me the same day!! Awesome instructor and would advise to take him.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He is passionate about the subjects he covers in class, but his slides are extremely confusing, so you have to go to class and pay attention because there is almost never actual writing on them, just diagrams and graphs. He is a nice guy, but I have never struggled this much in a university requirement. Extremely hard and brought my GPA down a lot.

You think that you understand the material, you study the study guides he gives you, then you get the exam and are mind blown because it is nothing like you expected! Super hard exams that is for sure. Really boring lectures, too. Randomly gives in class work for credit so you should definitely go to lecture.
lectures are really easy, posts slides online, attendance not mandatory but sometimes there is in-class activities that we hand in, exams are really easy
He was a cool professor. The lectures could get kind of long and boring, but he was passionate about it which helped. His exams were kind of hard, but he always curved them.

He's really a cool dude. If you show up and pay attention you'll not only pass the class but learn a ton about the course. This class provides some real analysis of the real problems we face and he does a great job at addressing them.

Hernandez is a great lecturer who is really knowledgable about the topic and very passionate about what he teaches.  He gives difficult tests but if you thoroughly prepare you shouldn't have a problem- just make sure you remember specifics and be aware that he likes to trick people on exams.  If you want an easy class take someone else, though!

Prof. Hernandez is a nice guy and he's enthusiastic enough about material, but his tests are pretty challenging. That being said, he provides study guides for them, so it's up to you to prepare yourself. Also, during most lectures he has you turn in something to give participation points, so attend lecture! No textbook, which is a plus!

There's no textbook; all the power points were online. With that said, you still had to drag yourself to class and listen to his hour and a half lecture because about 2/3s of the time he'll have an in class assignment (basically attendance points). The material itself isn't hard, just a boring class to go to and felt like it wasted a lot of time.
Was helpful and really wants student participation. Puts PowerPoints online but goes through them too fast in class.

Good professor and interesting course. There was no textbook because the class was new, readings posted online. Good about questions but very limited office hours, go to TA for help on assignments. Attendance is checked by turning in answers to questions from lecture, so make sure you show up because it's random.

I had Professor Elemo for MC 201 and am currently taking her MC 492 class. She does an excellent job explaining complex readings and breaking them down clearly. She is thoughtful, supportive, and genuinely cares about her students' success.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Elemo's truly one of my favorite JMC professors. I had some medical issues towards the end of the semester and she was extremely understanding when it came to my absences (unlike some other professors). She's truly passionate about what she's teaching and talking to her about class material is extremely beneficial. Highly recommend!

I had her for MC 201. Though I didn't like her class my freshman year, I realized she's actually an amazing professor in MC325! She has amazing lectures, helpful during office hours, and overall a very understanding professor if you show up. Attendance is very important but you're allowed four absences which is much more than other professors.

MC377: Culture, Politics, and Postcolonialism: Profiling Sub-Saharan Africa
Here's the tea: Elemo is VERY supportive in providing resources and suggestions for final projects and large essays. Has engaging lectures and values class participation. Communication is key, highly recommend attending office hours. E-K is a great prof for this subject.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Contrary to popular belief, she's a very good prof. Her tone may be condescending at times, but she teaches well and has clear expectations. She is a tough grader but provides good advice. Just take her feedback into account and go to office hours if you're struggling and you'll be fine.

I did not feel like Elemo created a safe space for class discussions, as myself and many of my other peers felt afraid to answer questions incorrectly. Her grading was not very consistent in my experience. When I met with her for office hours she was understanding and helpful, although it was difficult to reach her.

I'd never felt so lost. In class I felt I strongly understood material, and was praised on my knowledge of the subject. Yet, she never gave me above a 3.0 on quizzes. When reaching out for help, I left with more questions than answers and felt absolutely humiliated for performing terribly my first year of college. Only did well due to the final.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Elemo-Kaka was definitely not my favorite, she was extremely passive aggressive and I did not learn much from her. She told us she was leaving a week before her departure and did not help with the transition of the new professor. She does provide A LOT of feedback but overall I would not take this class with her again.

Professor Elmo is tough, but you will learn a lot from her. She is a good teacher. She really makes it so that you can understand the material. You have to be willing to put in the work to receive a decent grade. She gives good feedback on assignments and if you take her feedback you can do well in the class.
Very lecture heavy in a discussion based recitation, calls us her colleague but passive aggressively.

Recommending MC231 with Prof Elemo for those looking a challenging and highly rewarding class. She made me think critically about course material (CM) and gave eye opening experiences throughout the entire sem. Advice: Go to office hours prepared, don't ask questions before class (do after or email), ground points in CM, speak up if confused.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She does not allow for participation and then gives people low grades for not participating. She grades very hard but it is unclear why you did not meet her expectations. She does not provide rubrics for essay prompts. She does not make herself available to students. Don't take a class with her if you want to get grades you deserve and work for.

She's an okay teacher, but she grades very hard. It's known throughout the class that she doesn't teach us well enough for the way she grades. I think there's a chance to be successful, but you need to be ready to go to all office hours. This class is supposed to be discussion-based because we already have a lecture, but all she does is talk.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Even if I get a 4.0, I wouldn't take a class with her again. I took it online because of COVID, and she was the most unsympathetic and unhelpful prof I've had at MSU. She is a tough grader with unclear expectations. I received the worst grades I have ever gotten in her class. Guard your GPA and your sanity by never taking a class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I found Elemo to be very unsympathetic, cold, and rude. Her tone of voice is even patronizing. Emailing her multiple times still doesn't get you a response - you have to go to her office (if she's even there when she's scheduled). I would not take another class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Hawkins is brand new to JMC/PTCD, but he is a truly great teacher. He really impressed me, and he did not shy away from tough topics, and fairly presented all the reasonable angles in this class, which is a pretty controversial class. I Highly, HIGHLY reccomend taking classes with him.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: My favorite JMC prof I've had thus far, he creates a comfy environment. I used to be so nervous to join discussions, but I was good to share my takes in this class. He gives genuine feedback, and will happily meet with you if you need help. He also lectures with slides instead of just yapping for a straight hour and a half. Pls take him he's great.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor Hawkins is a refreshing change in the PTCD lineup. He has moved away from the usual Straussian dogma toward a more critical, historically grounded approach. If you're a Straussian, you may not enjoy the critical readings so much, but there will be ample opportunity to interpret and grapple with the canonical texts. Participating is key!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Professor is nice but boring and too much HW. Relies on TA too much, and the TA is not good, I think his name was Jeremiah. Very complicated grading system and he calculated grades wrong. I got a 4.0 but he calculated my grade as a 3.5 and I had to email him for weeks to fix my grade and he seemed very annoyed that I was pointing out his mistake
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Just do the homework and practice exams. Everything is pretty straightforward.
Class was easy if you come and pay attention, which you have to.

I didn't really pay attention but still ended with a 4.0. Kind of odd grading scale but do the practice exams and they're super similar to exams. All quiz and exam content comes from lecture slides so read those carefully/watch lecture videos to study
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Just do the practice exam like 20 times and you'll do well. I know that may seem odd but I rarely paid attention in class but I did each one like 15-20 times and averaged an 85 on all my exams.
He really wants everyone to do well, which I respected.
pretty good.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Really cares that students do well. I almost missed the midterm and he emailed me to remind me to come!
Really engaging lectures I learned so much

Fantastic dude, super interesting lectures. Could be difficult if you aren't well acquainted with econ topics but there is a pretty generous curve and a lot of practice materials to work with for the class.

While you will have a 20-question quiz and 30 questions of homework a week, he explains everything very well in class. If you pay attention, it is a pretty easy class and he is willing to meet and help if you need it. While leactures can go over a lot he does a good job explaining it all in a way that kept me engaged.
he's a good guy but he makes his class so much harder than what it has to be. nice grade curve though (because of how hard he makes everything)

Not a bad professor. The professor himself is nice but the TA of the class is the worst TA I've ever met in any course. If u take this course and the same guy is ur TA DONT take the class. Also 0 excused absences so if your sick and don't want to spread illness, go anyway because apparently students general health doesn't matter to this class.

Awful class. For 98% of people it is a required class that is so obnoxiously hard. You stare at graphs all lecture, while he scribbles nonsense and says "are you all with me?", while no one is paying attention. Homework/quiz questions are not really learned in the lectures. Relies too much on TAs so he is able to "focus more on content",he doesn't

Seems like a good guy who teaches his class very harshly. Quizzes and tests are going to be hard, since there are 5–7 responses to choose from for each multiple-choice question and 3–5 responses for each T/F question. Nothing in this class is meant to help you achieve a good grade within reason, and that is wrong given the current student workload.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of the worst professors I've had. Lectures are insanely boring, you can't miss a single class for attendance or iclicker questions and there is an abnormal amount of homework and quizzes. The lecture slides are incomplete and so confusing, and the questions on hw are unnecessarily hard. Seems like a nice guy, but bad professor

I really enjoyed Professor Ziv's class. While the lectures were sometimes dense, he always took time to answer student questions clearly. When I couldn't stay after class, he responded to my emails within 24 hours with helpful explanations, showing his dedication to student success.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He was overall fine at teaching the concepts of the class, but the class was really poorly organized. He relied way too heavily on his TAs to do everything, and it led to slow grades, assignments being assigned to the wrong dates constantly, and multiple other problems. His grading system is also horrible and convoluted. Definitely a mixed bag.

Oren Ziv is one of the worst professors I have ever had, to the point where I don't even think he wants to teach. His lectures are disorganized, messy, and useless. He makes his TAs Ziv do all of the work, and as a result, grades and any feedback are slow to come out. Oren ziv is not eager to help and overall has a horrible attitude. Do not take.

The class is easy and he's just funny to listen to in general. It seems like he's always about to make a joke about something. If you do the homework and do well on the quizzes it should be workable 4.0.

Can raise your final grade depending on your circumstances.

At the beginning of the semester he would ask iclicker questions off the top of his head, confusing himself and the class as a whole. He uses pictures of graphs from textbooks and does a poor job of giving context for their use of variables. Lots of self teaching. If you do the homework the tests aren't hard, just confusing.

Even though I'm doing well in the class you have to pretty much teach yourself the material. He even gets confused and mixes up what he's talking about. I feel that the lectures confuse me so much when I thought I had a good understanding. Don't go to office hours, he will just confuse you more. Tests are based on the homework and aren't too bad.

Ziv is a great dude, and a great professor. No textbook was used in this class, so going to lecture is super important. I really enjoyed attending his lectures, don't think I missed a single one which is rare for me. The class seems confusing at first but he breaks it down well. Exams are easy assuming you go to class. No study guides are given
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This guy went to Harvard but I think he's a better student than teacher. I took his class a couple years ago, but I remember he was so bad at teaching that the curve in the class was substantial. Does a really bad job at explaining and helping comprehend the concepts. Then again, Econ classes are no fun at all unless you really have an interest.

i took pls120 and she was great! she's an attorney herself! attendance is mandatory!!! there's lots of reading + case briefs at first but you get used to them. 2 exams and a group project you have to ask and answer questions on. she lets you use notes on exams but they are handwritten so i had to rush a bit. highly recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

reviews.comment: Absolutely loved everything about this class except for the format of the exams. I ran out of time on all three exams because they were handwritten and were based on dense prompts. Fantastic lectures, Melissa is so funny, class would be perfect if she switched to lockdown browser for exams.

LOVED this class. At the start of the semester it was very daunting but once you understand the formula of how to write for her class you will be set. Very kind and knowledgeable professor, I will take another class with her in a heartbeat. This class is supposed to act as law school prep, so keep that in mind when considering taking 321.

Professor O'Shea is an amazing professor. She is so passionate about law and really understands what she is teaching. She offeres extra credit, and in the start of the semester there was a lot of homework that was heavily dependent on the reading. Attendance is mandatory but she typically lets us out a few minutes early so it makes up for it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor O'Shea is a GREAT teacher, she makes law very fun and interesting even if it is only an intro class. There is ALOT of reading, there aren't any textbooks required for the course but for the first half of the semester you will have to read cases. Only two exams and a project, both open note. Easy A!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She is an amazing professor and you can tell she cares about her students and wants them to succeed. Workload was heavy on reading, which is expected, but was never too overwhelming.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor O'shea is an amazing professor and clearly knows a lot about the material she teaches. As long as you are actively going to class and paying attention you will get an easy 4.0. The exams are open note and the final group project is not hard to get a good grade as long as you participate in your group and work together.

Professor O'Shea is excellent at what she teaches, and has only heightened my interest in Political Science. You do have to attend class to 4.0, but the lectures and materials are directly what will be on the midterms, for which are both open note. Highly recommend this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Melissa O'shea is a great professor that truly cares about her students. She encourages you to reach out if you have any questions at all. Attendance is mandatory to 4.0 the class. She is a good lecturer that makes class fun, is very knowledgable about law, and will help you to be successful.

This is an easy class, it is NOT a political science class. It is strictly law. She is a fair grader and its easy to pass the class. No one assignment will deck your grade. She is dramatic but it is an easy class to pass. You don't have to go to class but I would recommend it.

Make no mistake, this is an intro to law class. At the beginning, you read lots of cases, but she encourages skimming. She grades fairly, if not on the easier side. She has 2 tests, both of which were open written note, a final paper and a group project where she allows you to report group members who slack. She will not extend deadlines however.
I enjoyed this class but you need to come to all the classes. Both of the exams were open-note. You have to read a lot of cases in the beginning of the semester but you can just skim.

Professor O'shea is one of the funniest and most insightful professors I've encountered. It is evident how much she cares for her students and their understanding of the content. While attendance is mandatory, her engaging nature makes showing up to lecture worth it. If you are considering law school, I would 100% recommend taking a course with her

Professor O'shea's wit and sense of personality she includes in her lectures makes it worth showing up. While there is a fair amount of reading and homework, none of it is tedious. It is evident how much she truly listens to students needs and cares about their opinions. Attendance and effort is required, but her aim isn't to make you fail.

Professor O'shea is very knowledgeable and cares that students understand the content. Definitely my favorite class and favorite professor. This class was not only interesting but also gave me the foundations I need for law.
O'Shea is goated. If you are looking to go to law school take as many courses with her as possible. Not much more to say other than that.

Professor O'Shea is a great professor and she really knows what she is talking about. Her teaching style ensure that by the end of the class, you will have learned a lot about the Judicial Process. She does use the socratic method and cold calling for a grade which can be stressful but this makes sure that you actually learning the material
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor O'Shea is an amazing professor but her classes are not for students that do not like to go to class. She is great at answering questions about life and course work. I have taken many of her classes and she truly cares if her students understand the material and how she can change it for students in the future.

Professor O'Shea was an amazing Professor and Mentor for me at Michigan State. I took four classes with her and she was great every time. She was always available to outside of class and was super enthusiastic during class. I would not be in the position I am today without her, could not recommend her more.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I loved this class! Theres no textbook because she just posts all of the cases on D2L which is great. She makes every lecture interesting and funny and she often finished lectures early. The exams are tough but she allows you to have 3 pages of notes and gives good study tips. I learned the most in this class and I will actually remember it.

Professor OShea does challenge her students, but if you put in the effort the class shouldnt be too bad. Her class is one I always enjoy going. It is entertaining and very valuable for prelaw students. I would definitely take her class again.

O'Shea is nothing short of fantastic professor for MSU law. However, I did take a very difficult class for her and it is very easy to slip up with the material or miss points more often than not. But making sure each student understands the material to its full extent is a top priority to her, you may have to go to office hours at one point.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I took the honors option which was a fairly easy addition to the normal course work. The honors paper was graded pass/fail. You had to watch lectures on Tuesday and take the quiz the same day, so it was a time constraint difficulty. It was also a lot of work. There was a discussion post every week, case briefs, and papers. Still, decent professor.

Prof O'Shea was amazing! Very straightforward about expectations. Over the course of the semester, we had 6 case briefs, 1 majority opinion to write, and three exams. There were also 8 pop quizzes, she utilizes cold calling for participation, and takes attendance. Overall I would definitely take again-- difficult class but lots of opportunities
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Professor OShea was one of the best professors Ive had at MSU so far. She is very funny and makes her lectures a lot of fun. You are required to go to class to get clicker points & if you dont go you most likely wont pass the class. Her tests are extremely hard but I got a 70% on all of them and still 4.0d the class because of the project
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Very good professor, clearly outlines what are needed in the case briefs. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS unless you like court cases and dont mind reading. Personally I like both of those things so I had no problem putting in the effort. Tests can be difficult but DO THE EXTRA CREDIT. Always be ready to answer clicker questions in class. Overall good :)
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This professor will assign a lot of reading. You're gonna need to show up for class every day, since missing class too many times will take a major hit to your grade. Her lectures are satisfactory at best - they are watered down by tangents and opinion-preaching. The class is somewhat organized, but not really. I would not take her again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Exams are HARD & free response. Can use a notecard, but it's useless unless you really know the information. Kinda teaches what she wants rather than what the name of the course would suggest, but still interesting, as she's very intelligent and knows what she's doing. Definitely a very difficult semester. 1 paper & some case briefs w/ exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: All PwrPnts from class are online sometimes she will put them up before class starts w/ the Iclicker answers on them. All you need to do to 3.5 the class is do the all the work, attend all classes and on the exams write all info from PwrPnts and Iclicker ?'s on course pack. Both Exam class averages were 60% but they are open note. Very hard class!
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--SO MANY PAPERS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor O'shea is amazing! She is very knowledgeable and approachable. The work is nothing more than the work you should expect out of a 4 credit class! Her lectures are NOT boring at all because she's full of life.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Uses iClicker for attendance every day, the power points have questions scattered through. Only 2 midterms the whole year test wise (although she mentions the average grade for them is like 75%), other than that there are case briefs to read almost every night and you write occasional briefs. Easy class if you put in some work.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Prof. O'Shea is a very fair professor and will explicitly tell you what she wants in your case briefs, you do have to do ALL the work in the class or your grade will plummet. Also tests are harder than expected.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: She tells you exactly what she wants, exams are open book because they are difficult and picky. Make sure you take the time to read and come to lecture to know exactly what she wants for case briefs/exams. Overall she makes the class interesting and is pretty helpful
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Prof. O'shea is hilarious and really cares for the material and her students. She isn't boring to listen to, and she gives out and posts online her fabulous notes! I would definitely take a course with her again
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Very easy to follow as long as you highlight your course pack when she does
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: You can tell that she really cares about the material, and she knows it well. She will try to scare you away from the class during the first lecture by telling you that exams are hard. Although it's true, they count for only a small percentage of your grade relative to other MSU courses.
I love Professor O'Shea! She is super helpful and sweet. She also grades pretty easily. As long as you try in this class, you will get a 4.0!

Definitely not an easy class, but the people on here are way too harsh. As long as you go through your course pack and highlight where she finds her information from and really take time to go through it and study clicker questions, the exams are easy. It's just a lot of work and time dedication
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: It's a hard class but she is a really good professor who is clearly into what she is teaching.  Tough grader but go and talk to her so you know exactly what she wants on the test helps a ton
Great teacher. Hard Tests but gives extra credit assignments
She's nice and funny and wants the best for the students
very tough material. If you go to office hours, she helps out a lot. exams are really hard. final exam is a group project.

She seems nice but to get credit for the assignments or the project you have to have the same opinion as her. The case briefs were dense and awful to read and not easy to get a hold of. Eventually I did, but the test were nearly impossible. One was even open book and I studied & wrote all the stuff from the slides in my book and got a 40% somehow.

She is passionate about what she teaches and makes that very clear. However she will give you zeros on the case briefs she assigns if you miss tiny little parts. The class averages for the exams were around 40%, be prepared to not do well on any of the exams. Awful teacher but good at law.

Content is super interesting. Study it well and there's no reason this class will be too difficult. Pontifex has an ego and not everyone liked him, but I enjoyed his teaching style a lot. He is organized, straight-forward, and uses strategies during lecture to improve our chances of remembering the content (his examples given are always great)

Students should know that Professor Matthew Pontifex really cares about helping students understand the material. His lectures are engaging and he explains concepts clearly, which makes the class easy to follow. If you pay attention and participate, you will learn a lot. He is passionate about what he teaches and creates a positive environment.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: I think that professor Pontifex is very knowledgable in what he teaches. In lecture, he makes specific examples that make the content easier to understand. There is extra credit given in specific lectures. I got a 4.0 without getting above an 88% of a test but I also never missed a lecture or lab. I recommend reading the lecture material!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is pretty good with lecturing and knows what he's talking about but comes across kinda rude. I didn't find the content to be too bad but it's definitely test heavy and would recommend going to lectures to do well. Also has you do a check in for every class for points so those, the labs and the check for understanding quizzes are easy points

Extremely test heavy. If you do not take notes and do the quizzers, you WILL fail. A lot of the questions on the exam will be on the quizzers but there are a good portion of them that are curveballs and are meant to make you think. He does NOT post any of the slides he uses to teach the class and you're left with lecture PDFS that aren't very good.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Test heavy

reviews.comment: There are people with big egos, and then there's this guy. He enjoys being rude to students, and the exams are completely different from the lectures. Do not ever take his class, he is the worst.

This professor is way too full of him self. We spent multiple lectures talking about osteoporosis and it was one question on the exam. The exams are not what is covered in class and his notes are not easy to follow. Try and take this class with a professor that is less full of himself and his exams are easier.

honestly, you can tell he is full of himself and does not really care. if you have an excuse letter- don't count on anything being excused. lab was just an extension of lecture. do the quizzer but also look over notes heavy for exams. pay good attention

Definitely has his lectures memorized they're so seamless and easy to follow, which was the reason for my good grade. He makes the content interesting with relatable examples which stick in your memory.  He is part of the neuro/psych depts. and uses his knowledge to help his students. A bit egotistical but straightforward. I'd highly recommend!

He begins the first class by saying I will see most you again retaking this class. The class is difficult if you aren't putting in constant effort. He moves very fast through his slides in class and he doesn't upload them so take notes as fast as possible. Don't miss class literally you miss one lecture and its almost impossible to catch up.

Dr. Pontifex is a good professor. I found his lectures engaging and the material interesting. However the structure of the class is not set up for you to succeed. Since the majority of your grade is based off of 3 exams with no drops or extra credit, it makes it especially difficult to 4.0. My pharmacology class gave me more leeway than this.

I took his Kin 240 class as my first Kin classes last semester as a freshman and it was definitely challenging but I really loved his class! His lectures were very interesting and he had a great viewpoint on hard material to help you remember. I overall would say that he is a great professor!

Took KIN 240, do not miss class, you will fail. Lab can be hard depending on who your TA is, most of the time ends up being 2 hours. Pop quiz every lab on material learned, they can be rough. Overall, study a little after every class and you should be good for tests. Use the Quizzer online to study, it helps a ton. Good luck.

I thought that this class was pretty straightforward if you studied the skeleton notes and did the quizzer. The annoying thing about this class was how he never posted the slides or lectures. Also, in the labs, you had to take a quiz right after learning the material. He was a decent professor, but I did think he had some egotistical tendencies.

He was a good professor, but the class is tough. There's no final, so your grade depends heavily on the three exams. Use the quizzer he provides to prepare—it helps. Be ready to study a lot if you want a good grade. Attendance is mandatory and you get points for it.

Professor Pontifex was fine. His lectures were kind of boring at times but it was alr. This class is TEST HEAVY. The quizzes in labs are either easy or hard. ( mostly hard) We get no drops in anything. And the quizzes are what we learned that day.

Professor Pontifex was great. He was incredibly engaging during class. I know everyone says this is the hardest KIN course, and they aren't lying. But it has more to do with his grading criteria than the material itself. As long as you actively take notes, you'll be fine. That's why you should review review review!
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Pontifex is very smart and knows what he's talking about. If only what was in lecture directly correlated with exam material. There are only 3 exams and he makes a makeshift quizlet to study with, but it always felt like the questions on exams were never in the notes, quizlet, or lecture. I went to every lecture yet never felt prepared.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I think that while the course was very test heavy (most of your grade will be your tests) Dr. Pontifex and the TA's were very helpful with the material, and you can easily pass if you show up to class. Dr. Pontifex and the TA's are also really understanding and helpful with RCPD accomodations in my experience.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Start preparing for exams weeks ahead of time, since they're weighted so heavily. Go to lab to get points from quizzes/participation. Its unfortunate that he does not provide more opportunities to improve your grade, but focus on the important concepts he stresses in class and use the quizzer he provides and you will do well.

The class was interesting and engaging. However, your grade was based off of 3 exams. If you don't do well on the exams it is nearly impossible to get your grade back up. There are quizzes in lab and some are harder than others. There is only 45 points of participation the rest is graded.
Really nice guy, fun to listen to. The quizzes in lab are harder than the exams, exams are manageable if you use the quizzer.

Dr. Pontifex has an interesting personality but his tests are insanely easy. If you go through his makeshift quizlet, 100% of the time they will show up on the tests. His labs are easy but we have quizzes for each lab and some are harder than others. That depends on your TA though. Attendance is recorded and given for points.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Pontifex can be perceived as a bad or "arrogant" guy but I think that depends on how you are doing in the class. He does sometimes say some things that makes you be like "uh.. ok?!" but it makes the class sorta entertaining. The class is only graded on 3 exams and lab quizzes, BUT if you do the quizzers and study the skeleton notes you can 4.0

TBH this class needs to be restructured especially new lab instructors. If u utilize the quizzer for the test then u should be able to do really well on the exams. But, our entire grade is based on 3 exams and lab quizzes. The lab quizzes are the hardest part the stuff we learn in labs has nothing to do with the class which can ruin ur grade fast.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Pontifex is definitely arrogant and a little hard to approach with questions, but he knows his stuff. Very nice guy once you work up the courage to ask a question. Your grade is mainly based on 3 exams but if you utilize the online quizzer and review the "skeleton" notes you'll be just fine. 4.0 is achievable.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr.Pontifex is extremely arrogant and conceited, and I am not the only one that thinks this. Not only is the professor awful, but the class itself has awful criteria. Don't get me wrong, the information is useful and interesting, but your entire grade for this class is based off of 3 exams. So get ready to study. One of the hardest classes ever.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Pontifex is arrogant, but he is that way for a reason. He knows the class and teaches it well. This class is difficult, but it is absolutely possible to do well. Go to lecture, go to lab, and utilize the quizzer function in the syllabus. Many of the questions you see in the quizzer will be in the exam. Good luck!
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class is based off of 3 exam. The first one is the toughest in my opinion. You typically have labs every week and at the end of lab, you take a quiz. Only one chance for extra credit and its worth 10 pts. Highly recommend doing it. If you don't have to take the class, I would not recommend. As long as you study the notes, you should do well.

Dr. Pontifex is a really cool guy, but the class is hard. All you do every time you have class is get lectured too, and the class is basically graded by the 3 exams you take. He's a great guy, always willing to answer questions. The downside is KIN173 formatting is not good, and you can kiss a 4.0 goodbye off of one bad test.

Prof Pontifex has very engaging lectures and is very passionate about what he is teaching. First test is the hardest, but gets easier after that. He gives three exams, and a few small quizzes. Option for extra credit paper given at the end of the semester. As long as you study and attend lecture, a 4.0 is very attainable.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Pontifex was a good prof, his lectures are entertaining to say the least but he practically gives you the notes for every lecture which is really helpful. The class is pretty much based on three or four 100 point exam grades and the rest of the grades are pop quizzes and note checks. Just go to class because you won't do well if you skip.
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor is very difficult. If you don't go to class you will be behind. The class is mostly based on 3 one hundred point exams which he does not prepare you for. There are pop quizzes and quizzes due every week. If you aren't constantly paying attention or taking notes throughout every lecture you probably won't do good.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This prof is extremely arrogant... not saying he doesn't know his stuff, but his cockiness is annoying.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Freshman year I had him and it was fine. watching the lectures and showing up to the recitations is not enough for me personally. I did the skeleton notes and then proceeded to write my own notes so I could break concepts down and learn them. I feel that if you put in the effort and truly strive for an A it is completely achievable.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: In his class, there are really only three exams worth 100 points each and then a 6 point quiz every week. His exams are very hard to prepare for since there is no book and he only posts a 40 min lecture each week. He also gives pop quizzes that are all application-based. One of the pop quizzes we took this week was about statistics.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: At the start he gave off arrogant vibes, but as time passes I've grown to like him more. He's passionate about the content and tries to host engaging lectures. He gives fun examples to connect the material to the real world. Beware that he DOES sometimes give pop quizzes! The first exam was a struggle but by the second I figured it out how to study
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Hes a smart guy and knows his stuff, but he can come off as kind of arrogant bc of this. Hes not rude, just a tough teacher and really makes you put the work in to get a good grade and doesnt just hand out 4.0s. I was frustrated at first with this guy but now i know his vibe and how this class works, you just gotta get past the first exam.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The lectures seem easy at first but he doesn't prepare you for the exams at all, they are all very hard. There are 3 exams, the second one being the easiest in my opinion because I managed to get a B on it. The third final exam the class average was around 63% and I received a 76% which I was proud of. He curved the class grade at the end however.

This was one of the hardest classes I've ever taken, and I'm a Junior. Dr. Pontifex's exams are so hard it's nearly impossible to pass them. The average in the class was a 72. Please do yourself a favor and avoid this class at all costs so it doesn't ruin your GPA you worked so hard for. this class was harder than my Physiology class.

Really wouldn't recommend this course. Only total of 410 points. 300 from tests which are harder than you would expect. The other 110 points come from lab which the TAs do not grade easy on. You must go to class. Tests are confusing and meant to trick you. Labs questions are also confusing.
Honestly horrible professor and does not care about his students. He is also extremely cocky. Very hard and test questions made to trick you. Would never take a class with him ever again.

Good professor who knows what he's talking about. The exams are tough so you need to pay attention to lecture and write down everything HE says. Study hard for the first exam, its harder than you'd expect.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Listen to every word he says because the little things will become test questions and youre screwed if you dont listen. Most important thing is to study a lot more than you think- especially for the first exam. It was the hardest in my opinion and its hard to come back from bombing one since there arent many grades in the course. Labgo
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Clearly knows his stuff but the class is tougher than I predicted. Tests are nearly 100% application based & notes are all you have to study from. My #1 tip after taking this course would be to study way more than you think and VERY in-depth. Pontifex doesnt answer emails which is frustrating (in my experience)- also important to go to labs.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: avoid this class at all costs
Very good professor. Makes all lectures fun and interesting. Definitely study hard for the exams though.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Hilarious, very passionate about teaching and really knows his stuff. Make sure that you go to labs and do as well as you can on those because they affect your grade a lottt. Tests are challenging study more than you think you need to. The TA's are helpful, go see them at office hours before you need it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Extremely good lectures, if you're interested in KIN. His tests are a little challenging, but it you study outside of class a little you should do just fine. Also, do the labs, they will affect you grade ALOT.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: make sure you study outside of class a lot. exams are tough

I personally don't like the way that he teaches the class. He goes quickly through the lecture because he wants you to print off his notes and then fill in the blanks, which doesn't work well for me and how I study. Tests are application based and very had if not prepared for. You have to go to class to get the notes unless gotten from a friend.
He knows his stuff, very good about keeping the class interesting and giving examples and stories to remember things.

Absolutely loved him as a professor.  Lectures aren't boring and he keeps you engaged.  Connects to situations you can connect to and cracks a few jokes here and there.  Loved the material as well.  Not hard to 4.0 if you go to class and study.

Prof Pontifex is a pretty cool dude. I was never bored in class. He's clear during lectures and note taking is just simple fill in the blanks. He's young and in touch with todays generation, always manages to slip in a few jokes. Tests were sorta tricky but as long as you have the notes & actually understand the material you'll be fine.

Kin 173 is a pretty easy class to follow along with. Prof Pontifex is a pretty cool dude. I never found his lectures boring, he slides in some jokes every now and then. I thought he was pretty clear in explaining things. Tests can be sorta tricky, but as long as you get the notes & don't cram last minute you'll be fine!

Make sure you get a good TA because it will make or break you from a 3.5 and a 4.0. Class is harder than it needs to be but still easy to 4.0 if you do the work and study. Pontefix is a little arrogant at times too, but who isn't these days?

Pontifex is a great teacher. He does a good job of getting students involved and making class interesting. Rarely bored in lecture. However I felt that the lab was pretty unrelated and my TA was incompetent to put it nicely. The lab reports can be tricky, but if you take your time and follow the write up instructions you can make up for it.

Loved the professor, he knew exactly what he was talking about and make analogies and comparisons to different things to help us understand concepts. He kept the class interesting and I was never bored in class.
Go to class for notes. All easy fill in the blank notes anyway. The labs are pretty new and pointless to me, big waste of time in my opinion.

Many think this class is difficult since he added the lab for it. I did not like taking the lab portion of this class at all. If they kept the course without the lab it wouldn't be so bad. Just a heads up. The first actual lab write up is probably the hardest though.
Need to go to class for notes unless you get them from someone else.

Easy class if you go to class to fill in the notes. Lab reports can be difficult at first but get easier. Make yourself familiar with excel. Do the checks for understanding and you're fine. The last exam is the hardest by far but do well on the others and you shouldn't be to bad.

Best teacher I've ever had seriously. I wish he taught other classes so I can take them. Organized, caring, concise, lectures are so well planned. Anatomy has lots of content, so he makes sure we all understand it by explaining it in simplistic ways and telling us what we really need to know for exams. Incredible guy.

AMAZING!!!! One of the best professors I've had at msu. He cares about making sure all of his students know and understand all of the information he is teaching. Attendance isn't mandatory but he does give random extra credit quizzes for those who attend lecture. I would love to take another one of his classes.

Riley has been the best professor ive had at MSU. His office hours are sooooo helpful. The lectures can be pretty dense but he does verbally tell you what you should focus on for studying. He exapains things in the easiest and clearest way he can with it still making sense. Class is hard but he made it so much more managable. A++ for this man!

Riley is my favorite professor I have ever had at MSU. He is very approachable if you need to ask anything. Lectures are fast paced but he makes it interactive and lets you know exactly what you need to know for exams. Exams will be tough if you don't study, so put in the time and listen during lectures and it is an easy 4.0.

Professor Riley is definitely my favorite professor that I have had so far at MSU. He is extremely approachable, intriguing, down to earth, and relatable. He does a great job covering the material and making it clear what to know for the exams. You need to put in the time to study for this in order to be successful since it is mainly memorization.
Riley is very approachable and easy to talk too. Lectures go fast with non-stop note taking. He is very clear on how to be successful in this class.

He is a great professor- he cares for his students and wants everyone to succeed. This class is a ton of notes, you need to study a lot to do well on the exams (exams determine final grade). There is online homework and he gives some extra credit throughout the semester (mostly at the end of sem).

Riley is an amazing instructor. He cares so much about his students' success and it shows. He essentially tells you what is on the exams and highlights different material you should focus on when studying. If this was taught by anyone else, there's no chance I would've done as well as I did.
overall great

KIN 216 is talked about for being hard but that is the biggest misconception I have ever heard. This was my EASIEST class in college. The tests were easy and he would tell you exactly would be on it so its pretty impossible to do bad on an exam. The questions are straightforward. Just pay attention and don't skip class and do what you pay for.
Riley was a great professor to take anatomy with. This was a hard course, but he was very caring and genuinely wants his students to do well in his class.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of the best professors I have ever had. I was nervous about this tough class, but Riley made it so much easier. He genuinely wants everyone to succeed. He is straight to the point and tells you what you need to know. I can not imagine taking this class with another professor. I wish he taught every class I had to take!

Riley was a great prof to have for KIN216, it was a hard class that was made much easier by him. His lectures are long becasue its a lot of material, but they are interesting. He's funny, kind, understanding, and makes the class enjoyable. 10/10

I loved having Riley as my anatomy professor. He made the course so much more retainable and was excited about teaching. He is very adament that everyone passes his class and gives countless extra credit opportunities and ensures you understand the material. Would absolutely love to take this course again with him.
He's a great professor. Knows that the class is tough so he really takes his time and make sure that we are understanding what he teaches and his office hours are very helpful too.
Honestly, you can look up all the answers to everything if you're just looking for a 4.0.
Che is awesome! His class is very straightforward and all homework and exams are online with multiple attempts. If you can take the in person lecture you should!!
his class is very easy to follow along!

This professor misspoke multiple times in lecture videos but didn't edit them making them hard to follow along with. The amount of readings you are assigned each week with only 5 days to complete is overwhelming. I didn't understand the way things were being taught so I taught myself. He's a kind person, but the class could be structured better.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The professor does not care about the class at all, multiple times in his lecture video he would mess up over and over again but was too lazy to edit the video or restart. This class is basically a completely online class that has a structure so he does not have to Grade anything. The work load is very above average.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Communication is amazing. He always give you ample time and notification when something is due. He is super easy to contact and understanding. One of the best professors I have had while completing my business minor.
He gives extra credit opportunities that are decently easy to do, he also makes a lot of the quizzes use examples from homework so if you do the homework or  extra practice you will be good

The class is well-organized, with a clear syllabus and helpful structure. Quizzes and the final are untimed, and homework allows three attempts. Zoom and in-person support is available. There's one research project, Packback posts, and low-pressure grading. Extra credit and quiz reminders are a plus.
As long as you turn in your assignments and do the work it is more than possible to achieve a good grade in this class. There are lots of extra credit opportunities.

Took the online version, not a hard class. Lectures are monotone and read off slides. You'll need to buy the online textbook to do homework - $85. Also weekly discussion posts. Practice problems are very similar or even identical to the exams. LOTS of extra credit. For me the final didn't end up mattering, its only worth 10%.
Just do the assignments on time and you'll be fine. Do the extra credit opportunities!! Do not skip them

This course was well laid out. The textbook quickly and easily gets your grades in, so you are not left to wonder like many other courses. Peterson was always on top of things too, it was almost impossible to fall behind in the course. Just pay attention to reminders and emails in the course and you will be fine.

The professor was nice and genuine but a bit boring. The course includes conceptual and math questions. Homework had multiple attempts, and quizzes were online, time-flexible, and unproctored. Practice questions and homework closely match quiz questions. He also offers great extra credit opportunities, which I highly recommend doing.

He's a very strict professor when it comes to excuses. The course isn't hard per say, you really have to remember to do the work and set time to learn the materiel. The class is perfectly structured, you are never unsure when something is due. When I had severe mental episode, he made a plan with me to still be able to succeed in the class.

super nice guy and great class structure. totally online and he is reachable and understanding. I had to go to the ER during my semester on the day of a quiz and he was totally understanding about making it up and worked with me to find a good time. would take again 100%
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: If you have interest in accounting, this is a great class to test the waters. If you don't, avoid it at all costs. I found the online class to be incredibly boring, with anywhere from 3 to 10 lecture videos per week to watch (each 2-20 minutes long). He is VERY monotone. The platform used for hw, quizzes & exams is terrible and often glitches.

Overall, he's a very kind and easy-going professor. He will accommodate things like quizzes or participation points for you if anything happens in your life with no problem. I took the class in person, so participation was required. There were 4 quizzes and then the final exam. I strongly recommend using the practice problems to your advantage.

Not a hard class; however, there is a lot of content and information you need to learn. You get several attempts for homework assignments and quizzes are online with no lockdown browser. Prof. Peterson reads off the slides he makes but still tries to make a personal connection with his students. Overall an ok class with nothing extraordinary.

Posts lecture slides every week, typically 8-12 videos varying from 4-16 minutes. The textbook is very tedious but provides good practice questions that are seen in the quiz and shows a lot of graphs to connect your learning to an image. 1 homework assignment a week and quiz (exams) every 4 weeks. 1 large excel assignment.

I just hate paying for online courses. I only reached out to Che one time to ask a question about a quiz, and it led to him connecting me with one of his associates for a job prospect, which was sweet. The course is hit or miss. Weekly discussions on top of homework. Quizzes and exams require heavy study time unless you cheat. Not an easy 4.0.

Prof Peterson is super accessible, very helpful if you have a question. Will open more office hours if you can't come during his. While confusing in MBC, his syllabus directly states when all due dates are. Homework & quizzes can be a little confusing, but it's open note. It is accounting so it is confusing, but he is a great professor overall.
There is not much guidance given by this professor and the class was overall very confusing.

Gotta pay to do ur HW. L professor. no reason for you to not get 100 on every hw, it's online. quizzes, not too bad my lazy self got like 78s so you can probably do better. Easy W for the credit. simple weekly HW (with the use of "external resources") discussion posts you can do in 30 seconds (shout out to gpt) and 4 quizzes hither and thither.

Due dates are all over the place, the list of assignments blends in with the readings/lectures necessary so its confusing reading it section by section. Overwhelming amounts of stuff on your screen. Do not recommend this class, because it was difficult to maintain this class with many other due dates. Very poor system and way of teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: He uses Mybussinesscourse for the entire class. Meaning you will have to pay $120 just to be able to do the homework that everyone is just going to cheat on anyway. I took this class as a summer class and I didn't have the option to purchase any options for access besides 6 months for $120. He was cool overall as a prof though.

Terrible organization, had to update syllabus numerous times throughout the semester to make adjustments that should have already been there. Bad at checking his email, some peers reported that he took multiple days to respond and only gave a one word answer. If you have to take ACC 230, try a different professor.

I love Dr. Prior's lectures. She makes an already interesting topic even more interesting through her presentations. I love tying our textbook discussions to media of our choosing, it helps me think deeply about the topics and connect them to real-life. She is also very open to discussion about topics, which I appreciate and enjoy participating in.

Dr. Prior fine, this class is almost entirely a group project work wise, its also 50 percent of your grade. The class work is so easy. The issue I had was the group project in an online asynchronous class, we don't have a designated meeting time so it makes it so so hard to meet with your group, the project is all semester too. So be aware of that.

I took SOC 316 but it wasn't an option. great professor! just make sure you read all the assigned readings, as you have to tie most of them to whatever assignment you are doing. there is no final exam, just final project.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: She was an amazing professor, she was always very clear on how to do well in the class, and as long as you do your work and stay on top of it it'll be an easy 4.0

This was my favorite class! Dr. Prior is an amazing professor, and I hope to take another class with her again. She cares about her students and is very knowledgable about her field. Lots of extra credit opportunities, and the lectures were incredibly interesting. Class is based off the textbook. Just pay attention and go to class, and you're good.

I'm not even a sociology major and it was one of the most enjoyable classes I've ever had. Lectures were fun and I enjoyed the reading and in the past I've hated reading. It also wasn't that much reading as it's only 12 chapters from end of August to beginning of December. If you're on the fence about this class... take it.

Dr. Prior is a very enthusiastic and kind professor, however, her class (ISS 215) is very exam heavy and not as easy as you would expect an ISS course to be. The exams are quite difficult and the study guides do not help. If you are looking for an easy ISS course, this is not one for you.

Her lectures were fun because you can tell she actually enjoys what she does. I took her class for SOC 216, but it wasn't an option to choose. Nonetheless, it was a pretty solid class. The only parts I don't like are having to watch whole films outside of class and also just the mandatory attendance points. BUT! she's really good with RCPD VISAs.
Great professor!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: She was honestly the best professor I had this semester. Super caring and she really placed an emphasis on our learning and understanding. Even though you have to read a lot before class it is totally worth it to take this class with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Never take this prof, he pushes his political views and degrades his students. This class would be fine if he were open to other views. 2 hr lecture to hear him rant.

Worst professor I've had at MSU. He spends most of class pushing his political opinions and assigns work that forces students to support his agenda. Little relevant material is covered despite two-hour classes. Attending lectures felt like a complete waste of time. I don't know how he still has a job teaching anybody.

This teacher is the worst professor I've ever had. He only posts topics about his extreme political side, and if he doesn't agree with you on something he will give you a 0. I genuinely don't know how he's still employed at MSU. The content learned in this class is useless and disgusting.

Class is insanely easy, but the lectures are just the prof ranting about his personal views. It's pointless and annoying, as someone who agrees with his views, he makes me want to change my beliefs just so I don't side with him. He's also super condescending to students like we're clueless. Not worth it but also the easiest class I've ever taken.

This professor constantly shares his political views with the class, almost pushing them onto you. He presented multiple opportunities to go to politically charged protests for extra credit. While the class was easy, I would never take his class after the constant tangents about completely irrelevant political topics. Very unclear grading criteria.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--So many papers--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This course was nothing but offensive honestly. I feel I learned very little about the actual subject, and more about his political and outside views. Don't even try to email him, he wont respond for weeks with hardly a one sentence response. Ask about a low grade? He doesn't have an answer. Do not take this class with him. If I could drop, I would
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Graded by few things
