reviews.comment: He does a good job teaching as he is passionate about the material. Offers a lot of different and cool extra credit opportunities. Get ready to write, since every chapter is graded on one paper that is usually 5-8 pages long and covers many topics. If you can write well, you should do well. He is lenient with deadlines and cares about his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor is not a good teacher, the majority of the material that he teaches can be summarized in the readings that he requires before each class. The required readings can also be done half-assed before the class and still get full credit. Make sure you cite and answer the full question for each essay, that is how you will get a good grade.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Prof. Schauert is very caring to his students and knowledgeable about the subject material. His lectures can be a little boring at times and the essays are long but he's reasonable when it comes to grading if you put in effort. People who struggle don't pay attention in class, listen to what he says and make small mental notes and you'll do great.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This is one of my favorite teachers in college! I was deeply attracted by his teaching style. There is no in person final exam.The semester paper is very simple, you just need to look at the reading material a little. It feels less like a paper and more like a discussion and response. All you have to do is write this assignment and get 4.0.

Not sure why his rating is so low on here :-( Paul is my favorite professor I've had at MSU! His interest in what he teaches makes his lectures easier to follow along with. He posts everything for the course at once so you can read ahead. We have essays every module instead of exams, and a lot of EC opportunities during & at the end of the semester
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--So many papers

reviews.comment: I don't recommend this IAH to anyone who is taking hard classes at the time. There is no real homework but the essays throughout the course are excessive and time-consuming. Lectures are very long and his comments to students sometimes are backhanded. You can tell he is very passionate about what he is teaching but not about his student's success.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Schauert was such a good professor and honesty one of my favorites. I am confused as to why he has so many bad reviews on here because he is so passionate about what he teaches & makes class interesting.I learned the most I have learned in this class over any class here at MSU. He is very understanding & is willing to give extensions.

One the first day of class he told us that if we missed any references in our weekly 1000-1500 word essays it would be an automatic zero with a chance to resubmit for a cap of 70%.Readings were around 8-50 pages long, lectures are boring.He goes on rants that aren't on the essay.Take this class if you really love the history, barely any music.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He was ok. I went into this class expecting to learn about music but it was more like a U.S. History class that mentioned music. I understand that you need historical context to explore the music, but the music part probably only took up like 10% of the lecture which was super disappointing. The final project was def overkill for a 200 lvl IAH
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Do not take this class. I have never reviewed a professor before but he's so bad, I felt compelled to warn anyone considering his class. It's beyond horrible.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment:
Honestly such an amazing professor. He is super passionate about what he teaches, and if you are into music and music history, you will really enjoy this class. 
There are weekly hw assignments, but they are very easy which he uses as attendance. There are also three big essays that take up most of your grade, but it isn't hard to do well on them.
Class is only graded on 5 Essays through the whole semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr Schauert is one of the best professors I've ever had. Super nice, enthusiastic about what he teaches, and genuinely cares about his students. You're graded by only a few things, but they're very straightforward
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of the best professors I've had! I was in his class during the tragic event in February 2023, he responded in the most professional yet compassionate way. He genuinely cares about all his students. Exams are just essays that you have the whole unit to work on with very clear grading criteria. Just be open and honest, he will work with you!
This is easily the worst professor I have had at this University, and I am a senior. Take this class with literally anyone else.

Assigns a lot of reading/writings to do. Even though I spend DAYS on my essay and answer my questions with a large paragraph or more and a citation, there is still never enough there to satisfy him, he always wants more answered even if he doesn't ask. Even if you misplace one word he will make a comment about it, we are all human seriously.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment:
Class twice a week with multiple chapters of reading, videos, and a long question for attendance. 
He has so much reading you need to do bi weekly. 

In class he said derogatory terms to say "we don't say these anymore" that were offensive to people in the class. Very uncomfortable & not handled with care.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I feel bad giving her a lower rating because she is very sweet and kind to all of her students. But her lectures are long, boring, and tough to understand at times due to her strong accent. The midterm was randomly thrown onto us two days before the due date, in which the wrong time was told. Subpar lectures and unclear instructions define this.

(I had Qing for 201, not 202, but it wasn't an option) 

Professor Qing is an exceptional, extremely caring professor. If you want to 4.0 the class, go to her office hours and do her honors option! She gives extra credit (unlike other JMC professors) and show your appreciation for her. Nevertheless, try your best and your work will be recognized.

Professor Qing is one of the sweetest and most understanding professors I have ever had, and she is very knowledgable on a variety of political science and IR topics. Her class is somewhat disorganized and instructions for essays and assignments can be somewhat unclear, but she is usually more than willing to answer any questions you have.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Love her so much. Super intelligent and understanding that life happens.

I appreciated that Professor Qing took the time to make sure we understood her lectures (she recognises that her accent is hard to understand). My biggest problem is that her guidelines for assignments are incredibly vague.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I love Dr. Qing! I really enjoyed her MC 201 course. She was very accessible outside of class and got to know us as people in addition to students. Her research is very interesting. She was a fair grader and attending office hours was really helpful. She is very sweet and I'm so glad to have developed a relationship with her. 100% recommend!
She's an amazing teacher, my favorite at James Madison College.

I LOVED Professor Qing! She is a little hard to understand sometimes but has no problem repeating herself for you if needed. She does not take attendance but I would obviously recommend attending class as much as you can. I enjoyed her so much for MC220 that I am taking MC318 with her this semester!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Qing is very bright. While she is hard to understand, she adds valuable insight into class discussions that you don't get from many professors. MC201 is heavily discussion based. The classes would consist of a student-led ppt, a class discussion, and then Qing would end it all up with how our discussion relates to the reading.

She is a really nice, and intelligent teacher who cares about you being able to understand her and the material. Easy grader. She doesn't post a rubric and you don't really know what she wants you to do in your writings. 70% of the grades happen at the end, so it gets stressful. Class schedule gets wonky, and she isn't good with online stuff.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Qing is one of the most knowledgeable professors I've had at MSU; Her profound understanding of the Sino-US partnership is rare. She possesses a plethora of experience reading, writing, and teaching about Chinese foreign policy and ways to pacify Sino-U.S. relations. She is both competent and caring!
Absolutely wonderful and brilliant professor, she knows a lot and always wants what's best for her students. I've taken 2 of her classes and I would take anything she teaches.

Prof Qing is a wonderful lady. She can be difficult to understand but she knows this. She recognizes the language barrier and will help you to understand. She laughs regarding her troubles with English at times. Not really a tough grader and pretty open with due dates. She is not a very good grader and does take some time to submit. 4/5.

I graduated in 2004.  Dr. Qing is the most compassionate, enthusiastic, fair, and honest professor I've had. I am now a teacher and her spirit in the classroom inspires and informs my work.  I tried to take as many of her classes as were offered and I studied abroad with her in Beijing.   Go to office hours. Be passionate and informed--you'll learn

Excellent Prof.  Encourages Debate and provides good guide lines for understanding the reading.  Accent is a small barrier.  Don't ever underestimate her, she is very smart, and always paying attention.

While Professor Qing's accent may make it hard for some students to learn, she really wants students to understand the material and engage in discussion.  She is more than willing to go over things during office hours and her enthusiasm for the subject shows.  If you study what's on her slides for the exams then you should do fine.

Generally nice-clearly intelligent;somewhat haphazard in teaching style; accent and English as a second language pose a barrier between her and the class. Grading either w/0 rhyme/reason or aiming to hit arbitrary 2.5 bell curve. Many assignments I received had 'excellent work' coupled w/ mediocre grades. Two words: Knuckle Sandwich.
good professor, hard to understand most of the time but very interesting and intelligent.
I agree...  not a good professor.  She basically just wants you to debate in class instead of going over the readings.  Then, by the time you get to the midterm or exam, you get screwed over.
Don't take this professor.  It will be the end of your GPA.
Very nice, and pretty easy, even though grading can be a bit inconsistant and hard to predict. Be careful, or she might give you a "knuckle sandwich!"
If you can take her for Russian, do! She's a great professor and is very generous. It's Russian, so you're going to have to work, but as long as you keep up you'll do fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Lessons cover a lot of the class prep activities relating to the shows watched in class this semester. Very flexible with submitting late work and she if she doesn't allow corrections on summary activities (tests), they're usually given a bit of a curve anyway. Я люблю Жанну :)

Shannon is very kind and understanding, very flexible with late work as well, which is good considering the heavy workload for Russian. She's also pretty funny. Love hearing about the babushka lore. Lessons follow the Mezdhu Nami workbook until it's finished, then they go onto prep for the spring semester of 200 level RUS

The professor is nice, but this class is so boring. You have online prep videos, reading assignments, and a reading quiz all twice a week. We had two creative writing assignments and a final. Stories are originally in Russian and translated, so they don't make much sense and are very long and boring. Take another class if you can
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Class was easy, just a lot of homework involved. Teacher is nice and have had no grading issues either.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Do not take this class if you can. There is lots of reading every week and the stories are boring. I took this class because of the easy schedule but wish I hadn't.

If you have any other options, do not take this class. There is pre class work for every class, a reading assignment with questions, a quiz, and an online prep. It sounds easy but it's very time consuming. She will give pre class work the same days we have projects or writing assignments due. The lectures are very boring and just all her opinions.
She assigns way too much homework! I would not take this class if you do not want the heavy and boring work load.  Class is mandatory and she is not understanding. Expects A LOT!! Just the worst.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: He has a class policy of requesting an extension by 10pm. Me and my friend both missed the deadline but just to try our luck, we both emailed him. My friend got an extension while I didn't. How do you explain that!
Worst lecturer. Too strong an accent, can't comprehend what he is saying. He comes to the class, talks to himself for 80mins and leaves.
Took the slides and homework from the the professor who taught the class in the previous semester, changed his name and that's it! He doesn't care if you understand. However, easy exams!

Take her online FI 311, everything is on D2L and her lectures are good. Material is difficult in some areas but many resources to help you through. She has an accent that she warns about at the start of the semester, I don't think its a deal breaker. Good prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Very nice professor. Took the time to explain everything and in a way it made sense. A homework quiz attached with every chapter lecture, but she reviews all the questions in the videos and you get unlimited attempts. The exams were similar to the quizzes, overall easy class and great professor!
took her for M&A class, she is amazing.

One of the most enjoyable classes. I went to the lectures sometimes just to cheer up. Whatever happened in the news/reality, she made relevant jokes and is super positive. I never know someone can make so many jokes about finance. I learn a lot about everything else in addition to finance.

I took her class as I heard there was not a textbook but ended up likeing finance a lot more than I can ever imagine. She gave a lot of flexibilities and really cared for her students to succeed. I am good with computer stuff so the class is a lot easier for me than my friends. Remember it's a finance class.

This class has lots of practice material and homework. Even after doing that, I still didn't feel comfortable at all on the 2 exams in the class. If you take it hybrid, showing up to her class is optional and you can take it like a normal online class. If you're fine with teaching yourself and finance is easy for you, take her. If not, don't

Her lectures are very interactive and she talks a lot about career development. Attendance not mandatory and no textbook. two exams 50% of your grade. the other half basically participation by finishing them.
I like her online setup. She is quite lenient but there are rules like the rest said. I abide by the rules and ace the class!!
GOAT. But I might be biased. I am actuarial science major :) She made everything super organized and accessible!

Took her fi311 freshman year. Her session was really small then and now it looks like all her sessions are overbooked :)

She required attendance for participation points but heard that this is changing. She is very caring and even helped me during my job search. Watch out, she has a dark sense of humor :)

I took her this summer as she no longer does classes for non business students. Manageable and she barely emailed so I am not sure what others refer to. Clear announcements. Engineering background so this class is much easier compared to core engineering classes.

She creates all materials herself and no textbook fee is just a bonus. This class is not my major, just for a minor in business for non business folks. Too bad that all good professors return to teach business cores :(
She cares a lot about academic integrity and fairness, which is why I like her so much. Just do my work and no need to worry that someone may get ahead because they ask for special treatment.
One of my worst professors
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: The homework was easy but never once did the quizzes we had show up in D2L on things due and maybe I missed it in the syllabus but I never saw anything about having to do quizzes each week so I missed half of them which resulted in my low grade. When I emailed her about it she told me that she is not allowing me to go back and complete them.
She is the rudest professor I have ever had. Her questions make no sense and she is honestly so conceited and full of herself. Do not take her.

2 homework assignments and a quiz due each week. She gives out the homework answers, but homework still takes time as its done on excel. Grades super easy; there was 2 assignments I did not fully complete and still got full credit. Her emails are weird and she can be hard to understand as english is not her first language.

I would personally be on the fence about taking a class with this professor again. The instructor is clear about what needs to be done, but if you need help or don't understand as well as you should, you have to figure it out.

Its an online async class, there are two assignments due each Sunday, both are very easy and are essentially participation grades. She explains concepts very thoroughly and goes through the homework with videos. There is a lot of material to rely on to complete assignments and the exam is open note.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The concepts she teaches are complex and difficult to understand. However, in the online section, she literally gives the homework answers out in her lecture videos before the assignment is even due so it's a free 100%. Every exam question is on Chegg, and if the numbers are different, the formulas are still there. Overall, was an easy class.

Her class has a lot of difficult concepts, but she gives a lot of examples in her lecture videos, so you can kind of work through it. The exams are pretty difficult as well, but are open note. She likes to send really long emails and just overall has a lot of rules, which no one has time to read.
So much leniency with assignments, especially in the online asynchronous section. Hard to not 4.0 this class

She wants her students to succeed, gives ample assignments for easy grades, curves exams by awarding bonus points, and seems to be very available to help if you need it. The exams are a bit tough though. My one complaint is that she emails frequently and the emails are written as though they are an in conversation between her and you.
Grasp of English language not good enough to teach classes. Very difficult to understand. Says things that make little to no sense.
Ok literally so bad. Everything is all over the place all the time. Sends emails like its a conversation between them and the student it is SO confusing.
Full of himself and arrogant.

Qiu's class is clearly organized and not very difficult. The material is complex and challenging, but she sets you up to succeed. Only one homework assignment and one study quiz per week for the most part, which are free points because she gives you unlimited attempts for the quizzes. Exams are very similar to quizzes. Good professor.

Pretty easy class, just be ready to spend a decent amount of time on it. Qiu is a pretty tough professor, but her exams are not very difficult. I'd say most people who did poorly likely don't learn very well in an online environment, so if that describes you, I'd avoid this course.

As someone who isn't a finance major this class was cake. While it was all online that is what made it an easy 4.0 you just do the assignment and quiz every week, literally just do the work and you will get a 4.0 .

I'm a psych major with NO finance experience and I am doing just fine in this class. You only have to avg 80% on the exams to 4.0 with the way it is set up and I've done minimal homework/studying. Also, keep in mind the prof is not a native English speaker, so I don't think she's trying to be passive aggressive on purpose like some said on here
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This professor seems very bitter. She is passive agressive and very petty in all announcements and emails. I have never been so unmotivated to do well in a class. Everyone in this class constantly complains about it. Extremely intense for a beginner finance coarse.

The professor seems like a nice person but she teaches like we already know the material. Most of us are not finance majors but she throws around a lot of technical terms which can be confusing. This class is definitely not the added stress I needed from an elective class in my senior year. Do not take this class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Her class was easy, the exams were a little tricky but she holds zoom meetings on Sunday if anyone has any questions which is helpful since its an online class. She will let you turn in late work for full credit. I ended up with a 3.5 I was 4 points away from a 4.0, don't be me. DO YOUR WORK AND THE EXTRA CREDIT. YOU WILL NOT REGRET IT.

If you take the class for credits or for a grade it will be frustrating. However, if you are willing to put in a little extra time to understand the deeper meaning of the formulas and processes, rather than just using the excel functions, it will come more naturally. And you will probably leave the class with a good grade. Exams are tough.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Not a native English speaker and difficult to understand.

A horrible class and a horrible professor. The class is only 100 points, 60 of which are from exams. If you think you really know the topic, you're wrong! You apparently don't! She loves to change the wording on questions ever so slightly making you think/feel like you already found the answers so read carefully--could literally be 2 words changed
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you think you can manage this class with outside studying, you're wrong. I bought textbooks, watched hours of youtube videos, went over the slides constantly, but her questions have nothing to do with any of them. Quite frankly its almost as if she spent less than 3 minutes constructing these questions on the quiz. AVOID THIS CLASS. I MEAN IT.

I can't stand this class. She does not teach effectively because she just gives all quiz and assignment answers out--you don't even need to try, literally just copy. I don't understand any of the material being taught and she does not treat this course as an introductory level. Little to NO communication from her ever it's frustrating.

this class was unbearably hard for no reason. The professor just doesnt teach well, lectures arent structured well and hard to follow. 2 exams make up your entire grade basically and are very difficult.
2 Exams make up 60% of your grade.

This class is the absolute worst. The exams are 60 percent of your grade, so if you do poorly, good luck! The concepts are not clear during the lectures and she keeps repeating "if you don't know it, that's okay,," but then doesn't ever explain it in another way that would help. She is a very nice professor, but the structure of the class sucks.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Im taking this class now and its supposed to be a beginners finance basically. Its taught like its meant for 5th year finance majors. Its really hard to understand how to do problems off a sexcel spreadsheet. It doen't work for me at all, we need to learn basics before we get into the heavy stuff. I would rather learn the easy way then to not learn

Gives us 2-3 lecture videos per week. One goes over notes the other ones go over the homework assignment. She basically shows you how to do it and gives you the answer. 2 big assignments for which she also gives you the answers. 2 exams that you definitely need to study for

Her teaching style is kind of like if Type to Learn was taught by a motivational speaker. If you're a masochist looking for an easy course that tests the boundaries of how much work someone can do without learning anything, then this "finance" course is the one for you.

Mingming was not a great professor but this class is easy af. 3 exams and attendance is only what you are graded on. Plus most questions in the exam are copied from her sample paper. If you are smart enough to understand basic finance, this class is the easiest 4.0 ever.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Let Professor Quan know if you use or plan to use AI, or your grade will be affected. The material was a bit outdated and relied heavily on articles and external sources, followed by multiple small quizzes, a discussion, and an exam. While reading-heavy, it was an easy A (exams are heavily based on the external sources and articles).
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: He is very cautious about plaigarism/ChatGPT so be careful. However the class itself was super easy. You just have to read quite a bit and write discussion posts and take some small quizzes. Thats really it though
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: As this class is an online class, I can't say much about the professor but more on the course. I really enjoyed this class and was really interested that I actually want this class to be made in person as well. The downside is that there are a lot of reading to do and wish that it could be summarized down a bit.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: I took ANP 429 and it was a very enjoyable and memorable experience. Class is friendly to beginner ethnographers and the professor is caring towards your progress. The readings are not overwhelming by any means and the class structures you well for the final project. Amazing professor with a lot of experience and knowledge in the field.

Prof Quan has interesting material however there is so much reading and additional articles that it was unrealistic for me to actually process the content even though I was genuinely interested and wanted to learn. If he cut out the excess requirements I would have had a much deeper understanding of everything we covered.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took ANP 200 in an online asynchronous format over the summer as an elective. I don't remember much from the course other than the materials were super easy and the class required minimal work. If you do the assignments you will have no issues in this course.

Mr. Steinberg is an all-around great professor. He teaches tough topics with respect and passion. He truly cares about everyone and wants you to succeed, so take all the extra credit opportunities he gives you. Reading the textbook DEF helps, and being engaged in recitation is just locking it all in.

This is my second class with Dr. Steinberg and I would defend this man with my dying breaths. He's caring with his students, and makes his lectures interesting even if you aren't a history person. His rubrics are straight-forward, so as long as you do what's asked you'll do perfectly fine. Couple random quizzes but he warns you ahead of time.

the goat. Funny af, very knowledgable, interesting lectures, easy assignments, pretty absorbable content. Just make sure you go to recitations as that's where he holds the quizzes (which are super easy if you pay attention). Def going to take as many of his classes as I can.

he's a great guy and his lectures are always entertaining. make sure you attend the recitations, those are mandatory. only assignments were readings, the mid-term and final essays, and very short and easy  multiple choice quizzes on fridays. as long as you pay attention to the lectures and take notes then you're all set!
Great professor and even better person. He had engaging lectures and was extremely knowledgeable on the course content. Assignments were straightforward and related to lecture content.
Professor Steinberg was an amazing professor. He offered extra credit opportunities, made his lectures super interesting, and was not a harsh grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Great and passionate professor. As long as you go to the lectures and do your assignments you'll be golden. Generous with extra credit. Hilarious, I always referred to him as "if Michael Scott was a professor"! Entertaining, good at answering emails, and cares about his students. Wants us all to succeed!

Prof Steinberg is my lord and savior. Funny guy. Certified history hater but he made it my favorite class of the semester. We learned about genocide but he taught it in a way were we understood the gravity and importance of the events without being insensitive. There are a few easy quizzes and bigger essays but 100/10 class hes amazing

Dare I say it, the GOAT. He's super forgiving and willing to give out extra credit or make-up opportunities to students who need them. He's also legitimately hilarious and makes a super delicate and tragic topic somewhat light-hearted and approachable. I cannot recommend him enough, easily one of the best professors here at MSU.

He is so funny and so chill. His class was very interesting so I enjoyed going to lectures even thought you didn't really have to. We were graded on 3 at-home essays and 6 quizzes in recitation. Super easy class to take as an ISS. He is so sweet, I'm going to miss having a class with him!

Gosh he's great, just take the class. His TA's are amazing, he's hilarious. Lecture can get long bc he talks a lot but like, just take notes and chill. The topic is heavy but he is able to lighten the mood. I'd take any of his classes again. Genuinely going to miss this class.

There's a reason Dr. Steinberg has such high ratings. Not only is he able to take such a devastating subject and crack a few jokes, but he's so passionate and wants you to understand and take to heart what he's teaching. It's easy to pass if you show up to lecture and follow his rubrics, but I can not stress it enough to take one of his classes.

Professor Steinberg is one of the best history professors. His lectures were funny and easy to follow along with. He also posted his lectures after class to catch up on if needed. The grades were based on one midterm, one final, and attendance/participation; however, he gave a study guide and clear guidelines on what he wanted to see.

Easy class, course material is heavy but it's very digestible if you go to lectures. Steinberg is funny too, so I ended up looking forward to morning lectures. Recitation attendance is mandatory and workload isn't heavy aside from the readings. Extra credit was offered for the first few weeks to pad your grade or make up for quizzes. Silly guy.

Ronen is an amazing professor! Although the course material is not the most interesting, he made it very entertaining with his side comments and funny jokes! He is such a sweet guy and offers several extra credit opportunities. I wish I could take another class with this professor. The class only has 100 points, but its very easy if you listen.

The class is super easy, and the lectures aren't required but help a lot and aren't dreadful, he tries to make them entertaining. But recitation attendance is mandatory and worth like 15% of your grade. No homework really and only like 4 quizzes

Ronen was such an amazing professor. He had a way of making heavy course topics seem "light" with major emphasis on how each impacted history. He also a great sense of humor. Only gave 5 short quizzes, 2 exams, and 3 essays over the whole semester.

Dr. Steinberg is a fantastic professor, he's funny, kind, and very genuine overall. Our seminar was not in his field of expertise persay, and he was very transparent about it. You read papers, present one with a group, and then focus on writing a research paper. Overall easy, good vibes and plenty of sunshine.

Lectures were easy to follow, and prof answered all questions asked. Always cracking jokes and being unintentionally funny. Lecture wasn't required but recitation was, but my TA excused me when I was sick. Two papers, one midsemester and one for the final. Readings almost every week, but most were provided by prof on D2L.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Steinberg is an amazing professor and probably my favorite at MSU. Took him in the fall and I genuinely looked forward to each class meeting and was sad when the semester ended. Super kind, funny, and caring man. Take him if you can!!
Absolutely AMAZING prof. I had Ronen in the fall and he was super personable, funny, and caring! If you have the chance to take a class with him DO IT 100%!!
Professor Steinberg was amazing. I looked forward to his lectures every day and I never missed a class.

Had him for hybrid course, hilarious dude. Can't say I loved the class topic but he makes it bearable. Super easy, basically lectures/reading and the occasional paper/quiz. Attendance wasn't mandatory and he offers EC
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Hilarious--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The reviews speak for themselves. I haven't seen many professors with 30 five-star ratings on here. Truly an amazing professor, he is enlightened in his understanding of higher education. He does not penalize you for not understanding. He understands that a degree is necessary for us to succeed, and he wants to make sure everybody does well.

This man is so unintentionally funny! I was laughing every lecture. Very understanding of students getting bored with the material so he made sure to give breaks during lecture. A ton of reading but the TA's did a good job of covering said readings. The class was only out of 100 points so only a few points could be missed to keep an A.

One of the best professors I've had at MSU. He really cares about what he teaches and makes it interesting by tying in personal experiences, etc. Bi-weekly 10 minute quizzes but you get the prep materials beforehand. If you can take a class with him, do it! One midterm essay, one final paper that's it. Very easy yet interesting class, easy grading.
Funniest prof ever and easy class!

Dr Steinberg made it wonderful our class was unexpectedly online via zoom which sucked, but he did his best to not only make this class interactive as well as always have a good story to tell. He was very relatable. It was very clear from him and the TAs that they want you to succeed. The class itself is very heavy with reading content.
be ware
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--So many papers--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He is super easy going and he's a funny guy! There were a few books we needed to read but they were cheap and some he provided on d2l. He kept it interesting. Easy to pass if you pay attention and read for the most part.  Would love if he taught more courses.

Super interesting lectures and entertaining! Class is lecture heavy but not difficult though I do recommend taking notes. Really cares about the information and students. Would 100% take again and recommend taking, the best professor I've had at MSU!

Literally one of my favorite instructors I have ever had! He changes his content for the students and gives extended deadlines for students. Very accommodating. I would look forward to going to his lectures. I want to find other classes with him, even if they don't count towards my major.
reviews.rating_tags: Hilarious--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: he was really nice and would change stuff if the students had difficulty.  funny guy too!

He was a really good teacher! The lectures were very enjoyable and contained lots of information and covered EVERYTHING that were on tests and quizzes. The class work is definitely manageable as you only have to deal with quizzes and essays, would definitely take this class again!

He was an amazing professor. He honestly made class enjoyable and something to look forward to. You can tell he is very passionate about history. I went to every class in the start of the semester but I got lazier as it got colder and stopped and I still managed to 4.0 Your grade is based on a couple of small online quizzes and 4 essays. super easy

Ronen was a very great professor to have. His class was a very chill, yet intellectual environment. You could tell he knew the subject matter very well and is genuinely incredibly smart. All of the assignments in this class are essays and short quizzes which if you attend lectures and put a tiny bit of effort in you'll do well.

Ronen was an absolutely amazing professor. Lectures are interesting and he really tried to make the material interesting. The grading in the course is very easy-going. Show up to class and you're guaranteed an A!

For the people saying you have to do lots of reading and if you skip pass you wont pass are completely wrong. The professor creates a study guide and has his lecture notes at the ready for you when you take the 5-10 question quizzes. There are 4 essays in his class and they are all under 2 pages long. I would 100% take this professor again!!!
ronen is gr8, today was our last lecture which was #sad. but this class is very easy and ronen is very engaging as well as hilarious. I've never been bored in this class.

Prof Steinberg is a great lecturer, for our course we had about 8 quizzes and 4 essays that made up your grade, if you chose not to go to the lectures the quizzes were pretty tough but if you have good attendance then youll pass this class easily.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: one of the best teachers I have ever had
The professor is very easy going and you have to try to fail
Amazing prof., so genuine and made learning online feel actually normal. So accommodating and helpful with anything!!

I took ISS210 in Fall 2020, it was completely online and the class was asynchronous. As a freshmen he was definitely one of my favorite professors. With a few lectures to watch weekly, + short discussion posts & about 3 essays over the semester. If you watch the content you can do well in class. His lectures were fun to watch, he's funny & sweet

Great class. As a freshman I thoroughly enjoyed this class. Lectures are a bit long, but the material you learn about is very interesting. TAs were great, and prof was hilarious. Some reading, but not too overwhelming. Very fair.

There is a lot of content provided in this class but Steinberg makes it really easy to get through. You do have to read the textbook and this semester we had to read a 200+ page book and write an essay on it while citing it so you have to read it. Quizzes can be hard but not bad. If you want a 4.0 in the class, you have to really put in the effort.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had Professor Steinberg for ISS 210 and I really enjoyed his class. He is very funny and I think he makes learning the content fun. The work load is not too bad. There are quite a few lectures though. Overall I enjoyed his class!

Professor Steinberg is, so far, my favorite professor at MSU. His lectures are never dry and he manages to make them relatively upbeat given the subject matter. There are only 2 "papers" at the beginning of the semester but they're only about 3 pages total between them. I highly recommend Professor Steinberg.

He was an amazing professor and always made me excited to come to class. I would definitely recommend finding a course that he teaches because you will not leave uninformed. He makes the class very interesting and is hilarious, too!

Ronen was a real gem. He was always able to make large lecture halls feel smaller. He'd crack jokes but still always get the information across. Amazing class, that you won't leave bored or feeling as if you wasted your money.
Really good perspective on things in history. Very knowledgeable and has a good delivery.

I loved coming to Ronen's classes. He is so funny and his lectures are informative. Everything you need to know on quizzes are said in lectures. I was never tricked on any test or quiz. Make sure you show up to recitation, where you go over readings and quizzes. Very helpful!

Ronen is the sweetest professor I have had so far at MSU. His unique sense of humor makes for a wonderful learning environment that keeps the student engaged in the classwork. He makes me want to be a better person. I would highly recommend taking a class with Ronen if you have the opportunity!

Ronen is the coolest dude ever. He is so funny, and also quite attractive as well. His lectures are very interesting as well as his accent. He has an accent, but his English is flawless and he's not hard to understand. His accent just adds to his hotness factor. Totally loved the guy and would take his class again.

Really great class and a really great, and engaging professor. Course material is a combination of reading primary sources and lecture material. Does not post the lecture slides online, but they don't have much text anyway. He likes to hear students' opinions and interpretations of the history. Assigns really interesting books to read.

Great professor and hilarious. English isn't his first language so its pretty funny when he doesn't get American puns/jokes. He is unusually knowledgeable about pop culture and wants students to enjoy learning about history.  Participation is a big deal for him but he reads the flow and attitude of the class so he won't press students to talk.

Makes history interesting which is saying a lot from a girl who hates history. Super funny. Lectures are kind of long and it can get easy to stop paying attention, but if you do pay attention, he doesn't do half bad at lecturing. Liked him a lot & would take him again if necessary.  Also, looks a little bit like Ted from How I Met Your Mother.

Knowledgable. Funny. Makes the material interesting, for those with a desire to learn the material or not. Uses great sources for media. Great sense of humor. WIlling to work with the student. Flexible. Doesn't assign a lot of homework, but enough to understand the course's outlines. The best history teacher I've ever had. He is phenomenal!

Rick is the greatest college professor I ever had the pleasure of meeting. He is just an absolute wealth of knowledge- not just about the industry but about so many things. He's someone who you want to learn absolutely everything you can from. One of the greatest resources not just in the MSU film program, but at MSU as a whole.

Once a week during class, you will watch a film. The discussions and analysis of the films is very interesting, but you do need to go to class, because there are weekly essays, along with the fact that attendance is graded harshly the more you miss. The professor is very nice and interesting.
I love movies, he made me love them more.
Professor Rick, goes above and beyond for both his students and lectures. I learned so much in this class, not only about film, but life in general. One of the best professors I've ever had.

The most insane class I have taken at MSU. Easy class, you watch a movie once a week and have a couple of one-pagers about the readings, but Prof. Blackwood is the highlight of the class. Not only are his lectures unhinged but he shares lots of stories from his past. While attendance is mandatory, you always want to show up to see what he says.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Take this professor. I was legit excited to go to every one of his classes. He is very smart and knows what he's talking about, and he really for his students to learn.

Professor Blackwood was the most interesting professor I have had yet. Yes, the class was long, but we mainly watched movies and heard the professor's stories and insights on life.  I felt this class was an amazing opportunity to reflect on my life and goals. Homework was easy and engaging.

If you are looking for someone that will give you something meaningful for your time this is the man. Professor Blackwood, and those who work within his classes, really care about the material, and greatly encourage curiosity. While attendance is mandatory you're not wasting your time going. Read the material it seriously teaching good life lessons

Rick is an amazing professor who really cares about what and how we learned. Yes, class was early, but you were watching amazing movies all the time. Don't miss class because you'll miss out on great lectures and analyses that Rick provides throughout class. Would take it again just to have Rick as a professor again.

He was one of the most inspiring professors I've had at MSU. I found his IAH course deeply engaging and meaningful. He made complex topics feel approachable, encouraged open discussion, and always connected the material to real-world relevance. His passion for teaching and genuine care for students really stood out.

Prof Blackwood is very interesting, and the class itself isn't stressful at all despite all the content. Weekly readings, and biweekly one page papers that are graded on whether or not you did the reading. Lectures were long and sometimes droned on, but usually his extensive knowledge is fascinating enough to keep you awake and paying attention.

I enjoyed having Professor Blackwood, he has a lot of real-world experience to share from his days working in Hollywood. Lectures are very long, but they contain lots of wisdom, and I feel like I learned a lot from his class.

I enjoyed my time in this class. It was very interesting and we watched movies every Wednesday. We only had 2 papers and 3 one-pagers which were really only participation. The papers aren't even graded that harshly. Come to class every day and he will literally cancel your final and midterm. Wish he taught other classes so I could take them.

Showing up to these lectures was always the highlight of my week. He really cares about expanding your perspective and making you smarter. Movies in IAH 241G were triggering so be cautious of that. We had two papers and three one pagers which were easy if you did the readings and watched movies outside of class.
Professor Blackwood is an amazing professor. He really cares about his students and you will learn a lot.

Professor Blackwood is an interesting guy lots of life experiences. Fair grader and only assigned 2 longer-papers and a 3 short 1-2 page papers. Rarely gave quizzes and midterms as long as everyone showed up to class and was engaged in the material.Easy to get along with and enjoys what he's doing. The class was very worth it. Make sure to show up!
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Absolutely amazing class! He gives such good lectures you forget you're in class. He shows you movies and explains how they created them. He has amazing experiences in the real world, that he is more than willing to share with you! Highly recommend, you will not be disappointed with this class at all. I learned so much from this class!
Loved the class, great coursework, readings, and films. Enjoyed just about every lecture. Great class even greater professor.

This class was the one I looked forward to every week: Prof. Blackwood's stories and lectures were engaging and interesting, and I left each one feeling like I learned something. His screenwriting and filmmaking industry experience offered invaluable context to the class content. Class was discussion based and there was ample time to ask questions.

For a class that has 75% of the final grade dedicated to your exam performance, you'd think there would be more material to actually practice for it.
The professor himself is nice, but asks WAY too much of his students.
(Depending on your major or minor, you could substitute something like STT 200 [much easier] for this class - ask your advisor.)
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Ron is a great guy but probably the worst teacher I have ever had. You could know the material and study a lot and still not know the answers to the exams. In a class where exams are 75% of the grade he as a teacher seems to take pride in making his exams hard and his questions confusingly worded with multiple correct answers. Take someone else!

Ron is a great guy. He's funny, he's engaging in his lectures, and is clearly passionate about his teaching career. However, his exam's are near impossible. The answers are so incredibly similar that it will make your head spin, yes physically spin. The exams are worth 75% of your grade so each one feels like stepping onto a battlefield. Good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Tamborini is passionate about the subject, but the class was tough. Exams are 75% of your grade and mostly (points-wise) short-answer, making them difficult. Weekly case studies are meticulously graded and don't help much. Lectures are hard to follow and don't clearly connect to assignments.

Tamborini is one of the sweetest and most caring professors I've ever had. He is knowledgable and passionate about mass communication and his lectures are packed but entertaining. He does talk a lot but the content is interesting and makes it better. LOVES his wife, HATES UMich, and always cracks dad jokes with us.

My fav professor by FAR. sweetest man you will ever meet and an amazing, clear, professor. I have 0 clue why he has any bad reviews. the class has 0 hw, and the exams are not that hard as long as you study a quizlet and look at the study guide. entertaining lectures and does not take attendance, yet somehow everyone shows up. LOVES his wife,10/10.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is great as a person, but when it comes to the class itself be careful. Attendance isnt necessary but there are 5 pop up papers at the end of the class scattered through the semester that count as a grade. The exams are a lot of information. If you need to take this class and its your only option, do it, but if you have other options, do that.

Worst professor I have ever had. So lecture heavy he doenst post the slides which are from the 1900s btw talks like an ice age character and goes through them so quick without enough time to write everything down. No homework basically your entire grade is based off of exams with the exception of the random in class participation papers. stay away.

Professor Tamborini is a very nice guy, his lectures are boring and his class is super exam heavy. 80% of your grade is how you do on the exams and the exams are difficult, he references back to lectures on your test so attend the lectures. Also BEWARE of pop quiz writing exerts at the end of classes to count towards your attendance grade.

He is very nice but he does give out random in class papers throughout the semester and he will have you do his research projects as apart of your grade but there options to get out of it. Also attendance isn't mandatory but the lectures aren't posted at a later time

Hands down one of the most caring professors at MSU. Attendance is taken through random small writing assignments. His slides are not on D2L so attending lecture is important. He cares about his students and teaches exactly what is on the exam. Textbook is (not) required but the lectures are from the book. Would totally have a class with him again!

Tamborini is cool. Simple lectures that are helpful (he tells you what will be on the exam). Just take notes and you'll be set. This course does require in-person research participation done outside of class on 5 separate occasions which is a little annoying but it is very easy, and worth a large chunk of your grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Tamborini is really pleasant, and his lectures were very clear and didn't have any useless information. The assignments were really reasonable, and the exams were really doable if you attended lectures and took good notes. I would absolutely take more classes from him.

I loved Dr. Tamborini! He's great at lecturing I never read the book except for skimming through it while studying for the exams. If you show up to the lectures and take notes, you'll be fine. I'd suggest making friends in the class to get notes from if you have to miss. Study for exams and you'll be fine. Sign up for research ASAP they're easy.

you HAVE to attend class. attendance isnt graded, but you will find everything very difficult if you skip. the three exams count for 75% of the grade. grading was very lenient. there is a stats section in the final third of the class, beware. had to teach myself all of that.

Tamborini is quite the professor. The class itself is complex and the way he teaches it doesn't really help. He tries his best though. The case studies and individual research assignments are graded very harshly and you have to participate in research studies. He is an interesting guy but the course was just straight up confusing.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very good professor but you need to show up to class, he doesn't post the slides and he gives surprise papers that's worth a good part of the grade. also required to participate in research.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This professor gave very few things for a grade and the majority of your grades were exams. He gives no extra credit opportunity and does not curve or round at all. The textbook is definitely used as well as online readings.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: In this course there is very few things that count for a grade. The main thing is the three exams you must take in person. He talks extremely fast and does not offer PowerPoints so you have to come to class to take notes but it is hard to take notes when the class moves that fast.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: 75% of this class was 3 exams. Very hard to listen in class when he rushes through loads of slides that he does not post online. Cannot miss class because you will miss a lot and he gives surprise end of class assignments from time to time. Exams also have a lot of information that you have to study. Good guy, not my fav class.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Ron's class wasn't too difficult, but could've been a lot easier. He doesn't post his slideshows, so you spend most class sessions writing down everything as quickly as you can, which really stood in the way of actually listening to him. You can tell he loves what he teaches as he is incredibly informational.

Professor Tamborini is a fun, great guy. However, I found it difficult to do good in this class because 1) If you miss one lecture, there is no way to access notes unless you know someone that takes notes 2) Your grade is pretty much only based on closed note exams 3) It's hard to fulfill the research requirement b/c if you miss one, you're SOL
His class was great!! Easy enough and he was engaging which helped a lot.
You need to attend this class or else you will not do well. If you just go to the lectures and it won't be hard at all
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I personally struggled a good bit in this class. The weekly case studies consumed a great bit of my time, but I learned next to nothing from them.  Also, be prepared for 3/4 of your grade to come from 3 large exams.  I wouldn't recommend this class unless it is a requirement for your degree.  It is one of the hardest classes I've taken at MSU.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Make sure to go to lectures, that is how he takes attendance
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Was such a good professor, cared about student success and was sarcastic and funny to lighten to heavy lectures. It was a really lecture heavy class, but he did a great job covering the content and giving the information needed to be successful on tests. Participation is necessary because there are random writing assignments.
Take notes, and attend class, do the research and short paper. Make the effort to go to class, and you will succeed.

Very detailed in his lectures.  Explains the why's behind the formulas which helped me learn efficiently.  This is a study-intensive course.  I took it in the summer so it was condensed to a six-week session.  If you really want to learn this curriculum, I highly recommend Professor Tamborini for this course!  5 STAR!

Tony Mandarich

Terrible class and teachers. You are given #'s of lectures with an insane amount of content to memorize for THREE EXAMS for the whole semester and small assignments that do nothing to your grade. No open notes and Tamborini does not care about student success. The TA doesn't help either so the whole class is a joke. DO NOT TAKE THIS IF YOU CAN.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I am a comm major, meaning I have to do well in this class. The professor truly cares about his lectures and you can tell he has a great interest in the subject, but the exams will kill you. The only points are 3 major exams and some other stuff, but mainly exams. They are all "As discussed in class..." questions - very hard to remember every word
reviews.rating_tags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I do not recommend taking this class. He does not care about his students and does not want to see you succeed. Lots of lecture videos and 3 big exams. Be careful. Avoid this class and professor if you can.

The way this class was structured was not fun. The lectures aren't super easy to follow, the TA's are really the only reason I ever understood anything at all in this class. The professor seemed nice enough although it was an asynchronous class. If you don't put a lot of effort into this class, you'll probably fail or barely pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: While this class is lecture heavy, Professor Tamborini puts a lot of effort into this course and his lectures. He is organized and someone I would recommend. You do have to try in this class to receive a good grade, but if you put the effort in, you'll do good. Overall, I like him as a professor!

Professor Tamborini is a great teacher. You can tell he truly cares about his students and wants them to succeed. He was very accommodating during COVID. He gives great examples in his lectures, though the topics are a bit boring at times. If you take good notes you will get an A no problem.

All of the information in the lectures is important to the exam. Tamborini tries to be fun and engaging, lots of dad humor is involved. Exams are hard if you don't study, sometimes can be helpful to read the book if you want an additional review. If you study your notes and make flashcards you should be okay.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: COM 275 with Dr. Tamborini was fine. I didn't really enjoy the class, but I'm not a COM major. I don't think I learned much and I didn't do great in the class, but Dr. Tamborini is pretty nice. Your grade is pretty much only dependent on exams, plus a few points for participating In research and showing up for class.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Tamborini is a great lecturer. He keeps you entertained and if you pay attention, all the answers are right in front of you. If you don't show up to class you will not pass. He does not post his lectures or powerpoints so you need to be there. Research participation is a big part of your grade. Show up, pay attention, pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Tamborini is a good professor. His lectures are interesting and the course load is reasonable.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: To fully understand the course you really need to pay attention in lectures. Seeking help from Ron helped substantially on exams and reflected on my grade. The workload is hefty and to succeed on the exams Id recommend studying at least a week ahead. Rons a good professor but just not for this course.

I honestly had no beef with Ron, he's an alright guy and his lectures were fine. The only thing that I didn't like was the fact that he purposefully made his exams really difficult and that each of the 3 were worth 25%, which is 75% of your grade. I did awesome on all the homework assignments, but that hard work is not reflected in my final grade.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Take Bergen's class instead even if you have to rework your whole schedule.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Ron was a great instructor. Puts real life examples into lectures. If you don't put forth effort to study, you will likely struggle. Exams get easier throughout the course. (Material becomes more interesting)
Tamborini was a really good professor! He gave interesting lectures and I never had a problem paying attention. He's very straight-forward and his lectures are definitely worth knowing and relevant.
He can be entertaining, but is a tough grader, and it's only on exams and reaction papers that are random, so you better hope you're there on those 5 days.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Gives really interesting lectures, and is always engaged with the class. A really happy guy with a good outlook on life. Easy to listen to, and enjoys what he is doing. I never minded showing up to his class, since most of his lectures were worth the listen.

Possibly one of my favorite professors! Lectures are always interesting. He makes the test very easy to study for. Funny, kind, and all around great professor!!! Makes the subject fun and interesting! There are pop writing assignments, but if you always go to class you'll be fine. The research activities were very fun too.

He kinda drags on in his lectures. He doesn't post his lectures online so you have to go to class. If you only write down the yellow stuff, your golden. I would just listen to my music and write down the notes in class. Tests are easy if you study.

I don't know why he's not rated higher. His classes are fun and he's enthusiastic and full of knowledge about the subject. His lectures are organized and put together well. The tests are extremely easy if you take notes, study with flash cards and memorize a couple of mnemonic devices. I never opened the book and I dstill got an excellent grade.

This class is hard, but if you show up to every class and study it is possible to get a 4.0. I really liked Dr. Tamborini, he is funny and laid back. You have to do case studies every week but they really help you understand the concepts and help you prepare for the tests.

Ron tries really hard to be funny. He's a super nice guy and you have to appreciate the effort he puts into his lectures. You can tell he really wants you to understand, but the class is hard and the material isn't easy. Must attend lectures and study if you want to pass. READ THE BOOK

Ron tries really hard to be funny, and you've got to appreciate his efforts. As a prof I think he's good, it's just the course that's hard. Be prepared to take tons of notes, and a highlighter doesn't go amiss either.
Great professor. Very sarcastically funny and helpful when asking questions. I absolutely loved COM 275 with him!

Very challenging class. Tamborini seems like a pretty cool dude, but a terrible professor. Lots of pointless case studies and reading. Half the time he would teach the class a concept, and then tell us he taught the wrong thing. You MUST attend class in order to get a passing grade. Only good part is that there are a few hottie TAs.

Aweful! All you do is write notes off the PowerPoint screen.  Completely pointless when he could easily posts these notes online like most normal instructors.  Nearly impossible to get an A and no fun class activities, media examples (videos, etc.); we don't even get to watch a single movie in this class. Tries to trick you on his test!
Ron is a very good professor, but if you do not bother to do any work and don't study then you will have a hard time in his classes.
I spent more time writing notes so fast and furious that it was difficult to comprehend his lectures. Very challenging prof!
Great prof.  Clearly got all information across with good examples of things that relate to your lives as college kids.
Overall, he is organized and detailed. The class has a lot of notes, but most of the information is in the book. He can get off topic some, but not nearly as much as other profs. Good class.
Woah, is dr. t ever hot.  He's simply a spectacular specimen of a MAN.  Hard to focus on the course material when you've got dr. hotbottom up there shakin' his thang.
Dr. T is hottttt.  Oh, and he's smart too.  Do your work and study and you'll be fine.

Avoid this professor at all costs. Stay an extra semester at State if you have to... it will be time well spent. All my friends who took 200 with him failed. He's boring and his exams aren't reflective of your knowledge.
No Comments
interesting and pretty funny.  exams are a little tricky but you will do fine if you study!
VERY tough exams - entirely true/false.  However, he's entertaining and the class is informative.  He gives extra credit if you tell a joke at the beginning of class!
This teacher wants you to fail!! Beware!!

Extremely sweet professor and cares about all students who step into her classroom. She provides structured, thought-out feedback and provides interactive lectures that teaches you lessons about everyday life. As an "unmotivated" student, I'd always look forwards to class. If I could retake any class at MSU, it 100% would be this one.

Her lectures are really fun and easy to relate with in real life scenarios and she also addresses present day issues. Grading isn't too strict but will give useful comments to improve on, a lot of group work. If she sees potential, she helps give resources and references how to succeed and do more outside of class. Took the class for fun, worth it!
Really nice to talk to, helps students a lot and if you have issues with grades she loves helping students with where they can earn more points. Also very funny and lectures are fun and interactive

Dr Taylor is very passionate about her class, and she's very caring of all her students. Some of the assignments are a little annoying but she tries to make them fun. She's one of the sweetest people ever, and I would recommend going to her office hours. The only 2 big gradings in the class is the midterm (notecard allowed) and group project.
Sweetest professor ever.

She is very kind and caring and actually cares about her students. Attendance is strict and lectures can be boring but she jumps around a lot which makes it engaging. I wasn't a fan of the topic but she quickly made it my favorite course. Do the in class activities and as long as your group for the final is decent it's easy.

Dr. Taylor is a kind and caring professor. She would always say in class "this is not a building block course and we are going to be jumping around" which is 100% true. There usually always an in-class assignment so make sure to pay attention to the lectures.

Professor Taylor was an average professor. She had some boring lectures/ annoying homework assignments, but was fair in her grading. Group project/ midterm exam make up the bulk of the grade. Final group project was annoying to complete.

She is so nice. I have a 98% in this class its very easy. Just do the work and you're good. She offers extra credit and grades work super fast. I emailed her about an issue and she responded within minutes and fixed the issue. I love her, she's so sweet I'd do anything for her. Please take this class and show her love.

Literally one of the kindest professors I've ever had. Always receiving and listening to feedback, knows all her students and you can tell she loves her job. Weekly homework but it's never difficult and no exam, just a group presentation.

Dr. Taylor is just an alright professor. She explains things very clearly but she can sometimes talk to you like a baby, and other times talk too fast. The homework is pretty easy as long as you pay attention, and the midterm exam wasn't the worst. A lot of guest speakers and their content will be on the exam. No readings though which is good.

She is such a sweet teacher; you can tell she loves her job and teaching this subject. I really like her classes and hearing her talk. She takes your answers from the homework and incorporates them into the next lecture. She's very open to feedback and will adjust her class and teaching format to keep her students happy. I enjoy her class.

Dr. Taylor is my pride and joy. Had her for 202 and 208L, amazing experience in both would take a million times over. So kind, helpful, understanding, funny, smart, everything you want in a professor. learn to use excel beforehand and the class is much easier. If you have the chance to take her, do it! Praise Dr. Taylor, she deserves the world.

Although she overcomplicates the class a bit and is very nit-picky eith grading, she is incredibly nice and understanding. I didn't mind the group work, but having to show up every day or getting a 0 for the in class activities kinda suck. One midterm with cheat sheets, weekly homework and a final presentation/paper. Interesting class

Dr. Taylor is the best professor I've ever had. She truly cares about her students and will help you with anything you need. There's a regular amount of work but it's not too difficult just can be time consuming. Just show up to class and participate.
It is unbelievable how hard she tries to make this class way harder than it needs to be.

The class was setup in groups and almost everything is done with your group. The final is a semester long project which you present and work on in groups it's a good chunk of the grade. There is a textbook but she provides it. There's only one test and it's the midterm which if you don't do well can drop your grade a decent amount.

I so grateful for how this course is set up. you never feel like your wastin your time bc the lectures, readings, hw, quizzes, exam are all related and you feel prepared. she also offers a cheatsheet and it counts towards your hw grade which is a free grade !
Dr. Taylor is an incredibly supportive professor. She provides valuable insights during group projects, ensuring that we meet all the requirements and achieve the grades we desire.

Dr. Taylor makes every class activity feel engaging and exciting. Whether it's a workshop or lecture, she always incorporates interactive elements that keep us engaged while making the learning experience enjoyable and productive.

I really appreciate how Dr. Taylor encourages us during group projects. She's always available for questions, offers constructive feedback, and ensures we stay on track to meet the expectations for the project.

Working on group projects with Dr. Taylor is such a positive experience. She encourages collaboration and offers helpful suggestions to ensure we all contribute and meet the project's goals. Her support makes a huge difference in the outcome.

Dr. Taylor is incredibly supportive during group projects. She goes above and beyond to ensure that we meet the expectations and achieve the grades we want through her constructive feedback. Her guidance helps us stay on track, and she's always available to help when needed.

I really appreciate how Professor Taylor creates a positive learning environment. She always encourages her students to ask questions and gives thoughtful feedback that helps us grow and do well in her course
I love how organized and well-prepared Professor Taylor is for each class. She makes every topic easy to follow and provides helpful examples from real life that deepen my understanding.
I'm so grateful to have had Professor Taylor as an instructor. She is insightful, approachable, and truly cares about her students' growth and success. I always feel supported in her class.

She is a knowledgeable and approachable professor who creates a welcoming environment for all students. Professor taylor provides clear explanations and is always happy to offer assistance when needed during her office hours or after class

Professor taylor is one of the best professors I've had. She genuinely cares about her students' success and goes out of her way to make sure we understand the material by offering workshops and in class activities. Her feedback is always helpful and encouraging.

I have learned so much in Professor Taylor's class. She is patient, approachable, and has an amazing ability to break down difficult material. Her passion for teaching really shines through because of how well organized and constructed the course is. You can tell so much work is put into it to ensure her student's success

Professor taylor is a fantastic instructor who makes every class engaging and enjoyable. She explains complex topics in a way that is easy to understand, and she's always there to help when you need it.

Professor taylor consistently goes above and beyond to create an engaging and informative learning experience. Their enthusiasm for the subject is contagious, and their clear explanations help students feel successful and confident in their work.

professor taylor is both knowledgeable and approachable, making it easy to engage with the content. They offer constructive feedback and make sure every student has the support they need. I feel more confident in my academic abilities after taking this class.

I feel incredibly fortunate to have had Professor taylor as an instructor. They create a classroom atmosphere that encourages collaboration and critical thinking. They always make time for students and offer valuable insights into the material as well as clear up any confusion on any topic.

Professor taylor is an excellent communicator who truly knows how to make learning enjoyable. Their passion for the subject and dedication to students shines through in every lesson. I've gained confidence and a deeper appreciation for the topic.

I am so grateful for Professor taylor's teaching. They are approachable, patient, and always willing to help. I have gained a deeper understanding of the subject thanks to their clear and effective teaching methods.

Professor taylor brings enthusiasm and energy to every lecture. They explain complicated topics in a way that is both clear and engaging. I look forward to their class each week as well as the interesting in class activities that we do

What stands out about Professor Taylor is their genuine care for their students' success. They go above and beyond to ensure we grasp the material and are always willing to answer questions. I feel encouraged and supported throughout the course which i rarely find that with other professors.

Dr. Taylor is one of the best professors I've had!!! She has an incredible ability to make the subject interesting and accessible. I've learned so much from their clear explanations and thoughtful feedback. She puts so much work and effort into her course and honestly, you can see that being reflected in the course!

Dr. Taylor presents material in an organized and approachable way, and they are always available to offer guidance. I feel motivated and confident in my learning because of their teaching style and support

since we have a final project as our final, she is very understanding and supportive if we had group members who are not contributing or delaying the group's progress, and instead, grades your individual work and is very fair.

Dr. Taylor an outstanding instructor who always creates an engaging and supportive learning environment. Their passion for the subject is infectious, making complex concepts easier to understand. I truly appreciate their dedication to helping students succeed.

her lectures are always interesting and makes you more aware about your environment. I really liked the group project because it targets regions in the world that need environmental attention, which puts the spotlight on places of where people are struggling but the media doesn't highlight that.

something i like is that on the midterm, the workshops &in class activites are really a preperation for your midterm so thats why she provides so much detailed and good feedback on them (which is why many people say shes a tough grader)

What I like is that the reading &lectures go hand in hand which is amazing bc the lectures explain things that i might not understood in the readings. shes available and willing to meet with you for office hours to help you on areas where you are struggling as well as offer extra credit to help your grade. She also has a lot of drops in the course!
