She is very observant and notices her hard working students and gives out stickers and thanks them for doing so well in the course which is very rare that any professor hardly even recognizes ur face. She also support you so much bc she provides the textbook for you and everything you need for free!

Dr. Taylor is very supportive and is willing to help you to make sure you do you best in her course. She puts so much time and effort into grading and giving constructive feedback. Some people say shes a tough grader, but really, she just wants you to do excellent on the midterm bc everything you do in class is preparing you for it.

Talked during every work time so we never could work in class during the time she had set aside. Class activities often have no meaning or impact. Topics are easy but assignments are often tedious and take a lot of time.
Very caring teacher, just a lot of time wasted in class

Very kind and will like you if you participate and try. Attendance is taken and she is a VERY tough grader but does not take off high amounts of points. Low points taken off but very easy to loose points. Overall great teacher for a mandatory class.

The entire class is group projects, it's awful. She is a REALLY tough grader and if you make even one tiny mistake or you don't explain something enough, you'll fail the assignment completely. She also barely gives feedback, so you usually don't know what you did wrong. The assignments are easy but there's so many each week. She's super nice tho

Taylor's lecture's aren't the *most* engaging, but she is very kind, smart and posts the lectures online if you need to review. There is only one person midterm exam and the final is a group project that you work on throughout the semester. If you put just a little bit of effort in, you should get a 4.0.

Dr. Taylor is super nice, I just was not interested in the class topics at all. If you miss a class you lose a lot of points so you need to  go to  every single  lecture. It was hard to pay attention but if you really like the Earth and the environment then this is a good fit because Dr. Taylor is a great  person.

Dr. Taylor is super nice and very passionate about her studies. Lectures were okay. She gives a lot of in class and outside of class activities/homework. Only one exam, which is the midterm. There is a big group project at the end.

Great professor, only one exam the midterm. Even if you flunk midterm you're still able to get a good grade in class. Lots of group work- the final is a group project. Talking to her helps build a good relationship.

I loved Dr. Taylor's class! She is so much fun and, outside of some of the videos she shows, the lectures are really enjoyable! In-class assignments are worth more of your grade than you think so don't miss class! Slightly challenging mid-term but she does let you have a cheat sheet. Everything is graded to the absolute letter of her rubrics.

Dr. Taylor is a nice and somewhat understanding professor. Most of your grade in that class will consist of your attendance, the in-class learning activities, and the labs she gives to you the night before. If you show up to class and do your work, then you should be perfectly fine.

I loved Dr. Taylor. In a class of over 200, she was somehow able to know my name. Course consists of weekly homework's (Quiz and Discussion Post) which are both take home and fairly easy. Show up to class, take notes, and it is literally the easiest 4.0. After the midterm, there is no need to take notes. Final is a CAP project presentation.
Great professor, homework is very easy. A lot of it is group based activities. Exams are kind of hard, but Dr. Taylor is an accommodating professor who cares for her students success.

Dr. Taylor was one of my favorite professors during the fall. She is super caring and wants all of her students succeed. Her grading criteria is very clear. If you ever have any questions she is always open to ask and provides very good feedback. If you have to opportunity to take any of her classes, do it!
This professor cares about your success and gives you many resources to help you succeed in the class. It's very flexible when it comes to assignments and homework. I recommend this professor!

Dr. Taylor was a solid professor until she started enforcing word counts on discussion posts. She will mark you down even if you are a 20 words shy, with solid content, and then still mark you down when you meet the word count using filler content and dragging it out. She needs to learn to pick one, quantity or quality. You can't have both.

Overall Dr. Taylor is a great professor. You can tell she wants you to do well in the class, and she will work with you when possible. The only reason I am not giving her a 5 is because she does weekly discussions posts, and is a stickler about minimum word counts. Remove the word count minimums and I would easily give her a 5.

Dr. Taylor genuinely cares about her students and how well they are doing in her class. She also adjusted certain aspects of the class based on feedback from us. This class is very lecture heavy but they are all very applicable in our everyday lives. We also are assigned groups at the very beginning of the semester to work in throughout the class.
She really knows what she is doing and had interesting lectures. She has a quick pace, which might not be the best for everybody.

He's a very nice guy but the quizzes are setting you up for failure. He doesn't give you practice questions so you're blindsided by the questions. The midterms are very difficult as well. He doesn't even try to help his students do well
The mid terms are rough, it's like 25-30 multiple choice questions and then 2 short answer questions with like 8 parts each. I would never even finish the exams.

His class was by far one of the most difficult to sit through. He is a really nice professor, but the class is difficult. I would recommend going to class and reading the book because it really helps. Otherwise you will be completely lost.
hardest class you will take as an econ major or even at MSU. But professor Woodbury does a great job making sure you understand the material. He gives chances for extra credit, so go to class.
I liked his class EC380, quiz's were very easy, midterms kinda hard, final was pretty tough but an easy 3.0 overall. Good class and good teacher.

Dry, uninteresting lectures. Book work was easy, yet didn't have much to do with what was done in lecture or on exams. Overall an easy course, just be careful not to fall asleep: it's really easy in this class.
Completely unable to connect with students. The work was easy, but the lectures are tedious and uninspired.

Dry class, dryer professor.  Be prepared to listen to mundane details like OSHA-specified toilet seat dimensions.  Be prepared to get no credit on homework because you didn't staple it.  Be prepared to get test questions wrong because he gave the wrong answers.

Great guy. I knew exactly what was expected of me. His policies are great (all hw due at end of semester, exam absences allow alternative test days, extra credit, etc...), caring, funny, responds to emails asap, makes difficult content manageable. Lectures are so long and boring but its really not that bad. I feel inspired to succeed in his class
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Made difficult concepts and topics easier to learn because of his teaching style. Always kept a good vibe throughout the lecture and explained things well.

This class is a lot of information but he makes it way easier to understand and wants everyone to succeed. He makes little jokes and is great at explaining concepts. Exams are based on the lectures- studying the learning objectives from each class is very helpful. Overall great professor!!

He really knows the information inside and out. He made me appreciate the subject and is SO GOOD at breaking science down into understandable context. Plus, he's kinda funny which helps. The lectures are long, but once I got into the material I enjoyed learning. The way he has the class outlined is perfect and makes it so easy to know what to study
Great professor and guy. He gives you all the materials you need to do well on the exams and records all the lectures which are not mandatory to attend. easy to 4.0 if you put in effort.
Probably the best prof I have had so far.
Very chill and funny professor.

Loved Dr. Towse. The class itself is a little harder but he makes it reasonable. Lots of exams but study and you'll be fine. It is always very clear what will be on the exam and the questions aren't super hard. There is lots of extra credit and an exam gets dropped. An easy 4.0 if you just go to class, study the slides, and do the HW + EC.
It was easier to watch the videos of his lecture than actually watching his lecture

He started lecture one with telling us how great of a professor he is and then how hard his class is. There are so many exams it's hard to keep up and the second you fall behind it's just an endless climb back up until the semester is finally over. If you can avoid him I would.

He is a really sweet professor and fun to go to class for. His i-clicker didn't give you points but going to class was very beneficial as he explains his topics very clear and makes sure everyone understands the material. he gives exit polls for class info that was confusing so he can reteach it next class a different way. so nice i love it

if you have to take this class, take it with Dr. Towse. he's very friendly, funny, and makes lectures enjoyable. his class is very organized and also gives a lot of extra credit assignments which are super easy and fun. he tells you exactly what to study for each exam. if you stay on top of it and put in the effort, you can easily 4.0 this class.

Dr. Towse is actually the goat. The class is somewhat difficult but showing up to lecture and doing the assignments makes it very manageable. Lectures are long but very interesting and he is very funny. Current favorite professor at MSU.
Dr. Towse is a great professor and wants you to do well. If you attend lectures and study the learning objectives you can do very well in the class.

explains expectations first day. don't really need to go to class because he records lectures and posts them to d2l. lots of connection assignments but they genuinely help, and some questions from them are on exams. you actually have to watch/read the material, but if you do that and re-read notes a couple times before exams, you will be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: PSL 250 is not an easy class by any means, the information is complex and there is a lot of work for it. However, Dr. Towse sets up the class with students success in mind. He's a very enthusiastic professor and gives you all of the materials to succeed. I could not recommend Dr. Towse enough, he's great.

Towse is the best professor I've had at MSU. He is very attentive to students understanding and feedback. Pay attention and you will do great. Don't let the material scare you! He truly made physiology interesting for me.

This professor is amazing and very good at explaining things. Lectures can get long however he keeps them entertaining and also records them if you cannot make it. You need to study hard for exams but he lets you drop one. Overall good class, educating.
Great Professor

Dr. Towse is the best professor I have ever had. I was scared to take PSY250 because of the difficulty, but because of him, this is the easiest class I am taking. Very good at explaining concepts and gives breaks to ask questions and reset. Puts the learning objective that is associated with that slide at the bottom makes studying so super easy.
THIS GUY IS AN ABSOLUTE GEM

The class was harder, and there was a lot of information, but he was an amazing lecturer, and I never got super confused because he explained things so well, he was also very funny, with a dry sense of humor that kept you engaged. Love, love, loved him.

It's basically just exams as the grade and some extra credit. I study's for days for every single exam and still walked out with Cs almost every time. They is just WAY TOO MUCH material it seems impossible to get all of the information. You study for a week and then take an exam every time. I think he has good ratings because he is a genuine guy

The content in PSL250 can be difficult, but Professor Towse is willing to help. I found the way he formatted his slides and wrote on them helpful. There is extra credit available, too. I enjoyed the class with him.

I love Dr. Towse and his class. I actually found the content interesting when I normally don't. He makes it interesting and funny, but you definitely have to study because the only grades are the exams, which are kind of hard.

This class is AMAZING. It may seem intimidating since your grade is based ONLY on exams. Don't let that scare you. Go to lectures, and take good notes. Dr. Towse is an excellent teacher. I looked forward to going to class every day. He makes the material very interesting, and easy to learn. This is by far my favorite class ive taken here.

exams were hard, but they're basically rewordings of the learning targets
funny professor though

This class is super hard, and your grade is 100% exams plus a little bit of extra credit. That being said, Dr. Towse is an amazing professor who clearly knows a lot about the subject but is also very empathetic towards his students. Him and his TA team are very understanding and willing to help students succeed if you reach out to them.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Loved professor Towse--offers 25 points of extra credit which got me my 4.0. Only downside for me was tough to communicate with if not in person. Otherwise, go to class and study for the exams and you'll do just fine. It took me some time to understand the structure as a non-stem major, but I got the hang of it. Class giggles are a bonus too.

He is very passionate about what he teaches an if you study well you will get 4.0. Class is only 6 test grades, he drops the lowest, and offers 25 points of ec which is a lot of the point offered in the class. If you need to take this class do not worry, he says that it is really difficult at the beginning but i took it with 18 credits and did fine
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Towse has been my favorite professor so far. He cares about his students, has lots of office hours, and keeps class very interesting. He has a sense of humor that makes everyone laugh and not hate being in class. I did spend lots of time outside of class studying because it is a difficult class, but a 4.0 is very possible.

PSL 250 is a hard class but Dr Towse is definitely one of the best teaching it, he makes it straightforward and entertaining and I would definitely recommend going for his physiology class if you're required to take it.

Prof is amazing & funny. Lots of content, but all interesting. Grade is based solely on 6 exams, you can drop 1, not the final. Reading the textbook isn't necessary if you go to/watch every lecture. Offers extra credit opportunities. Decent time/energy commitment, but worth it. Study learning objectives, go to office hours, and don't procrastinate!

Best professor i've had at Michigan State so far this class can be very hard but he makes it so that you are able to understand the content and explains it very well. The class has 6 exams but he did this so you can do bad on two and still get a decent grade in the class. Go over learning objectives and do the smart book assignments.

Lectures are very informative, clear learning objectives, revisits points that he didn't explain well, and he's entertaining. Material is dense but he makes it interesting. Use the learning objectives to study for exams and you'll be ok. Best class I've taken here and one of the best professors. Extra credit is available.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He is very good at explaining things in class always open to answering questions after class. The only grades are the 6 exams but if you study enough you will do fine and there is LOTS of extra credit that is very easy to complete. Going to the lectures in person helps since he finds easy ways for you to understand the challenging material.

The class is based off of 6 exams, If you don't do well on exams, I suggest you don't take this class, it is an extreme amount of information and not enjoyable if you get overwhelmed with school easily.

You have six exams, the last one being the final. You are allowed to drop one exam, but not the final. He gives a lot of extra credit. Attendance is not mandatory, but you should always go to his lectures because his video recordings have bad audio. I gave up toward the end, but a 4.0 is attainable if you read the textbook and attend lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I highly recommend attending class for this course!! It's very easy to skip, but the uploaded videos are often low quality and make it difficult to hear him. Also, he uses a lot of witty dad humor which makes the class enjoyable.
reviews.rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: The course transitioned from online to in-person exams. It only grades based on six exams, with optional extra credits. Students can drop one exam, but not the final, which counts heavily with 160 points over 80 questions. The course heavily relies on anatomy knowledge. Taking anatomy first is advised, even though it's not a prerequisite.

Professor Towse is very consistent and I appreciate that I always know what to expect from him. Our class has been exam heavy but he gives extra credit opportunities. If you apply yourself, you will do well in the course.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Fantastic professor-really cares about his students and their success/understanding. This is a tough class but if all the study materials provided are used, a 4.0 is achievable. Grades are only based on 5 exams but his lectures are great. Homework online and physical attendance is optional so it's really up to how hard a student is willing to work.

Towse is an amazing professor. He makes jokes in class and has lots of opportunity for class involvement in the lecture. His lectures are exactly like the textbook so if you missed something in class you can easily go back and read about it. He has lots of opportunity for extra credit and it helps a lot with comprehension of content

Dr. Towse was by far one of my favorite professors. I did not do as good as I wanted in his class, but he sets you up to understand concepts. He's a genuine man and cares about his students. The content of the course is test heavy and has a lot of information. Just stay on top of learning objectives and you're good.

Finished with a 3.5 because I gave up at the end but a 4.0 is very possible if you genuinely put effort in. You're only graded on 5 exams but have the opportunity to get 30 extra points. Do not procrastinate because there is a lot of material and he asks little details about certain information that you wouldn't expect to be tested on.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Genuinely the WORST experience I have ever had with a prof. Rude to students who do not show up to lecture. Exams are the ONLY thing you get graded on, super long homework every week that's not even graded. Do not take this class, avoid this class in all ways possible if he is teaching it. Your grade will suffer if you have him as your professor.

If you hate Lockdown browser do not take this class. That is how the exams are administered and you will get flagged for ANYTHING. Then he makes you take the exam in person and is a brat about it. He will not accommodate you with any health problems or family situations in case of needing to take an exam another time. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you have prior anatomy/molecular bio knowledge and are interested in this kind of content, this class is very do-able. There are 5 exams (1/5 exams dropped) plus cumulative final which seems daunting but theres less content on each exam which makes it far easier to study. Only exams count for grade, but I highly recommend utilizing the textbook

This class was pretty difficult. It is all exam based (6 exams) but the pro of this is that you are tested on a smaller amount of information. There was extra credit offered that really helped. He records every lecture so you can go back and rewatch it online. Dr. Towse is an amazing professor. Study and go to the lectures and you will do well.

I took PSL250 as an engineer to fulfil my bioscience requirement. Was not wanting to come to class initially. After the first few lectures, I grew fond of what he taught. Wonderful stuff! He's funny, willing to answer questions and even wants to discuss very theoretical questions such as cable theory -- that isn't on the syllabus! An amazing class.

Prof. T is hilarious, and super chill. His class has a reputation of being hard, but he makes it easy. He provides so many resources to help you study, and I learned so much from his lectures. He is an amazing teacher and I wish he taught every KIN class. I would be successful in every one if this was the case.
Amazing professor always kept the class engaged and explained everything so well. Very understanding. Exam heavy
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Wow, there are so many exams and they aren't all that easy either. Dr. Towse tries hard to keep students engaged with jokes and such but I would not recommend this class unless you have to take it. 6 exams and only one gets dropped, and it's not the final which is worth the most. But he does offer extra credit which is super helpful.

One of the first classes I ever took at MSU. The lectures are kind of long but he keeps them interesting and gives multiple breaks throughout. It's very easy to get a good grade in his class and he's SOOOOOO funny. Genuinely one of my favorite classes I've taken to this day.

Prof. Towse is a great professor! He cares about teaching and is very knowledgeable. Although lectures aren't mandatory, it's important to go or watch the recordings online. The entire grade is based on biweekly exams so you likely won't get the grade you want without reviewing the lecture, doing notes on the textbook, and the extra work.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Towse was a good professor. This class is very well laid out which I can appreciate. For a non-STEM student, the class material was very difficult, but manageable as long as you put in lots of effort and time studying. I would rate Dr. Towse higher if he were to finish everything he put onto his slides during class time, this rarely occurred.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Let me tell you why this person is absolutely amazing: professor Towdse is an immensely talented professor who deserves the world. He is an extremely kind, caring and respectable person and his classes are amazingly well taught. You can always ask him any question about physiology and I never felt intimidated in his classes. He also loves teaching!

Professor Towse was an amazing professor he wants all his students to do well. Out of all the professors who teach PSL 250 take it with him! He is great at accommodating to student feedback and listens to your needs. Yes the class is hard work but if you focus on the learning objectives you should do just fine.

Professor Towse is by far my favorite professor at MSU. Lectures were interesting and you could tell he wanted students to learn material. Very open to student opinions and always willing to help explain concepts. I learned a lot in this class - definitely take him. Also sent a thoughtful email to students and past students after the shooting.
Such a great professor! Interesting class and he is more than willing to give you help if you need it.

My favorite class I've taken at MSU, Dr. Towse is funny and genuinely cares about your success in the class. The course structure is great, with everything all being in one system (Connect) and I actually look forward to going to class. I would recommend this class to everyone considering taking it!

Towse is consistent in how he runs the course. Homework is all on the same site & always due the same time every week. Lectures are optional & recorded but there were a lot of technical difficulties so I would encourage you to just go to class. 1 negative was lecture speed and structure being hard, slides were mostly pictures so easy to miss detail
Very nice and funny, and records lecture in case you cannot make it to class. Learning pace is easy to follow along and gives out EC each chapter!

Had him fall 22. He used McGraw-Hill Connect to supplement his lectures. The best part of Connect is he highlighted the important/relevant info from the textbook to read for the Connect homework. Throughout the course, he'd implement feedback from students to change pace of teaching and material to cover. Some quiz questions on exams, but not all.
He is awesome! Funny and caring and listens to students feedback. He does a great job with the material and is extremely fair.
One of my favorite professors, she was passionate about accounting and genuinely cared about her students' success. Made a required course engaging and worth taking.

Her teaching style is more unique than any professor I have ever had. Literally just take this class with Dr Tracy, she's the best. Do be prepared to have some conversations in class, the more you talk the more you get out of it. She's very chill and there isn't a lot of homework or just overall work.

The most kind individual you will ever come across. Class enjoyment is based on participation but it is very easy to participate. I would recommend taking this class with her to ANYONE! She really cares about all of her students and is truly just amazing. Some of the work may get annoying to complete but it is not graded on correctness. 10/10
Really great, the course is more relaxed as long as you participate. Really cares about her students

Easily the best class I have ever taken. Almost like a high school teacher but you actually learn material you need in and out of the classroom. Cares about you so much. Participation in the class makes the class easier and better so be a part of that. A few group projects but fun, especially to make friends. Love her to death.

Very chill and fun class. No heavy essays. Not a heavy workload. She makes the class more collaborative and fun. You write is smaller increments, have a couple assignments a week. You read one book in her class, not a hard read though (it is interesting) - only $9 on Amazon. Fun projects along the way.
Great professor, very chill and nice. Workload was not bad at all.

One of the most fun and easiest classes you'll take. All you have to do is show up to ANY of the lectures she holds throughout the day and, throughout the semester, write 3-4 papers about anything you want. The level of fun is definitely dependent on your classmates, but it's otherwise still enjoyable. Book is $7 on amazon. Most caring prof. ever

This was the best class and best professor I have ever had. You will enjoy this class and professor more than any other class or professor you will have. If you participate and raise your hand, you will gain more knowledge about life than you ever thought possible. You do not have to write a single essay, and everything is graded on participation.

Dr. Swiecki was one of my favorite professors from my first year at MSU. She has such a passion for teaching and each student that she has taught. The course is designed very well, and the lectures are fun and easy. She has you read one book, but it's short and interesting. There are a few and presentations, but again very easy and straightforward.

Tracie is so caring, understanding, and sometimes even funny. If you need to take WRA101, GET IN A CLASS WITH HER, you won't regret it! The class consists of a weekly homework post and 4 course projects, with a final reflection assignment. She is super lenient about homework/projects and will always be there for you if you need help.

I had Tracy last semester (fall 2024) If you are an incoming freshman take her class. The class is easy all you need to do is show up. You do writing journal things which are easy and then a lot of class discussions and group work. Essays too. I'm not a reader or writer but this class made writing fun and interesting to me. take 100% easy class!!!!

Tracie is so kind and understanding. All the assignments and even attendance are very flexible. She never fails to make class fun and interesting. I didn't like high school English classes but I loved this one. She is my favorite professor and I wish she taught another class I could take!

Tracie was a great professor, all the classes and topics we discussed were engaging and interesting. There is not too much work, just a weekly discussion post, and 4 course projects. Would definitely recommend Tracie to anyone.

This is a very open class and she is such an amazing professor. She makes everyday unique and fun. She has a positive energy that she brings in every class and really wants to see her students succeed. The feedback she gives is helpful and I've learned a lot from her

By far my favorite professor I have had at MSU. Her class was amazing; you got to engage in topics the class was interested in, and I was genuinely excited for this class every week. I could not recommend her more, will never forget the memories made in this class.
One of my favorite professors of all time. Holds think tanks after the class is done for all older students to get together and chat. 10/10 professor

Professor Swiecki is amazing! I would recommend her class to anyone! She makes the classroom environment so positive and is one of the only professors I've met so far that truly cares about her students. Her grading philosophy is centered around actually wanting students to succeed and the work load is so manageable! Take this class!
Awesome professor, by far my favorite. Conversations with her are the best! She is very understanding, highly recommend.
Very good teacher Take her class.

the best class you will take at MSU. by far my favorite professor/teacher ever. the kindest soul with the most passion. come to class, please! talk in class, PLEASE! you learn so much with Tracie. she is the best, be a interesting class and she will love you. recommend 100000%

If you are a student Michigan State and need to still your writing credit TAKE THIS CLASS RN YOU WON'T REGRET IT!
This professor is down to earth, she accepts late work, it's not heavily graded papers, we talked more than we wrote honestly, my class was so funny, and I will never forget it. An easy 4.0

The most caring and thoughtful professor ever! She even continues to reach out years after you have taken her class! Her class is very conversational based, so as long as you talk you will enjoy the class
Tracie was by far my favorite proffessor this semester.  she was a great teacher and cared for us outside of class.  would highly reccomend her and would def take her class again!!
Promise you'll have a good time in this class. Small class is good for interacting and learning proficiently. Lots of self learning in this class. My favorite class i've ever taken at MSU

Professor Swiecki was one of the first professors I met here at MSU yet still has a place within my favorites. She builds a community within her classroom and ensures everyone is respected and seen. I had family issues during the semester that effected my attendance, but she was more worried about me than the class. Wish she taught higher levels :(

Tracie is the best teacher I have had at MSU.  The last thing you worry about is your grade.  You will get a 4.0, it is a class you actually are excited to go to and she is great.  Shoutout Tracie for caring more about the students then grades.
Unbelievably flexible, extremely understanding.

I absolutely loved having Professor Swiecki! I would recommend this class with her to anyone. There is a lot of in class discussions, attendance is mandatory, but there are an allowed amount of absences before it effects your grade. Easy 4.0 as long as you go to class and put effort into your writing!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Best teacher I have ever had. Inspired me to be a better writer and showed me that writing comes in more ways than the original 5 paragraph essay. Truly could not recommend her more. The best.

Tracie was absolutely the best for this writing class. She cares about your learning and gives you feedback that doesn't criticize but teaches. The class is focused on your life and opinions so a lot of the writing prompts are easy to answer with your own thoughts. 100% recommend this class with her!

She was a great prof that really wants students to succeed. Grades entirely on if you do the work and that's it. Easy 4.0. She has a lot of discussions with groups and the class. Really fun and caring professor overall.

Very good professor.  Let's you submit most things late as long as there's no activity on it in class the next day.  All grades are participation/effort, even the essays.  I was even able to submit an essay in comic sans and I didn't lose anything from it.  Frequently does in class discussions.  As long as you put effort in you can expect a 4.0

One of my favorite profs. She is an extremely understanding and great teacher. However, she really wants students to participate in class so if you are not willing to talk about your opinions in class, she isn't for you. Easy class, attendance isn't mandatory but I would recommend going at least once a week to show your face.
Not enough uplifting words- she is AMAZING! The best teacher I have had my first year of college. Very genuine.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Super nice professor. Leaves great feedback on work. Well constructed course which teaches lots of helpful things applicable in and outside the classroom. Overall experience was top-notch.
Lots of great learning in class!
Super sweet, gives really good feedback and lots of essays and projects but helpful and thorough process for each paper
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--So many papers

reviews.comment: Super helpful goes through everything in class. Explains concepts well. Im not sure why he is rated so low. I would take this class again with him

Absolutely horrible. Offered no help, was quiet. Anytime I would ask or help his exact words were "just try and keep thinking I can't help you". Genuinely have no idea how he even managed to get the opportunity.
Stay far away.
You have to use two different cameras and your phone and your microphone to show every inch of your room. You still will get confronted and told that you are cheating. It's brutal
Don't bother asking for help. He will just tell u to work harder and that you need to dedicate hours a day including weekends to pass the class

This class is sent from the fiery pits of hell. You are forced to pay for a class that has absolutely zero instruction. You watch pre recorded videos and do countless hours of assignments just for quizzes to ask you to do something you have never practiced. They do not care if you do well and actually prefer if students do poorly.There is zero help

Was a great Professor, only hindered by his students. With a class of over 1200 students on most semesters, his only option is is a flipped lecture model where the student does the majority of the work outside of class and then s reviewed in recitations by TA's. Always very kind and understanding in office hours, where most students didn't go.

this class is horrible. TA's never go over quizzes so if u didn't know what u were doing, u still won't! everyones reviews talking about academic dishonesty and cheating are true. They will accuse u and it is horrible and scary. I hated this class and couldn't wait for it to be over. very tough and the instructors don't care everyone nearly fails.

Accuse you of cheating when they have no proof in class of you doing so. Very strict and will flag your code if it looks somewhat like someone else's. You could get an ADR just by looking around. Would never take this class here again, especially with the way that class is run and it's only taught by TAs and the professor just sits around.

This was such a hard class for being a introductory. If you love yourself, don't take this class. You never see the professor and it's taught by TA's who also don't know what they are doing, it's like they are learning with you. While this class isn't necessarily hard, there is so much work that it makes it near impossible.
Called me the slurs in class
Called me the n word in class
Absolutely the worst professor/class ever. They do not care about you. Accuse you of cheating and treat you terribly

This class was truly the worst part of my entire year. The structure of the class is extremely disorganized. Chances are you'll never meet the professors for actual instruction since they just force teaching assistants to teach the lectures. If you can avoid this class, do it, because it will do wonders for your mental health!
class taught entirely by the ta's you not once see the professor

Not that good of a professor though he is better than the other two CSE instructors, I have never met my professor face to face in person, they let the TA's which are fellow students at MSU teach the course when the professors are much better suited to do so.

if you care about your gpa dont take this class EVER. This class is tought entirely by TA's you will never meet the professors, you will also get accused of cheating very regularly and when you do meet the professors they will treat u like ur worthless!

realistically, this class is a heavy weed-out class for business majors, but genuinely not as bad as I thought. it definitely progresses in difficulty, so if you didnt understand a section, you HAVE to to be able to keep going in the class. you learn quick that you have to make it click yourself, ask A LOT of questions, and stay on top of it.

The professors are overall absent in the class, so you rely really heavily on the TA's. They're helpful. The class is not impossible, but you absolutely have to stay on top of it. missing one lecture sets you back weeks. Gets insanely hard in the last 3 weeks of class. Utilize the lessons, extra practice, and takes notes like your life rides on it.

Professors teach through online videos done outside of the lab, which sucks. They are absent during the lab, so you are relying on the class being run by 2 TAs. The class can be pretty difficult when you are receiving an error but have no clue why it is there. Overall, the class is miserable but required for business students.

This class is TA taught for the labs while the lectures are professor videos from 2-3 years ago. Based on the type of material learned in this class, the professors should be teaching the labs in person while the TA's are there to help. No extra credit either so try and keep up with the labs & quizzes, while also doing well on the exams!

It is comical that TAs run this CSE102 class taken by many first-year business students. While these TA's know how to code they are unequipped to teach students and the professors are unreachable. 
Tyler Lovell seems like a nice guy but he is a terrible teacher and shouldn't work with students even remotely; this course should be completely online.

Take your time and learn the material before moving on to the next section. This class is incredibly difficult but doable. Online or not, you're going to be teaching yourself and I found that this type of class clicks for some people while it is incredibly difficult for others.

Tyler is god awful. This course actually makes me think it was created by the devil. They intentionally make everything way harder for the students. I am a business major yet they want to suck the life out of me in a coding class. The projects and tests are horrendous and they expect so much for a basic intro coding course. Make it make sense.

There are so many students that you will never come in contact with the professors. Taught by TAs. Its a hard class, and they say all the resources are there, but its just an uninviting environment and there always seems to be an issue. This class tested my limits for sure (as it does for a lot of students). Just be prepared.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: CSE 102 is one of the absolute worst classes I have taken. The class is run by TAs, and trying to go to the professor's office hours accomplishes nothing. The exams/quizzes are tough and although they post practice quizzes, THEY ARE ONLY POSTED AFTER THE QUIZZES ARE DUE. Sometimes I question why Tyler is on the MSU payroll.

Ran CSE 102 class very poorly. Class is practically self taught and is one of the hardest courses at MSU. Implemented new programs and grading from last year and as a result the entire course was a complete disaster. I do not recommend taking this course at all unless you have to which most unfortunately do. Professors do better

Prof Lovell's class was the hardest one. I didn't meet the prof, since TAs run this class. Some TAs might be helpful, but only if you're lucky enough. The codes in the videos didn't match what we needed for quizzes, exams, homework, and projects. This class messed up my gpa and might affect getting into the business college.

Never once did I even meet the professor, very unengaged, and seemed like he could care less about CSE 102 as a whole. The class is taught by TAs who don't know what they are doing and won't help. Very exam heavy and the exams are worth 60% of your grade. Also quizzes every 3rd class period. Poorly organized class with a professor who doesn't care.

This class is really hard, but if you keep up with the weekly homework then you should be fine. You will never see the professors, and all of the sections are run by ta's. There are quizzes which are not bad if you've done the homework. Exams are tougher. The projects are the hardest part, but they are only worth 6% of your grade.

The professors are not even there, it depends on how good the ta's are to get help. This class is the absolute worst make sure to take time to pay attention to the labs and videos. Way too much homework and the exams are difficult.

I took cse102 with him and 2 other profs and you do not see them during class at all except for recorded lectures you have to watch outside of class. The first third of the class is pretty easy but after that good luck and I hope you get a TA that gives you help instead saying figure it out yourself. You'll be in the cse helprooms daily in cse102.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Great professor!  Really loves the content he teaches and has a passion for it. He was always understanding of circumstances and gave clear criteria of what was expected of his students.

Dr. Urquhart is a great professor! His class is very straightforward, he makes it clear what is expected of students. He really cares about student success, and even offered my class an opportunity to get points back on an exam we had a low class average on. Very caring prof!

Dr. Urquhart is great! He is super approachable and happy to answer questions. He tries to make lectures fun and usually we get out early. The class is straight forward and has a pretty light work load. Exams are mainly free response with some multiple choice. He offers a few extra credit opportunities too. Good class, great teacher!

Had fisheries and wildlife 101 with prof U (the course code was unavailable), online in the summer so maybe that's why the class was as lecture heavy as it was but I've taken other summer classes and this one was not it. 4-5 different hour long lectures a week. Easy class but I hated the long boring lectures. He is accommodating too which is nice.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I was out for mono for 3 weeks and Dr. U completely excused me from all lab assignments I missed. very short lectures then complete worksheets in class. Leave about an hour of time for pre classes, they're recorded lectures that you have to take notes on. tests mostly short answer, some multiple choice.

Dr Urquhart was one of my favorite professors at Briggs. He was so caring and he was focused on making sure everyone understood the material and were doubting themselves. The tests are a little difficult, but if you study the right material they were not bad. I wish I could have him for all of my Lyman Briggs classes

I did not have him as a professor although I turned to him for help as he is the assistant dean of lyman briggs. He offers amazing help.  If you have any issues in lyman briggs I highly suggest turning to him first as he knows what he is doing. I only have good things to say about Urquhart as he is amazing and very knowledgeable.
Lecture format was great, interesting topics. Exams were always straightforward and easy to pass. Do the pre-class assignments and study for the exams and it is an easy A.

A great professor! There are quite a bit of assignments for this course, but they are not hard at all. A few papers, weekly Cengage assignments which are unlimited attempts, and the exams. As long as you do your work you should get an A. overall a great professor whose caring, flexible, and wants to see his students succeed.

He was AMAZING! I loved going to this class not only because I love bio but because he truly made it fun and entertaining. His lectures were full of personal stories and videos and his exams were not hard. He gave out a study guide and if you put effort into that you had no issues. He really made my life easier and I learned a lot.
Professor Urquhart really cares about his students. I love this class and he makes the class really easy to understand.

Dr. U is an awesome biology professor! It is clear how much he cares about the subject, and he makes the class very doable. Tests are based almost exactly off presentation slides. Get ready to hear a lot about his trips to Nicaragua! Lots of extra credit is offered which is beneficial when the end of the semester creeps up on you!

Dr. U was by far the best professor I have had here. He made LB 144 a breeze. He provided note outlines from which he made all of his test questions. Don't bother buying the book. He told us exactly what to study for the exams. He gave an insane amount of extra credit so I didn't even have to worry about the final. Dr. U sets you up for success.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: amazing

He is extremely smart and try to be as clear as possible. if there is something you do not understand he will work with you to figure it out. In the class I had I could turn in hmk a week late for 10% less credit and turn it in a week before finals for 50% less credit! There is something very attractive about him too.

Dr. U was probably one of my favorite professors this semester. He clearly explains all of the material and if you as a question, his response is very clear. He is also a great person and interesting to talk to.
Definitely one of my favorite professors, will help you with problems, and has more personality than most professors.

Dr. Urquhart is awesome!!! He teaches exactly enough to make you understand, just go to class and pay attention. He lets students (like me) work on special projects with him outside of class, and even has class field trips!!! 4.0'd the class easy. Just beware of 155's chem portion.

Nice guy, but puts no effort into teaching. You won't learn anything from his lectures as he is so disorganized that you can barely see what he is even writing much less what he is talking about. Homework, quizzes, exams, and tutorials feels unrelated most of the time. Not sure what else to say, but I've simply learnt nothing from him.
well meaning but unreasonable. he's a great guy though

Do not take Professor Ignacio's courses if you want an easy grade. However, if you are willing to work hard, and if you are committed strongly to the course, you will learn a lot. He's also a hilarious guy and quite casual, so he's very approachable if you happen to have questions about the material.
GREAT GUY. Has great sense of humor and great attention to details. People who gave a low rating probably work hard enough.

about 15 min quiz during lec. one is cite theorem or definition, another one is short proof which is selected on the homework list (more than 10 questions a week). It is nearly impossible to solve the proof during the quiz time if you didn't do the homework.

Prof Ignacio assigned between 30 to 60 practice problems for each chapter, some of which used for tests. There were four homework assignments  only 3/10+ questions were harshly graded with no provided solutions. Many students dropped the class. The class's average was c-. The midterm was only 50 mins despite accounting for 30% of the final grade.

Ignacio is a king. He gives you lots of work, but if you actually do it you will learn lots. Tests are manageable with deep knowledge of content, but are still difficult. He curved everyone up 13% at the end though. Overall, he is direct and has a great sense of humour. 



Thank you very much. 





Best regards

I feel like he is well-meaning, but he has entirely unreasonable expectations for students. Lots of homework, though that's not the main problem; his tests are too difficult, and they're worth 4/6 of the grade. Just for reference, he literally gave  problem A5 from the 1995 Putnam as a test problem...
Professor seems so traumatised by his 1991 IMO result, that he wants ALL of his students to experience the same as he did.

It is so bad, I wanna give you a zero. But that's not possible so I give you a one. 

Genuinely the worse course I've taken at UofT. Please for the love of God don't take a class with this prof if you want to keep your sanity.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: IUT is a research prof & it shows. He does not care about you nor the quality of his teaching. He stole THE ENTIRE last year's MAT267 (assignments, tutorial handouts) & made it worse. He copied the syllabus, but removed the last line that let students drop their lowest grade. Better off watching YouTube lectures than attending his class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Worst prof I've ever had and the worst course I've ever taken. I have absolutely nothing good to say about him or his course.
The course average is C-. Bruh he literally said he was gonna curve... WHERE the curve at homie???
Life or MAT337

The lecture he offered was awful since he only repeats the exactly same material as the textbook. His test was horrible, you are required to finish 6 proof questions on midterm within 50 min and 13 proof questions on final in 2 hours. That's all you can imagine.
Escape
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Well-meaning but completely unreasonable expectations for workload. Assigns about 20 hours of homework per week. Midterm was 1 hour with 4 hours worth of questions. Final was not much better. You will learn a lot, but will still fail the course
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This class is hard. Really hard. Nevertheless, you'll come out knowing the material super well. It will be a significant time commitment, but well worth it if you're interested in analysis. I feel confident about most of the material in Folland now, and have learnt a tremendous amount this semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Inspirational

reviews.comment: He gives a lot of homework and grades very few of them. Pays no effort in organizing his lectures. Does not even tell you how long the final exam is before it starts!

Why would UofT let him milk a nice salary and research grant from the Canadian public education system when he could have stayed at the University of Michigan and "help" the students to "learn" there.

Although well-meaning, Uriarte-Tuero ran this course poorly. Absurd amounts of homework; dry lectures; poor communication and unreasonable expectations (e.g. announcing an open book policy <48 hours before a midterm, and allowing only 10 minutes to submit solutions online); and so many more problems that can't fit in a 350 character limit.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He reads off the textbook. Puts no effort into making slides. Assigns a lot of homework (that requires 20+ pages solutions) and marks only a few of them very very harshly. I'd definitely advise to avoid his class if possible!

Absolutely pounded us with homework and basically laughed in our faces when we made the comment in class that it was very heavy. Tests were much too long for the allotted time, which he admitted to knowing beforehand but decided to keep them that length anyways. Read the lectures straight from the textbook. Simply terrible.

He is a decent person and a smart guy, but he is not a suitable professor for an upper year math class. You can tell he is just here for the research opportunities as his lectures are nothing more than reciting the textbook word by word. I have found Youtube videos explaining materials better than him. He also have some trouble following the syllab

Please avoid if you take at least 2 courses. Nearly 60 questions in homework1 with small sections in each one. Assign nearly half of the questions on the textbook. Do not respond email at all. Horrible experience.
Professor assigns a lot of homework, as if he thinks the students only take 1 course in the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Ignacio assigns a LOT of homework. By that, I mean every single problem in the book. He refuses to help with homework, his exams are crazy hard, and he makes sarcastic comments that nobody thinks is funny. Stay away!

Comes off extremely firm in the first couple weeks of class, but he is extremely willing to help you if you get to know him. I had the luxury of having in a lecture of 15 other people so it was very easy to ask questions. Once you ask questions, and show that you care about the grade in his class, he is more than willing to make sure you 4.0
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

reviews.comment: We were always behind schedule. The end of the semester was extremely rushed. Tests were close to impossible, mostly because he held the expectation that honors students should know concepts and notation that we didn't know.
Rushed towards the end of the semester when things started to get more difficult and was not the best at answering questions, but was a good professor overall.

Professor Ustunol is very personable as a professor, and is clearly passionate about the chemistry and processing of food. This class welcomes and encourages worldly perspectives that you can share through the platform PackBack for response assignments related to lectures. Occasional quizzes are very easy and taken at home, un-proctored.

As a freshman, this was an amazing class for my first semester.  Dr. U clearly cares a lot about her students, and really does want you to do well in her course. Her class is very organized and straightforward. Her lectures can be a little long sometimes, but she explains her material very well, and is clearly very educated on the topic. Love her!

I'm not fond of STEM but Dr. Ustunol made FSC super interesting. If you aren't a good test taker she has tons of other assignments to boost your grade. You do have to study a bit for the exams but nothing too challenging and the material was fun to learn about.

Dr. U is a great professor and clearly knows her stuff, she has a ton of real world experience and provides great advice. Super easy homework and discussion board assignments. Only complaint is that lectures are quite boring and at 8:30am. Attendance is required, but exams are mostly based off of lecture material so make sure to show up.

Class is easy but lectures are very boring. She is big on attendance and your attendance grade counts for 10% of the final grade in the end which sucks. Course material was easy to locate but confusing at points because deadlines were always changing.

Her lectures were impossible to attend due to her boring lectures and monotone voices. I had her at 8:30 am... I would wake up, join for attendance, answer the for credit top hat multiple choice questions and leave. The quizzes were so hard even though I used the lectures religiously. I was super surprised when I 4.0

Professor Ustunol is professional and intelligent in her field, but the lectures were a bit dry and monotone. There was 10 short quizzes (no cumulative exams) and a Packback assignment each week. 5 easy homework assignments spread out over the semester. Just put in a bit of effort and you'll be fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Ustonol is probably one of the best professors in the MSU food science department. As long as you pay attention and put in a little bit of work you should do fine. She has neat little antidotes that relate to class material and i personally enjoyed! 10/10 would recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: She's an ok professor, but the lectures are quite dull at times, and she didn't take attendance consistently but still factors it into your grade. The tests are intimidating at first but once you get used to it they're pretty easy, and despite attendance being mandatory its only worth 5% so you can afford to miss if you understand the material.
The quizzes are a bit intimidating at first, but they are easy if you come to class and pay attention. She grades the quizzes and exam rather harshly. Easy group project at the end of the semester.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

reviews.comment: Lab reports are long and graded extremely hard. Each lab report can take excess of 5-6 hours to make and is over  10 pages at times (never shorter than 3). Basically you will be writing an essay a week.  The lab class itself is not hard, but the reports are. Expect to communicate with you TA about any issues and grading not Ustunol herself.

Her lectures can not be downloaded. You must have an internet connection to view them. The sound of her voice is incredibly irritating to the point of being almost unbearable. I dread every lecture I have to listen to from her.  Wish the class was in an overall different format to make it more user friendly (i.e. downloadable power points)

Dr. Umucu cares about student success. His goal is to make sure you are developing as a professional. I had the pleasure of taking an independent study with him and he allowed me opportunities to gain connections to the scientific community as well as to develop my professional writing skills. To sum up my experience:Transformative.

Dr. Umucu actually cares about student success. His goal is to make sure you are developing as a professional. I had the pleasure of taking an independent study with him and he allowed me opportunities to gain connections to the scientific community as well as to develop my professional writing skills. To sum up my experience:Transformative.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Dr. Umucu provides a lot of great feedback. He values in having discussions with us students and encourages us to have deep discussions with each other. He takes a lot of time to make sure we understand something and then provides more to develop our understanding too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Caring

reviews.comment: Although this class was difficult and a lot of busy work, the professor was very easy to get along with. If you put in the work and show you care you will be completely fine. John Urbance is one of the best professors I've had.

Watched this man almost break a students laptop the other day. Professor was heated because the kid had asked why he was marked off when he had done it perfectly according to the rubric. Prof then checked his own rubric and the kid was right. How are you supposed to get a good grade when youre graded based on whims and mood swings?

This is the worst class I have taken at MSU. His personality is incredibly difficult to get along with. He grades based on opinions. For presentations he takes A LOT of points off because he doesn't enjoy the way things look. Incorrectly posts directions on D2L, we called him out on it, he raised his voice and shoved my lab-mates laptop away. Rude

The course itself wasn't that difficult. However, during lab periods we would often go over the end time to finish things up. He's very particular about how he wants things done, but he checks in often and gives directions on what to fix most of the time. He does seem to care about the students and was nice enough to offer in-person demonstrations.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: While his class was easy, it's only busywork and boring lectures. He goes over class time by 30min every lab. He makes us work in breakout rooms for 1-2 hours doing stupid busy work like cropping photos, making flowcharts and copy/pasting text. If you try to write anything that isn't EXACTLY what he wants, you have to completely redo it.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: Professor Urbance's classes are typically going to go over their expected time. He expects people to wait until he gives them "the go ahead" to leave. He doesn't reply to emails, is very picky with presentations, and does not take suggestions.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: This class is so unorganized he has no idea whats going on, so lazy waits till 3 days before things are due to post the material that we need, and don't even bother emailing him or his TA they were unresponsive and no help. this class was a joke, not sure he even knows the material hes teaching he can never answer your questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Where do I start? he is very rude, he is unresponsive when it comes to emails he pretends not to see that you emailed him, and will continue to send out mass emails. Cannot put out a syllabus for a class so you won't know what your being graded on or where your points are coming from. Lazy. tests are nothing like the reviews don't even waste time
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: When I first enrolled in this course I came to this website to see what this professor was about. All the reviews said he was a jerk and a horrible professor. I very much disagree. He's not a super tough grader. He sometimes can be unorganized, and directions can often be unclear. He also rambles on a lot, but he is by no means a bad guy or prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Very nice guy that knows what he's talking about. Class consisted of quizzes at the start of each class and then he demonstrated most of the procedures which was very helpful.

Dr. Urbance, for most, is a tough personality to get along with, but if you show that you are trying and working hard you will do fine. Despite his tough exterior he does care if students succeed. He gets easily irritated but ask him questions about how to get projects done correctly.

STAY AWAY. His classes are the most unorganized classes I've ever had the misfortune to take. Doesn't explain anything well, but still sends email after email repeating the same, confusing instructions. He doesn't lead recitation or any of the labs and doesn't really seem to care about his students.

This class is BY FAR the most unprofessionally led class I have ever taken at MSU. It's only worth 2 credits, but the workload definitely exceeds it. Dr. Urbance constantly emails us late at night the day before things are due and all he talks about is how perfect his work in college is and how ours has to look exactly like his or we won't pass!!!!
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Course not and miss. Changes happen early in week. Group presentation. Good looking man.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: If you go to his office hours and ask for help, he might yell at you if you don't understand a simple concept. Don't take it personally thought, that's just how he is. He's incredibly smart and knows what he's talking about, but teaching it is not his strongest suit. He's not a bad guy, but I wouldn't take a class with him again if i didnt have to.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Urbance is the least favorite professor I have had to date. I had him for a biology lab and he made you do a lot of busy work, such as writing over 100 pages of protocol into a lab notebook even though they are posted in a word document for you already. He is also very specific about his grading. He likes things done his way.

He's a tough grader and his personality can be hard to get by, but most of the difficulty comes from misconceptions.  He expects the best work from his students which is understandable since you're paying to take this class.  If you ask for help, it'll be there, but come to class prepared and with your work all ready and you should be fine.
SO unhelpful and rude

Besides being unable to lead an organized lecture, Urbance's condescending and rude personality made his class extremely difficult. It seems to me that he is on a serious power trip, which is unfortunate, because he has the potential to be a decent professor if he were actually down to earth and could deliver the material in a more coherent way.

This professor is HORRIBLE. No matter how much  you study it is guaranteed you will not do as well as you want or expect in this class. If you have questions, ask the TAs not him! He will think you are stupid so refrain from asking him questions at any point.
It's just a bio lab, so his involvement was minimal; however, what he has done during recitations has been pretty easy to understand, and he's been lenient for things like exams in other bio classes.
Great guy, great professor. Not too difficult and clearly presents info. Straightforward class, and the guy sounds like the dad in Finding Nemo to top it all off.
Not a very good teacher. Often times acts like a "know-it-all". He has no patience for people that aren't already geniuses in the subject he is teaching. Wouldn't take his class again.

If you are looking for a helpful professor who likes his job, Urbance is not who you want. He is grumpy and arrogant most of the time. He also makes you feel stupid if you make a mistake. The class is hard because his questions are not straight from the book, they are real world situations that you need to apply your very basic knowledge to.
His exams are extremely difficult. Good luck.

Urbance makes sport of people not able to understand material, talks about the test average of always being 61%, and lectures are disorganized, but have mandatory clicker questions. You could read the book and do the homework and still get a 75%. He refuses to curve.

I lost a bit of faith in MSU's ability to care for their student's success after taking this class. The exams were impossible but they were extremely difficult. His tests are indeed designed to trick you. Study hard and DO NOT CRAM. He expects you to "know" the material, not just have memorized the notes. Read the book and watch online lectures.

This class was pretty hard. He talks really fast and is disorganized during lecture so your notes aren't that helpful. most of his test questions were trick questions or over small details we never covered

Dr. Urbance was a horrible professor. If given the chance, take someone else. He always had an arrogant attitude and made the entire class feel stupid. His exams were unnecessarily hard and even though I studied for hours, I was only able to get above a 70 once.

When you come in on the first day of class and a professor says his exams are harder than anyone else who teaches the class, its just setting up for failure. He was often arrogant and pretentious. I encourage any future BS111 students to avoid his sections.

Dr. Urbance was extremely unhelpful and rude in his assesment of the classes ability to comprehend material. Condescending attitudes help no one learn and I would highly recommend you do not register for his section or switch as soon as possible. Exams are over subjects never covered in class.
I 4.0'ed a Microbio class the semester before this one. I failed this class. Ridiculously hard exams and expectations.

If you have the option, do not take BS 111 with him.  His exams  are really hard.  I studied a lot for them, and didn't get anything higher than a 64%.  The exam questions are over minute details that he wouldn't even talk about in class.  The majority of the class was failing for most of the semester.
Worst professor EVER!! Don't take him, whatever you do don't have Urbance!!! He had the highest number of kids fail BS111, in the history of the class! Save yourself...don't take his class
No Comments
Comedy and culture class.
I emailed him asking for some help on a film and he told me to look at my notes. I'm pretty sure if I had the information needed in my notes I wouldn't email him.
Crude.

I'm a very diligent student. Mr. Urish is not the greatest trip I've taken. He's "fair." Everything outlined in his syllabus is EXACTLY, word-for-word, what he'll hold you accountable... But as far as being a friendly, approachable man, he's a bit intimidating. Treat him with respect still. Otherwise he'll only salivate all over your face screaming

i have no idea where to start. the class is interesting however he is nuts. he is a huge jerk, if you ever need help (which you WILL) he'll completely brush you off-repeatedly. he expects you to memorize every word and fact from the book, every word that comes out of his mouth.honestly dont take this if you care about your GPA.=]
didnt care about us, just hurried through things nad was never really interested in us!! oh and so boring
This dude is an A #1 A**hole! In my entire time at MSU, I have never had a prof. be more of a d**k than this guy. His rules are pathetic and ridiculous.

this teacher is just an jerk, if you're enrolled in his class just drop it. makes the biggest case out of everything. it would be helpful if the stuff he made a big deal about was anything important but theyre not. really just avoid at all cost.
be ware! initiallycomes off easygoing, but is really a hardass, opinionated jackass
Decent prof, just show up to class and make sure you follow EVERY rule of MLA!
Get out while you still can!!!
Very good prof, just do the work.
He's an air head. He really needs help!

Easy class, easy grader. Class feels like high school (no phones out, must pay attention, participation), this was annoying for a university required class. Her advice was condescending, vague yet overcomplicated, ask lots of questions to fully understand the group project before getting too far in. Don't overcomplicate anything though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I had her for IAH 207. She graded everything very easily; I got A's on almost every assignment. She will literally love you if you participate in the discussions, so say anything and she will love it or agree with you.

She approved my article for this presentation assignment and then once I presented, she noticed my article didn't have an author and required me to do the assignment all over again for a different article, after she already approved the other one. Her classes are extremely boring and she is always rambling about something not even related. Easy tho

Dr. Weiss will call you out if you are blatantly not paying attention in class, which honestly I think is fair. She's a great person and her lectures are often interesting if you actually care about what we're learning in class. If you do your work it's such an easy and enjoyable class.
There is a lot of group projects/work, and she doesn't let you use electronics during the class unless she says we can get them out.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. She is rude and constantly called me out in class for odd reasons. Forces you to work in groups and gives horrible instructions for every assignment. She wants you to practically read her mind for half of the criteria. Has a few students in the class that she picks on and a few that she favors. Don't take it!

Very condescending and rude, even when just asking for clarification. It's a simple class and assignments aren't too difficult, but I wish I dropped week 1. The class included a group project and the whole process was just beyond unorganized. If you can, choose another IAH.

Worst class I've ever taken. Professor Weiss is condescending, rude and unorganized. Every lecture felt like torture and every student groans at her assignments. Not a hard class but I would've taken a more difficult class if I knew I would be learning anything. Take any other IAH please.

Professor Weiss is extremely willing to help students achieve success in the class. Grading tends to be pretty lenient, although there's a lot of required reading / watching films outside of class. From what I've heard, one of the better IAH profs here.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I took this class because of her good reviews big mistake. This professor was so infuriating to work w/ directions were never clear, lectures made no since, assigned huge reading before every class and had a whole GROUP project entirely on your own out of class. This class was way too much stress and work for an IAH class but TENDED to grade easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She is a good prof and very nice. However, her lectures were dragged out and unengaging. Attendance is "mandatory" but you can easily get away with skipping classes if you participate occasionally. The irritating thing about this class was how you had to attend 5 events from the program that she was involved in which had no relation to the class.

Professor Weiss is very caring. Sometimes, she isn't as clear as she could be, but she makes her class very manageable. If you put even a small amount of effort in and submit on time, class is an easy A.

So condescending. So passive-aggressive. Lectures are insufferable, she drags out every concept unnecessarily. Course work is fairly easy but there is just so much for this type of class. Requires a lot of out of class participation for films. Asked her a question about how she prefers I turn in an assignment and she was so difficult to talk to.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: She is one of the best professors I have had at MSU. She is really kind and understanding of everything. There are some events you have to attend on the weekend but other than that and the reading she assigns her class is really easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Weiss is a very kind professor, she isn't strict about grading or attendance. I would recommend showing up to class often though as it can be easy to get lost in some of the content she teaches. She does post everything on D2L though so if you miss a class you have access to everything you missed. Sometimes she doesn't explain things well.
Dr. Weiss was really kind and accommodating when it came to getting assignments in. She really cared about our comprehension of the topics presented. Great professor and an enjoyable class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: She made it clear in the beginning that the she wanted her relationships with students to be more peer based rather than professor/student, which made it easy to reach out. She was the sweetest, but also knew when she was being taken advantage of. Most I had to write in this class was 150 words, and a simple group presentation at the the end.

I had Dr. Weiss for IAH 210, I highly recommend taking the class with her because she is such an amazing teacher and is very passionate about the class. The class content is not super heavy and the work load is very manageable. I loved taking her class so much, I am taking a second level IAH with her next sem.
She is the best professor i have seen. She lets you have your our opinions  and  nurtures them. Her classes are very interactive and she is very good in grading as long as you show good effort.

This class was easy, and I learned a lot. I took it online in the fall semester, but you could tell he cares about students and the material. He gives great feedback, and I recommend him if you need to take the 1 credit classes.

One of the nicest professors I've ever met. His actual teaching is also great.I cannot fathom how there's even one bad review here. He cares deeply about his students, and his course is VERY clear/easy on how to get a good grade. There was not too much reading, the videos were the perfect length. Take any of his courses with no fear.

CAS 204, fully async class. Workload is light and assignment criteria is clear. It's set up as a "choose your own adventure" system where you do 3 of 8 assignments that best fit you/your major. From his videos he seems like a caring and understanding professor. No exams or quizzes or anything, just take your time on assignments and you'll get a 4.0

Unless you want to learn absolutely nothing and listen to him ramble about nothing for the entire class period, don't take this class. There are 4 projects the entire semester and the only other grade is in person attendance. Take Jeff or Maral's section of this class if you actually want to get an education worth paying for.
