Professor Vernon Miller is very unorganized. The course itself is very confusing and the lectures are unncessarily long. Attendance is required in recitation and each time missed is a point deduction. The TA's are not helpful and the workload is extremely hard for an elective. Do not take this class.

This class is an elective but is treated like a mandatory requirement. The tests and recitations are unnecessarily hard and there is no guidance by Professor Miller. He stumbles upon his words and expects you to read 35-40 pages of articles. The course is VERY poorly structured and includes multiple "suprise quizzes" during lecture.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: This class sucks. It is organized so poorly the recitations are horrible almost 2 hours of just listening to the same stuff talked about in lecture. huge group project that you get no time in class or anything to work on, it makes no sense that this is a group project because you do independent interviews. The lectures are so boring no no just no

He was okay overall, but the class often felt hard to follow. Lectures could get off track, and it wasn't always clear what we were supposed to be learning. This made studying for exams a bit confusing since they didn't always match what we focused on in class.
He rambles and sometime you can't take away anything from what he says during lecture. Super caring and wants his students to succeed. As long as you put in the effort its a good class to take.

Lectures are very heavy, and the dude just kinda rambles for 2 hours. I often left wondering "What was I supposed to learn there". There is also a semester long group project that is very unorganized and far too difficult because of that. He also doesn't allow phones during the lecture, and on several occasions took them away from students

Professor Miller is a good guy and he is very passionate about what he teaches but I definitely wouldn't of passed this class if it wasn't for such an amazing TA in Hubert Miller. Lectures were rushed and packed with a ton of information and the structure of the semester long group presentation was confusing.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Worst setup class I have ever seen. Lectures go full two hours and cover an unreal amount of content. His speech is very garbled not articulate hard to understand. He does not take attendance and says the class is optional but he has pop quizzes for grades. There is also a semester-long group project that is extremely vague and unorganized.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: I enjoyed the class, professor, and lectures. There were 1-2 chapters of reading each week. We had two group projects where the grading was a bit unclear but helpful extra credit on the exams were offered. Attendance wasn't mandatory although we were given points for in-person assignments. We were often let out early. Good experience overall.

This class was awful. Unorganized, lectures are heavy with information, yet you have no time to take notes because he talks super fast and is impossible to understand (you also have to hand-write everything because he doesn't allow technology). The group project was unnecessarily long and difficult, especially if members don't do their part.
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: if you match his compassion for the subject, he makes for an absolutely amazing professor. helped me realize how interesting a class like organizational communication can be!

Professor Miller is clearly knowledgable and passionate but I dreaded going to lecture because of how quickly he talks and there was too much content covered each time. Grade is based off 3 exams and a group project. The lectures are not 'optional' because there is in class assignments and points. You have to put in the work to get a good grade.

Professor Miller has good intentions for his students, his lectures were long and confusing, especially when taking notes. He is always helpful and very nice, but the class is made up of 3 exams (300 pts) and the final project done in rec (100 pts). With very minimal studying, always attending, a great proj. group, and extra credit I got a 3.5
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Class is super lecture heavy and goes the entire two hours. On the syllabus it says the lecture is optional, but there are quizzes for points in lecture. There's a group project which is long and confusing. Professor is passionate about the subject but talks way too fast and there's too much content that's crammed into exams. Final is cumulative.

I took this class as an elective and I do not recommend this class AT ALL. Very hard. Exam heavy. Huge group project that is very confusing. Grades very hard. He is a nice person just the class layout needs to change.

I like Professor Miller as a person; tries to involve all the students and relate to them. I didn't like how lecture heavy it is. lectures always went full 2 hours and were fast paced and not easy to understand what was being said. Group project was a disaster, but if you get a decent group it is an easy A. Lots of EC if you attend let and rec.
Talks so fast. The group project in this class was the longest thing ever. I hated this class it made me almost want to drop out of school completely.

he talks so fast and I wanna fall asleep every lecture because he just repeats the same thing over and over again. I hated the group project we had to do too, this is one of my least favorite classes I've taken
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Miller is awesome! He is kind, funny, and dedicated to what he teaches. He LOVES helping his students! He is very approachable and will answer any questions that come up! The thing I didn't like about this class was the group project. It was very stressful and demanding.

She is a very kind a helpful person. She gives a ton of extra credit and is very willing to help in office hours or through email. The midterm and final were hard, but you do get a cheat sheet, so it's not that bad. Overall, I really liked her and would take the class again.

Although CEM 252 is rough and can be hard taking this class with Dr.Vasileiou is a must she is so nice and understanding. I had ran out of tries on my aktiv homework she would reset them for me and my friends and she really cares that her students are learning. She gave us a 1pg cheat sheet for the midterm and 2pgs front and back for the Final.

It is clear that this professor cares deeply about this content, while also understanding that for most people, this class is a necessary stepping stone toward other classes. I really appreciated her enthusiasm, and it helped me get through the class. Also, for exams, you get a cheat sheet.

I took this course in-person. Seems to be much tougher when taken in person vs online. Does offer a ton of extra credit though. Attendance is not mandatory. Very little homework.

Would recommend her as a professor, but take online if at all possible.

She was an solid professor. She taught fairly well, but her Greek accent sometimes was hard to understand, but it wasn't too thick. Naturally, since this is orgo 2, it wasn't really easy, but that'd apply to any professor. Only major complaint was that the midterm (and probably final) were very difficult and had poor averages.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: As a professor, she is good, not bad, but not great either. Lots of extra credit makes it easy, but you can learn the topics much more quickly by just attending recitation. Many times, I found that teaching myself while doing the homework was much more efficient, and the lectures were useless besides earning extra credit points.

I took the online version of Orgo 2 (I think they are getting rid of it though). Had sooo much extra credit. Responded to emails within minutes. Was always willing to stop lecture to answer questions. Great professor.
She was a great professor, this is a very hard class though. Don't let yourself fall behind at all, or you are cooked. You need to remeber stuff from cem 251

Dr. Vasileiou is a AMAZING professor for orgo II. Lectures were twice a week, and attendance was rewarded with extra credit, which she also gave ALOT of through quizzes, homework, exams, etc. Her lectures were super easy to understand, and she was always willing to answer questions throughout the lecture or through email. Best professor for orgo!!!
So so sweet and understanding. TAKE HER FOR ORGO.

Online organic chemistry 2 with Vasileiou was significantly easier than organic chemistry 1. She is a great teacher who is always willing to answer questions during lecture, with quizzes and exams that match up well with what was taught in class. She offers a lot of extra credit, through TopHat questions during lecture and other opportunities.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Vasileiou was a great professor, I was worried about the class since I took it online, but despite the content being hard, it was taught well. There is tons of extra credit in the class so it made a high grade easy to achieve. Professor Vasileiou was super easy to reach and very accommodating if I needed to switch a quiz to another day
Made it so easy but recitations are necessary if you don't understand, lots of time that content specifically came up on quizzes
Lots of extra credit provided, so make sure to get as much as you can. Everything is online and she provides help for any homework in her office hours

TAKE THIS CLASS ONLINE WITH HER!!!!! Seriously one of the easier classes I've ever taken, extra credit for just going to the lectures online and answering the tophat questions. Weekly quizzes and 2 exams with no lockdown browser. Basically gives out extra credit for breathing. Super sweet lady and explains and teaches orgo extremely well.

Love this woman, she makes this class incredibly easy to do well. 10% extra credit, lecture is optional, recitation is optional. Only downside is it is a late zoom lecture from 6-7:20pm but she records the lecture. Exams and quizzes are very fair and based off the homework, and all exams + quizzes are online no lockdown browser.

I was nervous to take organic chemistry over the summer but Dr. Vasileiou was an amazing professor. She understood we had lives outside of lecture and often recorded it and gave extra credit to those who watch the live lectures. She was very clear in teaching and broke everything well. If I have a chemistry class I would take it if she taught it.

Organic chemistry is a difficult subject to understand, but Dr. Vasileiou makes the topic easy to understand. She offers so much extra credit so make sure to use those opportunities. Although this is a virtual course, she is EASILY accessible: easy to meet for office hours or test/quiz accommodations. Online homework is a bit tricky so ask for help

Words cant explain how grateful I am to be able to have had Dr.Vasileiou as my orgo professor. If you want to 4.0 this class, YOU CAN! She makes it easy as long as you put in the work and are willing to learn. You don't have to break your back or be a try hard. she gives lots of extra credit and should I say makes enjoying chemistry possible?

I did most of my learning from the textbook, but it goes way more in depth than what is required. Lectures are optional but have tons of extra credit as clickers. There's a good amount of other extra credit too. She is very nice and will give extensions on the quizzes if you need. Accessibility is limited but expected for a class this large.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: Great teacher for ORGO . Gives many opportunities to do well in the class.  10/10

She is an amazing woman and is always available to help you out. She is quite knowledgeable and makes 252 so easy to understand. She also offers lots of extra credit, and is flexible when you have to miss a quiz. I would advise to do the cengage homework early so you aren't cramming at the last minute. Super chill.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I truly love this woman and I only wish her the best. The class is hard, but she makes it easy for her students and she truly cares about everyone. Homework is hard and most of it is not like what we do in class. She offers lots of extra credit and she cares a lot for her students. Quizzes are MCQ, exams are MCQ and short answer

Dr. V is amazing and clearly cares about the success of her students. The amount of extra credit she offers is insane, and assures students' success in the course. Homework is hard but extra credit accounts for that. Quizzes and exams are easy and also have extra credit in them. If you see her as an option for orgs II definitely choose her.

Class overall seems fine but the homework is infuriating and goes against her reasoning for it helping you learn. You get five attempts per question but the question changes with each attempt and it doesn't actually explain what you did wrong or show the correct answer making is useless and irritating. At least tests are multi choice.

Her teaching style makes topics clear and easy to understand which helps you succeed on exams/quizzes. She's very kind and gives tons of extra credit opportunities. The homework can be annoying but it is really helpful. Easy to get a good grade and to actually understand orgo.

She is fine if you want to take this class and then never think about it again, the only way you will really learn in her class is by teaching it to yourself. she doesn't really teach anything apart from reading the slides. the only way to do decent in this class is by doing the extra credit which you have to pay for. DONT take if you wish to learn

Please take her class for orgo!! She is such an amazing professor. She really wants her students to succeed and offers so much extra credit it is literally impossible to fail the class. People saying its her fault that the averages were so low but organic chemistry is an extremely hard course that I thought she taught very well. Would recommend.
best orgo professor

Dr. Vasileiou was quite literally the best teacher I have ever had for any chemistry class i've ever taken. Not only does she give lots of extra credit but explains the material perfectly. Her tests are exactly what the lecture is on and aren't too difficult. I think it would be impossible to fail this class.

Lots of extra credit offered in this class. Quizzes/ exams are very clear with what will be on them. Personally, I thought the bookwork problems were sometimes more challenging than the tests. Attendance to lecture helps grade with in class EC everyday! She walks through the practice questions which I found really helpful.

If you need an easy orgo class Dr. Vasileiou is your best bet. I do think, quality wise, she is good, but with how stale the lectures are and how easy she makes earning extra credit, actual learning is difficult. I do not recommend if you will actually need orgo in the future unless you can concentrate super well.

This professor was the fairest professor I have ever had for a chemistry class. She gave so much extra credit that if you do it all it is IMPOSSIBLE to get anything less than a B. She gave amazing practice problems for each lecture that were nearly identical to the quizzes. The midterm was a bit tough but very doable. I recommend taking orgo w/ her

She seems like a nice lady, and you will get a good grade in this class if you apply yourself. My problem is that the class is too easy and I didnt learn anything. As a premed student thats a problem. I had her class online synchronized, her zoom lectures are awful. Camera off, cant ask questions just chat, made it difficult to engage.

Great Orgo prof. Class is not overly hard. Dr. V's office hours are very good, you learn a lot when you ask her questions. By far one of my favorite profs I've had so far. I also retained a lot of what was taught in her class.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: She was great and made it super easy to do well in the class. Everything was organized and there were no surprises when it came to quizzes and exams. Tons of extra credit to boost your grade if you need it. The homework can be a pain at times (but the TA's at office hours are extremely helpful). Super nice and accommodating if you have to email.

Loved this class. She goes slow with the content so you actually understand. Makes orgo easy and fun to learn! Her tests are not crazy hard and are simple. Definitely attend lectures and take notes tho because due to her strong accent its hard to rewatch and properly make sense of what is being said. However, she is amazing and accommodating !
She is really nice. Since it's Orgo, make sure you attend her lecture and ask if you have any confusion. The extra credits will save your life but try to score well on quizzes and exams
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Without her generous amount of extra credit I probably would have had a 3.0, but ended with a 4.0. She has moves very quick but gives direct lectures that are easy to take notes off of.

Such an amazing professor. She makes a weed out class like orgo into an easy a. So much extra credit is offered on all quizzes and exams. Focus during her lectures and it will be easy to pass. Activities even replace the lowest quiz grades. Do the practice sets she offers before each quiz, and you will be fine. Also answers emails very quickly.

She wants her students to pass, and she offers a lot of extra credit opportunities. She is understanding of health issues and let me take a quiz at a later date when I was too sick to take it on time. Textbook is required to purchase, but I never used it. As long as you attend all lectures and do the homework you will easily pass.

She is a very nice professor, she explains things thoroughly although can be a bit confusing at times. Orgo is going to be a hard class in general but I found keeping up with her pace and organization a bit difficult.

This professor makes the class so easy to understand. Some of the hardest topics of organic chem are made easy because of her teaching style. She is very fair with grading and her quizzes and exams are super easy and straightforward. She gives so much extra credit is almost impossible to fail or even end with a C for that matter. Her class is great
I absolutely adored Professor Vasileiou! She explains organic chemistry in an open way that makes it easier to understand. Definitely one of my favorite professors so far!!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Professor Vasileiou made organic chemistry manageable and enjoyable. I took her for the summer class which is accelerated, so be ware that it is fast paced. She is superb in her abilities to explain concepts and gives out practice problems that replicate her exams. Attending class is extra credit and it is super manageable to 4.0

It's orgo, it's going to be hard. Being online made it slightly easier to access notes, but there is lots of studying! All lectures were posted, so most attendance weren't mandatory except for quiz periods, but extra credit was offered DURING class. She's slightly hard to understand but she knows her stuff. Plenty of points in the class!

Good class. I was challenged but I learned a lot. She offered a lot of alternative assignments to continue our understanding yet have the chance to earn extra credit. Going into this class I thought I was going to hate it but now I actually enjoy organic chemistry

Dr. Vasileiou made Orgo 1 more than possible to 4.0, let alone pass. Her lectures are very clear and to the point -- take notes on what she taught, stay on top of homework, and do practice problems -- you will be just fine. She offers lots of extra credit which is helpful considering the difficult content. I highly recommend her as a professor!

Dr V was amazing for orgo 1. Her extra credit made the class very forgiving if you did bad on the quizzes. If you take notes based on the lectures the quizzes will be super easy for you. Def take this prof

Overall a cool prof, hard class material but nothing much you can do about it. Allows honors option, answers emails fairly quickly. Quizzes and tests are kinda hard but she doesn't try to trick you, answers every question during lecture, lots of opportunities for extra points.

She is an amazing professor. She has very clear grading criteria, and offers a lot of extra credit points. Many people complain about organic chemistry being a difficult subject, however she breaks it down so nicely. Her exams and quizzes are like her notes, and she is very flexible with grading and due dates! She wants to see students succeed!

I loved her. She cared so much if people understood what was going on in lecture and answered about every question people asked which was almost a little too much because at some points I wished we would have already moved on. Each quiz and exam has extra credit on it. Responds to emails very timely. Wish she was teaching orgo 2 in the spring!
Great professor. Lectures are very easy to follow and understand and she gives a TON of extra credit so it's very hard not to get an A.

She gives the best lectures and makes sure that you understand the concepts. All her lectures are on chalkboard so she does not post her lectures online. She provides ample practice problems which are really helpful. DO NOT FALL BEHIND, otherwise it will be very difficult to catch up. make sure to do the practice problems and mechanism practice.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: if you don't get an A in this class you should probably reconsider your major.

AMAZING teacher and the class is not too difficult given the topic is orgo which in itself is hard. Gives lots of extra credit, and explains topics very well. Definitely the best in the organic chemistry department at MSU. Do whatever you can to get in to her class vs others!!

Dr. Vasileiou is an AMAZING orgo prof. She is very clear on what she expects on all of her exams and 'pop quizzes.' They say the quizzes are random, but she will give you a heads up about them and tell you exactly what will be on there. As long as you attend the lecture and take thorough notes, there is no reason to not 4.0.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Everything on here is absolutely correct. Dr. Vasileiou is an amazing teacher and a LIFESAVER when it comes to orgo. Don't take anyone else. Extremely nice, everyone in the class LOVES her. Takes really difficult material and breaks it down methodically. Whatever they're paying her, it isn't enough!
Amazing professor who knows what she's talking about. She makes orgo understandable. Prepares you really well for every quiz and exam. Also gives you many opportunities for success and extra credit.

Organic chemistry sucks, but taking it with this professor made it better. Her accent makes it a little difficult to understand her at times, but you can expect exactly what the exam is going to be like and she is very clear with her notes

She writes all notes on the chalk board which i liked. Exams are all written with maybe 4 multiple choice questions. 3 exams and a final and 5 quizzes. one quiz and one exam gets dropped. Would recommend doing the honors option, a 10-15pg paper on a drug or a concept, really pretty simple. She's super nice and really chill.

Dr. Vasileiou is the best for orgo at MSU. She genuinely cares about her students and wants to see you succeed in her class. There is a lot of material and it's a tough class but she is very straightforward about what will be on exams and quizzes. Highly recommend going to recitation, it's really helpful! Extra points on every exam and quiz too.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Nothing from lecture gets posted online so if you dont go you wont pass. Class is fairly easy considering the challenging topics
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She writes lectures on the chalkboard so you need to go to class. Recitations were helpful too. An 85% is a 4.0 so it is do-able. She puts extra points on quizzes and exams. Graded by 4 quizzes, 2 exams, and a final. If you study and go to recitation it's not impossible. The workload is low since there's no homework, just studying.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: She makes the exams fair and gives students every opportunity to do well in the course, she knows the material extremely well and is very good at relaying this knowledge to her students.

This lady completely sold me on organic chemistry. The exams are reasonable, and the lectures are interesting (especially when she gives you backgrounds on some of the chemicals she's talking about). She's very helpful if you have a question, and won't hesitate to give refer you to someone for research.

Only grades are exams, quizzes, and possible extra credit. No homework to cushion your grade, but less work to do overall. Exams are very hard. However, lots of partial credit is given if it shows that you tried. Hard subject but best professor to teach it.
reviews.rating_tags: TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: This class is all lecture and memorization. She is Greek and has a very heavy accent. I would recommend taking this class at a time other than 8 am. Do the practice problems or it will be nearly impossible to pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: She's such an amazing professor and really knows what she's talking about. She's open to class discussion. She gives difficult tests, but they don't stray from the class material. She never tests on anything that she doesn't explicitly lay out in lecture. I highly recommend her for anyone taking organic chem.

Very organized professor, sets students up for success (gives EC and drops 1 exam and 1 quiz). Gives lots of practice problems. What you need to know for the exams is made very clear. She is very Greek and has an accent but I have never had a hard time understanding her. She writes very clearly on the board. I 4.0'd 251 and I'm in 252 with her now.
Great teacher. Gives lots of extra credit on each test and quiz. Also gives two chances to do extra credit assignments.
